verses.text: Jesus said to him, “Do you see these great buildings? There will not be left here one stone on another, which will not be thrown down.”

verses.name: Mark 13:3
verses.text: As he sat on the Mount of Olives opposite the temple, Peter, James, John, and Andrew asked him privately,

verses.name: Mark 13:4
verses.text: “Tell us, when will these things be? What is the sign that these things are all about to be fulfilled?”

verses.name: Mark 13:5
verses.text: Jesus, answering, began to tell them, “Be careful that no one leads you astray.

verses.name: Mark 13:6
verses.text: For many will come in my name, saying, ‘I am he!’ and will lead many astray.

verses.name: Mark 13:7
verses.text: “When you hear of wars and rumors of wars, don’t be troubled. For those must happen, but the end is not yet.

verses.name: Mark 13:8
verses.text: For nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom. There will be earthquakes in various places. There will be famines and troubles. These things are the beginning of birth pains.

verses.name: Mark 13:9
verses.text: But watch yourselves, for they will deliver you up to councils. You will be beaten in synagogues. You will stand before rulers and kings for my sake, for a testimony to them.

verses.name: Mark 13:10
verses.text: The Good News must first be preached to all the nations.

verses.name: Mark 13:11
verses.text: When they lead you away and deliver you up, don’t be anxious beforehand, or premeditate what you will say, but say whatever will be given you in that hour. For it is not you who speak, but the Holy Spirit.

verses.name: Mark 13:12
verses.text: “Brother will deliver up brother to death, and the father his child. Children will rise up against parents, and cause them to be put to death.

verses.name: Mark 13:13
verses.text: You will be hated by all men for my name’s sake, but he who endures to the end, the same will be saved.

verses.name: Mark 13:14
verses.text: But when you see the abomination of desolation, spoken of by Daniel the prophet, standing where it ought not (let the reader understand), then let those who are in Judea flee to the mountains,

verses.name: Mark 13:15
verses.text: and let him who is on the housetop not go down, nor enter in, to take anything out of his house.

verses.name: Mark 13:16
verses.text: Let him who is in the field not return back to take his cloak.

verses.name: Mark 13:17
verses.text: But woe to those who are with child and to those who nurse babies in those days!

verses.name: Mark 13:18
verses.text: Pray that your flight won’t be in the winter.

verses.name: Mark 13:19
verses.text: For in those days there will be oppression, such as there has not been the like from the beginning of the creation which God created until now, and never will be.

verses.name: Mark 13:20
verses.text: Unless the Lord had shortened the days, no flesh would have been saved; but for the sake of the chosen ones, whom he picked out, he shortened the days.

verses.name: Mark 13:21
verses.text: Then if anyone tells you, ‘Look, here is the Christ!’ or, ‘Look, there!’ don’t believe it.

verses.name: Mark 13:22
verses.text: For there will arise false christs and false prophets, and will show signs and wonders, that they may lead astray, if possible, even the chosen ones.

verses.name: Mark 13:23
verses.text: But you watch. “Behold, I have told you all things beforehand.

verses.name: Mark 13:24
verses.text: But in those days, after that oppression, the sun will be darkened, the moon will not give its light,

verses.name: Mark 13:25
verses.text: the stars will be falling from the sky, and the powers that are in the heavens will be shaken.

verses.name: Mark 13:26
verses.text: Then they will see the Son of Man coming in clouds with great power and glory.

verses.name: Mark 13:27
verses.text: Then he will send out his angels, and will gather together his chosen ones from the four winds, from the ends of the earth to the ends of the sky.

verses.name: Mark 13:28
verses.text: “Now from the fig tree, learn this parable. When the branch has now become tender, and produces its leaves, you know that the summer is near;

verses.name: Mark 13:29
verses.text: even so you also, when you see these things coming to pass, know that it is near, at the doors.

verses.name: Mark 13:30
verses.text: Most certainly I say to you, this generation will not pass away until all these things happen.

verses.name: Mark 13:31
verses.text: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will not pass away.

verses.name: Mark 13:32
verses.text: But of that day or that hour no one knows, not even the angels in heaven, nor the Son, but only the Father.

verses.name: Mark 13:33
verses.text: Watch, keep alert, and pray; for you don’t know when the time is.

verses.name: Mark 13:34
verses.text: “It is like a man, traveling to another country, having left his house, and given authority to his servants, and to each one his work, and also commanded the doorkeeper to keep watch.

verses.name: Mark 13:35
verses.text: Watch therefore, for you don’t know when the lord of the house is coming, whether at evening, or at midnight, or when the rooster crows, or in the morning;

verses.name: Mark 13:36
verses.text: lest coming suddenly he might find you sleeping.

verses.name: Mark 13:37
verses.text: What I tell you, I tell all: Watch.”

verses.name: Mark 14:1
verses.text: It was now two days before the feast of the Passover and the unleavened bread, and the chief priests and the scribes sought how they might seize him by deception, and kill him.

verses.name: Mark 14:2
verses.text: For they said, “Not during the feast, because there might be a riot among the people.”

verses.name: Mark 14:3
verses.text: While he was at Bethany, in the house of Simon the leper, as he sat at the table, a woman came having an alabaster jar of ointment of pure nard—very costly. She broke the jar, and poured it over his head.

verses.name: Mark 14:4
verses.text: But there were some who were indignant among themselves, saying, “Why has this ointment been wasted?

verses.name: Mark 14:5
verses.text: For this might have been sold for more than three hundred denarii, and given to the poor.” They grumbled against her.

verses.name: Mark 14:6
verses.text: But Jesus said, “Leave her alone. Why do you trouble her? She has done a good work for me.

verses.name: Mark 14:7
verses.text: For you always have the poor with you, and whenever you want to, you can do them good; but you will not always have me.

verses.name: Mark 14:8
verses.text: She has done what she could. She has anointed my body beforehand for the burying.

verses.name: Mark 14:9
verses.text: Most certainly I tell you, wherever this Good News may be preached throughout the whole world, that which this woman has done will also be spoken of for a memorial of her.”

verses.name: Mark 14:10
verses.text: Judas Iscariot, who was one of the twelve, went away to the chief priests, that he might deliver him to them.

verses.name: Mark 14:11
verses.text: They, when they heard it, were glad, and promised to give him money. He sought how he might conveniently deliver him.

verses.name: Mark 14:12
verses.text: On the first day of unleavened bread, when they sacrificed the Passover, his disciples asked him, “Where do you want us to go and prepare that you may eat the Passover?”

verses.name: Mark 14:13
verses.text: He sent two of his disciples, and said to them, “Go into the city, and there you will meet a man carrying a pitcher of water. Follow him,

verses.name: Mark 14:14
verses.text: and wherever he enters in, tell the master of the house, ‘The Teacher says, “Where is the guest room, where I may eat the Passover with my disciples?”’

verses.name: Mark 14:15
verses.text: He will himself show you a large upper room furnished and ready. Get ready for us there.”

verses.name: Mark 14:16
verses.text: His disciples went out, and came into the city, and found things as he had said to them, and they prepared the Passover.

verses.name: Mark 14:17
verses.text: When it was evening he came with the twelve.

verses.name: Mark 14:18
verses.text: As they sat and were eating, Jesus said, “Most certainly I tell you, one of you will betray me—he who eats with me.”

verses.name: Mark 14:19
verses.text: They began to be sorrowful, and to ask him one by one, “Surely not I?” And another said, “Surely not I?”

verses.name: Mark 14:20
verses.text: He answered them, “It is one of the twelve, he who dips with me in the dish.

verses.name: Mark 14:21
verses.text: For the Son of Man goes, even as it is written about him, but woe to that man by whom the Son of Man is betrayed! It would be better for that man if he had not been born.”

verses.name: Mark 14:22
verses.text: As they were eating, Jesus took bread, and when he had blessed, he broke it, and gave to them, and said, “Take, eat. This is my body.”

verses.name: Mark 14:23
verses.text: He took the cup, and when he had given thanks, he gave to them. They all drank of it.

verses.name: Mark 14:24
verses.text: He said to them, “This is my blood of the new covenant, which is poured out for many.

verses.name: Mark 14:25
verses.text: Most certainly I tell you, I will no more drink of the fruit of the vine, until that day when I drink it anew in God’s Kingdom.”

verses.name: Mark 14:26
verses.text: When they had sung a hymn, they went out to the Mount of Olives.

verses.name: Mark 14:27
verses.text: Jesus said to them, “All of you will be made to stumble because of me tonight, for it is written, ‘I will strike the shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered.’

verses.name: Mark 14:28
verses.text: However, after I am raised up, I will go before you into Galilee.”

verses.name: Mark 14:29
verses.text: But Peter said to him, “Although all will be offended, yet I will not.”

verses.name: Mark 14:30
verses.text: Jesus said to him, “Most certainly I tell you, that you today, even this night, before the rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times.”

verses.name: Mark 14:31
verses.text: But he spoke all the more, “If I must die with you, I will not deny you.” They all said the same thing.

verses.name: Mark 14:32
verses.text: They came to a place which was named Gethsemane. He said to his disciples, “Sit here, while I pray.”

verses.name: Mark 14:33
verses.text: He took with him Peter, James, and John, and began to be greatly troubled and distressed.

verses.name: Mark 14:34
verses.text: He said to them, “My soul is exceedingly sorrowful, even to death. Stay here, and watch.”

verses.name: Mark 14:35
verses.text: He went forward a little, and fell on the ground, and prayed that, if it were possible, the hour might pass away from him.

verses.name: Mark 14:36
verses.text: He said, “Abba, Father, all things are possible to you. Please remove this cup from me. However, not what I desire, but what you desire.”

verses.name: Mark 14:37
verses.text: He came and found them sleeping, and said to Peter, “Simon, are you sleeping? Couldn’t you watch one hour?

verses.name: Mark 14:38
verses.text: Watch and pray, that you may not enter into temptation. The spirit indeed is willing, but the flesh is weak.”

verses.name: Mark 14:39
verses.text: Again he went away, and prayed, saying the same words.

verses.name: Mark 14:40
verses.text: Again he returned, and found them sleeping, for their eyes were very heavy, and they didn’t know what to answer him.

verses.name: Mark 14:41
verses.text: He came the third time, and said to them, “Sleep on now, and take your rest. It is enough. The hour has come. Behold, the Son of Man is betrayed into the hands of sinners.

verses.name: Mark 14:42
verses.text: Arise, let us be going. Behold, he who betrays me is at hand.”

verses.name: Mark 14:43
verses.text: Immediately, while he was still speaking, Judas, one of the twelve, came—and with him a multitude with swords and clubs, from the chief priests, the scribes, and the elders.

verses.name: Mark 14:44
verses.text: Now he who betrayed him had given them a sign, saying, “Whomever I will kiss, that is he. Seize him, and lead him away safely.”

verses.name: Mark 14:45
verses.text: When he had come, immediately he came to him, and said, “Rabbi! Rabbi!” and kissed him.

verses.name: Mark 14:46
verses.text: They laid their hands on him, and seized him.

verses.name: Mark 14:47
verses.text: But a certain one of those who stood by drew his sword, and struck the servant of the high priest, and cut off his ear.

verses.name: Mark 14:48
verses.text: Jesus answered them, “Have you come out, as against a robber, with swords and clubs to seize me?

verses.name: Mark 14:49
verses.text: I was daily with you in the temple teaching, and you didn’t arrest me. But this is so that the Scriptures might be fulfilled.”

verses.name: Mark 14:50
verses.text: They all left him, and fled.

verses.name: Mark 14:51
verses.text: A certain young man followed him, having a linen cloth thrown around himself over his naked body. The young men grabbed him,

verses.name: Mark 14:52
verses.text: but he left the linen cloth, and fled from them naked.

verses.name: Mark 14:53
verses.text: They led Jesus away to the high priest. All the chief priests, the elders, and the scribes came together with him.

verses.name: Mark 14:54
verses.text: Peter had followed him from a distance, until he came into the court of the high priest. He was sitting with the officers, and warming himself in the light of the fire.

verses.name: Mark 14:55
verses.text: Now the chief priests and the whole council sought witnesses against Jesus to put him to death, and found none.

verses.name: Mark 14:56
verses.text: For many gave false testimony against him, and their testimony didn’t agree with each other.

verses.name: Mark 14:57
verses.text: Some stood up, and gave false testimony against him, saying,

verses.name: Mark 14:58
verses.text: “We heard him say, ‘I will destroy this temple that is made with hands, and in three days I will build another made without hands.’”

verses.name: Mark 14:59
verses.text: Even so, their testimony did not agree.

verses.name: Mark 14:60
verses.text: The high priest stood up in the middle, and asked Jesus, “Have you no answer? What is it which these testify against you?”

verses.name: Mark 14:61
verses.text: But he stayed quiet, and answered nothing. Again the high priest asked him, “Are you the Christ, the Son of the Blessed?”

verses.name: Mark 14:62
verses.text: Jesus said, “I am. You will see the Son of Man sitting at the right hand of Power, and coming with the clouds of the sky.”

verses.name: Mark 14:63
verses.text: The high priest tore his clothes, and said, “What further need have we of witnesses?

verses.name: Mark 14:64
verses.text: You have heard the blasphemy! What do you think?” They all condemned him to be worthy of death.

verses.name: Mark 14:65
verses.text: Some began to spit on him, and to cover his face, and to beat him with fists, and to tell him, “Prophesy!” The officers struck him with the palms of their hands.

verses.name: Mark 14:66
verses.text: As Peter was in the courtyard below, one of the maids of the high priest came,

verses.name: Mark 14:67
verses.text: and seeing Peter warming himself, she looked at him, and said, “You were also with the Nazarene, Jesus!”

verses.name: Mark 14:68
verses.text: But he denied it, saying, “I neither know, nor understand what you are saying.” He went out on the porch, and the rooster crowed.

verses.name: Mark 14:69
verses.text: The maid saw him, and began again to tell those who stood by, “This is one of them.”

verses.name: Mark 14:70
verses.text: But he again denied it. After a little while again those who stood by said to Peter, “You truly are one of them, for you are a Galilean, and your speech shows it.”

verses.name: Mark 14:71
verses.text: But he began to curse, and to swear, “I don’t know this man of whom you speak!”

verses.name: Mark 14:72
verses.text: The rooster crowed the second time. Peter remembered the word, how that Jesus said to him, “Before the rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times.” When he thought about that, he wept.

verses.name: Mark 15:1
verses.text: Immediately in the morning the chief priests, with the elders and scribes, and the whole council, held a consultation, and bound Jesus, and carried him away, and delivered him up to Pilate.

verses.name: Mark 15:2
verses.text: Pilate asked him, “Are you the King of the Jews?” He answered, “So you say.”

verses.name: Mark 15:3
verses.text: The chief priests accused him of many things.

verses.name: Mark 15:4
verses.text: Pilate again asked him, “Have you no answer? See how many things they testify against you!”

verses.name: Mark 15:5
verses.text: But Jesus made no further answer, so that Pilate marveled.

verses.name: Mark 15:6
verses.text: Now at the feast he used to release to them one prisoner, whom they asked of him.

verses.name: Mark 15:7
verses.text: There was one called Barabbas, bound with his fellow insurgents, men who in the insurrection had committed murder.

verses.name: Mark 15:8
verses.text: The multitude, crying aloud, began to ask him to do as he always did for them.

verses.name: Mark 15:9
verses.text: Pilate answered them, saying, “Do you want me to release to you the King of the Jews?”

verses.name: Mark 15:10
verses.text: For he perceived that for envy the chief priests had delivered him up.

verses.name: Mark 15:11
verses.text: But the chief priests stirred up the multitude, that he should release Barabbas to them instead.

verses.name: Mark 15:12
verses.text: Pilate again asked them, “What then should I do to him whom you call the King of the Jews?”

verses.name: Mark 15:13
verses.text: They cried out again, “Crucify him!”

verses.name: Mark 15:14
verses.text: Pilate said to them, “Why, what evil has he done?” But they cried out exceedingly, “Crucify him!”

verses.name: Mark 15:15
verses.text: Pilate, wishing to please the multitude, released Barabbas to them, and handed over Jesus, when he had flogged him, to be crucified.

verses.name: Mark 15:16
verses.text: The soldiers led him away within the court, which is the Praetorium; and they called together the whole cohort.

verses.name: Mark 15:17
verses.text: They clothed him with purple, and weaving a crown of thorns, they put it on him.

verses.name: Mark 15:18
verses.text: They began to salute him, “Hail, King of the Jews!”

verses.name: Mark 15:19
verses.text: They struck his head with a reed, and spat on him, and bowing their knees, did homage to him.

verses.name: Mark 15:20
verses.text: When they had mocked him, they took the purple off him, and put his own garments on him. They led him out to crucify him.

verses.name: Mark 15:21
verses.text: They compelled one passing by, coming from the country, Simon of Cyrene, the father of Alexander and Rufus, to go with them, that he might bear his cross.

verses.name: Mark 15:22
verses.text: They brought him to the place called Golgotha, which is, being interpreted, “The place of a skull.”

verses.name: Mark 15:23
verses.text: They offered him wine mixed with myrrh to drink, but he didn’t take it.

verses.name: Mark 15:24
verses.text: Crucifying him, they parted his garments among them, casting lots on them, what each should take.

verses.name: Mark 15:25
verses.text: It was the third hour, and they crucified him.

verses.name: Mark 15:26
verses.text: The superscription of his accusation was written over him, “THE KING OF THE JEWS.”

verses.name: Mark 15:27
verses.text: With him they crucified two robbers; one on his right hand, and one on his left.

verses.name: Mark 15:28
verses.text: The Scripture was fulfilled, which says, “He was counted with transgressors.”

verses.name: Mark 15:29
verses.text: Those who passed by blasphemed him, wagging their heads, and saying, “Ha! You who destroy the temple, and build it in three days,

verses.name: Mark 15:30
verses.text: save yourself, and come down from the cross!”

verses.name: Mark 15:31
verses.text: Likewise, also the chief priests mocking among themselves with the scribes said, “He saved others. He can’t save himself.

verses.name: Mark 15:32
verses.text: Let the Christ, the King of Israel, now come down from the cross, that we may see and believe him.” Those who were crucified with him also insulted him.

verses.name: Mark 15:33
verses.text: When the sixth hour had come, there was darkness over the whole land until the ninth hour.

verses.name: Mark 15:34
verses.text: At the ninth hour Jesus cried with a loud voice, saying, “Eloi, Eloi, lama sabachthani?” which is, being interpreted, “My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?”

verses.name: Mark 15:35
verses.text: Some of those who stood by, when they heard it, said, “Behold, he is calling Elijah.”

verses.name: Mark 15:36
verses.text: One ran, and filling a sponge full of vinegar, put it on a reed, and gave it to him to drink, saying, “Let him be. Let’s see whether Elijah comes to take him down.”

verses.name: Mark 15:37
verses.text: Jesus cried out with a loud voice, and gave up the spirit.

verses.name: Mark 15:38
verses.text: The veil of the temple was torn in two from the top to the bottom.

verses.name: Mark 15:39
verses.text: When the centurion, who stood by opposite him, saw that he cried out like this and breathed his last, he said, “Truly this man was the Son of God!”

verses.name: Mark 15:40
verses.text: There were also women watching from afar, among whom were both Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James the less and of Joses, and Salome;

verses.name: Mark 15:41
verses.text: who, when he was in Galilee, followed him, and served him; and many other women who came up with him to Jerusalem.

verses.name: Mark 15:42
verses.text: When evening had now come, because it was the Preparation Day, that is, the day before the Sabbath,

verses.name: Mark 15:43
verses.text: Joseph of Arimathaea, a prominent council member who also himself was looking for God’s Kingdom, came. He boldly went in to Pilate, and asked for Jesus’ body.

verses.name: Mark 15:44
verses.text: Pilate marveled if he were already dead; and summoning the centurion, he asked him whether he had been dead long.

verses.name: Mark 15:45
verses.text: When he found out from the centurion, he granted the body to Joseph.

verses.name: Mark 15:46
verses.text: He bought a linen cloth, and taking him down, wound him in the linen cloth, and laid him in a tomb which had been cut out of a rock. He rolled a stone against the door of the tomb.

verses.name: Mark 15:47
verses.text: Mary Magdalene and Mary, the mother of Joses, saw where he was laid.

verses.name: Mark 16:1
verses.text: When the Sabbath was past, Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James, and Salome, bought spices, that they might come and anoint him.

verses.name: Mark 16:2
verses.text: Very early on the first day of the week, they came to the tomb when the sun had risen.

verses.name: Mark 16:3
verses.text: They were saying among themselves, “Who will roll away the stone from the door of the tomb for us?”

verses.name: Mark 16:4
verses.text: for it was very big. Looking up, they saw that the stone was rolled back.

verses.name: Mark 16:5
verses.text: Entering into the tomb, they saw a young man sitting on the right side, dressed in a white robe, and they were amazed.

verses.name: Mark 16:6
verses.text: He said to them, “Don’t be amazed. You seek Jesus, the Nazarene, who has been crucified. He has risen. He is not here. Behold, the place where they laid him!

verses.name: Mark 16:7
verses.text: But go, tell his disciples and Peter, ‘He goes before you into Galilee. There you will see him, as he said to you.’”

verses.name: Mark 16:8
verses.text: They went out, and fled from the tomb, for trembling and astonishment had come on them. They said nothing to anyone; for they were afraid.

verses.name: Mark 16:9
verses.text: Now when he had risen early on the first day of the week, he appeared first to Mary Magdalene, from whom he had cast out seven demons.

verses.name: Mark 16:10
verses.text: She went and told those who had been with him, as they mourned and wept.

verses.name: Mark 16:11
verses.text: When they heard that he was alive, and had been seen by her, they disbelieved.

verses.name: Mark 16:12
verses.text: After these things he was revealed in another form to two of them, as they walked, on their way into the country.

verses.name: Mark 16:13
verses.text: They went away and told it to the rest. They didn’t believe them, either.

verses.name: Mark 16:14
verses.text: Afterward he was revealed to the eleven themselves as they sat at the table, and he rebuked them for their unbelief and hardness of heart, because they didn’t believe those who had seen him after he had risen.

verses.name: Mark 16:15
verses.text: He said to them, “Go into all the world, and preach the Good News to the whole creation.

verses.name: Mark 16:16
verses.text: He who believes and is baptized will be saved; but he who disbelieves will be condemned.

verses.name: Mark 16:17
verses.text: These signs will accompany those who believe: in my name they will cast out demons; they will speak with new languages;

verses.name: Mark 16:18
verses.text: they will take up serpents; and if they drink any deadly thing, it will in no way hurt them; they will lay hands on the sick, and they will recover.”

verses.name: Mark 16:19
verses.text: So then the Lord, after he had spoken to them, was received up into heaven, and sat down at the right hand of God.

verses.name: Mark 16:20
verses.text: They went out, and preached everywhere, the Lord working with them, and confirming the word by the signs that followed. Amen.

verses.name: Luke 1:1
verses.text: Since many have undertaken to set in order a narrative concerning those matters which have been fulfilled among us,

verses.name: Luke 1:2
verses.text: even as those who from the beginning were eyewitnesses and servants of the word delivered them to us,

verses.name: Luke 1:3
verses.text: it seemed good to me also, having traced the course of all things accurately from the first, to write to you in order, most excellent Theophilus;

verses.name: Luke 1:4
verses.text: that you might know the certainty concerning the things in which you were instructed.

verses.name: Luke 1:5
verses.text: There was in the days of Herod, the king of Judea, a certain priest named Zacharias, of the priestly division of Abijah. He had a wife of the daughters of Aaron, and her name was Elizabeth.

verses.name: Luke 1:6
verses.text: They were both righteous before God, walking blamelessly in all the commandments and ordinances of the Lord.

verses.name: Luke 1:7
verses.text: But they had no child, because Elizabeth was barren, and they both were well advanced in years.

verses.name: Luke 1:8
verses.text: Now while he executed the priest’s office before God in the order of his division,

verses.name: Luke 1:9
verses.text: according to the custom of the priest’s office, his lot was to enter into the temple of the Lord and burn incense.

verses.name: Luke 1:10
verses.text: The whole multitude of the people were praying outside at the hour of incense.

verses.name: Luke 1:11
verses.text: An angel of the Lord appeared to him, standing on the right side of the altar of incense.

verses.name: Luke 1:12
verses.text: Zacharias was troubled when he saw him, and fear fell upon him.

verses.name: Luke 1:13
verses.text: But the angel said to him, “Don’t be afraid, Zacharias, because your request has been heard, and your wife, Elizabeth, will bear you a son, and you shall call his name John.

verses.name: Luke 1:14
verses.text: You will have joy and gladness; and many will rejoice at his birth.

verses.name: Luke 1:15
verses.text: For he will be great in the sight of the Lord, and he will drink no wine nor strong drink. He will be filled with the Holy Spirit, even from his mother’s womb.

verses.name: Luke 1:16
verses.text: He will turn many of the children of Israel to the Lord, their God.

verses.name: Luke 1:17
verses.text: He will go before him in the spirit and power of Elijah, ‘to turn the hearts of the fathers to the children,’ and the disobedient to the wisdom of the just; to prepare a people prepared for the Lord.”

verses.name: Luke 1:18
verses.text: Zacharias said to the angel, “How can I be sure of this? For I am an old man, and my wife is well advanced in years.”

verses.name: Luke 1:19
verses.text: The angel answered him, “I am Gabriel, who stands in the presence of God. I was sent to speak to you, and to bring you this good news.

verses.name: Luke 1:20
verses.text: Behold, you will be silent and not able to speak, until the day that these things will happen, because you didn’t believe my words, which will be fulfilled in their proper time.”

verses.name: Luke 1:21
verses.text: The people were waiting for Zacharias, and they marveled that he delayed in the temple.

verses.name: Luke 1:22
verses.text: When he came out, he could not speak to them, and they perceived that he had seen a vision in the temple. He continued making signs to them, and remained mute.

verses.name: Luke 1:23
verses.text: When the days of his service were fulfilled, he departed to his house.

verses.name: Luke 1:24
verses.text: After these days Elizabeth, his wife, conceived, and she hid herself five months, saying,

verses.name: Luke 1:25
verses.text: “Thus has the Lord done to me in the days in which he looked at me, to take away my reproach among men.”

verses.name: Luke 1:26
verses.text: Now in the sixth month, the angel Gabriel was sent from God to a city of Galilee, named Nazareth,

verses.name: Luke 1:27
verses.text: to a virgin pledged to be married to a man whose name was Joseph, of David’s house. The virgin’s name was Mary.

verses.name: Luke 1:28
verses.text: Having come in, the angel said to her, “Rejoice, you highly favored one! The Lord is with you. Blessed are you among women!”

verses.name: Luke 1:29
verses.text: But when she saw him, she was greatly troubled at the saying, and considered what kind of salutation this might be.

verses.name: Luke 1:30
verses.text: The angel said to her, “Don’t be afraid, Mary, for you have found favor with God.

verses.name: Luke 1:31
verses.text: Behold, you will conceive in your womb, and give birth to a son, and will call his name ‘Jesus.’

verses.name: Luke 1:32
verses.text: He will be great, and will be called the Son of the Most High. The Lord God will give him the throne of his father, David,

verses.name: Luke 1:33
verses.text: and he will reign over the house of Jacob forever. There will be no end to his Kingdom.”

verses.name: Luke 1:34
verses.text: Mary said to the angel, “How can this be, seeing I am a virgin?”

verses.name: Luke 1:35
verses.text: The angel answered her, “The Holy Spirit will come on you, and the power of the Most High will overshadow you. Therefore also the holy one who is born from you will be called the Son of God.

verses.name: Luke 1:36
verses.text: Behold, Elizabeth, your relative, also has conceived a son in her old age; and this is the sixth month with her who was called barren.

verses.name: Luke 1:37
verses.text: For nothing spoken by God is impossible.”

verses.name: Luke 1:38
verses.text: Mary said, “Behold, the servant of the Lord; let it be done to me according to your word.” The angel departed from her.

verses.name: Luke 1:39
verses.text: Mary arose in those days and went into the hill country with haste, into a city of Judah,

verses.name: Luke 1:40
verses.text: and entered into the house of Zacharias and greeted Elizabeth.

verses.name: Luke 1:41
verses.text: When Elizabeth heard Mary’s greeting, the baby leaped in her womb, and Elizabeth was filled with the Holy Spirit.

verses.name: Luke 1:42
verses.text: She called out with a loud voice, and said, “Blessed are you among women, and blessed is the fruit of your womb!

verses.name: Luke 1:43
verses.text: Why am I so favored, that the mother of my Lord should come to me?

verses.name: Luke 1:44
verses.text: For behold, when the voice of your greeting came into my ears, the baby leaped in my womb for joy!

verses.name: Luke 1:45
verses.text: Blessed is she who believed, for there will be a fulfillment of the things which have been spoken to her from the Lord!”

verses.name: Luke 1:46
verses.text: Mary said, “My soul magnifies the Lord.

verses.name: Luke 1:47
verses.text: My spirit has rejoiced in God my Savior,

verses.name: Luke 1:48
verses.text: for he has looked at the humble state of his servant. For behold, from now on, all generations will call me blessed.

verses.name: Luke 1:49
verses.text: For he who is mighty has done great things for me. Holy is his name.

verses.name: Luke 1:50
verses.text: His mercy is for generations of generations on those who fear him.

verses.name: Luke 1:51
verses.text: He has shown strength with his arm. He has scattered the proud in the imagination of their hearts.

verses.name: Luke 1:52
verses.text: He has put down princes from their thrones. And has exalted the lowly.

verses.name: Luke 1:53
verses.text: He has filled the hungry with good things. He has sent the rich away empty.

verses.name: Luke 1:54
verses.text: He has given help to Israel, his servant, that he might remember mercy,

verses.name: Luke 1:55
verses.text: As he spoke to our fathers, to Abraham and his offspring forever.”

verses.name: Luke 1:56
verses.text: Mary stayed with her about three months, and then returned to her house.

verses.name: Luke 1:57
verses.text: Now the time that Elizabeth should give birth was fulfilled, and she gave birth to a son.

verses.name: Luke 1:58
verses.text: Her neighbors and her relatives heard that the Lord had magnified his mercy towards her, and they rejoiced with her.

verses.name: Luke 1:59
verses.text: On the eighth day, they came to circumcise the child; and they would have called him Zacharias, after the name of his father.

verses.name: Luke 1:60
verses.text: His mother answered, “Not so; but he will be called John.”

verses.name: Luke 1:61
verses.text: They said to her, “There is no one among your relatives who is called by this name.”

verses.name: Luke 1:62
verses.text: They made signs to his father, what he would have him called.

verses.name: Luke 1:63
verses.text: He asked for a writing tablet, and wrote, “His name is John.” They all marveled.

verses.name: Luke 1:64
verses.text: His mouth was opened immediately, and his tongue freed, and he spoke, blessing God.

verses.name: Luke 1:65
verses.text: Fear came on all who lived around them, and all these sayings were talked about throughout all the hill country of Judea.

verses.name: Luke 1:66
verses.text: All who heard them laid them up in their heart, saying, “What then will this child be?” The hand of the Lord was with him.

verses.name: Luke 1:67
verses.text: His father, Zacharias, was filled with the Holy Spirit, and prophesied, saying,

verses.name: Luke 1:68
verses.text: “Blessed be the Lord, the God of Israel, for he has visited and redeemed his people;

verses.name: Luke 1:69
verses.text: and has raised up a horn of salvation for us in the house of his servant David

verses.name: Luke 1:70
verses.text: (as he spoke by the mouth of his holy prophets who have been from of old),

verses.name: Luke 1:71
verses.text: salvation from our enemies, and from the hand of all who hate us;

verses.name: Luke 1:72
verses.text: to show mercy towards our fathers, to remember his holy covenant,

verses.name: Luke 1:73
verses.text: the oath which he swore to Abraham, our father,

verses.name: Luke 1:74
verses.text: to grant to us that we, being delivered out of the hand of our enemies, should serve him without fear,

verses.name: Luke 1:75
verses.text: In holiness and righteousness before him all the days of our life.

verses.name: Luke 1:76
verses.text: And you, child, will be called a prophet of the Most High, for you will go before the face of the Lord to prepare his ways,

verses.name: Luke 1:77
verses.text: to give knowledge of salvation to his people by the remission of their sins,

verses.name: Luke 1:78
verses.text: because of the tender mercy of our God, whereby the dawn from on high will visit us,

verses.name: Luke 1:79
verses.text: to shine on those who sit in darkness and the shadow of death; to guide our feet into the way of peace.”

verses.name: Luke 1:80
verses.text: The child was growing, and becoming strong in spirit, and was in the desert until the day of his public appearance to Israel.

verses.name: Luke 2:1
verses.text: Now in those days, a decree went out from Caesar Augustus that all the world should be enrolled.

verses.name: Luke 2:2
verses.text: This was the first enrollment made when Quirinius was governor of Syria.

verses.name: Luke 2:3
verses.text: All went to enroll themselves, everyone to his own city.

verses.name: Luke 2:4
verses.text: Joseph also went up from Galilee, out of the city of Nazareth, into Judea, to David’s city, which is called Bethlehem, because he was of the house and family of David;

verses.name: Luke 2:5
verses.text: to enroll himself with Mary, who was pledged to be married to him as wife, being pregnant.

verses.name: Luke 2:6
verses.text: While they were there, the day had come for her to give birth.

verses.name: Luke 2:7
verses.text: She gave birth to her firstborn son. She wrapped him in bands of cloth, and laid him in a feeding trough, because there was no room for them in the inn.

verses.name: Luke 2:8
verses.text: There were shepherds in the same country staying in the field, and keeping watch by night over their flock.

verses.name: Luke 2:9
verses.text: Behold, an angel of the Lord stood by them, and the glory of the Lord shone around them, and they were terrified.

verses.name: Luke 2:10
verses.text: The angel said to them, “Don’t be afraid, for behold, I bring you good news of great joy which will be to all the people.

verses.name: Luke 2:11
verses.text: For there is born to you today, in David’s city, a Savior, who is Christ the Lord.

verses.name: Luke 2:12
verses.text: This is the sign to you: you will find a baby wrapped in strips of cloth, lying in a feeding trough.”

verses.name: Luke 2:13
verses.text: Suddenly, there was with the angel a multitude of the heavenly army praising God, and saying,

verses.name: Luke 2:14
verses.text: “Glory to God in the highest, on earth peace, good will toward men.”

verses.name: Luke 2:15
verses.text: When the angels went away from them into the sky, the shepherds said to one another, “Let’s go to Bethlehem, now, and see this thing that has happened, which the Lord has made known to us.”

verses.name: Luke 2:16
verses.text: They came with haste, and found both Mary and Joseph, and the baby was lying in the feeding trough.

verses.name: Luke 2:17
verses.text: When they saw it, they publicized widely the saying which was spoken to them about this child.

verses.name: Luke 2:18
verses.text: All who heard it wondered at the things which were spoken to them by the shepherds.

verses.name: Luke 2:19
verses.text: But Mary kept all these sayings, pondering them in her heart.

verses.name: Luke 2:20
verses.text: The shepherds returned, glorifying and praising God for all the things that they had heard and seen, just as it was told them.

verses.name: Luke 2:21
verses.text: When eight days were fulfilled for the circumcision of the child, his name was called Jesus, which was given by the angel before he was conceived in the womb.

verses.name: Luke 2:22
verses.text: When the days of their purification according to the law of Moses were fulfilled, they brought him up to Jerusalem, to present him to the Lord

verses.name: Luke 2:23
verses.text: (as it is written in the law of the Lord, “Every male who opens the womb shall be called holy to the Lord”),

verses.name: Luke 2:24
verses.text: and to offer a sacrifice according to that which is said in the law of the Lord, “A pair of turtledoves, or two young pigeons.”

verses.name: Luke 2:25
verses.text: Behold, there was a man in Jerusalem whose name was Simeon. This man was righteous and devout, looking for the consolation of Israel, and the Holy Spirit was on him.

verses.name: Luke 2:26
verses.text: It had been revealed to him by the Holy Spirit that he should not see death before he had seen the Lord’s Christ.

verses.name: Luke 2:27
verses.text: He came in the Spirit into the temple. When the parents brought in the child, Jesus, that they might do concerning him according to the custom of the law,

verses.name: Luke 2:28
verses.text: then he received him into his arms, and blessed God, and said,

verses.name: Luke 2:29
verses.text: “Now you are releasing your servant, Master, according to your word, in peace;

verses.name: Luke 2:30
verses.text: for my eyes have seen your salvation,

verses.name: Luke 2:31
verses.text: which you have prepared before the face of all peoples;

verses.name: Luke 2:32
verses.text: a light for revelation to the nations, and the glory of your people Israel.”

verses.name: Luke 2:33
verses.text: Joseph and his mother were marveling at the things which were spoken concerning him,

verses.name: Luke 2:34
verses.text: and Simeon blessed them, and said to Mary, his mother, “Behold, this child is set for the falling and the rising of many in Israel, and for a sign which is spoken against.

verses.name: Luke 2:35
verses.text: Yes, a sword will pierce through your own soul, that the thoughts of many hearts may be revealed.”

verses.name: Luke 2:36
verses.text: There was one Anna, a prophetess, the daughter of Phanuel, of the tribe of Asher (she was of a great age, having lived with a husband seven years from her virginity,

verses.name: Luke 2:37
verses.text: and she had been a widow for about eighty-four years), who didn’t depart from the temple, worshiping with fastings and petitions night and day.

verses.name: Luke 2:38
verses.text: Coming up at that very hour, she gave thanks to the Lord, and spoke of him to all those who were looking for redemption in Jerusalem.

verses.name: Luke 2:39
verses.text: When they had accomplished all things that were according to the law of the Lord, they returned into Galilee, to their own city, Nazareth.

verses.name: Luke 2:40
verses.text: The child was growing, and was becoming strong in spirit, being filled with wisdom, and the grace of God was upon him.

verses.name: Luke 2:41
verses.text: His parents went every year to Jerusalem at the feast of the Passover.

verses.name: Luke 2:42
verses.text: When he was twelve years old, they went up to Jerusalem according to the custom of the feast,

verses.name: Luke 2:43
verses.text: and when they had fulfilled the days, as they were returning, the boy Jesus stayed behind in Jerusalem. Joseph and his mother didn’t know it,

verses.name: Luke 2:44
verses.text: but supposing him to be in the company, they went a day’s journey, and they looked for him among their relatives and acquaintances.

verses.name: Luke 2:45
verses.text: When they didn’t find him, they returned to Jerusalem, looking for him.

verses.name: Luke 2:46
verses.text: After three days they found him in the temple, sitting in the middle of the teachers, both listening to them, and asking them questions.

verses.name: Luke 2:47
verses.text: All who heard him were amazed at his understanding and his answers.

verses.name: Luke 2:48
verses.text: When they saw him, they were astonished, and his mother said to him, “Son, why have you treated us this way? Behold, your father and I were anxiously looking for you.”

verses.name: Luke 2:49
verses.text: He said to them, “Why were you looking for me? Didn’t you know that I must be in my Father’s house?”

verses.name: Luke 2:50
verses.text: They didn’t understand the saying which he spoke to them.

verses.name: Luke 2:51
verses.text: And he went down with them, and came to Nazareth. He was subject to them, and his mother kept all these sayings in her heart.

verses.name: Luke 2:52
verses.text: And Jesus increased in wisdom and stature, and in favor with God and men.

verses.name: Luke 3:1
verses.text: Now in the fifteenth year of the reign of Tiberius Caesar, Pontius Pilate being governor of Judea, and Herod being tetrarch of Galilee, and his brother Philip tetrarch of the region of Ituraea and Trachonitis, and Lysanias tetrarch of Abilene,

verses.name: Luke 3:2
verses.text: in the high priesthood of Annas and Caiaphas, the word of God came to John, the son of Zacharias, in the wilderness.

verses.name: Luke 3:3
verses.text: He came into all the region around the Jordan, preaching the baptism of repentance for remission of sins.

verses.name: Luke 3:4
verses.text: As it is written in the book of the words of Isaiah the prophet, “The voice of one crying in the wilderness, ‘Make ready the way of the Lord. Make his paths straight.

verses.name: Luke 3:5
verses.text: Every valley will be filled. Every mountain and hill will be brought low. The crooked will become straight, and the rough ways smooth.

verses.name: Luke 3:6
verses.text: All flesh will see God’s salvation.’”

verses.name: Luke 3:7
verses.text: He said therefore to the multitudes who went out to be baptized by him, “You offspring of vipers, who warned you to flee from the wrath to come?

verses.name: Luke 3:8
verses.text: Therefore produce fruits worthy of repentance, and don’t begin to say among yourselves, ‘We have Abraham for our father;’ for I tell you that God is able to raise up children to Abraham from these stones!

verses.name: Luke 3:9
verses.text: Even now the ax also lies at the root of the trees. Every tree therefore that doesn’t produce good fruit is cut down, and thrown into the fire.”

verses.name: Luke 3:10
verses.text: The multitudes asked him, “What then must we do?”

verses.name: Luke 3:11
verses.text: He answered them, “He who has two coats, let him give to him who has none. He who has food, let him do likewise.”

verses.name: Luke 3:12
verses.text: Tax collectors also came to be baptized, and they said to him, “Teacher, what must we do?”

verses.name: Luke 3:13
verses.text: He said to them, “Collect no more than that which is appointed to you.”

verses.name: Luke 3:14
verses.text: Soldiers also asked him, saying, “What about us? What must we do?” He said to them, “Extort from no one by violence, neither accuse anyone wrongfully. Be content with your wages.”

verses.name: Luke 3:15
verses.text: As the people were in expectation, and all men reasoned in their hearts concerning John, whether perhaps he was the Christ,

verses.name: Luke 3:16
verses.text: John answered them all, “I indeed baptize you with water, but he comes who is mightier than I, the strap of whose sandals I am not worthy to loosen. He will baptize you in the Holy Spirit and fire,

verses.name: Luke 3:17
verses.text: whose fan is in his hand, and he will thoroughly cleanse his threshing floor, and will gather the wheat into his barn; but he will burn up the chaff with unquenchable fire.”

verses.name: Luke 3:18
verses.text: Then with many other exhortations he preached good news to the people,

verses.name: Luke 3:19
verses.text: but Herod the tetrarch, being reproved by him for Herodias, his brother’s wife, and for all the evil things which Herod had done,

verses.name: Luke 3:20
verses.text: added this also to them all, that he shut up John in prison.

verses.name: Luke 3:21
verses.text: Now when all the people were baptized, Jesus also had been baptized, and was praying. The sky was opened,

verses.name: Luke 3:22
verses.text: and the Holy Spirit descended in a bodily form like a dove on him; and a voice came out of the sky, saying “You are my beloved Son. In you I am well pleased.”

verses.name: Luke 3:23
verses.text: Jesus himself, when he began to teach, was about thirty years old, being the son (as was supposed) of Joseph, the son of Heli,

verses.name: Luke 3:24
verses.text: the son of Matthat, the son of Levi, the son of Melchi, the son of Jannai, the son of Joseph,

verses.name: Luke 3:25
verses.text: the son of Mattathias, the son of Amos, the son of Nahum, the son of Esli, the son of Naggai,

verses.name: Luke 3:26
verses.text: the son of Maath, the son of Mattathias, the son of Semein, the son of Joseph, the son of Judah,

verses.name: Luke 3:27
verses.text: the son of Joanan, the son of Rhesa, the son of Zerubbabel, the son of Shealtiel, the son of Neri,

verses.name: Luke 3:28
verses.text: the son of Melchi, the son of Addi, the son of Cosam, the son of Elmodam, the son of Er,

verses.name: Luke 3:29
verses.text: the son of Jose, the son of Eliezer, the son of Jorim, the son of Matthat, the son of Levi,

verses.name: Luke 3:30
verses.text: the son of Simeon, the son of Judah, the son of Joseph, the son of Jonan, the son of Eliakim,

verses.name: Luke 3:31
verses.text: the son of Melea, the son of Menan, the son of Mattatha, the son of Nathan, the son of David,

verses.name: Luke 3:32
verses.text: the son of Jesse, the son of Obed, the son of Boaz, the son of Salmon, the son of Nahshon,

verses.name: Luke 3:33
verses.text: the son of Amminadab, the son of Aram, the son of Hezron, the son of Perez, the son of Judah,

verses.name: Luke 3:34
verses.text: the son of Jacob, the son of Isaac, the son of Abraham, the son of Terah, the son of Nahor,

verses.name: Luke 3:35
verses.text: the son of Serug, the son of Reu, the son of Peleg, the son of Eber, the son of Shelah,

verses.name: Luke 3:36
verses.text: the son of Cainan, the son of Arphaxad, the son of Shem, the son of Noah, the son of Lamech,

verses.name: Luke 3:37
verses.text: the son of Methuselah, the son of Enoch, the son of Jared, the son of Mahalaleel, the son of Cainan,

verses.name: Luke 3:38
verses.text: the son of Enos, the son of Seth, the son of Adam, the son of God.

verses.name: Luke 4:1
verses.text: Jesus, full of the Holy Spirit, returned from the Jordan, and was led by the Spirit into the wilderness

verses.name: Luke 4:2
verses.text: for forty days, being tempted by the devil. He ate nothing in those days. Afterward, when they were completed, he was hungry.

verses.name: Luke 4:3
verses.text: The devil said to him, “If you are the Son of God, command this stone to become bread.”

verses.name: Luke 4:4
verses.text: Jesus answered him, saying, “It is written, ‘Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word of God.’”

verses.name: Luke 4:5
verses.text: The devil, leading him up on a high mountain, showed him all the kingdoms of the world in a moment of time.

verses.name: Luke 4:6
verses.text: The devil said to him, “I will give you all this authority, and their glory, for it has been delivered to me; and I give it to whomever I want.

verses.name: Luke 4:7
verses.text: If you therefore will worship before me, it will all be yours.”

verses.name: Luke 4:8
verses.text: Jesus answered him, “Get behind me Satan! For it is written, ‘You shall worship the Lord your God, and you shall serve him only.’”

verses.name: Luke 4:9
verses.text: He led him to Jerusalem, and set him on the pinnacle of the temple, and said to him, “If you are the Son of God, cast yourself down from here,

verses.name: Luke 4:10
verses.text: for it is written, ‘He will put his angels in charge of you, to guard you;’

verses.name: Luke 4:11
verses.text: and, ‘On their hands they will bear you up, lest perhaps you dash your foot against a stone.’”

verses.name: Luke 4:12
verses.text: Jesus answering, said to him, “It has been said, ‘You shall not tempt the Lord your God.’”

verses.name: Luke 4:13
verses.text: When the devil had completed every temptation, he departed from him until another time.

verses.name: Luke 4:14
verses.text: Jesus returned in the power of the Spirit into Galilee, and news about him spread through all the surrounding area.

verses.name: Luke 4:15
verses.text: He taught in their synagogues, being glorified by all.

verses.name: Luke 4:16
verses.text: He came to Nazareth, where he had been brought up. He entered, as was his custom, into the synagogue on the Sabbath day, and stood up to read.

verses.name: Luke 4:17
verses.text: The book of the prophet Isaiah was handed to him. He opened the book, and found the place where it was written,

verses.name: Luke 4:18
verses.text: “The Spirit of the Lord is on me, because he has anointed me to preach good news to the poor. He has sent me to heal the broken hearted, to proclaim release to the captives, recovering of sight to the blind, to deliver those who are crushed,

verses.name: Luke 4:19
verses.text: and to proclaim the acceptable year of the Lord.”

verses.name: Luke 4:20
verses.text: He closed the book, gave it back to the attendant, and sat down. The eyes of all in the synagogue were fastened on him.

verses.name: Luke 4:21
verses.text: He began to tell them, “Today, this Scripture has been fulfilled in your hearing.”

verses.name: Luke 4:22
verses.text: All testified about him, and wondered at the gracious words which proceeded out of his mouth, and they said, “Isn’t this Joseph’s son?”

verses.name: Luke 4:23
verses.text: He said to them, “Doubtless you will tell me this parable, ‘Physician, heal yourself! Whatever we have heard done at Capernaum, do also here in your hometown.’”

verses.name: Luke 4:24
verses.text: He said, “Most certainly I tell you, no prophet is acceptable in his hometown.

verses.name: Luke 4:25
verses.text: But truly I tell you, there were many widows in Israel in the days of Elijah, when the sky was shut up three years and six months, when a great famine came over all the land.

verses.name: Luke 4:26
verses.text: Elijah was sent to none of them, except to Zarephath, in the land of Sidon, to a woman who was a widow.

verses.name: Luke 4:27
verses.text: There were many lepers in Israel in the time of Elisha the prophet, yet not one of them was cleansed, except Naaman, the Syrian.”

verses.name: Luke 4:28
verses.text: They were all filled with wrath in the synagogue, as they heard these things.

verses.name: Luke 4:29
verses.text: They rose up, threw him out of the city, and led him to the brow of the hill that their city was built on, that they might throw him off the cliff.

verses.name: Luke 4:30
verses.text: But he, passing through the middle of them, went his way.

verses.name: Luke 4:31
verses.text: He came down to Capernaum, a city of Galilee. He was teaching them on the Sabbath day,

verses.name: Luke 4:32
verses.text: and they were astonished at his teaching, for his word was with authority.

verses.name: Luke 4:33
verses.text: In the synagogue there was a man who had a spirit of an unclean demon, and he cried out with a loud voice,

verses.name: Luke 4:34
verses.text: saying, “Ah! what have we to do with you, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know you who you are: the Holy One of God!”

verses.name: Luke 4:35
verses.text: Jesus rebuked him, saying, “Be silent, and come out of him!” When the demon had thrown him down in the middle of them, he came out of him, having done him no harm.

verses.name: Luke 4:36
verses.text: Amazement came on all, and they spoke together, one with another, saying, “What is this word? For with authority and power he commands the unclean spirits, and they come out!”

verses.name: Luke 4:37
verses.text: News about him went out into every place of the surrounding region.

verses.name: Luke 4:38
verses.text: He rose up from the synagogue, and entered into Simon’s house. Simon’s mother-in-law was afflicted with a great fever, and they begged him for her.

verses.name: Luke 4:39
verses.text: He stood over her, and rebuked the fever; and it left her. Immediately she rose up and served them.

verses.name: Luke 4:40
verses.text: When the sun was setting, all those who had any sick with various diseases brought them to him; and he laid his hands on every one of them, and healed them.

verses.name: Luke 4:41
verses.text: Demons also came out of many, crying out, and saying, “You are the Christ, the Son of God!” Rebuking them, he didn’t allow them to speak, because they knew that he was the Christ.

verses.name: Luke 4:42
verses.text: When it was day, he departed and went into an uninhabited place, and the multitudes looked for him, and came to him, and held on to him, so that he wouldn’t go away from them.

verses.name: Luke 4:43
verses.text: But he said to them, “I must preach the good news of God’s Kingdom to the other cities also. For this reason I have been sent.”

verses.name: Luke 4:44
verses.text: He was preaching in the synagogues of Galilee.

verses.name: Luke 5:1
verses.text: Now while the multitude pressed on him and heard the word of God, he was standing by the lake of Gennesaret.

verses.name: Luke 5:2
verses.text: He saw two boats standing by the lake, but the fishermen had gone out of them, and were washing their nets.

verses.name: Luke 5:3
verses.text: He entered into one of the boats, which was Simon’s, and asked him to put out a little from the land. He sat down and taught the multitudes from the boat.

verses.name: Luke 5:4
verses.text: When he had finished speaking, he said to Simon, “Put out into the deep, and let down your nets for a catch.”

verses.name: Luke 5:5
verses.text: Simon answered him, “Master, we worked all night, and took nothing; but at your word I will let down the net.”

verses.name: Luke 5:6
verses.text: When they had done this, they caught a great multitude of fish, and their net was breaking.

verses.name: Luke 5:7
verses.text: They beckoned to their partners in the other boat, that they should come and help them. They came, and filled both boats, so that they began to sink.

verses.name: Luke 5:8
verses.text: But Simon Peter, when he saw it, fell down at Jesus’ knees, saying, “Depart from me, for I am a sinful man, Lord.”

verses.name: Luke 5:9
verses.text: For he was amazed, and all who were with him, at the catch of fish which they had caught;

verses.name: Luke 5:10
verses.text: and so also were James and John, sons of Zebedee, who were partners with Simon. Jesus said to Simon, “Don’t be afraid. From now on you will be catching people alive.”

verses.name: Luke 5:11
verses.text: When they had brought their boats to land, they left everything, and followed him.

verses.name: Luke 5:12
verses.text: While he was in one of the cities, behold, there was a man full of leprosy. When he saw Jesus, he fell on his face, and begged him, saying, “Lord, if you want to, you can make me clean.”

verses.name: Luke 5:13
verses.text: He stretched out his hand, and touched him, saying, “I want to. Be made clean.” Immediately the leprosy left him.

verses.name: Luke 5:14
verses.text: He commanded him to tell no one, “But go your way, and show yourself to the priest, and offer for your cleansing according to what Moses commanded, for a testimony to them.”

verses.name: Luke 5:15
verses.text: But the report concerning him spread much more, and great multitudes came together to hear, and to be healed by him of their infirmities.

verses.name: Luke 5:16
verses.text: But he withdrew himself into the desert, and prayed.

verses.name: Luke 5:17
verses.text: On one of those days, he was teaching; and there were Pharisees and teachers of the law sitting by, who had come out of every village of Galilee, Judea, and Jerusalem. The power of the Lord was with him to heal them.

verses.name: Luke 5:18
verses.text: Behold, men brought a paralyzed man on a cot, and they sought to bring him in to lay before Jesus.

verses.name: Luke 5:19
verses.text: Not finding a way to bring him in because of the multitude, they went up to the housetop, and let him down through the tiles with his cot into the middle before Jesus.

verses.name: Luke 5:20
verses.text: Seeing their faith, he said to him, “Man, your sins are forgiven you.”

verses.name: Luke 5:21
verses.text: The scribes and the Pharisees began to reason, saying, “Who is this that speaks blasphemies? Who can forgive sins, but God alone?”

verses.name: Luke 5:22
verses.text: But Jesus, perceiving their thoughts, answered them, “Why are you reasoning so in your hearts?

verses.name: Luke 5:23
verses.text: Which is easier to say, ‘Your sins are forgiven you;’ or to say, ‘Arise and walk?’

verses.name: Luke 5:24
verses.text: But that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins” (he said to the paralyzed man), “I tell you, arise, and take up your cot, and go to your house.”

verses.name: Luke 5:25
verses.text: Immediately he rose up before them, and took up that which he was laying on, and departed to his house, glorifying God.

verses.name: Luke 5:26
verses.text: Amazement took hold on all, and they glorified God. They were filled with fear, saying, “We have seen strange things today.”

verses.name: Luke 5:27
verses.text: After these things he went out, and saw a tax collector named Levi sitting at the tax office, and said to him, “Follow me!”

verses.name: Luke 5:28
verses.text: He left everything, and rose up and followed him.

verses.name: Luke 5:29
verses.text: Levi made a great feast for him in his house. There was a great crowd of tax collectors and others who were reclining with them.

verses.name: Luke 5:30
verses.text: Their scribes and the Pharisees murmured against his disciples, saying, “Why do you eat and drink with the tax collectors and sinners?”

verses.name: Luke 5:31
verses.text: Jesus answered them, “Those who are healthy have no need for a physician, but those who are sick do.

verses.name: Luke 5:32
verses.text: I have not come to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.”

verses.name: Luke 5:33
verses.text: They said to him, “Why do John’s disciples often fast and pray, likewise also the disciples of the Pharisees, but yours eat and drink?”

verses.name: Luke 5:34
verses.text: He said to them, “Can you make the friends of the bridegroom fast, while the bridegroom is with them?

verses.name: Luke 5:35
verses.text: But the days will come when the bridegroom will be taken away from them. Then they will fast in those days.”

verses.name: Luke 5:36
verses.text: He also told a parable to them. “No one puts a piece from a new garment on an old garment, or else he will tear the new, and also the piece from the new will not match the old.

verses.name: Luke 5:37
verses.text: No one puts new wine into old wine skins, or else the new wine will burst the skins, and it will be spilled, and the skins will be destroyed.

verses.name: Luke 5:38
verses.text: But new wine must be put into fresh wine skins, and both are preserved.

verses.name: Luke 5:39
verses.text: No man having drunk old wine immediately desires new, for he says, ‘The old is better.’”

verses.name: Luke 6:1
verses.text: Now on the second Sabbath after the first, he was going through the grain fields. His disciples plucked the heads of grain and ate, rubbing them in their hands.

verses.name: Luke 6:2
verses.text: But some of the Pharisees said to them, “Why do you do that which is not lawful to do on the Sabbath day?”

verses.name: Luke 6:3
verses.text: Jesus, answering them, said, “Haven’t you read what David did when he was hungry, he, and those who were with him;

verses.name: Luke 6:4
verses.text: how he entered into God’s house, and took and ate the show bread, and gave also to those who were with him, which is not lawful to eat except for the priests alone?”

verses.name: Luke 6:5
verses.text: He said to them, “The Son of Man is lord of the Sabbath.”

verses.name: Luke 6:6
verses.text: It also happened on another Sabbath that he entered into the synagogue and taught. There was a man there, and his right hand was withered.

verses.name: Luke 6:7
verses.text: The scribes and the Pharisees watched him, to see whether he would heal on the Sabbath, that they might find an accusation against him.

verses.name: Luke 6:8
verses.text: But he knew their thoughts; and he said to the man who had the withered hand, “Rise up, and stand in the middle.” He arose and stood.

verses.name: Luke 6:9
verses.text: Then Jesus said to them, “I will ask you something: Is it lawful on the Sabbath to do good, or to do harm? To save a life, or to kill?”

verses.name: Luke 6:10
verses.text: He looked around at them all, and said to the man, “Stretch out your hand.” He did, and his hand was restored as sound as the other.

verses.name: Luke 6:11
verses.text: But they were filled with rage, and talked with one another about what they might do to Jesus.

verses.name: Luke 6:12
verses.text: In these days, he went out to the mountain to pray, and he continued all night in prayer to God.

verses.name: Luke 6:13
verses.text: When it was day, he called his disciples, and from them he chose twelve, whom he also named apostles:

verses.name: Luke 6:14
verses.text: Simon, whom he also named Peter; Andrew, his brother; James; John; Philip; Bartholomew;

verses.name: Luke 6:15
verses.text: Matthew; Thomas; James, the son of Alphaeus; Simon, who was called the Zealot;

verses.name: Luke 6:16
verses.text: Judas the son of James; and Judas Iscariot, who also became a traitor.

verses.name: Luke 6:17
verses.text: He came down with them, and stood on a level place, with a crowd of his disciples, and a great number of the people from all Judea and Jerusalem, and the sea coast of Tyre and Sidon, who came to hear him and to be healed of their diseases;

verses.name: Luke 6:18
verses.text: as well as those who were troubled by unclean spirits, and they were being healed.

verses.name: Luke 6:19
verses.text: All the multitude sought to touch him, for power came out of him and healed them all.

verses.name: Luke 6:20
verses.text: He lifted up his eyes to his disciples, and said, “Blessed are you who are poor, God’s Kingdom is yours.

verses.name: Luke 6:21
verses.text: Blessed are you who hunger now, for you will be filled. Blessed are you who weep now, for you will laugh.

verses.name: Luke 6:22
verses.text: Blessed are you when men shall hate you, and when they shall exclude and mock you, and throw out your name as evil, for the Son of Man’s sake.

verses.name: Luke 6:23
verses.text: Rejoice in that day, and leap for joy, for behold, your reward is great in heaven, for their fathers did the same thing to the prophets.

verses.name: Luke 6:24
verses.text: “But woe to you who are rich! For you have received your consolation.

verses.name: Luke 6:25
verses.text: Woe to you, you who are full now, for you will be hungry. Woe to you who laugh now, for you will mourn and weep.

verses.name: Luke 6:26
verses.text: Woe, when men speak well of you, for their fathers did the same thing to the false prophets.

verses.name: Luke 6:27
verses.text: “But I tell you who hear: love your enemies, do good to those who hate you,

verses.name: Luke 6:28
verses.text: bless those who curse you, and pray for those who mistreat you.

verses.name: Luke 6:29
verses.text: To him who strikes you on the cheek, offer also the other; and from him who takes away your cloak, don’t withhold your coat also.

verses.name: Luke 6:30
verses.text: Give to everyone who asks you, and don’t ask him who takes away your goods to give them back again.

verses.name: Luke 6:31
verses.text: “As you would like people to do to you, do exactly so to them.

verses.name: Luke 6:32
verses.text: If you love those who love you, what credit is that to you? For even sinners love those who love them.

verses.name: Luke 6:33
verses.text: If you do good to those who do good to you, what credit is that to you? For even sinners do the same.

verses.name: Luke 6:34
verses.text: If you lend to those from whom you hope to receive, what credit is that to you? Even sinners lend to sinners, to receive back as much.

verses.name: Luke 6:35
verses.text: But love your enemies, and do good, and lend, expecting nothing back; and your reward will be great, and you will be children of the Most High; for he is kind toward the unthankful and evil.

verses.name: Luke 6:36
verses.text: “Therefore be merciful, even as your Father is also merciful.

verses.name: Luke 6:37
verses.text: Don’t judge, and you won’t be judged. Don’t condemn, and you won’t be condemned. Set free, and you will be set free.

verses.name: Luke 6:38
verses.text: “Give, and it will be given to you: good measure, pressed down, shaken together, and running over, will be given to you. For with the same measure you measure it will be measured back to you.”

verses.name: Luke 6:39
verses.text: He spoke a parable to them. “Can the blind guide the blind? Won’t they both fall into a pit?

verses.name: Luke 6:40
verses.text: A disciple is not above his teacher, but everyone when he is fully trained will be like his teacher.

verses.name: Luke 6:41
verses.text: Why do you see the speck of chaff that is in your brother’s eye, but don’t consider the beam that is in your own eye?

verses.name: Luke 6:42
verses.text: Or how can you tell your brother, ‘Brother, let me remove the speck of chaff that is in your eye,’ when you yourself don’t see the beam that is in your own eye? You hypocrite! First remove the beam from your own eye, and then you can see clearly to remove the speck of chaff that is in your brother’s eye.

verses.name: Luke 6:43
verses.text: For there is no good tree that produces rotten fruit; nor again a rotten tree that produces good fruit.

verses.name: Luke 6:44
verses.text: For each tree is known by its own fruit. For people don’t gather figs from thorns, nor do they gather grapes from a bramble bush.

verses.name: Luke 6:45
verses.text: The good man out of the good treasure of his heart brings out that which is good, and the evil man out of the evil treasure of his heart brings out that which is evil, for out of the abundance of the heart, his mouth speaks.

verses.name: Luke 6:46
verses.text: “Why do you call me, ‘Lord, Lord,’ and don’t do the things which I say?

verses.name: Luke 6:47
verses.text: Everyone who comes to me, and hears my words, and does them, I will show you who he is like.

verses.name: Luke 6:48
verses.text: He is like a man building a house, who dug and went deep, and laid a foundation on the rock. When a flood arose, the stream broke against that house, and could not shake it, because it was founded on the rock.

verses.name: Luke 6:49
verses.text: But he who hears, and doesn’t do, is like a man who built a house on the earth without a foundation, against which the stream broke, and immediately it fell, and the ruin of that house was great.”

verses.name: Luke 7:1
verses.text: After he had finished speaking in the hearing of the people, he entered into Capernaum.

verses.name: Luke 7:2
verses.text: A certain centurion’s servant, who was dear to him, was sick and at the point of death.

verses.name: Luke 7:3
verses.text: When he heard about Jesus, he sent to him elders of the Jews, asking him to come and save his servant.

verses.name: Luke 7:4
verses.text: When they came to Jesus, they begged him earnestly, saying, “He is worthy for you to do this for him,

verses.name: Luke 7:5
verses.text: for he loves our nation, and he built our synagogue for us.”

verses.name: Luke 7:6
verses.text: Jesus went with them. When he was now not far from the house, the centurion sent friends to him, saying to him, “Lord, don’t trouble yourself, for I am not worthy for you to come under my roof.

verses.name: Luke 7:7
verses.text: Therefore I didn’t even think myself worthy to come to you; but say the word, and my servant will be healed.

verses.name: Luke 7:8
verses.text: For I also am a man placed under authority, having under myself soldiers. I tell this one, ‘Go!’ and he goes; and to another, ‘Come!’ and he comes; and to my servant, ‘Do this,’ and he does it.”

verses.name: Luke 7:9
verses.text: When Jesus heard these things, he marveled at him, and turned and said to the multitude who followed him, “I tell you, I have not found such great faith, no, not in Israel.”

verses.name: Luke 7:10
verses.text: Those who were sent, returning to the house, found that the servant who had been sick was well.

verses.name: Luke 7:11
verses.text: Soon afterwards, he went to a city called Nain. Many of his disciples, along with a great multitude, went with him.

verses.name: Luke 7:12
verses.text: Now when he came near to the gate of the city, behold, one who was dead was carried out, the only son of his mother, and she was a widow. Many people of the city were with her.

verses.name: Luke 7:13
verses.text: When the Lord saw her, he had compassion on her, and said to her, “Don’t cry.”

verses.name: Luke 7:14
verses.text: He came near and touched the coffin, and the bearers stood still. He said, “Young man, I tell you, arise!”

verses.name: Luke 7:15
verses.text: He who was dead sat up, and began to speak. And he gave him to his mother.

verses.name: Luke 7:16
verses.text: Fear took hold of all, and they glorified God, saying, “A great prophet has arisen among us!” and, “God has visited his people!”

verses.name: Luke 7:17
verses.text: This report went out concerning him in the whole of Judea, and in all the surrounding region.

verses.name: Luke 7:18
verses.text: The disciples of John told him about all these things.

verses.name: Luke 7:19
verses.text: John, calling to himself two of his disciples, sent them to Jesus, saying, “Are you the one who is coming, or should we look for another?”

verses.name: Luke 7:20
verses.text: When the men had come to him, they said, “John the Baptizer has sent us to you, saying, ‘Are you he who comes, or should we look for another?’”

verses.name: Luke 7:21
verses.text: In that hour he cured many of diseases and plagues and evil spirits; and to many who were blind he gave sight.

verses.name: Luke 7:22
verses.text: Jesus answered them, “Go and tell John the things which you have seen and heard: that the blind receive their sight, the lame walk, the lepers are cleansed, the deaf hear, the dead are raised up, and the poor have good news preached to them.

verses.name: Luke 7:23
verses.text: Blessed is he who finds no occasion for stumbling in me.”

verses.name: Luke 7:24
verses.text: When John’s messengers had departed, he began to tell the multitudes about John, “What did you go out into the wilderness to see? A reed shaken by the wind?

verses.name: Luke 7:25
verses.text: But what did you go out to see? A man clothed in soft clothing? Behold, those who are gorgeously dressed, and live delicately, are in kings’ courts.

verses.name: Luke 7:26
verses.text: But what did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and much more than a prophet.

verses.name: Luke 7:27
verses.text: This is he of whom it is written, ‘Behold, I send my messenger before your face, who will prepare your way before you.’

verses.name: Luke 7:28
verses.text: “For I tell you, among those who are born of women there is not a greater prophet than John the Baptizer, yet he who is least in God’s Kingdom is greater than he.”

verses.name: Luke 7:29
verses.text: When all the people and the tax collectors heard this, they declared God to be just, having been baptized with John’s baptism.

verses.name: Luke 7:30
verses.text: But the Pharisees and the lawyers rejected the counsel of God, not being baptized by him themselves.

verses.name: Luke 7:31
verses.text: “To what then will I liken the people of this generation? What are they like?

verses.name: Luke 7:32
verses.text: They are like children who sit in the marketplace, and call to one another, saying, ‘We piped to you, and you didn’t dance. We mourned, and you didn’t weep.’

verses.name: Luke 7:33
verses.text: For John the Baptizer came neither eating bread nor drinking wine, and you say, ‘He has a demon.’

verses.name: Luke 7:34
verses.text: The Son of Man has come eating and drinking, and you say, ‘Behold, a gluttonous man, and a drunkard; a friend of tax collectors and sinners!’

verses.name: Luke 7:35
verses.text: Wisdom is justified by all her children.”

verses.name: Luke 7:36
verses.text: One of the Pharisees invited him to eat with him. He entered into the Pharisee’s house, and sat at the table.

verses.name: Luke 7:37
verses.text: Behold, a woman in the city who was a sinner, when she knew that he was reclining in the Pharisee’s house, brought an alabaster jar of ointment.

verses.name: Luke 7:38
verses.text: Standing behind at his feet weeping, she began to wet his feet with her tears, and she wiped them with the hair of her head, kissed his feet, and anointed them with the ointment.

verses.name: Luke 7:39
verses.text: Now when the Pharisee who had invited him saw it, he said to himself, “This man, if he were a prophet, would have perceived who and what kind of woman this is who touches him, that she is a sinner.”

verses.name: Luke 7:40
verses.text: Jesus answered him, “Simon, I have something to tell you.” He said, “Teacher, say on.”

verses.name: Luke 7:41
verses.text: “A certain lender had two debtors. The one owed five hundred denarii, and the other fifty.

verses.name: Luke 7:42
verses.text: When they couldn’t pay, he forgave them both. Which of them therefore will love him most?”

verses.name: Luke 7:43
verses.text: Simon answered, “He, I suppose, to whom he forgave the most.” He said to him, “You have judged correctly.”

verses.name: Luke 7:44
verses.text: Turning to the woman, he said to Simon, “Do you see this woman? I entered into your house, and you gave me no water for my feet, but she has wet my feet with her tears, and wiped them with the hair of her head.

verses.name: Luke 7:45
verses.text: You gave me no kiss, but she, since the time I came in, has not ceased to kiss my feet.

verses.name: Luke 7:46
verses.text: You didn’t anoint my head with oil, but she has anointed my feet with ointment.

verses.name: Luke 7:47
verses.text: Therefore I tell you, her sins, which are many, are forgiven, for she loved much. But to whom little is forgiven, the same loves little.”

verses.name: Luke 7:48
verses.text: He said to her, “Your sins are forgiven.”

verses.name: Luke 7:49
verses.text: Those who sat at the table with him began to say to themselves, “Who is this who even forgives sins?”

verses.name: Luke 7:50
verses.text: He said to the woman, “Your faith has saved you. Go in peace.”

verses.name: Luke 8:1
verses.text: Soon afterwards, he went about through cities and villages, preaching and bringing the good news of God’s Kingdom. With him were the twelve,

verses.name: Luke 8:2
verses.text: and certain women who had been healed of evil spirits and infirmities: Mary who was called Magdalene, from whom seven demons had gone out;

verses.name: Luke 8:3
verses.text: and Joanna, the wife of Chuzas, Herod’s steward; Susanna; and many others; who served them from their possessions.

verses.name: Luke 8:4
verses.text: When a great multitude came together, and people from every city were coming to him, he spoke by a parable.

verses.name: Luke 8:5
verses.text: “The farmer went out to sow his seed. As he sowed, some fell along the road, and it was trampled under foot, and the birds of the sky devoured it.

verses.name: Luke 8:6
verses.text: Other seed fell on the rock, and as soon as it grew, it withered away, because it had no moisture.

verses.name: Luke 8:7
verses.text: Other fell amid the thorns, and the thorns grew with it, and choked it.

verses.name: Luke 8:8
verses.text: Other fell into the good ground, and grew, and produced one hundred times as much fruit.” As he said these things, he called out, “He who has ears to hear, let him hear!”

verses.name: Luke 8:9
verses.text: Then his disciples asked him, “What does this parable mean?”

verses.name: Luke 8:10
verses.text: He said, “To you it is given to know the mysteries of God’s Kingdom, but to the rest in parables; that ‘seeing they may not see, and hearing they may not understand.’

verses.name: Luke 8:11
verses.text: Now the parable is this: The seed is the word of God.

verses.name: Luke 8:12
verses.text: Those along the road are those who hear, then the devil comes, and takes away the word from their heart, that they may not believe and be saved.
