verses.name: Luke 19:48
verses.text: They couldn’t find what they might do, for all the people hung on to every word that he said.

verses.name: Luke 20:1
verses.text: On one of those days, as he was teaching the people in the temple and preaching the Good News, the priests and scribes came to him with the elders.

verses.name: Luke 20:2
verses.text: They asked him, “Tell us: by what authority do you do these things? Or who is giving you this authority?”

verses.name: Luke 20:3
verses.text: He answered them, “I also will ask you one question. Tell me:

verses.name: Luke 20:4
verses.text: the baptism of John, was it from heaven, or from men?”

verses.name: Luke 20:5
verses.text: They reasoned with themselves, saying, “If we say, ‘From heaven,’ he will say, ‘Why didn’t you believe him?’

verses.name: Luke 20:6
verses.text: But if we say, ‘From men,’ all the people will stone us, for they are persuaded that John was a prophet.”

verses.name: Luke 20:7
verses.text: They answered that they didn’t know where it was from.

verses.name: Luke 20:8
verses.text: Jesus said to them, “Neither will I tell you by what authority I do these things.”

verses.name: Luke 20:9
verses.text: He began to tell the people this parable. “A man planted a vineyard, and rented it out to some farmers, and went into another country for a long time.

verses.name: Luke 20:10
verses.text: At the proper season, he sent a servant to the farmers to collect his share of the fruit of the vineyard. But the farmers beat him, and sent him away empty.

verses.name: Luke 20:11
verses.text: He sent yet another servant, and they also beat him, and treated him shamefully, and sent him away empty.

verses.name: Luke 20:12
verses.text: He sent yet a third, and they also wounded him, and threw him out.

verses.name: Luke 20:13
verses.text: The lord of the vineyard said, ‘What shall I do? I will send my beloved son. It may be that seeing him, they will respect him.’

verses.name: Luke 20:14
verses.text: “But when the farmers saw him, they reasoned among themselves, saying, ‘This is the heir. Come, let’s kill him, that the inheritance may be ours.’

verses.name: Luke 20:15
verses.text: They threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him. What therefore will the lord of the vineyard do to them?

verses.name: Luke 20:16
verses.text: He will come and destroy these farmers, and will give the vineyard to others.” When they heard it, they said, “May it never be!”

verses.name: Luke 20:17
verses.text: But he looked at them, and said, “Then what is this that is written, ‘The stone which the builders rejected, the same was made the chief cornerstone?’

verses.name: Luke 20:18
verses.text: Everyone who falls on that stone will be broken to pieces, but it will crush whomever it falls on to dust.”

verses.name: Luke 20:19
verses.text: The chief priests and the scribes sought to lay hands on him that very hour, but they feared the people—for they knew he had spoken this parable against them.

verses.name: Luke 20:20
verses.text: They watched him, and sent out spies, who pretended to be righteous, that they might trap him in something he said, so as to deliver him up to the power and authority of the governor.

verses.name: Luke 20:21
verses.text: They asked him, “Teacher, we know that you say and teach what is right, and aren’t partial to anyone, but truly teach the way of God.

verses.name: Luke 20:22
verses.text: Is it lawful for us to pay taxes to Caesar, or not?”

verses.name: Luke 20:23
verses.text: But he perceived their craftiness, and said to them, “Why do you test me?

verses.name: Luke 20:24
verses.text: Show me a denarius. Whose image and inscription are on it?” They answered, “Caesar’s.”

verses.name: Luke 20:25
verses.text: He said to them, “Then give to Caesar the things that are Caesar’s, and to God the things that are God’s.”

verses.name: Luke 20:26
verses.text: They weren’t able to trap him in his words before the people. They marveled at his answer, and were silent.

verses.name: Luke 20:27
verses.text: Some of the Sadducees came to him, those who deny that there is a resurrection.

verses.name: Luke 20:28
verses.text: They asked him, “Teacher, Moses wrote to us that if a man’s brother dies having a wife, and he is childless, his brother should take the wife, and raise up children for his brother.

verses.name: Luke 20:29
verses.text: There were therefore seven brothers. The first took a wife, and died childless.

verses.name: Luke 20:30
verses.text: The second took her as wife, and he died childless.

verses.name: Luke 20:31
verses.text: The third took her, and likewise the seven all left no children, and died.

verses.name: Luke 20:32
verses.text: Afterward the woman also died.

verses.name: Luke 20:33
verses.text: Therefore in the resurrection whose wife of them will she be? For the seven had her as a wife.”

verses.name: Luke 20:34
verses.text: Jesus said to them, “The children of this age marry, and are given in marriage.

verses.name: Luke 20:35
verses.text: But those who are considered worthy to attain to that age and the resurrection from the dead, neither marry, nor are given in marriage.

verses.name: Luke 20:36
verses.text: For they can’t die any more, for they are like the angels, and are children of God, being children of the resurrection.

verses.name: Luke 20:37
verses.text: But that the dead are raised, even Moses showed at the bush, when he called the Lord ‘The God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.’

verses.name: Luke 20:38
verses.text: Now he is not the God of the dead, but of the living, for all are alive to him.”

verses.name: Luke 20:39
verses.text: Some of the scribes answered, “Teacher, you speak well.”

verses.name: Luke 20:40
verses.text: They didn’t dare to ask him any more questions.

verses.name: Luke 20:41
verses.text: He said to them, “Why do they say that the Christ is David’s son?

verses.name: Luke 20:42
verses.text: David himself says in the book of Psalms, ‘The Lord said to my Lord, “Sit at my right hand,

verses.name: Luke 20:43
verses.text: until I make your enemies the footstool of your feet.”’

verses.name: Luke 20:44
verses.text: “David therefore calls him Lord, so how is he his son?”

verses.name: Luke 20:45
verses.text: In the hearing of all the people, he said to his disciples,

verses.name: Luke 20:46
verses.text: “Beware of those scribes who like to walk in long robes, and love greetings in the marketplaces, the best seats in the synagogues, and the best places at feasts;

verses.name: Luke 20:47
verses.text: who devour widows’ houses, and for a pretense make long prayers: these will receive greater condemnation.”

verses.name: Luke 21:1
verses.text: He looked up, and saw the rich people who were putting their gifts into the treasury.

verses.name: Luke 21:2
verses.text: He saw a certain poor widow casting in two small brass coins.

verses.name: Luke 21:3
verses.text: He said, “Truly I tell you, this poor widow put in more than all of them,

verses.name: Luke 21:4
verses.text: for all these put in gifts for God from their abundance, but she, out of her poverty, put in all that she had to live on.”

verses.name: Luke 21:5
verses.text: As some were talking about the temple and how it was decorated with beautiful stones and gifts, he said,

verses.name: Luke 21:6
verses.text: “As for these things which you see, the days will come, in which there will not be left here one stone on another that will not be thrown down.”

verses.name: Luke 21:7
verses.text: They asked him, “Teacher, so when will these things be? What is the sign that these things are about to happen?”

verses.name: Luke 21:8
verses.text: He said, “Watch out that you don’t get led astray, for many will come in my name, saying, ‘I am he,’ and, ‘The time is at hand.’ Therefore don’t follow them.

verses.name: Luke 21:9
verses.text: When you hear of wars and disturbances, don’t be terrified, for these things must happen first, but the end won’t come immediately.”

verses.name: Luke 21:10
verses.text: Then he said to them, “Nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom.

verses.name: Luke 21:11
verses.text: There will be great earthquakes, famines, and plagues in various places. There will be terrors and great signs from heaven.

verses.name: Luke 21:12
verses.text: But before all these things, they will lay their hands on you and will persecute you, delivering you up to synagogues and prisons, bringing you before kings and governors for my name’s sake.

verses.name: Luke 21:13
verses.text: It will turn out as a testimony for you.

verses.name: Luke 21:14
verses.text: Settle it therefore in your hearts not to meditate beforehand how to answer,

verses.name: Luke 21:15
verses.text: for I will give you a mouth and wisdom which all your adversaries will not be able to withstand or to contradict.

verses.name: Luke 21:16
verses.text: You will be handed over even by parents, brothers, relatives, and friends. They will cause some of you to be put to death.

verses.name: Luke 21:17
verses.text: You will be hated by all men for my name’s sake.

verses.name: Luke 21:18
verses.text: And not a hair of your head will perish.

verses.name: Luke 21:19
verses.text: “By your endurance you will win your lives.

verses.name: Luke 21:20
verses.text: “But when you see Jerusalem surrounded by armies, then know that its desolation is at hand.

verses.name: Luke 21:21
verses.text: Then let those who are in Judea flee to the mountains. Let those who are in the middle of her depart. Let those who are in the country not enter therein.

verses.name: Luke 21:22
verses.text: For these are days of vengeance, that all things which are written may be fulfilled.

verses.name: Luke 21:23
verses.text: Woe to those who are pregnant and to those who nurse infants in those days! For there will be great distress in the land, and wrath to this people.

verses.name: Luke 21:24
verses.text: They will fall by the edge of the sword, and will be led captive into all the nations. Jerusalem will be trampled down by the Gentiles, until the times of the Gentiles are fulfilled.

verses.name: Luke 21:25
verses.text: There will be signs in the sun, moon, and stars; and on the earth anxiety of nations, in perplexity for the roaring of the sea and the waves;

verses.name: Luke 21:26
verses.text: men fainting for fear, and for expectation of the things which are coming on the world: for the powers of the heavens will be shaken.

verses.name: Luke 21:27
verses.text: Then they will see the Son of Man coming in a cloud with power and great glory.

verses.name: Luke 21:28
verses.text: But when these things begin to happen, look up, and lift up your heads, because your redemption is near.”

verses.name: Luke 21:29
verses.text: He told them a parable. “See the fig tree, and all the trees.

verses.name: Luke 21:30
verses.text: When they are already budding, you see it and know by your own selves that the summer is already near.

verses.name: Luke 21:31
verses.text: Even so you also, when you see these things happening, know that God’s Kingdom is near.

verses.name: Luke 21:32
verses.text: Most certainly I tell you, this generation will not pass away until all things are accomplished.

verses.name: Luke 21:33
verses.text: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will by no means pass away.

verses.name: Luke 21:34
verses.text: “So be careful, or your hearts will be loaded down with carousing, drunkenness, and cares of this life, and that day will come on you suddenly.

verses.name: Luke 21:35
verses.text: For it will come like a snare on all those who dwell on the surface of all the earth.

verses.name: Luke 21:36
verses.text: Therefore be watchful all the time, praying that you may be counted worthy to escape all these things that will happen, and to stand before the Son of Man.”

verses.name: Luke 21:37
verses.text: Every day Jesus was teaching in the temple, and every night he would go out and spend the night on the mountain that is called Olivet.

verses.name: Luke 21:38
verses.text: All the people came early in the morning to him in the temple to hear him.

verses.name: Luke 22:1
verses.text: Now the feast of unleavened bread, which is called the Passover, was approaching.

verses.name: Luke 22:2
verses.text: The chief priests and the scribes sought how they might put him to death, for they feared the people.

verses.name: Luke 22:3
verses.text: Satan entered into Judas, who was also called Iscariot, who was counted with the twelve.

verses.name: Luke 22:4
verses.text: He went away, and talked with the chief priests and captains about how he might deliver him to them.

verses.name: Luke 22:5
verses.text: They were glad, and agreed to give him money.

verses.name: Luke 22:6
verses.text: He consented, and sought an opportunity to deliver him to them in the absence of the multitude.

verses.name: Luke 22:7
verses.text: The day of unleavened bread came, on which the Passover must be sacrificed.

verses.name: Luke 22:8
verses.text: Jesus sent Peter and John, saying, “Go and prepare the Passover for us, that we may eat.”

verses.name: Luke 22:9
verses.text: They said to him, “Where do you want us to prepare?”

verses.name: Luke 22:10
verses.text: He said to them, “Behold, when you have entered into the city, a man carrying a pitcher of water will meet you. Follow him into the house which he enters.

verses.name: Luke 22:11
verses.text: Tell the master of the house, ‘The Teacher says to you, “Where is the guest room, where I may eat the Passover with my disciples?”’

verses.name: Luke 22:12
verses.text: He will show you a large, furnished upper room. Make preparations there.”

verses.name: Luke 22:13
verses.text: They went, found things as Jesus had told them, and they prepared the Passover.

verses.name: Luke 22:14
verses.text: When the hour had come, he sat down with the twelve apostles.

verses.name: Luke 22:15
verses.text: He said to them, “I have earnestly desired to eat this Passover with you before I suffer,

verses.name: Luke 22:16
verses.text: for I tell you, I will no longer by any means eat of it until it is fulfilled in God’s Kingdom.”

verses.name: Luke 22:17
verses.text: He received a cup, and when he had given thanks, he said, “Take this, and share it among yourselves,

verses.name: Luke 22:18
verses.text: for I tell you, I will not drink at all again from the fruit of the vine, until God’s Kingdom comes.”

verses.name: Luke 22:19
verses.text: He took bread, and when he had given thanks, he broke, and gave it to them, saying, “This is my body which is given for you. Do this in memory of me.”

verses.name: Luke 22:20
verses.text: Likewise, he took the cup after supper, saying, “This cup is the new covenant in my blood, which is poured out for you.

verses.name: Luke 22:21
verses.text: But behold, the hand of him who betrays me is with me on the table.

verses.name: Luke 22:22
verses.text: The Son of Man indeed goes, as it has been determined, but woe to that man through whom he is betrayed!”

verses.name: Luke 22:23
verses.text: They began to question among themselves, which of them it was who would do this thing.

verses.name: Luke 22:24
verses.text: There arose also a contention among them, which of them was considered to be greatest.

verses.name: Luke 22:25
verses.text: He said to them, “The kings of the nations lord it over them, and those who have authority over them are called ‘benefactors.’

verses.name: Luke 22:26
verses.text: But not so with you. But one who is the greater among you, let him become as the younger, and one who is governing, as one who serves.

verses.name: Luke 22:27
verses.text: For who is greater, one who sits at the table, or one who serves? Isn’t it he who sits at the table? But I am among you as one who serves.

verses.name: Luke 22:28
verses.text: But you are those who have continued with me in my trials.

verses.name: Luke 22:29
verses.text: I confer on you a kingdom, even as my Father conferred on me,

verses.name: Luke 22:30
verses.text: that you may eat and drink at my table in my Kingdom. You will sit on thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.”

verses.name: Luke 22:31
verses.text: The Lord said, “Simon, Simon, behold, Satan asked to have all of you, that he might sift you as wheat,

verses.name: Luke 22:32
verses.text: but I prayed for you, that your faith wouldn’t fail. You, when once you have turned again, establish your brothers.”

verses.name: Luke 22:33
verses.text: He said to him, “Lord, I am ready to go with you both to prison and to death!”

verses.name: Luke 22:34
verses.text: He said, “I tell you, Peter, the rooster will by no means crow today until you deny that you know me three times.”

verses.name: Luke 22:35
verses.text: He said to them, “When I sent you out without purse, and wallet, and shoes, did you lack anything?” They said, “Nothing.”

verses.name: Luke 22:36
verses.text: Then he said to them, “But now, whoever has a purse, let him take it, and likewise a wallet. Whoever has none, let him sell his cloak, and buy a sword.

verses.name: Luke 22:37
verses.text: For I tell you that this which is written must still be fulfilled in me: ‘He was counted with transgressors.’ For that which concerns me has an end.”

verses.name: Luke 22:38
verses.text: They said, “Lord, behold, here are two swords.” He said to them, “That is enough.”

verses.name: Luke 22:39
verses.text: He came out, and went, as his custom was, to the Mount of Olives. His disciples also followed him.

verses.name: Luke 22:40
verses.text: When he was at the place, he said to them, “Pray that you don’t enter into temptation.”

verses.name: Luke 22:41
verses.text: He was withdrawn from them about a stone’s throw, and he knelt down and prayed,

verses.name: Luke 22:42
verses.text: saying, “Father, if you are willing, remove this cup from me. Nevertheless, not my will, but yours, be done.”

verses.name: Luke 22:43
verses.text: An angel from heaven appeared to him, strengthening him.

verses.name: Luke 22:44
verses.text: Being in agony he prayed more earnestly. His sweat became like great drops of blood falling down on the ground.

verses.name: Luke 22:45
verses.text: When he rose up from his prayer, he came to the disciples, and found them sleeping because of grief,

verses.name: Luke 22:46
verses.text: and said to them, “Why do you sleep? Rise and pray that you may not enter into temptation.”

verses.name: Luke 22:47
verses.text: While he was still speaking, behold, a multitude, and he who was called Judas, one of the twelve, was leading them. He came near to Jesus to kiss him.

verses.name: Luke 22:48
verses.text: But Jesus said to him, “Judas, do you betray the Son of Man with a kiss?”

verses.name: Luke 22:49
verses.text: When those who were around him saw what was about to happen, they said to him, “Lord, shall we strike with the sword?”

verses.name: Luke 22:50
verses.text: A certain one of them struck the servant of the high priest, and cut off his right ear.

verses.name: Luke 22:51
verses.text: But Jesus answered, “Let me at least do this”—and he touched his ear, and healed him.

verses.name: Luke 22:52
verses.text: Jesus said to the chief priests, captains of the temple, and elders, who had come against him, “Have you come out as against a robber, with swords and clubs?

verses.name: Luke 22:53
verses.text: When I was with you in the temple daily, you didn’t stretch out your hands against me. But this is your hour, and the power of darkness.”

verses.name: Luke 22:54
verses.text: They seized him, and led him away, and brought him into the high priest’s house. But Peter followed from a distance.

verses.name: Luke 22:55
verses.text: When they had kindled a fire in the middle of the courtyard, and had sat down together, Peter sat among them.

verses.name: Luke 22:56
verses.text: A certain servant girl saw him as he sat in the light, and looking intently at him, said, “This man also was with him.”

verses.name: Luke 22:57
verses.text: He denied Jesus, saying, “Woman, I don’t know him.”

verses.name: Luke 22:58
verses.text: After a little while someone else saw him, and said, “You also are one of them!” But Peter answered, “Man, I am not!”

verses.name: Luke 22:59
verses.text: After about one hour passed, another confidently affirmed, saying, “Truly this man also was with him, for he is a Galilean!”

verses.name: Luke 22:60
verses.text: But Peter said, “Man, I don’t know what you are talking about!” Immediately, while he was still speaking, a rooster crowed.

verses.name: Luke 22:61
verses.text: The Lord turned, and looked at Peter. Then Peter remembered the Lord’s word, how he said to him, “Before the rooster crows you will deny me three times.”

verses.name: Luke 22:62
verses.text: He went out, and wept bitterly.

verses.name: Luke 22:63
verses.text: The men who held Jesus mocked him and beat him.

verses.name: Luke 22:64
verses.text: Having blindfolded him, they struck him on the face and asked him, “Prophesy! Who is the one who struck you?”

verses.name: Luke 22:65
verses.text: They spoke many other things against him, insulting him.

verses.name: Luke 22:66
verses.text: As soon as it was day, the assembly of the elders of the people were gathered together, both chief priests and scribes, and they led him away into their council, saying,

verses.name: Luke 22:67
verses.text: “If you are the Christ, tell us.” But he said to them, “If I tell you, you won’t believe,

verses.name: Luke 22:68
verses.text: and if I ask, you will in no way answer me or let me go.

verses.name: Luke 22:69
verses.text: From now on, the Son of Man will be seated at the right hand of the power of God.”

verses.name: Luke 22:70
verses.text: They all said, “Are you then the Son of God?” He said to them, “You say it, because I am.”

verses.name: Luke 22:71
verses.text: They said, “Why do we need any more witness? For we ourselves have heard from his own mouth!”

verses.name: Luke 23:1
verses.text: The whole company of them rose up and brought him before Pilate.

verses.name: Luke 23:2
verses.text: They began to accuse him, saying, “We found this man perverting the nation, forbidding paying taxes to Caesar, and saying that he himself is Christ, a king.”

verses.name: Luke 23:3
verses.text: Pilate asked him, “Are you the King of the Jews?” He answered him, “So you say.”

verses.name: Luke 23:4
verses.text: Pilate said to the chief priests and the multitudes, “I find no basis for a charge against this man.”

verses.name: Luke 23:5
verses.text: But they insisted, saying, “He stirs up the people, teaching throughout all Judea, beginning from Galilee even to this place.”

verses.name: Luke 23:6
verses.text: But when Pilate heard Galilee mentioned, he asked if the man was a Galilean.

verses.name: Luke 23:7
verses.text: When he found out that he was in Herod’s jurisdiction, he sent him to Herod, who was also in Jerusalem during those days.

verses.name: Luke 23:8
verses.text: Now when Herod saw Jesus, he was exceedingly glad, for he had wanted to see him for a long time, because he had heard many things about him. He hoped to see some miracle done by him.

verses.name: Luke 23:9
verses.text: He questioned him with many words, but he gave no answers.

verses.name: Luke 23:10
verses.text: The chief priests and the scribes stood, vehemently accusing him.

verses.name: Luke 23:11
verses.text: Herod with his soldiers humiliated him and mocked him. Dressing him in luxurious clothing, they sent him back to Pilate.

verses.name: Luke 23:12
verses.text: Herod and Pilate became friends with each other that very day, for before that they were enemies with each other.

verses.name: Luke 23:13
verses.text: Pilate called together the chief priests, the rulers, and the people,

verses.name: Luke 23:14
verses.text: and said to them, “You brought this man to me as one that perverts the people, and behold, having examined him before you, I found no basis for a charge against this man concerning those things of which you accuse him.

verses.name: Luke 23:15
verses.text: Neither has Herod, for I sent you to him, and see, nothing worthy of death has been done by him.

verses.name: Luke 23:16
verses.text: I will therefore chastise him and release him.”

verses.name: Luke 23:17
verses.text: Now he had to release one prisoner to them at the feast.

verses.name: Luke 23:18
verses.text: But they all cried out together, saying, “Away with this man! Release to us Barabbas!”—

verses.name: Luke 23:19
verses.text: one who was thrown into prison for a certain revolt in the city, and for murder.

verses.name: Luke 23:20
verses.text: Then Pilate spoke to them again, wanting to release Jesus,

verses.name: Luke 23:21
verses.text: but they shouted, saying, “Crucify! Crucify him!”

verses.name: Luke 23:22
verses.text: He said to them the third time, “Why? What evil has this man done? I have found no capital crime in him. I will therefore chastise him and release him.”

verses.name: Luke 23:23
verses.text: But they were urgent with loud voices, asking that he might be crucified. Their voices and the voices of the chief priests prevailed.

verses.name: Luke 23:24
verses.text: Pilate decreed that what they asked for should be done.

verses.name: Luke 23:25
verses.text: He released him who had been thrown into prison for insurrection and murder, for whom they asked, but he delivered Jesus up to their will.

verses.name: Luke 23:26
verses.text: When they led him away, they grabbed one Simon of Cyrene, coming from the country, and laid on him the cross, to carry it after Jesus.

verses.name: Luke 23:27
verses.text: A great multitude of the people followed him, including women who also mourned and lamented him.

verses.name: Luke 23:28
verses.text: But Jesus, turning to them, said, “Daughters of Jerusalem, don’t weep for me, but weep for yourselves and for your children.

verses.name: Luke 23:29
verses.text: For behold, the days are coming in which they will say, ‘Blessed are the barren, the wombs that never bore, and the breasts that never nursed.’

verses.name: Luke 23:30
verses.text: Then they will begin to tell the mountains, ‘Fall on us!’ and tell the hills, ‘Cover us.’

verses.name: Luke 23:31
verses.text: For if they do these things in the green tree, what will be done in the dry?”

verses.name: Luke 23:32
verses.text: There were also others, two criminals, led with him to be put to death.

verses.name: Luke 23:33
verses.text: When they came to the place that is called The Skull, they crucified him there with the criminals, one on the right and the other on the left.

verses.name: Luke 23:34
verses.text: Jesus said, “Father, forgive them, for they don’t know what they are doing.” Dividing his garments among them, they cast lots.

verses.name: Luke 23:35
verses.text: The people stood watching. The rulers with them also scoffed at him, saying, “He saved others. Let him save himself, if this is the Christ of God, his chosen one!”

verses.name: Luke 23:36
verses.text: The soldiers also mocked him, coming to him and offering him vinegar,

verses.name: Luke 23:37
verses.text: and saying, “If you are the King of the Jews, save yourself!”

verses.name: Luke 23:38
verses.text: An inscription was also written over him in letters of Greek, Latin, and Hebrew: “THIS IS THE KING OF THE JEWS.”

verses.name: Luke 23:39
verses.text: One of the criminals who was hanged insulted him, saying, “If you are the Christ, save yourself and us!”

verses.name: Luke 23:40
verses.text: But the other answered, and rebuking him said, “Don’t you even fear God, seeing you are under the same condemnation?

verses.name: Luke 23:41
verses.text: And we indeed justly, for we receive the due reward for our deeds, but this man has done nothing wrong.”

verses.name: Luke 23:42
verses.text: He said to Jesus, “Lord, remember me when you come into your Kingdom.”

verses.name: Luke 23:43
verses.text: Jesus said to him, “Assuredly I tell you, today you will be with me in Paradise.”

verses.name: Luke 23:44
verses.text: It was now about the sixth hour, and darkness came over the whole land until the ninth hour.

verses.name: Luke 23:45
verses.text: The sun was darkened, and the veil of the temple was torn in two.

verses.name: Luke 23:46
verses.text: Jesus, crying with a loud voice, said, “Father, into your hands I commit my spirit!” Having said this, he breathed his last.

verses.name: Luke 23:47
verses.text: When the centurion saw what was done, he glorified God, saying, “Certainly this was a righteous man.”

verses.name: Luke 23:48
verses.text: All the multitudes that came together to see this, when they saw the things that were done, returned home beating their breasts.

verses.name: Luke 23:49
verses.text: All his acquaintances, and the women who followed with him from Galilee, stood at a distance, watching these things.

verses.name: Luke 23:50
verses.text: Behold, a man named Joseph, who was a member of the council, a good and righteous man

verses.name: Luke 23:51
verses.text: (he had not consented to their counsel and deed), from Arimathaea, a city of the Jews, who was also waiting for God’s Kingdom:

verses.name: Luke 23:52
verses.text: this man went to Pilate, and asked for Jesus’ body.

verses.name: Luke 23:53
verses.text: He took it down, and wrapped it in a linen cloth, and laid him in a tomb that was cut in stone, where no one had ever been laid.

verses.name: Luke 23:54
verses.text: It was the day of the Preparation, and the Sabbath was drawing near.

verses.name: Luke 23:55
verses.text: The women, who had come with him out of Galilee, followed after, and saw the tomb, and how his body was laid.

verses.name: Luke 23:56
verses.text: They returned, and prepared spices and ointments. On the Sabbath they rested according to the commandment.

verses.name: Luke 24:1
verses.text: But on the first day of the week, at early dawn, they and some others came to the tomb, bringing the spices which they had prepared.

verses.name: Luke 24:2
verses.text: They found the stone rolled away from the tomb.

verses.name: Luke 24:3
verses.text: They entered in, and didn’t find the Lord Jesus’ body.

verses.name: Luke 24:4
verses.text: While they were greatly perplexed about this, behold, two men stood by them in dazzling clothing.

verses.name: Luke 24:5
verses.text: Becoming terrified, they bowed their faces down to the earth. They said to them, “Why do you seek the living among the dead?

verses.name: Luke 24:6
verses.text: He isn’t here, but is risen. Remember what he told you when he was still in Galilee,

verses.name: Luke 24:7
verses.text: saying that the Son of Man must be delivered up into the hands of sinful men, and be crucified, and the third day rise again?”

verses.name: Luke 24:8
verses.text: They remembered his words,

verses.name: Luke 24:9
verses.text: returned from the tomb, and told all these things to the eleven, and to all the rest.

verses.name: Luke 24:10
verses.text: Now they were Mary Magdalene, Joanna, and Mary the mother of James. The other women with them told these things to the apostles.

verses.name: Luke 24:11
verses.text: These words seemed to them to be nonsense, and they didn’t believe them.

verses.name: Luke 24:12
verses.text: But Peter got up and ran to the tomb. Stooping and looking in, he saw the strips of linen lying by themselves, and he departed to his home, wondering what had happened.

verses.name: Luke 24:13
verses.text: Behold, two of them were going that very day to a village named Emmaus, which was sixty stadia from Jerusalem.

verses.name: Luke 24:14
verses.text: They talked with each other about all of these things which had happened.

verses.name: Luke 24:15
verses.text: While they talked and questioned together, Jesus himself came near, and went with them.

verses.name: Luke 24:16
verses.text: But their eyes were kept from recognizing him.

verses.name: Luke 24:17
verses.text: He said to them, “What are you talking about as you walk, and are sad?”

verses.name: Luke 24:18
verses.text: One of them, named Cleopas, answered him, “Are you the only stranger in Jerusalem who doesn’t know the things which have happened there in these days?”

verses.name: Luke 24:19
verses.text: He said to them, “What things?” They said to him, “The things concerning Jesus, the Nazarene, who was a prophet mighty in deed and word before God and all the people;

verses.name: Luke 24:20
verses.text: and how the chief priests and our rulers delivered him up to be condemned to death, and crucified him.

verses.name: Luke 24:21
verses.text: But we were hoping that it was he who would redeem Israel. Yes, and besides all this, it is now the third day since these things happened.

verses.name: Luke 24:22
verses.text: Also, certain women of our company amazed us, having arrived early at the tomb;

verses.name: Luke 24:23
verses.text: and when they didn’t find his body, they came saying that they had also seen a vision of angels, who said that he was alive.

verses.name: Luke 24:24
verses.text: Some of us went to the tomb, and found it just like the women had said, but they didn’t see him.”

verses.name: Luke 24:25
verses.text: He said to them, “Foolish men, and slow of heart to believe in all that the prophets have spoken!

verses.name: Luke 24:26
verses.text: Didn’t the Christ have to suffer these things and to enter into his glory?”

verses.name: Luke 24:27
verses.text: Beginning from Moses and from all the prophets, he explained to them in all the Scriptures the things concerning himself.

verses.name: Luke 24:28
verses.text: They came near to the village, where they were going, and he acted like he would go further.

verses.name: Luke 24:29
verses.text: They urged him, saying, “Stay with us, for it is almost evening, and the day is almost over.” He went in to stay with them.

verses.name: Luke 24:30
verses.text: When he had sat down at the table with them, he took the bread and gave thanks. Breaking it, he gave it to them.

verses.name: Luke 24:31
verses.text: Their eyes were opened, and they recognized him, and he vanished out of their sight.

verses.name: Luke 24:32
verses.text: They said to one another, “Weren’t our hearts burning within us, while he spoke to us along the way, and while he opened the Scriptures to us?”

verses.name: Luke 24:33
verses.text: They rose up that very hour, returned to Jerusalem, and found the eleven gathered together, and those who were with them,

verses.name: Luke 24:34
verses.text: saying, “The Lord is risen indeed, and has appeared to Simon!”

verses.name: Luke 24:35
verses.text: They related the things that happened along the way, and how he was recognized by them in the breaking of the bread.

verses.name: Luke 24:36
verses.text: As they said these things, Jesus himself stood among them, and said to them, “Peace be to you.”

verses.name: Luke 24:37
verses.text: But they were terrified and filled with fear, and supposed that they had seen a spirit.

verses.name: Luke 24:38
verses.text: He said to them, “Why are you troubled? Why do doubts arise in your hearts?

verses.name: Luke 24:39
verses.text: See my hands and my feet, that it is truly me. Touch me and see, for a spirit doesn’t have flesh and bones, as you see that I have.”

verses.name: Luke 24:40
verses.text: When he had said this, he showed them his hands and his feet.

verses.name: Luke 24:41
verses.text: While they still didn’t believe for joy, and wondered, he said to them, “Do you have anything here to eat?”

verses.name: Luke 24:42
verses.text: They gave him a piece of a broiled fish and some honeycomb.

verses.name: Luke 24:43
verses.text: He took them, and ate in front of them.

verses.name: Luke 24:44
verses.text: He said to them, “This is what I told you, while I was still with you, that all things which are written in the law of Moses, the prophets, and the psalms, concerning me must be fulfilled.”

verses.name: Luke 24:45
verses.text: Then he opened their minds, that they might understand the Scriptures.

verses.name: Luke 24:46
verses.text: He said to them, “Thus it is written, and thus it was necessary for the Christ to suffer and to rise from the dead the third day,

verses.name: Luke 24:47
verses.text: and that repentance and remission of sins should be preached in his name to all the nations, beginning at Jerusalem.

verses.name: Luke 24:48
verses.text: You are witnesses of these things.

verses.name: Luke 24:49
verses.text: Behold, I send out the promise of my Father on you. But wait in the city of Jerusalem until you are clothed with power from on high.”

verses.name: Luke 24:50
verses.text: He led them out as far as Bethany, and he lifted up his hands, and blessed them.

verses.name: Luke 24:51
verses.text: While he blessed them, he withdrew from them, and was carried up into heaven.

verses.name: Luke 24:52
verses.text: They worshiped him, and returned to Jerusalem with great joy,

verses.name: Luke 24:53
verses.text: and were continually in the temple, praising and blessing God. Amen.

verses.name: John 1:1
verses.text: In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.

verses.name: John 1:2
verses.text: The same was in the beginning with God.

verses.name: John 1:3
verses.text: All things were made through him. Without him was not anything made that has been made.

verses.name: John 1:4
verses.text: In him was life, and the life was the light of men.

verses.name: John 1:5
verses.text: The light shines in the darkness, and the darkness hasn’t overcome it.

verses.name: John 1:6
verses.text: There came a man, sent from God, whose name was John.

verses.name: John 1:7
verses.text: The same came as a witness, that he might testify about the light, that all might believe through him.

verses.name: John 1:8
verses.text: He was not the light, but was sent that he might testify about the light.

verses.name: John 1:9
verses.text: The true light that enlightens everyone was coming into the world.

verses.name: John 1:10
verses.text: He was in the world, and the world was made through him, and the world didn’t recognize him.

verses.name: John 1:11
verses.text: He came to his own, and those who were his own didn’t receive him.

verses.name: John 1:12
verses.text: But as many as received him, to them he gave the right to become God’s children, to those who believe in his name:

verses.name: John 1:13
verses.text: who were born not of blood, nor of the will of the flesh, nor of the will of man, but of God.

verses.name: John 1:14
verses.text: The Word became flesh, and lived among us. We saw his glory, such glory as of the one and only Son of the Father, full of grace and truth.

verses.name: John 1:15
verses.text: John testified about him. He cried out, saying, “This was he of whom I said, ‘He who comes after me has surpassed me, for he was before me.’”

verses.name: John 1:16
verses.text: From his fullness we all received grace upon grace.

verses.name: John 1:17
verses.text: For the law was given through Moses. Grace and truth were realized through Jesus Christ.

verses.name: John 1:18
verses.text: No one has seen God at any time. The one and only Son, who is in the bosom of the Father, he has declared him.

verses.name: John 1:19
verses.text: This is John’s testimony, when the Jews sent priests and Levites from Jerusalem to ask him, “Who are you?”

verses.name: John 1:20
verses.text: He declared, and didn’t deny, but he declared, “I am not the Christ.”

verses.name: John 1:21
verses.text: They asked him, “What then? Are you Elijah?” He said, “I am not.” “Are you the prophet?” He answered, “No.”

verses.name: John 1:22
verses.text: They said therefore to him, “Who are you? Give us an answer to take back to those who sent us. What do you say about yourself?”

verses.name: John 1:23
verses.text: He said, “I am the voice of one crying in the wilderness, ‘Make straight the way of the Lord,’ as Isaiah the prophet said.”

verses.name: John 1:24
verses.text: The ones who had been sent were from the Pharisees.

verses.name: John 1:25
verses.text: They asked him, “Why then do you baptize, if you are not the Christ, nor Elijah, nor the prophet?”

verses.name: John 1:26
verses.text: John answered them, “I baptize in water, but among you stands one whom you don’t know.

verses.name: John 1:27
verses.text: He is the one who comes after me, who is preferred before me, whose sandal strap I’m not worthy to loosen.”

verses.name: John 1:28
verses.text: These things were done in Bethany beyond the Jordan, where John was baptizing.

verses.name: John 1:29
verses.text: The next day, he saw Jesus coming to him, and said, “Behold, the Lamb of God, who takes away the sin of the world!

verses.name: John 1:30
verses.text: This is he of whom I said, ‘After me comes a man who is preferred before me, for he was before me.’

verses.name: John 1:31
verses.text: I didn’t know him, but for this reason I came baptizing in water: that he would be revealed to Israel.”

verses.name: John 1:32
verses.text: John testified, saying, “I have seen the Spirit descending like a dove out of heaven, and it remained on him.

verses.name: John 1:33
verses.text: I didn’t recognize him, but he who sent me to baptize in water, he said to me, ‘On whomever you will see the Spirit descending, and remaining on him, the same is he who baptizes in the Holy Spirit.’

verses.name: John 1:34
verses.text: I have seen, and have testified that this is the Son of God.”

verses.name: John 1:35
verses.text: Again, the next day, John was standing with two of his disciples,

verses.name: John 1:36
verses.text: and he looked at Jesus as he walked, and said, “Behold, the Lamb of God!”

verses.name: John 1:37
verses.text: The two disciples heard him speak, and they followed Jesus.

verses.name: John 1:38
verses.text: Jesus turned, and saw them following, and said to them, “What are you looking for?” They said to him, “Rabbi” (which is to say, being interpreted, Teacher), “where are you staying?”

verses.name: John 1:39
verses.text: He said to them, “Come, and see.” They came and saw where he was staying, and they stayed with him that day. It was about the tenth hour.

verses.name: John 1:40
verses.text: One of the two who heard John, and followed him, was Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother.

verses.name: John 1:41
verses.text: He first found his own brother, Simon, and said to him, “We have found the Messiah!” (which is, being interpreted, Christ).

verses.name: John 1:42
verses.text: He brought him to Jesus. Jesus looked at him, and said, “You are Simon the son of Jonah. You shall be called Cephas” (which is by interpretation, Peter).

verses.name: John 1:43
verses.text: On the next day, he was determined to go out into Galilee, and he found Philip. Jesus said to him, “Follow me.”

verses.name: John 1:44
verses.text: Now Philip was from Bethsaida, of the city of Andrew and Peter.

verses.name: John 1:45
verses.text: Philip found Nathanael, and said to him, “We have found him, of whom Moses in the law, and the prophets, wrote: Jesus of Nazareth, the son of Joseph.”

verses.name: John 1:46
verses.text: Nathanael said to him, “Can any good thing come out of Nazareth?” Philip said to him, “Come and see.”

verses.name: John 1:47
verses.text: Jesus saw Nathanael coming to him, and said about him, “Behold, an Israelite indeed, in whom is no deceit!”

verses.name: John 1:48
verses.text: Nathanael said to him, “How do you know me?” Jesus answered him, “Before Philip called you, when you were under the fig tree, I saw you.”

verses.name: John 1:49
verses.text: Nathanael answered him, “Rabbi, you are the Son of God! You are King of Israel!”

verses.name: John 1:50
verses.text: Jesus answered him, “Because I told you, ‘I saw you underneath the fig tree,’ do you believe? You will see greater things than these!”

verses.name: John 1:51
verses.text: He said to him, “Most certainly, I tell you all, hereafter you will see heaven opened, and the angels of God ascending and descending on the Son of Man.”

verses.name: John 2:1
verses.text: The third day, there was a marriage in Cana of Galilee. Jesus’ mother was there.

verses.name: John 2:2
verses.text: Jesus also was invited, with his disciples, to the marriage.

verses.name: John 2:3
verses.text: When the wine ran out, Jesus’ mother said to him, “They have no wine.”

verses.name: John 2:4
verses.text: Jesus said to her, “Woman, what does that have to do with you and me? My hour has not yet come.”

verses.name: John 2:5
verses.text: His mother said to the servants, “Whatever he says to you, do it.”

verses.name: John 2:6
verses.text: Now there were six water pots of stone set there after the Jews’ way of purifying, containing two or three metretes apiece.

verses.name: John 2:7
verses.text: Jesus said to them, “Fill the water pots with water.” They filled them up to the brim.

verses.name: John 2:8
verses.text: He said to them, “Now draw some out, and take it to the ruler of the feast.” So they took it.

verses.name: John 2:9
verses.text: When the ruler of the feast tasted the water now become wine, and didn’t know where it came from (but the servants who had drawn the water knew), the ruler of the feast called the bridegroom,

verses.name: John 2:10
verses.text: and said to him, “Everyone serves the good wine first, and when the guests have drunk freely, then that which is worse. You have kept the good wine until now!”

verses.name: John 2:11
verses.text: This beginning of his signs Jesus did in Cana of Galilee, and revealed his glory; and his disciples believed in him.

verses.name: John 2:12
verses.text: After this, he went down to Capernaum, he, and his mother, his brothers, and his disciples; and they stayed there a few days.

verses.name: John 2:13
verses.text: The Passover of the Jews was at hand, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.

verses.name: John 2:14
verses.text: He found in the temple those who sold oxen, sheep, and doves, and the changers of money sitting.

verses.name: John 2:15
verses.text: He made a whip of cords, and threw all out of the temple, both the sheep and the oxen; and he poured out the changers’ money, and overthrew their tables.

verses.name: John 2:16
verses.text: To those who sold the doves, he said, “Take these things out of here! Don’t make my Father’s house a marketplace!”

verses.name: John 2:17
verses.text: His disciples remembered that it was written, “Zeal for your house will eat me up.”

verses.name: John 2:18
verses.text: The Jews therefore answered him, “What sign do you show us, seeing that you do these things?”

verses.name: John 2:19
verses.text: Jesus answered them, “Destroy this temple, and in three days I will raise it up.”

verses.name: John 2:20
verses.text: The Jews therefore said, “It took forty-six years to build this temple! Will you raise it up in three days?”

verses.name: John 2:21
verses.text: But he spoke of the temple of his body.

verses.name: John 2:22
verses.text: When therefore he was raised from the dead, his disciples remembered that he said this, and they believed the Scripture, and the word which Jesus had said.

verses.name: John 2:23
verses.text: Now when he was in Jerusalem at the Passover, during the feast, many believed in his name, observing his signs which he did.

verses.name: John 2:24
verses.text: But Jesus didn’t entrust himself to them, because he knew everyone,

verses.name: John 2:25
verses.text: and because he didn’t need for anyone to testify concerning man; for he himself knew what was in man.

verses.name: John 3:1
verses.text: Now there was a man of the Pharisees named Nicodemus, a ruler of the Jews.

verses.name: John 3:2
verses.text: The same came to him by night, and said to him, “Rabbi, we know that you are a teacher come from God, for no one can do these signs that you do, unless God is with him.”

verses.name: John 3:3
verses.text: Jesus answered him, “Most certainly, I tell you, unless one is born anew, he can’t see God’s Kingdom.”

verses.name: John 3:4
verses.text: Nicodemus said to him, “How can a man be born when he is old? Can he enter a second time into his mother’s womb, and be born?”

verses.name: John 3:5
verses.text: Jesus answered, “Most certainly I tell you, unless one is born of water and spirit, he can’t enter into God’s Kingdom.

verses.name: John 3:6
verses.text: That which is born of the flesh is flesh. That which is born of the Spirit is spirit.

verses.name: John 3:7
verses.text: Don’t marvel that I said to you, ‘You must be born anew.’

verses.name: John 3:8
verses.text: The wind blows where it wants to, and you hear its sound, but don’t know where it comes from and where it is going. So is everyone who is born of the Spirit.”

verses.name: John 3:9
verses.text: Nicodemus answered him, “How can these things be?”

verses.name: John 3:10
verses.text: Jesus answered him, “Are you the teacher of Israel, and don’t understand these things?

verses.name: John 3:11
verses.text: Most certainly I tell you, we speak that which we know, and testify of that which we have seen, and you don’t receive our witness.

verses.name: John 3:12
verses.text: If I told you earthly things and you don’t believe, how will you believe if I tell you heavenly things?

verses.name: John 3:13
verses.text: No one has ascended into heaven, but he who descended out of heaven, the Son of Man, who is in heaven.

verses.name: John 3:14
verses.text: As Moses lifted up the serpent in the wilderness, even so must the Son of Man be lifted up,

verses.name: John 3:15
verses.text: that whoever believes in him should not perish, but have eternal life.

verses.name: John 3:16
verses.text: For God so loved the world, that he gave his one and only Son, that whoever believes in him should not perish, but have eternal life.

verses.name: John 3:17
verses.text: For God didn’t send his Son into the world to judge the world, but that the world should be saved through him.

verses.name: John 3:18
verses.text: He who believes in him is not judged. He who doesn’t believe has been judged already, because he has not believed in the name of the one and only Son of God.

verses.name: John 3:19
verses.text: This is the judgment, that the light has come into the world, and men loved the darkness rather than the light; for their works were evil.

verses.name: John 3:20
verses.text: For everyone who does evil hates the light, and doesn’t come to the light, lest his works would be exposed.

verses.name: John 3:21
verses.text: But he who does the truth comes to the light, that his works may be revealed, that they have been done in God.”

verses.name: John 3:22
verses.text: After these things, Jesus came with his disciples into the land of Judea. He stayed there with them, and baptized.

verses.name: John 3:23
verses.text: John also was baptizing in Enon near Salim, because there was much water there. They came, and were baptized.

verses.name: John 3:24
verses.text: For John was not yet thrown into prison.

verses.name: John 3:25
verses.text: There arose therefore a questioning on the part of John’s disciples with some Jews about purification.

verses.name: John 3:26
verses.text: They came to John, and said to him, “Rabbi, he who was with you beyond the Jordan, to whom you have testified, behold, the same baptizes, and everyone is coming to him.”

verses.name: John 3:27
verses.text: John answered, “A man can receive nothing, unless it has been given him from heaven.

verses.name: John 3:28
verses.text: You yourselves testify that I said, ‘I am not the Christ,’ but, ‘I have been sent before him.’

verses.name: John 3:29
verses.text: He who has the bride is the bridegroom; but the friend of the bridegroom, who stands and hears him, rejoices greatly because of the bridegroom’s voice. This, my joy, therefore is made full.

verses.name: John 3:30
verses.text: He must increase, but I must decrease.

verses.name: John 3:31
verses.text: He who comes from above is above all. He who is from the earth belongs to the earth, and speaks of the earth. He who comes from heaven is above all.

verses.name: John 3:32
verses.text: What he has seen and heard, of that he testifies; and no one receives his witness.

verses.name: John 3:33
verses.text: He who has received his witness has set his seal to this, that God is true.

verses.name: John 3:34
verses.text: For he whom God has sent speaks the words of God; for God gives the Spirit without measure.

verses.name: John 3:35
verses.text: The Father loves the Son, and has given all things into his hand.

verses.name: John 3:36
verses.text: One who believes in the Son has eternal life, but one who disobeys the Son won’t see life, but the wrath of God remains on him.”

verses.name: John 4:1
verses.text: Therefore when the Lord knew that the Pharisees had heard that Jesus was making and baptizing more disciples than John

verses.name: John 4:2
verses.text: (although Jesus himself didn’t baptize, but his disciples),

verses.name: John 4:3
verses.text: he left Judea, and departed into Galilee.

verses.name: John 4:4
verses.text: He needed to pass through Samaria.

verses.name: John 4:5
verses.text: So he came to a city of Samaria, called Sychar, near the parcel of ground that Jacob gave to his son, Joseph.

verses.name: John 4:6
verses.text: Jacob’s well was there. Jesus therefore, being tired from his journey, sat down by the well. It was about the sixth hour.

verses.name: John 4:7
verses.text: A woman of Samaria came to draw water. Jesus said to her, “Give me a drink.”

verses.name: John 4:8
verses.text: For his disciples had gone away into the city to buy food.

verses.name: John 4:9
verses.text: The Samaritan woman therefore said to him, “How is it that you, being a Jew, ask for a drink from me, a Samaritan woman?” (For Jews have no dealings with Samaritans.)

verses.name: John 4:10
verses.text: Jesus answered her, “If you knew the gift of God, and who it is who says to you, ‘Give me a drink,’ you would have asked him, and he would have given you living water.”

verses.name: John 4:11
verses.text: The woman said to him, “Sir, you have nothing to draw with, and the well is deep. So where do you get that living water?

verses.name: John 4:12
verses.text: Are you greater than our father, Jacob, who gave us the well, and drank of it himself, as did his children, and his livestock?”

verses.name: John 4:13
verses.text: Jesus answered her, “Everyone who drinks of this water will thirst again,

verses.name: John 4:14
verses.text: but whoever drinks of the water that I will give him will never thirst again; but the water that I will give him will become in him a well of water springing up to eternal life.”

verses.name: John 4:15
verses.text: The woman said to him, “Sir, give me this water, so that I don’t get thirsty, neither come all the way here to draw.”

verses.name: John 4:16
verses.text: Jesus said to her, “Go, call your husband, and come here.”

verses.name: John 4:17
verses.text: The woman answered, “I have no husband.” Jesus said to her, “You said well, ‘I have no husband,’

verses.name: John 4:18
verses.text: for you have had five husbands; and he whom you now have is not your husband. This you have said truly.”

verses.name: John 4:19
verses.text: The woman said to him, “Sir, I perceive that you are a prophet.

verses.name: John 4:20
verses.text: Our fathers worshiped in this mountain, and you Jews say that in Jerusalem is the place where people ought to worship.”

verses.name: John 4:21
verses.text: Jesus said to her, “Woman, believe me, the hour comes, when neither in this mountain, nor in Jerusalem, will you worship the Father.

verses.name: John 4:22
verses.text: You worship that which you don’t know. We worship that which we know; for salvation is from the Jews.

verses.name: John 4:23
verses.text: But the hour comes, and now is, when the true worshipers will worship the Father in spirit and truth, for the Father seeks such to be his worshipers.

verses.name: John 4:24
verses.text: God is spirit, and those who worship him must worship in spirit and truth.”

verses.name: John 4:25
verses.text: The woman said to him, “I know that Messiah comes, he who is called Christ. When he has come, he will declare to us all things.”

verses.name: John 4:26
verses.text: Jesus said to her, “I am he, the one who speaks to you.”

verses.name: John 4:27
verses.text: At this, his disciples came. They marveled that he was speaking with a woman; yet no one said, “What are you looking for?” or, “Why do you speak with her?”

verses.name: John 4:28
verses.text: So the woman left her water pot, and went away into the city, and said to the people,

verses.name: John 4:29
verses.text: “Come, see a man who told me everything that I did. Can this be the Christ?”

verses.name: John 4:30
verses.text: They went out of the city, and were coming to him.

verses.name: John 4:31
verses.text: In the meanwhile, the disciples urged him, saying, “Rabbi, eat.”

verses.name: John 4:32
verses.text: But he said to them, “I have food to eat that you don’t know about.”

verses.name: John 4:33
verses.text: The disciples therefore said to one another, “Has anyone brought him something to eat?”

verses.name: John 4:34
verses.text: Jesus said to them, “My food is to do the will of him who sent me, and to accomplish his work.

verses.name: John 4:35
verses.text: Don’t you say, ‘There are yet four months until the harvest?’ Behold, I tell you, lift up your eyes, and look at the fields, that they are white for harvest already.

verses.name: John 4:36
verses.text: He who reaps receives wages, and gathers fruit to eternal life; that both he who sows and he who reaps may rejoice together.

verses.name: John 4:37
verses.text: For in this the saying is true, ‘One sows, and another reaps.’

verses.name: John 4:38
verses.text: I sent you to reap that for which you haven’t labored. Others have labored, and you have entered into their labor.”

verses.name: John 4:39
verses.text: From that city many of the Samaritans believed in him because of the word of the woman, who testified, “He told me everything that I did.”

verses.name: John 4:40
verses.text: So when the Samaritans came to him, they begged him to stay with them. He stayed there two days.

verses.name: John 4:41
verses.text: Many more believed because of his word.

verses.name: John 4:42
verses.text: They said to the woman, “Now we believe, not because of your speaking; for we have heard for ourselves, and know that this is indeed the Christ, the Savior of the world.”

verses.name: John 4:43
verses.text: After the two days he went out from there and went into Galilee.

verses.name: John 4:44
verses.text: For Jesus himself testified that a prophet has no honor in his own country.

verses.name: John 4:45
verses.text: So when he came into Galilee, the Galileans received him, having seen all the things that he did in Jerusalem at the feast, for they also went to the feast.

verses.name: John 4:46
verses.text: Jesus came therefore again to Cana of Galilee, where he made the water into wine. There was a certain nobleman whose son was sick at Capernaum.

verses.name: John 4:47
verses.text: When he heard that Jesus had come out of Judea into Galilee, he went to him, and begged him that he would come down and heal his son, for he was at the point of death.

verses.name: John 4:48
verses.text: Jesus therefore said to him, “Unless you see signs and wonders, you will in no way believe.”

verses.name: John 4:49
verses.text: The nobleman said to him, “Sir, come down before my child dies.”

verses.name: John 4:50
verses.text: Jesus said to him, “Go your way. Your son lives.” The man believed the word that Jesus spoke to him, and he went his way.

verses.name: John 4:51
verses.text: As he was now going down, his servants met him and reported, saying “Your child lives!”

verses.name: John 4:52
verses.text: So he inquired of them the hour when he began to get better. They said therefore to him, “Yesterday at the seventh hour, the fever left him.”

verses.name: John 4:53
verses.text: So the father knew that it was at that hour in which Jesus said to him, “Your son lives.” He believed, as did his whole house.

verses.name: John 4:54
verses.text: This is again the second sign that Jesus did, having come out of Judea into Galilee.

verses.name: John 5:1
verses.text: After these things, there was a feast of the Jews, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.

verses.name: John 5:2
verses.text: Now in Jerusalem by the sheep gate, there is a pool, which is called in Hebrew, “Bethesda”, having five porches.

verses.name: John 5:3
verses.text: In these lay a great multitude of those who were sick, blind, lame, or paralyzed, waiting for the moving of the water;

verses.name: John 5:4
verses.text: for an angel went down at certain times into the pool, and stirred up the water. Whoever stepped in first after the stirring of the water was healed of whatever disease he had.

verses.name: John 5:5
verses.text: A certain man was there, who had been sick for thirty-eight years.

verses.name: John 5:6
verses.text: When Jesus saw him lying there, and knew that he had been sick for a long time, he asked him, “Do you want to be made well?”

verses.name: John 5:7
verses.text: The sick man answered him, “Sir, I have no one to put me into the pool when the water is stirred up, but while I’m coming, another steps down before me.”

verses.name: John 5:8
verses.text: Jesus said to him, “Arise, take up your mat, and walk.”

verses.name: John 5:9
verses.text: Immediately, the man was made well, and took up his mat and walked. Now it was the Sabbath on that day.

verses.name: John 5:10
verses.text: So the Jews said to him who was cured, “It is the Sabbath. It is not lawful for you to carry the mat.”

verses.name: John 5:11
verses.text: He answered them, “He who made me well, the same said to me, ‘Take up your mat, and walk.’”

verses.name: John 5:12
verses.text: Then they asked him, “Who is the man who said to you, ‘Take up your mat, and walk’?”

verses.name: John 5:13
verses.text: But he who was healed didn’t know who it was, for Jesus had withdrawn, a crowd being in the place.

verses.name: John 5:14
verses.text: Afterward Jesus found him in the temple, and said to him, “Behold, you are made well. Sin no more, so that nothing worse happens to you.”

verses.name: John 5:15
verses.text: The man went away, and told the Jews that it was Jesus who had made him well.

verses.name: John 5:16
verses.text: For this cause the Jews persecuted Jesus, and sought to kill him, because he did these things on the Sabbath.

verses.name: John 5:17
verses.text: But Jesus answered them, “My Father is still working, so I am working, too.”

verses.name: John 5:18
verses.text: For this cause therefore the Jews sought all the more to kill him, because he not only broke the Sabbath, but also called God his own Father, making himself equal with God.

verses.name: John 5:19
verses.text: Jesus therefore answered them, “Most certainly, I tell you, the Son can do nothing of himself, but what he sees the Father doing. For whatever things he does, these the Son also does likewise.

verses.name: John 5:20
verses.text: For the Father has affection for the Son, and shows him all things that he himself does. He will show him greater works than these, that you may marvel.

verses.name: John 5:21
verses.text: For as the Father raises the dead and gives them life, even so the Son also gives life to whom he desires.

verses.name: John 5:22
verses.text: For the Father judges no one, but he has given all judgment to the Son,

verses.name: John 5:23
verses.text: that all may honor the Son, even as they honor the Father. He who doesn’t honor the Son doesn’t honor the Father who sent him.

verses.name: John 5:24
verses.text: “Most certainly I tell you, he who hears my word, and believes him who sent me, has eternal life, and doesn’t come into judgment, but has passed out of death into life.

verses.name: John 5:25
verses.text: Most certainly, I tell you, the hour comes, and now is, when the dead will hear the Son of God’s voice; and those who hear will live.

verses.name: John 5:26
verses.text: For as the Father has life in himself, even so he gave to the Son also to have life in himself.

verses.name: John 5:27
verses.text: He also gave him authority to execute judgment, because he is a son of man.

verses.name: John 5:28
verses.text: Don’t marvel at this, for the hour comes, in which all that are in the tombs will hear his voice,

verses.name: John 5:29
verses.text: and will come out; those who have done good, to the resurrection of life; and those who have done evil, to the resurrection of judgment.

verses.name: John 5:30
verses.text: I can of myself do nothing. As I hear, I judge, and my judgment is righteous; because I don’t seek my own will, but the will of my Father who sent me.

verses.name: John 5:31
verses.text: “If I testify about myself, my witness is not valid.

verses.name: John 5:32
verses.text: It is another who testifies about me. I know that the testimony which he testifies about me is true.

verses.name: John 5:33
verses.text: You have sent to John, and he has testified to the truth.

verses.name: John 5:34
verses.text: But the testimony which I receive is not from man. However, I say these things that you may be saved.

verses.name: John 5:35
verses.text: He was the burning and shining lamp, and you were willing to rejoice for a while in his light.

verses.name: John 5:36
verses.text: But the testimony which I have is greater than that of John, for the works which the Father gave me to accomplish, the very works that I do, testify about me, that the Father has sent me.

verses.name: John 5:37
verses.text: The Father himself, who sent me, has testified about me. You have neither heard his voice at any time, nor seen his form.

verses.name: John 5:38
verses.text: You don’t have his word living in you; because you don’t believe him whom he sent.

verses.name: John 5:39
verses.text: “You search the Scriptures, because you think that in them you have eternal life; and these are they which testify about me.

verses.name: John 5:40
verses.text: Yet you will not come to me, that you may have life.

verses.name: John 5:41
verses.text: I don’t receive glory from men.

verses.name: John 5:42
verses.text: But I know you, that you don’t have God’s love in yourselves.

verses.name: John 5:43
verses.text: I have come in my Father’s name, and you don’t receive me. If another comes in his own name, you will receive him.

verses.name: John 5:44
verses.text: How can you believe, who receive glory from one another, and you don’t seek the glory that comes from the only God?

verses.name: John 5:45
verses.text: “Don’t think that I will accuse you to the Father. There is one who accuses you, even Moses, on whom you have set your hope.

verses.name: John 5:46
verses.text: For if you believed Moses, you would believe me; for he wrote about me.

verses.name: John 5:47
verses.text: But if you don’t believe his writings, how will you believe my words?”

verses.name: John 6:1
verses.text: After these things, Jesus went away to the other side of the sea of Galilee, which is also called the Sea of Tiberias.

verses.name: John 6:2
verses.text: A great multitude followed him, because they saw his signs which he did on those who were sick.

verses.name: John 6:3
verses.text: Jesus went up into the mountain, and he sat there with his disciples.

verses.name: John 6:4
verses.text: Now the Passover, the feast of the Jews, was at hand.

verses.name: John 6:5
verses.text: Jesus therefore lifting up his eyes, and seeing that a great multitude was coming to him, said to Philip, “Where are we to buy bread, that these may eat?”

verses.name: John 6:6
verses.text: This he said to test him, for he himself knew what he would do.

verses.name: John 6:7
verses.text: Philip answered him, “Two hundred denarii worth of bread is not sufficient for them, that everyone of them may receive a little.”

verses.name: John 6:8
verses.text: One of his disciples, Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother, said to him,

verses.name: John 6:9
verses.text: “There is a boy here who has five barley loaves and two fish, but what are these among so many?”

verses.name: John 6:10
verses.text: Jesus said, “Have the people sit down.” Now there was much grass in that place. So the men sat down, in number about five thousand.

verses.name: John 6:11
verses.text: Jesus took the loaves; and having given thanks, he distributed to the disciples, and the disciples to those who were sitting down; likewise also of the fish as much as they desired.

verses.name: John 6:12
verses.text: When they were filled, he said to his disciples, “Gather up the broken pieces which are left over, that nothing be lost.”

verses.name: John 6:13
verses.text: So they gathered them up, and filled twelve baskets with broken pieces from the five barley loaves, which were left over by those who had eaten.

verses.name: John 6:14
verses.text: When therefore the people saw the sign which Jesus did, they said, “This is truly the prophet who comes into the world.”

verses.name: John 6:15
verses.text: Jesus therefore, perceiving that they were about to come and take him by force, to make him king, withdrew again to the mountain by himself.

verses.name: John 6:16
verses.text: When evening came, his disciples went down to the sea,

verses.name: John 6:17
verses.text: and they entered into the boat, and were going over the sea to Capernaum. It was now dark, and Jesus had not come to them.

verses.name: John 6:18
verses.text: The sea was tossed by a great wind blowing.

verses.name: John 6:19
verses.text: When therefore they had rowed about twenty-five or thirty stadia, they saw Jesus walking on the sea, and drawing near to the boat; and they were afraid.

verses.name: John 6:20
verses.text: But he said to them, “It is I. Don’t be afraid.”

verses.name: John 6:21
verses.text: They were willing therefore to receive him into the boat. Immediately the boat was at the land where they were going.

verses.name: John 6:22
verses.text: On the next day, the multitude that stood on the other side of the sea saw that there was no other boat there, except the one in which his disciples had embarked, and that Jesus hadn’t entered with his disciples into the boat, but his disciples had gone away alone.

verses.name: John 6:23
verses.text: However boats from Tiberias came near to the place where they ate the bread after the Lord had given thanks.

verses.name: John 6:24
verses.text: When the multitude therefore saw that Jesus wasn’t there, nor his disciples, they themselves got into the boats, and came to Capernaum, seeking Jesus.

verses.name: John 6:25
verses.text: When they found him on the other side of the sea, they asked him, “Rabbi, when did you come here?”

verses.name: John 6:26
verses.text: Jesus answered them, “Most certainly I tell you, you seek me, not because you saw signs, but because you ate of the loaves, and were filled.

verses.name: John 6:27
verses.text: Don’t work for the food which perishes, but for the food which remains to eternal life, which the Son of Man will give to you. For God the Father has sealed him.”

verses.name: John 6:28
verses.text: They said therefore to him, “What must we do, that we may work the works of God?”

verses.name: John 6:29
verses.text: Jesus answered them, “This is the work of God, that you believe in him whom he has sent.”

verses.name: John 6:30
verses.text: They said therefore to him, “What then do you do for a sign, that we may see, and believe you? What work do you do?

verses.name: John 6:31
verses.text: Our fathers ate the manna in the wilderness. As it is written, ‘He gave them bread out of heaven to eat.’”

verses.name: John 6:32
verses.text: Jesus therefore said to them, “Most certainly, I tell you, it wasn’t Moses who gave you the bread out of heaven, but my Father gives you the true bread out of heaven.

verses.name: John 6:33
verses.text: For the bread of God is that which comes down out of heaven, and gives life to the world.”

verses.name: John 6:34
verses.text: They said therefore to him, “Lord, always give us this bread.”

verses.name: John 6:35
verses.text: Jesus said to them, “I am the bread of life. He who comes to me will not be hungry, and he who believes in me will never be thirsty.

verses.name: John 6:36
verses.text: But I told you that you have seen me, and yet you don’t believe.

verses.name: John 6:37
verses.text: All those whom the Father gives me will come to me. He who comes to me I will in no way throw out.

verses.name: John 6:38
verses.text: For I have come down from heaven, not to do my own will, but the will of him who sent me.

verses.name: John 6:39
verses.text: This is the will of my Father who sent me, that of all he has given to me I should lose nothing, but should raise him up at the last day.

verses.name: John 6:40
verses.text: This is the will of the one who sent me, that everyone who sees the Son, and believes in him, should have eternal life; and I will raise him up at the last day.”

verses.name: John 6:41
verses.text: The Jews therefore murmured concerning him, because he said, “I am the bread which came down out of heaven.”

verses.name: John 6:42
verses.text: They said, “Isn’t this Jesus, the son of Joseph, whose father and mother we know? How then does he say, ‘I have come down out of heaven?’”

verses.name: John 6:43
verses.text: Therefore Jesus answered them, “Don’t murmur among yourselves.

verses.name: John 6:44
verses.text: No one can come to me unless the Father who sent me draws him, and I will raise him up in the last day.

verses.name: John 6:45
verses.text: It is written in the prophets, ‘They will all be taught by God.’ Therefore everyone who hears from the Father, and has learned, comes to me.

verses.name: John 6:46
verses.text: Not that anyone has seen the Father, except he who is from God. He has seen the Father.

verses.name: John 6:47
verses.text: Most certainly, I tell you, he who believes in me has eternal life.

verses.name: John 6:48
verses.text: I am the bread of life.

verses.name: John 6:49
verses.text: Your fathers ate the manna in the wilderness, and they died.

verses.name: John 6:50
verses.text: This is the bread which comes down out of heaven, that anyone may eat of it and not die.

verses.name: John 6:51
verses.text: I am the living bread which came down out of heaven. If anyone eats of this bread, he will live forever. Yes, the bread which I will give for the life of the world is my flesh.”

verses.name: John 6:52
verses.text: The Jews therefore contended with one another, saying, “How can this man give us his flesh to eat?”

verses.name: John 6:53
verses.text: Jesus therefore said to them, “Most certainly I tell you, unless you eat the flesh of the Son of Man and drink his blood, you don’t have life in yourselves.

verses.name: John 6:54
verses.text: He who eats my flesh and drinks my blood has eternal life, and I will raise him up at the last day.

verses.name: John 6:55
verses.text: For my flesh is food indeed, and my blood is drink indeed.

verses.name: John 6:56
verses.text: He who eats my flesh and drinks my blood lives in me, and I in him.

verses.name: John 6:57
verses.text: As the living Father sent me, and I live because of the Father; so he who feeds on me, he will also live because of me.

verses.name: John 6:58
verses.text: This is the bread which came down out of heaven—not as our fathers ate the manna, and died. He who eats this bread will live forever.”

verses.name: John 6:59
verses.text: He said these things in the synagogue, as he taught in Capernaum.

verses.name: John 6:60
verses.text: Therefore many of his disciples, when they heard this, said, “This is a hard saying! Who can listen to it?”

verses.name: John 6:61
verses.text: But Jesus knowing in himself that his disciples murmured at this, said to them, “Does this cause you to stumble?

verses.name: John 6:62
verses.text: Then what if you would see the Son of Man ascending to where he was before?

verses.name: John 6:63
verses.text: It is the spirit who gives life. The flesh profits nothing. The words that I speak to you are spirit, and are life.

verses.name: John 6:64
verses.text: But there are some of you who don’t believe.” For Jesus knew from the beginning who they were who didn’t believe, and who it was who would betray him.

verses.name: John 6:65
verses.text: He said, “For this cause I have said to you that no one can come to me, unless it is given to him by my Father.”

verses.name: John 6:66
verses.text: At this, many of his disciples went back, and walked no more with him.

verses.name: John 6:67
verses.text: Jesus said therefore to the twelve, “You don’t also want to go away, do you?”

verses.name: John 6:68
verses.text: Simon Peter answered him, “Lord, to whom would we go? You have the words of eternal life.

verses.name: John 6:69
verses.text: We have come to believe and know that you are the Christ, the Son of the living God.”

verses.name: John 6:70
verses.text: Jesus answered them, “Didn’t I choose you, the twelve, and one of you is a devil?”

verses.name: John 6:71
verses.text: Now he spoke of Judas, the son of Simon Iscariot, for it was he who would betray him, being one of the twelve.

verses.name: John 7:1
verses.text: After these things, Jesus was walking in Galilee, for he wouldn’t walk in Judea, because the Jews sought to kill him.

verses.name: John 7:2
verses.text: Now the feast of the Jews, the Feast of Booths, was at hand.

verses.name: John 7:3
verses.text: His brothers therefore said to him, “Depart from here, and go into Judea, that your disciples also may see your works which you do.

verses.name: John 7:4
verses.text: For no one does anything in secret, and himself seeks to be known openly. If you do these things, reveal yourself to the world.”

verses.name: John 7:5
verses.text: For even his brothers didn’t believe in him.

verses.name: John 7:6
verses.text: Jesus therefore said to them, “My time has not yet come, but your time is always ready.

verses.name: John 7:7
verses.text: The world can’t hate you, but it hates me, because I testify about it, that its works are evil.

verses.name: John 7:8
verses.text: You go up to the feast. I am not yet going up to this feast, because my time is not yet fulfilled.”

verses.name: John 7:9
verses.text: Having said these things to them, he stayed in Galilee.

verses.name: John 7:10
verses.text: But when his brothers had gone up to the feast, then he also went up, not publicly, but as it were in secret.

verses.name: John 7:11
verses.text: The Jews therefore sought him at the feast, and said, “Where is he?”

verses.name: John 7:12
verses.text: There was much murmuring among the multitudes concerning him. Some said, “He is a good man.” Others said, “Not so, but he leads the multitude astray.”

verses.name: John 7:13
verses.text: Yet no one spoke openly of him for fear of the Jews.

verses.name: John 7:14
verses.text: But when it was now the middle of the feast, Jesus went up into the temple and taught.

verses.name: John 7:15
verses.text: The Jews therefore marveled, saying, “How does this man know letters, having never been educated?”

verses.name: John 7:16
verses.text: Jesus therefore answered them, “My teaching is not mine, but his who sent me.

verses.name: John 7:17
verses.text: If anyone desires to do his will, he will know about the teaching, whether it is from God, or if I am speaking from myself.

verses.name: John 7:18
verses.text: He who speaks from himself seeks his own glory, but he who seeks the glory of him who sent him is true, and no unrighteousness is in him.

verses.name: John 7:19
verses.text: Didn’t Moses give you the law, and yet none of you keeps the law? Why do you seek to kill me?”

verses.name: John 7:20
verses.text: The multitude answered, “You have a demon! Who seeks to kill you?”

verses.name: John 7:21
verses.text: Jesus answered them, “I did one work, and you all marvel because of it.
