verses.name: John 7:22
verses.text: Moses has given you circumcision (not that it is of Moses, but of the fathers), and on the Sabbath you circumcise a boy.

verses.name: John 7:23
verses.text: If a boy receives circumcision on the Sabbath, that the law of Moses may not be broken, are you angry with me, because I made a man completely healthy on the Sabbath?

verses.name: John 7:24
verses.text: Don’t judge according to appearance, but judge righteous judgment.”

verses.name: John 7:25
verses.text: Therefore some of them of Jerusalem said, “Isn’t this he whom they seek to kill?

verses.name: John 7:26
verses.text: Behold, he speaks openly, and they say nothing to him. Can it be that the rulers indeed know that this is truly the Christ?

verses.name: John 7:27
verses.text: However we know where this man comes from, but when the Christ comes, no one will know where he comes from.”

verses.name: John 7:28
verses.text: Jesus therefore cried out in the temple, teaching and saying, “You both know me, and know where I am from. I have not come of myself, but he who sent me is true, whom you don’t know.

verses.name: John 7:29
verses.text: I know him, because I am from him, and he sent me.”

verses.name: John 7:30
verses.text: They sought therefore to take him; but no one laid a hand on him, because his hour had not yet come.

verses.name: John 7:31
verses.text: But of the multitude, many believed in him. They said, “When the Christ comes, he won’t do more signs than those which this man has done, will he?”

verses.name: John 7:32
verses.text: The Pharisees heard the multitude murmuring these things concerning him, and the chief priests and the Pharisees sent officers to arrest him.

verses.name: John 7:33
verses.text: Then Jesus said, “I will be with you a little while longer, then I go to him who sent me.

verses.name: John 7:34
verses.text: You will seek me, and won’t find me; and where I am, you can’t come.”

verses.name: John 7:35
verses.text: The Jews therefore said among themselves, “Where will this man go that we won’t find him? Will he go to the Dispersion among the Greeks, and teach the Greeks?

verses.name: John 7:36
verses.text: What is this word that he said, ‘You will seek me, and won’t find me; and where I am, you can’t come’?”

verses.name: John 7:37
verses.text: Now on the last and greatest day of the feast, Jesus stood and cried out, “If anyone is thirsty, let him come to me and drink!

verses.name: John 7:38
verses.text: He who believes in me, as the Scripture has said, from within him will flow rivers of living water.”

verses.name: John 7:39
verses.text: But he said this about the Spirit, which those believing in him were to receive. For the Holy Spirit was not yet given, because Jesus wasn’t yet glorified.

verses.name: John 7:40
verses.text: Many of the multitude therefore, when they heard these words, said, “This is truly the prophet.”

verses.name: John 7:41
verses.text: Others said, “This is the Christ.” But some said, “What, does the Christ come out of Galilee?

verses.name: John 7:42
verses.text: Hasn’t the Scripture said that the Christ comes of the offspring of David, and from Bethlehem, the village where David was?”

verses.name: John 7:43
verses.text: So there arose a division in the multitude because of him.

verses.name: John 7:44
verses.text: Some of them would have arrested him, but no one laid hands on him.

verses.name: John 7:45
verses.text: The officers therefore came to the chief priests and Pharisees, and they said to them, “Why didn’t you bring him?”

verses.name: John 7:46
verses.text: The officers answered, “No man ever spoke like this man!”

verses.name: John 7:47
verses.text: The Pharisees therefore answered them, “You aren’t also led astray, are you?

verses.name: John 7:48
verses.text: Have any of the rulers believed in him, or of the Pharisees?

verses.name: John 7:49
verses.text: But this multitude that doesn’t know the law is accursed.”

verses.name: John 7:50
verses.text: Nicodemus (he who came to him by night, being one of them) said to them,

verses.name: John 7:51
verses.text: “Does our law judge a man, unless it first hears from him personally and knows what he does?”

verses.name: John 7:52
verses.text: They answered him, “Are you also from Galilee? Search, and see that no prophet has arisen out of Galilee.”

verses.name: John 7:53
verses.text: Everyone went to his own house,

verses.name: John 8:1
verses.text: but Jesus went to the Mount of Olives.

verses.name: John 8:2
verses.text: Now very early in the morning, he came again into the temple, and all the people came to him. He sat down, and taught them.

verses.name: John 8:3
verses.text: The scribes and the Pharisees brought a woman taken in adultery. Having set her in the middle,

verses.name: John 8:4
verses.text: they told him, “Teacher, we found this woman in adultery, in the very act.

verses.name: John 8:5
verses.text: Now in our law, Moses commanded us to stone such women. What then do you say about her?”

verses.name: John 8:6
verses.text: They said this testing him, that they might have something to accuse him of. But Jesus stooped down, and wrote on the ground with his finger.

verses.name: John 8:7
verses.text: But when they continued asking him, he looked up and said to them, “He who is without sin among you, let him throw the first stone at her.”

verses.name: John 8:8
verses.text: Again he stooped down, and with his finger wrote on the ground.

verses.name: John 8:9
verses.text: They, when they heard it, being convicted by their conscience, went out one by one, beginning from the oldest, even to the last. Jesus was left alone with the woman where she was, in the middle.

verses.name: John 8:10
verses.text: Jesus, standing up, saw her and said, “Woman, where are your accusers? Did no one condemn you?”

verses.name: John 8:11
verses.text: She said, “No one, Lord.” Jesus said, “Neither do I condemn you. Go your way. From now on, sin no more.”

verses.name: John 8:12
verses.text: Again, therefore, Jesus spoke to them, saying, “I am the light of the world. He who follows me will not walk in the darkness, but will have the light of life.”

verses.name: John 8:13
verses.text: The Pharisees therefore said to him, “You testify about yourself. Your testimony is not valid.”

verses.name: John 8:14
verses.text: Jesus answered them, “Even if I testify about myself, my testimony is true, for I know where I came from, and where I am going; but you don’t know where I came from, or where I am going.

verses.name: John 8:15
verses.text: You judge according to the flesh. I judge no one.

verses.name: John 8:16
verses.text: Even if I do judge, my judgment is true, for I am not alone, but I am with the Father who sent me.

verses.name: John 8:17
verses.text: It’s also written in your law that the testimony of two people is valid.

verses.name: John 8:18
verses.text: I am one who testifies about myself, and the Father who sent me testifies about me.”

verses.name: John 8:19
verses.text: They said therefore to him, “Where is your Father?” Jesus answered, “You know neither me, nor my Father. If you knew me, you would know my Father also.”

verses.name: John 8:20
verses.text: Jesus spoke these words in the treasury, as he taught in the temple. Yet no one arrested him, because his hour had not yet come.

verses.name: John 8:21
verses.text: Jesus said therefore again to them, “I am going away, and you will seek me, and you will die in your sins. Where I go, you can’t come.”

verses.name: John 8:22
verses.text: The Jews therefore said, “Will he kill himself, because he says, ‘Where I am going, you can’t come’?”

verses.name: John 8:23
verses.text: He said to them, “You are from beneath. I am from above. You are of this world. I am not of this world.

verses.name: John 8:24
verses.text: I said therefore to you that you will die in your sins; for unless you believe that I am he, you will die in your sins.”

verses.name: John 8:25
verses.text: They said therefore to him, “Who are you?” Jesus said to them, “Just what I have been saying to you from the beginning.

verses.name: John 8:26
verses.text: I have many things to speak and to judge concerning you. However he who sent me is true; and the things which I heard from him, these I say to the world.”

verses.name: John 8:27
verses.text: They didn’t understand that he spoke to them about the Father.

verses.name: John 8:28
verses.text: Jesus therefore said to them, “When you have lifted up the Son of Man, then you will know that I am he, and I do nothing of myself, but as my Father taught me, I say these things.

verses.name: John 8:29
verses.text: He who sent me is with me. The Father hasn’t left me alone, for I always do the things that are pleasing to him.”

verses.name: John 8:30
verses.text: As he spoke these things, many believed in him.

verses.name: John 8:31
verses.text: Jesus therefore said to those Jews who had believed him, “If you remain in my word, then you are truly my disciples.

verses.name: John 8:32
verses.text: You will know the truth, and the truth will make you free.”

verses.name: John 8:33
verses.text: They answered him, “We are Abraham’s offspring, and have never been in bondage to anyone. How do you say, ‘You will be made free’?”

verses.name: John 8:34
verses.text: Jesus answered them, “Most certainly I tell you, everyone who commits sin is the bondservant of sin.

verses.name: John 8:35
verses.text: A bondservant doesn’t live in the house forever. A son remains forever.

verses.name: John 8:36
verses.text: If therefore the Son makes you free, you will be free indeed.

verses.name: John 8:37
verses.text: I know that you are Abraham’s offspring, yet you seek to kill me, because my word finds no place in you.

verses.name: John 8:38
verses.text: I say the things which I have seen with my Father; and you also do the things which you have seen with your father.”

verses.name: John 8:39
verses.text: They answered him, “Our father is Abraham.” Jesus said to them, “If you were Abraham’s children, you would do the works of Abraham.

verses.name: John 8:40
verses.text: But now you seek to kill me, a man who has told you the truth, which I heard from God. Abraham didn’t do this.

verses.name: John 8:41
verses.text: You do the works of your father.” They said to him, “We were not born of sexual immorality. We have one Father, God.”

verses.name: John 8:42
verses.text: Therefore Jesus said to them, “If God were your father, you would love me, for I came out and have come from God. For I haven’t come of myself, but he sent me.

verses.name: John 8:43
verses.text: Why don’t you understand my speech? Because you can’t hear my word.

verses.name: John 8:44
verses.text: You are of your father, the devil, and you want to do the desires of your father. He was a murderer from the beginning, and doesn’t stand in the truth, because there is no truth in him. When he speaks a lie, he speaks on his own; for he is a liar, and the father of lies.

verses.name: John 8:45
verses.text: But because I tell the truth, you don’t believe me.

verses.name: John 8:46
verses.text: Which of you convicts me of sin? If I tell the truth, why do you not believe me?

verses.name: John 8:47
verses.text: He who is of God hears the words of God. For this cause you don’t hear, because you are not of God.”

verses.name: John 8:48
verses.text: Then the Jews answered him, “Don’t we say well that you are a Samaritan, and have a demon?”

verses.name: John 8:49
verses.text: Jesus answered, “I don’t have a demon, but I honor my Father, and you dishonor me.

verses.name: John 8:50
verses.text: But I don’t seek my own glory. There is one who seeks and judges.

verses.name: John 8:51
verses.text: Most certainly, I tell you, if a person keeps my word, he will never see death.”

verses.name: John 8:52
verses.text: Then the Jews said to him, “Now we know that you have a demon. Abraham died, and the prophets; and you say, ‘If a man keeps my word, he will never taste of death.’

verses.name: John 8:53
verses.text: Are you greater than our father, Abraham, who died? The prophets died. Who do you make yourself out to be?”

verses.name: John 8:54
verses.text: Jesus answered, “If I glorify myself, my glory is nothing. It is my Father who glorifies me, of whom you say that he is our God.

verses.name: John 8:55
verses.text: You have not known him, but I know him. If I said, ‘I don’t know him,’ I would be like you, a liar. But I know him, and keep his word.

verses.name: John 8:56
verses.text: Your father Abraham rejoiced to see my day. He saw it, and was glad.”

verses.name: John 8:57
verses.text: The Jews therefore said to him, “You are not yet fifty years old, and have you seen Abraham?”

verses.name: John 8:58
verses.text: Jesus said to them, “Most certainly, I tell you, before Abraham came into existence, I AM.”

verses.name: John 8:59
verses.text: Therefore they took up stones to throw at him, but Jesus was hidden, and went out of the temple, having gone through the middle of them, and so passed by.

verses.name: John 9:1
verses.text: As he passed by, he saw a man blind from birth.

verses.name: John 9:2
verses.text: His disciples asked him, “Rabbi, who sinned, this man or his parents, that he was born blind?”

verses.name: John 9:3
verses.text: Jesus answered, “Neither did this man sin, nor his parents; but, that the works of God might be revealed in him.

verses.name: John 9:4
verses.text: I must work the works of him who sent me, while it is day. The night is coming, when no one can work.

verses.name: John 9:5
verses.text: While I am in the world, I am the light of the world.”

verses.name: John 9:6
verses.text: When he had said this, he spat on the ground, made mud with the saliva, anointed the blind man’s eyes with the mud,

verses.name: John 9:7
verses.text: and said to him, “Go, wash in the pool of Siloam” (which means “Sent”). So he went away, washed, and came back seeing.

verses.name: John 9:8
verses.text: The neighbors therefore, and those who saw that he was blind before, said, “Isn’t this he who sat and begged?”

verses.name: John 9:9
verses.text: Others were saying, “It is he.” Still others were saying, “He looks like him.” He said, “I am he.”

verses.name: John 9:10
verses.text: They therefore were asking him, “How were your eyes opened?”

verses.name: John 9:11
verses.text: He answered, “A man called Jesus made mud, anointed my eyes, and said to me, ‘Go to the pool of Siloam, and wash.’ So I went away and washed, and I received sight.”

verses.name: John 9:12
verses.text: Then they asked him, “Where is he?” He said, “I don’t know.”

verses.name: John 9:13
verses.text: They brought him who had been blind to the Pharisees.

verses.name: John 9:14
verses.text: It was a Sabbath when Jesus made the mud and opened his eyes.

verses.name: John 9:15
verses.text: Again therefore the Pharisees also asked him how he received his sight. He said to them, “He put mud on my eyes, I washed, and I see.”

verses.name: John 9:16
verses.text: Some therefore of the Pharisees said, “This man is not from God, because he doesn’t keep the Sabbath.” Others said, “How can a man who is a sinner do such signs?” There was division among them.

verses.name: John 9:17
verses.text: Therefore they asked the blind man again, “What do you say about him, because he opened your eyes?” He said, “He is a prophet.”

verses.name: John 9:18
verses.text: The Jews therefore did not believe concerning him, that he had been blind, and had received his sight, until they called the parents of him who had received his sight,

verses.name: John 9:19
verses.text: and asked them, “Is this your son, whom you say was born blind? How then does he now see?”

verses.name: John 9:20
verses.text: His parents answered them, “We know that this is our son, and that he was born blind;

verses.name: John 9:21
verses.text: but how he now sees, we don’t know; or who opened his eyes, we don’t know. He is of age. Ask him. He will speak for himself.”

verses.name: John 9:22
verses.text: His parents said these things because they feared the Jews; for the Jews had already agreed that if any man would confess him as Christ, he would be put out of the synagogue.

verses.name: John 9:23
verses.text: Therefore his parents said, “He is of age. Ask him.”

verses.name: John 9:24
verses.text: So they called the man who was blind a second time, and said to him, “Give glory to God. We know that this man is a sinner.”

verses.name: John 9:25
verses.text: He therefore answered, “I don’t know if he is a sinner. One thing I do know: that though I was blind, now I see.”

verses.name: John 9:26
verses.text: They said to him again, “What did he do to you? How did he open your eyes?”

verses.name: John 9:27
verses.text: He answered them, “I told you already, and you didn’t listen. Why do you want to hear it again? You don’t also want to become his disciples, do you?”

verses.name: John 9:28
verses.text: They insulted him and said, “You are his disciple, but we are disciples of Moses.

verses.name: John 9:29
verses.text: We know that God has spoken to Moses. But as for this man, we don’t know where he comes from.”

verses.name: John 9:30
verses.text: The man answered them, “How amazing! You don’t know where he comes from, yet he opened my eyes.

verses.name: John 9:31
verses.text: We know that God doesn’t listen to sinners, but if anyone is a worshiper of God, and does his will, he listens to him.

verses.name: John 9:32
verses.text: Since the world began it has never been heard of that anyone opened the eyes of someone born blind.

verses.name: John 9:33
verses.text: If this man were not from God, he could do nothing.”

verses.name: John 9:34
verses.text: They answered him, “You were altogether born in sins, and do you teach us?” They threw him out.

verses.name: John 9:35
verses.text: Jesus heard that they had thrown him out, and finding him, he said, “Do you believe in the Son of God?”

verses.name: John 9:36
verses.text: He answered, “Who is he, Lord, that I may believe in him?”

verses.name: John 9:37
verses.text: Jesus said to him, “You have both seen him, and it is he who speaks with you.”

verses.name: John 9:38
verses.text: He said, “Lord, I believe!” and he worshiped him.

verses.name: John 9:39
verses.text: Jesus said, “I came into this world for judgment, that those who don’t see may see; and that those who see may become blind.”

verses.name: John 9:40
verses.text: Those of the Pharisees who were with him heard these things, and said to him, “Are we also blind?”

verses.name: John 9:41
verses.text: Jesus said to them, “If you were blind, you would have no sin; but now you say, ‘We see.’ Therefore your sin remains.

verses.name: John 10:1
verses.text: “Most certainly, I tell you, one who doesn’t enter by the door into the sheep fold, but climbs up some other way, the same is a thief and a robber.

verses.name: John 10:2
verses.text: But one who enters in by the door is the shepherd of the sheep.

verses.name: John 10:3
verses.text: The gatekeeper opens the gate for him, and the sheep listen to his voice. He calls his own sheep by name, and leads them out.

verses.name: John 10:4
verses.text: Whenever he brings out his own sheep, he goes before them, and the sheep follow him, for they know his voice.

verses.name: John 10:5
verses.text: They will by no means follow a stranger, but will flee from him; for they don’t know the voice of strangers.”

verses.name: John 10:6
verses.text: Jesus spoke this parable to them, but they didn’t understand what he was telling them.

verses.name: John 10:7
verses.text: Jesus therefore said to them again, “Most certainly, I tell you, I am the sheep’s door.

verses.name: John 10:8
verses.text: All who came before me are thieves and robbers, but the sheep didn’t listen to them.

verses.name: John 10:9
verses.text: I am the door. If anyone enters in by me, he will be saved, and will go in and go out, and will find pasture.

verses.name: John 10:10
verses.text: The thief only comes to steal, kill, and destroy. I came that they may have life, and may have it abundantly.

verses.name: John 10:11
verses.text: I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd lays down his life for the sheep.

verses.name: John 10:12
verses.text: He who is a hired hand, and not a shepherd, who doesn’t own the sheep, sees the wolf coming, leaves the sheep, and flees. The wolf snatches the sheep, and scatters them.

verses.name: John 10:13
verses.text: The hired hand flees because he is a hired hand, and doesn’t care for the sheep.

verses.name: John 10:14
verses.text: I am the good shepherd. I know my own, and I’m known by my own;

verses.name: John 10:15
verses.text: even as the Father knows me, and I know the Father. I lay down my life for the sheep.

verses.name: John 10:16
verses.text: I have other sheep, which are not of this fold. I must bring them also, and they will hear my voice. They will become one flock with one shepherd.

verses.name: John 10:17
verses.text: Therefore the Father loves me, because I lay down my life, that I may take it again.

verses.name: John 10:18
verses.text: No one takes it away from me, but I lay it down by myself. I have power to lay it down, and I have power to take it again. I received this commandment from my Father.”

verses.name: John 10:19
verses.text: Therefore a division arose again among the Jews because of these words.

verses.name: John 10:20
verses.text: Many of them said, “He has a demon, and is insane! Why do you listen to him?”

verses.name: John 10:21
verses.text: Others said, “These are not the sayings of one possessed by a demon. It isn’t possible for a demon to open the eyes of the blind, is it?”

verses.name: John 10:22
verses.text: It was the Feast of the Dedication at Jerusalem.

verses.name: John 10:23
verses.text: It was winter, and Jesus was walking in the temple, in Solomon’s porch.

verses.name: John 10:24
verses.text: The Jews therefore came around him and said to him, “How long will you hold us in suspense? If you are the Christ, tell us plainly.”

verses.name: John 10:25
verses.text: Jesus answered them, “I told you, and you don’t believe. The works that I do in my Father’s name, these testify about me.

verses.name: John 10:26
verses.text: But you don’t believe, because you are not of my sheep, as I told you.

verses.name: John 10:27
verses.text: My sheep hear my voice, and I know them, and they follow me.

verses.name: John 10:28
verses.text: I give eternal life to them. They will never perish, and no one will snatch them out of my hand.

verses.name: John 10:29
verses.text: My Father, who has given them to me, is greater than all. No one is able to snatch them out of my Father’s hand.

verses.name: John 10:30
verses.text: I and the Father are one.”

verses.name: John 10:31
verses.text: Therefore Jews took up stones again to stone him.

verses.name: John 10:32
verses.text: Jesus answered them, “I have shown you many good works from my Father. For which of those works do you stone me?”

verses.name: John 10:33
verses.text: The Jews answered him, “We don’t stone you for a good work, but for blasphemy: because you, being a man, make yourself God.”

verses.name: John 10:34
verses.text: Jesus answered them, “Isn’t it written in your law, ‘I said, you are gods?’

verses.name: John 10:35
verses.text: If he called them gods, to whom the word of God came (and the Scripture can’t be broken),

verses.name: John 10:36
verses.text: do you say of him whom the Father sanctified and sent into the world, ‘You blaspheme,’ because I said, ‘I am the Son of God?’

verses.name: John 10:37
verses.text: If I don’t do the works of my Father, don’t believe me.

verses.name: John 10:38
verses.text: But if I do them, though you don’t believe me, believe the works; that you may know and believe that the Father is in me, and I in the Father.”

verses.name: John 10:39
verses.text: They sought again to seize him, and he went out of their hand.

verses.name: John 10:40
verses.text: He went away again beyond the Jordan into the place where John was baptizing at first, and there he stayed.

verses.name: John 10:41
verses.text: Many came to him. They said, “John indeed did no sign, but everything that John said about this man is true.”

verses.name: John 10:42
verses.text: Many believed in him there.

verses.name: John 11:1
verses.text: Now a certain man was sick, Lazarus from Bethany, of the village of Mary and her sister, Martha.

verses.name: John 11:2
verses.text: It was that Mary who had anointed the Lord with ointment, and wiped his feet with her hair, whose brother, Lazarus, was sick.

verses.name: John 11:3
verses.text: The sisters therefore sent to him, saying, “Lord, behold, he for whom you have great affection is sick.”

verses.name: John 11:4
verses.text: But when Jesus heard it, he said, “This sickness is not to death, but for the glory of God, that God’s Son may be glorified by it.”

verses.name: John 11:5
verses.text: Now Jesus loved Martha, and her sister, and Lazarus.

verses.name: John 11:6
verses.text: When therefore he heard that he was sick, he stayed two days in the place where he was.

verses.name: John 11:7
verses.text: Then after this he said to the disciples, “Let’s go into Judea again.”

verses.name: John 11:8
verses.text: The disciples told him, “Rabbi, the Jews were just trying to stone you, and are you going there again?”

verses.name: John 11:9
verses.text: Jesus answered, “Aren’t there twelve hours of daylight? If a man walks in the day, he doesn’t stumble, because he sees the light of this world.

verses.name: John 11:10
verses.text: But if a man walks in the night, he stumbles, because the light isn’t in him.”

verses.name: John 11:11
verses.text: He said these things, and after that, he said to them, “Our friend, Lazarus, has fallen asleep, but I am going so that I may awake him out of sleep.”

verses.name: John 11:12
verses.text: The disciples therefore said, “Lord, if he has fallen asleep, he will recover.”

verses.name: John 11:13
verses.text: Now Jesus had spoken of his death, but they thought that he spoke of taking rest in sleep.

verses.name: John 11:14
verses.text: So Jesus said to them plainly then, “Lazarus is dead.

verses.name: John 11:15
verses.text: I am glad for your sakes that I was not there, so that you may believe. Nevertheless, let’s go to him.”

verses.name: John 11:16
verses.text: Thomas therefore, who is called Didymus, said to his fellow disciples, “Let’s go also, that we may die with him.”

verses.name: John 11:17
verses.text: So when Jesus came, he found that he had been in the tomb four days already.

verses.name: John 11:18
verses.text: Now Bethany was near Jerusalem, about fifteen stadia away.

verses.name: John 11:19
verses.text: Many of the Jews had joined the women around Martha and Mary, to console them concerning their brother.

verses.name: John 11:20
verses.text: Then when Martha heard that Jesus was coming, she went and met him, but Mary stayed in the house.

verses.name: John 11:21
verses.text: Therefore Martha said to Jesus, “Lord, if you would have been here, my brother wouldn’t have died.

verses.name: John 11:22
verses.text: Even now I know that, whatever you ask of God, God will give you.”

verses.name: John 11:23
verses.text: Jesus said to her, “Your brother will rise again.”

verses.name: John 11:24
verses.text: Martha said to him, “I know that he will rise again in the resurrection at the last day.”

verses.name: John 11:25
verses.text: Jesus said to her, “I am the resurrection and the life. He who believes in me will still live, even if he dies.

verses.name: John 11:26
verses.text: Whoever lives and believes in me will never die. Do you believe this?”

verses.name: John 11:27
verses.text: She said to him, “Yes, Lord. I have come to believe that you are the Christ, God’s Son, he who comes into the world.”

verses.name: John 11:28
verses.text: When she had said this, she went away, and called Mary, her sister, secretly, saying, “The Teacher is here, and is calling you.”

verses.name: John 11:29
verses.text: When she heard this, she arose quickly, and went to him.

verses.name: John 11:30
verses.text: Now Jesus had not yet come into the village, but was in the place where Martha met him.

verses.name: John 11:31
verses.text: Then the Jews who were with her in the house, and were consoling her, when they saw Mary, that she rose up quickly and went out, followed her, saying, “She is going to the tomb to weep there.”

verses.name: John 11:32
verses.text: Therefore when Mary came to where Jesus was, and saw him, she fell down at his feet, saying to him, “Lord, if you would have been here, my brother wouldn’t have died.”

verses.name: John 11:33
verses.text: When Jesus therefore saw her weeping, and the Jews weeping who came with her, he groaned in the spirit, and was troubled,

verses.name: John 11:34
verses.text: and said, “Where have you laid him?” They told him, “Lord, come and see.”

verses.name: John 11:35
verses.text: Jesus wept.

verses.name: John 11:36
verses.text: The Jews therefore said, “See how much affection he had for him!”

verses.name: John 11:37
verses.text: Some of them said, “Couldn’t this man, who opened the eyes of him who was blind, have also kept this man from dying?”

verses.name: John 11:38
verses.text: Jesus therefore, again groaning in himself, came to the tomb. Now it was a cave, and a stone lay against it.

verses.name: John 11:39
verses.text: Jesus said, “Take away the stone.” Martha, the sister of him who was dead, said to him, “Lord, by this time there is a stench, for he has been dead four days.”

verses.name: John 11:40
verses.text: Jesus said to her, “Didn’t I tell you that if you believed, you would see God’s glory?”

verses.name: John 11:41
verses.text: So they took away the stone from the place where the dead man was lying. Jesus lifted up his eyes, and said, “Father, I thank you that you listened to me.

verses.name: John 11:42
verses.text: I know that you always listen to me, but because of the multitude standing around I said this, that they may believe that you sent me.”

verses.name: John 11:43
verses.text: When he had said this, he cried with a loud voice, “Lazarus, come out!”

verses.name: John 11:44
verses.text: He who was dead came out, bound hand and foot with wrappings, and his face was wrapped around with a cloth. Jesus said to them, “Free him, and let him go.”

verses.name: John 11:45
verses.text: Therefore many of the Jews, who came to Mary and saw what Jesus did, believed in him.

verses.name: John 11:46
verses.text: But some of them went away to the Pharisees, and told them the things which Jesus had done.

verses.name: John 11:47
verses.text: The chief priests therefore and the Pharisees gathered a council, and said, “What are we doing? For this man does many signs.

verses.name: John 11:48
verses.text: If we leave him alone like this, everyone will believe in him, and the Romans will come and take away both our place and our nation.”

verses.name: John 11:49
verses.text: But a certain one of them, Caiaphas, being high priest that year, said to them, “You know nothing at all,

verses.name: John 11:50
verses.text: nor do you consider that it is advantageous for us that one man should die for the people, and that the whole nation not perish.”

verses.name: John 11:51
verses.text: Now he didn’t say this of himself, but being high priest that year, he prophesied that Jesus would die for the nation,

verses.name: John 11:52
verses.text: and not for the nation only, but that he might also gather together into one the children of God who are scattered abroad.

verses.name: John 11:53
verses.text: So from that day forward they took counsel that they might put him to death.

verses.name: John 11:54
verses.text: Jesus therefore walked no more openly among the Jews, but departed from there into the country near the wilderness, to a city called Ephraim. He stayed there with his disciples.

verses.name: John 11:55
verses.text: Now the Passover of the Jews was at hand. Many went up from the country to Jerusalem before the Passover, to purify themselves.

verses.name: John 11:56
verses.text: Then they sought for Jesus and spoke one with another, as they stood in the temple, “What do you think—that he isn’t coming to the feast at all?”

verses.name: John 11:57
verses.text: Now the chief priests and the Pharisees had commanded that if anyone knew where he was, he should report it, that they might seize him.

verses.name: John 12:1
verses.text: Then six days before the Passover, Jesus came to Bethany, where Lazarus was, who had been dead, whom he raised from the dead.

verses.name: John 12:2
verses.text: So they made him a supper there. Martha served, but Lazarus was one of those who sat at the table with him.

verses.name: John 12:3
verses.text: Mary, therefore, took a pound of ointment of pure nard, very precious, and anointed the feet of Jesus, and wiped his feet with her hair. The house was filled with the fragrance of the ointment.

verses.name: John 12:4
verses.text: Then Judas Iscariot, Simon’s son, one of his disciples, who would betray him, said,

verses.name: John 12:5
verses.text: “Why wasn’t this ointment sold for three hundred denarii, and given to the poor?”

verses.name: John 12:6
verses.text: Now he said this, not because he cared for the poor, but because he was a thief, and having the money box, used to steal what was put into it.

verses.name: John 12:7
verses.text: But Jesus said, “Leave her alone. She has kept this for the day of my burial.

verses.name: John 12:8
verses.text: For you always have the poor with you, but you don’t always have me.”

verses.name: John 12:9
verses.text: A large crowd therefore of the Jews learned that he was there, and they came, not for Jesus’ sake only, but that they might see Lazarus also, whom he had raised from the dead.

verses.name: John 12:10
verses.text: But the chief priests conspired to put Lazarus to death also,

verses.name: John 12:11
verses.text: because on account of him many of the Jews went away and believed in Jesus.

verses.name: John 12:12
verses.text: On the next day a great multitude had come to the feast. When they heard that Jesus was coming to Jerusalem,

verses.name: John 12:13
verses.text: they took the branches of the palm trees, and went out to meet him, and cried out, “Hosanna! Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord, the King of Israel!”

verses.name: John 12:14
verses.text: Jesus, having found a young donkey, sat on it. As it is written,

verses.name: John 12:15
verses.text: “Don’t be afraid, daughter of Zion. Behold, your King comes, sitting on a donkey’s colt.”

verses.name: John 12:16
verses.text: His disciples didn’t understand these things at first, but when Jesus was glorified, then they remembered that these things were written about him, and that they had done these things to him.

verses.name: John 12:17
verses.text: The multitude therefore that was with him when he called Lazarus out of the tomb, and raised him from the dead, was testifying about it.

verses.name: John 12:18
verses.text: For this cause also the multitude went and met him, because they heard that he had done this sign.

verses.name: John 12:19
verses.text: The Pharisees therefore said among themselves, “See how you accomplish nothing. Behold, the world has gone after him.”

verses.name: John 12:20
verses.text: Now there were certain Greeks among those that went up to worship at the feast.

verses.name: John 12:21
verses.text: These, therefore, came to Philip, who was from Bethsaida of Galilee, and asked him, saying, “Sir, we want to see Jesus.”

verses.name: John 12:22
verses.text: Philip came and told Andrew, and in turn, Andrew came with Philip, and they told Jesus.

verses.name: John 12:23
verses.text: Jesus answered them, “The time has come for the Son of Man to be glorified.

verses.name: John 12:24
verses.text: Most certainly I tell you, unless a grain of wheat falls into the earth and dies, it remains by itself alone. But if it dies, it bears much fruit.

verses.name: John 12:25
verses.text: He who loves his life will lose it. He who hates his life in this world will keep it to eternal life.

verses.name: John 12:26
verses.text: If anyone serves me, let him follow me. Where I am, there will my servant also be. If anyone serves me, the Father will honor him.

verses.name: John 12:27
verses.text: “Now my soul is troubled. What shall I say? ‘Father, save me from this time?’ But for this cause I came to this time.

verses.name: John 12:28
verses.text: Father, glorify your name!” Then there came a voice out of the sky, saying, “I have both glorified it, and will glorify it again.”

verses.name: John 12:29
verses.text: The multitude therefore, who stood by and heard it, said that it had thundered. Others said, “An angel has spoken to him.”

verses.name: John 12:30
verses.text: Jesus answered, “This voice hasn’t come for my sake, but for your sakes.

verses.name: John 12:31
verses.text: Now is the judgment of this world. Now the prince of this world will be cast out.

verses.name: John 12:32
verses.text: And I, if I am lifted up from the earth, will draw all people to myself.”

verses.name: John 12:33
verses.text: But he said this, signifying by what kind of death he should die.

verses.name: John 12:34
verses.text: The multitude answered him, “We have heard out of the law that the Christ remains forever. How do you say, ‘The Son of Man must be lifted up?’ Who is this Son of Man?”

verses.name: John 12:35
verses.text: Jesus therefore said to them, “Yet a little while the light is with you. Walk while you have the light, that darkness doesn’t overtake you. He who walks in the darkness doesn’t know where he is going.

verses.name: John 12:36
verses.text: While you have the light, believe in the light, that you may become children of light.” Jesus said these things, and he departed and hid himself from them.

verses.name: John 12:37
verses.text: But though he had done so many signs before them, yet they didn’t believe in him,

verses.name: John 12:38
verses.text: that the word of Isaiah the prophet might be fulfilled, which he spoke, “Lord, who has believed our report? To whom has the arm of the Lord been revealed?”

verses.name: John 12:39
verses.text: For this cause they couldn’t believe, for Isaiah said again,

verses.name: John 12:40
verses.text: “He has blinded their eyes and he hardened their heart, lest they should see with their eyes, and perceive with their heart, and would turn, and I would heal them.”

verses.name: John 12:41
verses.text: Isaiah said these things when he saw his glory, and spoke of him.

verses.name: John 12:42
verses.text: Nevertheless even of the rulers many believed in him, but because of the Pharisees they didn’t confess it, so that they wouldn’t be put out of the synagogue,

verses.name: John 12:43
verses.text: for they loved men’s praise more than God’s praise.

verses.name: John 12:44
verses.text: Jesus cried out and said, “Whoever believes in me, believes not in me, but in him who sent me.

verses.name: John 12:45
verses.text: He who sees me sees him who sent me.

verses.name: John 12:46
verses.text: I have come as a light into the world, that whoever believes in me may not remain in the darkness.

verses.name: John 12:47
verses.text: If anyone listens to my sayings, and doesn’t believe, I don’t judge him. For I came not to judge the world, but to save the world.

verses.name: John 12:48
verses.text: He who rejects me, and doesn’t receive my sayings, has one who judges him. The word that I spoke, the same will judge him in the last day.

verses.name: John 12:49
verses.text: For I spoke not from myself, but the Father who sent me, he gave me a commandment, what I should say, and what I should speak.

verses.name: John 12:50
verses.text: I know that his commandment is eternal life. The things therefore which I speak, even as the Father has said to me, so I speak.”

verses.name: John 13:1
verses.text: Now before the feast of the Passover, Jesus, knowing that his time had come that he would depart from this world to the Father, having loved his own who were in the world, he loved them to the end.

verses.name: John 13:2
verses.text: During supper, the devil having already put into the heart of Judas Iscariot, Simon’s son, to betray him,

verses.name: John 13:3
verses.text: Jesus, knowing that the Father had given all things into his hands, and that he came from God, and was going to God,

verses.name: John 13:4
verses.text: arose from supper, and laid aside his outer garments. He took a towel, and wrapped a towel around his waist.

verses.name: John 13:5
verses.text: Then he poured water into the basin, and began to wash the disciples’ feet, and to wipe them with the towel that was wrapped around him.

verses.name: John 13:6
verses.text: Then he came to Simon Peter. He said to him, “Lord, do you wash my feet?”

verses.name: John 13:7
verses.text: Jesus answered him, “You don’t know what I am doing now, but you will understand later.”

verses.name: John 13:8
verses.text: Peter said to him, “You will never wash my feet!” Jesus answered him, “If I don’t wash you, you have no part with me.”

verses.name: John 13:9
verses.text: Simon Peter said to him, “Lord, not my feet only, but also my hands and my head!”

verses.name: John 13:10
verses.text: Jesus said to him, “Someone who has bathed only needs to have his feet washed, but is completely clean. You are clean, but not all of you.”

verses.name: John 13:11
verses.text: For he knew him who would betray him, therefore he said, “You are not all clean.”

verses.name: John 13:12
verses.text: So when he had washed their feet, put his outer garment back on, and sat down again, he said to them, “Do you know what I have done to you?

verses.name: John 13:13
verses.text: You call me, ‘Teacher’ and ‘Lord.’ You say so correctly, for so I am.

verses.name: John 13:14
verses.text: If I then, the Lord and the Teacher, have washed your feet, you also ought to wash one another’s feet.

verses.name: John 13:15
verses.text: For I have given you an example, that you also should do as I have done to you.

verses.name: John 13:16
verses.text: Most certainly I tell you, a servant is not greater than his lord, neither one who is sent greater than he who sent him.

verses.name: John 13:17
verses.text: If you know these things, blessed are you if you do them.

verses.name: John 13:18
verses.text: I don’t speak concerning all of you. I know whom I have chosen. But that the Scripture may be fulfilled, ‘He who eats bread with me has lifted up his heel against me.’

verses.name: John 13:19
verses.text: From now on, I tell you before it happens, that when it happens, you may believe that I am he.

verses.name: John 13:20
verses.text: Most certainly I tell you, he who receives whomever I send, receives me; and he who receives me, receives him who sent me.”

verses.name: John 13:21
verses.text: When Jesus had said this, he was troubled in spirit, and testified, “Most certainly I tell you that one of you will betray me.”

verses.name: John 13:22
verses.text: The disciples looked at one another, perplexed about whom he spoke.

verses.name: John 13:23
verses.text: One of his disciples, whom Jesus loved, was at the table, leaning against Jesus’ breast.

verses.name: John 13:24
verses.text: Simon Peter therefore beckoned to him, and said to him, “Tell us who it is of whom he speaks.”

verses.name: John 13:25
verses.text: He, leaning back, as he was, on Jesus’ breast, asked him, “Lord, who is it?”

verses.name: John 13:26
verses.text: Jesus therefore answered, “It is he to whom I will give this piece of bread when I have dipped it.” So when he had dipped the piece of bread, he gave it to Judas, the son of Simon Iscariot.

verses.name: John 13:27
verses.text: After the piece of bread, then Satan entered into him. Then Jesus said to him, “What you do, do quickly.”

verses.name: John 13:28
verses.text: Now no man at the table knew why he said this to him.

verses.name: John 13:29
verses.text: For some thought, because Judas had the money box, that Jesus said to him, “Buy what things we need for the feast,” or that he should give something to the poor.

verses.name: John 13:30
verses.text: Therefore having received that morsel, he went out immediately. It was night.

verses.name: John 13:31
verses.text: When he had gone out, Jesus said, “Now the Son of Man has been glorified, and God has been glorified in him.

verses.name: John 13:32
verses.text: If God has been glorified in him, God will also glorify him in himself, and he will glorify him immediately.

verses.name: John 13:33
verses.text: Little children, I will be with you a little while longer. You will seek me, and as I said to the Jews, ‘Where I am going, you can’t come,’ so now I tell you.

verses.name: John 13:34
verses.text: A new commandment I give to you, that you love one another. Just as I have loved you, you also love one another.

verses.name: John 13:35
verses.text: By this everyone will know that you are my disciples, if you have love for one another.”

verses.name: John 13:36
verses.text: Simon Peter said to him, “Lord, where are you going?” Jesus answered, “Where I am going, you can’t follow now, but you will follow afterwards.”

verses.name: John 13:37
verses.text: Peter said to him, “Lord, why can’t I follow you now? I will lay down my life for you.”

verses.name: John 13:38
verses.text: Jesus answered him, “Will you lay down your life for me? Most certainly I tell you, the rooster won’t crow until you have denied me three times.

verses.name: John 14:1
verses.text: “Don’t let your heart be troubled. Believe in God. Believe also in me.

verses.name: John 14:2
verses.text: In my Father’s house are many homes. If it weren’t so, I would have told you. I am going to prepare a place for you.

verses.name: John 14:3
verses.text: If I go and prepare a place for you, I will come again, and will receive you to myself; that where I am, you may be there also.

verses.name: John 14:4
verses.text: Where I go, you know, and you know the way.”

verses.name: John 14:5
verses.text: Thomas said to him, “Lord, we don’t know where you are going. How can we know the way?”

verses.name: John 14:6
verses.text: Jesus said to him, “I am the way, the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father, except through me.

verses.name: John 14:7
verses.text: If you had known me, you would have known my Father also. From now on, you know him, and have seen him.”

verses.name: John 14:8
verses.text: Philip said to him, “Lord, show us the Father, and that will be enough for us.”

verses.name: John 14:9
verses.text: Jesus said to him, “Have I been with you such a long time, and do you not know me, Philip? He who has seen me has seen the Father. How do you say, ‘Show us the Father?’

verses.name: John 14:10
verses.text: Don’t you believe that I am in the Father, and the Father in me? The words that I tell you, I speak not from myself; but the Father who lives in me does his works.

verses.name: John 14:11
verses.text: Believe me that I am in the Father, and the Father in me; or else believe me for the very works’ sake.

verses.name: John 14:12
verses.text: Most certainly I tell you, he who believes in me, the works that I do, he will do also; and he will do greater works than these, because I am going to my Father.

verses.name: John 14:13
verses.text: Whatever you will ask in my name, that will I do, that the Father may be glorified in the Son.

verses.name: John 14:14
verses.text: If you will ask anything in my name, I will do it.

verses.name: John 14:15
verses.text: If you love me, keep my commandments.

verses.name: John 14:16
verses.text: I will pray to the Father, and he will give you another Counselor, that he may be with you forever,—

verses.name: John 14:17
verses.text: the Spirit of truth, whom the world can’t receive; for it doesn’t see him, neither knows him. You know him, for he lives with you, and will be in you.

verses.name: John 14:18
verses.text: I will not leave you orphans. I will come to you.

verses.name: John 14:19
verses.text: Yet a little while, and the world will see me no more; but you will see me. Because I live, you will live also.

verses.name: John 14:20
verses.text: In that day you will know that I am in my Father, and you in me, and I in you.

verses.name: John 14:21
verses.text: One who has my commandments, and keeps them, that person is one who loves me. One who loves me will be loved by my Father, and I will love him, and will reveal myself to him.”

verses.name: John 14:22
verses.text: Judas (not Iscariot) said to him, “Lord, what has happened that you are about to reveal yourself to us, and not to the world?”

verses.name: John 14:23
verses.text: Jesus answered him, “If a man loves me, he will keep my word. My Father will love him, and we will come to him, and make our home with him.

verses.name: John 14:24
verses.text: He who doesn’t love me doesn’t keep my words. The word which you hear isn’t mine, but the Father’s who sent me.

verses.name: John 14:25
verses.text: I have said these things to you, while still living with you.

verses.name: John 14:26
verses.text: But the Counselor, the Holy Spirit, whom the Father will send in my name, he will teach you all things, and will remind you of all that I said to you.

verses.name: John 14:27
verses.text: Peace I leave with you. My peace I give to you; not as the world gives, give I to you. Don’t let your heart be troubled, neither let it be fearful.

verses.name: John 14:28
verses.text: You heard how I told you, ‘I go away, and I come to you.’ If you loved me, you would have rejoiced, because I said ‘I am going to my Father;’ for the Father is greater than I.

verses.name: John 14:29
verses.text: Now I have told you before it happens so that, when it happens, you may believe.

verses.name: John 14:30
verses.text: I will no more speak much with you, for the prince of the world comes, and he has nothing in me.

verses.name: John 14:31
verses.text: But that the world may know that I love the Father, and as the Father commanded me, even so I do. Arise, let us go from here.

verses.name: John 15:1
verses.text: “I am the true vine, and my Father is the farmer.

verses.name: John 15:2
verses.text: Every branch in me that doesn’t bear fruit, he takes away. Every branch that bears fruit, he prunes, that it may bear more fruit.

verses.name: John 15:3
verses.text: You are already pruned clean because of the word which I have spoken to you.

verses.name: John 15:4
verses.text: Remain in me, and I in you. As the branch can’t bear fruit by itself, unless it remains in the vine, so neither can you, unless you remain in me.

verses.name: John 15:5
verses.text: I am the vine. You are the branches. He who remains in me, and I in him, the same bears much fruit, for apart from me you can do nothing.

verses.name: John 15:6
verses.text: If a man doesn’t remain in me, he is thrown out as a branch, and is withered; and they gather them, throw them into the fire, and they are burned.

verses.name: John 15:7
verses.text: If you remain in me, and my words remain in you, you will ask whatever you desire, and it will be done for you.

verses.name: John 15:8
verses.text: “In this is my Father glorified, that you bear much fruit; and so you will be my disciples.

verses.name: John 15:9
verses.text: Even as the Father has loved me, I also have loved you. Remain in my love.

verses.name: John 15:10
verses.text: If you keep my commandments, you will remain in my love; even as I have kept my Father’s commandments, and remain in his love.

verses.name: John 15:11
verses.text: I have spoken these things to you, that my joy may remain in you, and that your joy may be made full.

verses.name: John 15:12
verses.text: “This is my commandment, that you love one another, even as I have loved you.

verses.name: John 15:13
verses.text: Greater love has no one than this, that someone lay down his life for his friends.

verses.name: John 15:14
verses.text: You are my friends, if you do whatever I command you.

verses.name: John 15:15
verses.text: No longer do I call you servants, for the servant doesn’t know what his lord does. But I have called you friends, for everything that I heard from my Father, I have made known to you.

verses.name: John 15:16
verses.text: You didn’t choose me, but I chose you, and appointed you, that you should go and bear fruit, and that your fruit should remain; that whatever you will ask of the Father in my name, he may give it to you.

verses.name: John 15:17
verses.text: “I command these things to you, that you may love one another.

verses.name: John 15:18
verses.text: If the world hates you, you know that it has hated me before it hated you.

verses.name: John 15:19
verses.text: If you were of the world, the world would love its own. But because you are not of the world, since I chose you out of the world, therefore the world hates you.

verses.name: John 15:20
verses.text: Remember the word that I said to you: ‘A servant is not greater than his lord.’ If they persecuted me, they will also persecute you. If they kept my word, they will also keep yours.

verses.name: John 15:21
verses.text: But all these things will they do to you for my name’s sake, because they don’t know him who sent me.

verses.name: John 15:22
verses.text: If I had not come and spoken to them, they would not have had sin; but now they have no excuse for their sin.

verses.name: John 15:23
verses.text: He who hates me, hates my Father also.

verses.name: John 15:24
verses.text: If I hadn’t done among them the works which no one else did, they wouldn’t have had sin. But now have they seen and also hated both me and my Father.

verses.name: John 15:25
verses.text: But this happened so that the word may be fulfilled which was written in their law, ‘They hated me without a cause.’

verses.name: John 15:26
verses.text: “When the Counselor has come, whom I will send to you from the Father, the Spirit of truth, who proceeds from the Father, he will testify about me.

verses.name: John 15:27
verses.text: You will also testify, because you have been with me from the beginning.

verses.name: John 16:1
verses.text: “I have said these things to you so that you wouldn’t be caused to stumble.

verses.name: John 16:2
verses.text: They will put you out of the synagogues. Yes, the time comes that whoever kills you will think that he offers service to God.

verses.name: John 16:3
verses.text: They will do these things because they have not known the Father, nor me.

verses.name: John 16:4
verses.text: But I have told you these things, so that when the time comes, you may remember that I told you about them. I didn’t tell you these things from the beginning, because I was with you.

verses.name: John 16:5
verses.text: But now I am going to him who sent me, and none of you asks me, ‘Where are you going?’

verses.name: John 16:6
verses.text: But because I have told you these things, sorrow has filled your heart.

verses.name: John 16:7
verses.text: Nevertheless I tell you the truth: It is to your advantage that I go away, for if I don’t go away, the Counselor won’t come to you. But if I go, I will send him to you.

verses.name: John 16:8
verses.text: When he has come, he will convict the world about sin, about righteousness, and about judgment;

verses.name: John 16:9
verses.text: about sin, because they don’t believe in me;

verses.name: John 16:10
verses.text: about righteousness, because I am going to my Father, and you won’t see me any more;

verses.name: John 16:11
verses.text: about judgment, because the prince of this world has been judged.

verses.name: John 16:12
verses.text: “I have yet many things to tell you, but you can’t bear them now.

verses.name: John 16:13
verses.text: However when he, the Spirit of truth, has come, he will guide you into all truth, for he will not speak from himself; but whatever he hears, he will speak. He will declare to you things that are coming.

verses.name: John 16:14
verses.text: He will glorify me, for he will take from what is mine, and will declare it to you.

verses.name: John 16:15
verses.text: All things whatever the Father has are mine; therefore I said that he takes of mine, and will declare it to you.

verses.name: John 16:16
verses.text: A little while, and you will not see me. Again a little while, and you will see me.”

verses.name: John 16:17
verses.text: Some of his disciples therefore said to one another, “What is this that he says to us, ‘A little while, and you won’t see me, and again a little while, and you will see me;’ and, ‘Because I go to the Father’?”

verses.name: John 16:18
verses.text: They said therefore, “What is this that he says, ‘A little while’? We don’t know what he is saying.”

verses.name: John 16:19
verses.text: Therefore Jesus perceived that they wanted to ask him, and he said to them, “Do you inquire among yourselves concerning this, that I said, ‘A little while, and you won’t see me, and again a little while, and you will see me?’

verses.name: John 16:20
verses.text: Most certainly I tell you, that you will weep and lament, but the world will rejoice. You will be sorrowful, but your sorrow will be turned into joy.

verses.name: John 16:21
verses.text: A woman, when she gives birth, has sorrow, because her time has come. But when she has delivered the child, she doesn’t remember the anguish any more, for the joy that a human being is born into the world.

verses.name: John 16:22
verses.text: Therefore you now have sorrow, but I will see you again, and your heart will rejoice, and no one will take your joy away from you.

verses.name: John 16:23
verses.text: “In that day you will ask me no questions. Most certainly I tell you, whatever you may ask of the Father in my name, he will give it to you.

verses.name: John 16:24
verses.text: Until now, you have asked nothing in my name. Ask, and you will receive, that your joy may be made full.

verses.name: John 16:25
verses.text: I have spoken these things to you in figures of speech. But the time is coming when I will no more speak to you in figures of speech, but will tell you plainly about the Father.

verses.name: John 16:26
verses.text: In that day you will ask in my name; and I don’t say to you, that I will pray to the Father for you,

verses.name: John 16:27
verses.text: for the Father himself loves you, because you have loved me, and have believed that I came from God.

verses.name: John 16:28
verses.text: I came from the Father, and have come into the world. Again, I leave the world, and go to the Father.”

verses.name: John 16:29
verses.text: His disciples said to him, “Behold, now you are speaking plainly, and using no figures of speech.

verses.name: John 16:30
verses.text: Now we know that you know all things, and don’t need for anyone to question you. By this we believe that you came from God.”

verses.name: John 16:31
verses.text: Jesus answered them, “Do you now believe?

verses.name: John 16:32
verses.text: Behold, the time is coming, yes, and has now come, that you will be scattered, everyone to his own place, and you will leave me alone. Yet I am not alone, because the Father is with me.

verses.name: John 16:33
verses.text: I have told you these things, that in me you may have peace. In the world you have oppression; but cheer up! I have overcome the world.”

verses.name: John 17:1
verses.text: Jesus said these things, and lifting up his eyes to heaven, he said, “Father, the time has come. Glorify your Son, that your Son may also glorify you;

verses.name: John 17:2
verses.text: even as you gave him authority over all flesh, so he will give eternal life to all whom you have given him.

verses.name: John 17:3
verses.text: This is eternal life, that they should know you, the only true God, and him whom you sent, Jesus Christ.

verses.name: John 17:4
verses.text: I glorified you on the earth. I have accomplished the work which you have given me to do.

verses.name: John 17:5
verses.text: Now, Father, glorify me with your own self with the glory which I had with you before the world existed.

verses.name: John 17:6
verses.text: I revealed your name to the people whom you have given me out of the world. They were yours, and you have given them to me. They have kept your word.

verses.name: John 17:7
verses.text: Now they have known that all things whatever you have given me are from you,

verses.name: John 17:8
verses.text: for the words which you have given me I have given to them, and they received them, and knew for sure that I came from you, and they have believed that you sent me.

verses.name: John 17:9
verses.text: I pray for them. I don’t pray for the world, but for those whom you have given me, for they are yours.

verses.name: John 17:10
verses.text: All things that are mine are yours, and yours are mine, and I am glorified in them.

verses.name: John 17:11
verses.text: I am no more in the world, but these are in the world, and I am coming to you. Holy Father, keep them through your name which you have given me, that they may be one, even as we are.

verses.name: John 17:12
verses.text: While I was with them in the world, I kept them in your name. Those whom you have given me I have kept. None of them is lost, except the son of destruction, that the Scripture might be fulfilled.

verses.name: John 17:13
verses.text: But now I come to you, and I say these things in the world, that they may have my joy made full in themselves.

verses.name: John 17:14
verses.text: I have given them your word. The world hated them, because they are not of the world, even as I am not of the world.

verses.name: John 17:15
verses.text: I pray not that you would take them from the world, but that you would keep them from the evil one.

verses.name: John 17:16
verses.text: They are not of the world even as I am not of the world.

verses.name: John 17:17
verses.text: Sanctify them in your truth. Your word is truth.

verses.name: John 17:18
verses.text: As you sent me into the world, even so I have sent them into the world.

verses.name: John 17:19
verses.text: For their sakes I sanctify myself, that they themselves also may be sanctified in truth.

verses.name: John 17:20
verses.text: Not for these only do I pray, but for those also who will believe in me through their word,

verses.name: John 17:21
verses.text: that they may all be one; even as you, Father, are in me, and I in you, that they also may be one in us; that the world may believe that you sent me.

verses.name: John 17:22
verses.text: The glory which you have given me, I have given to them; that they may be one, even as we are one;

verses.name: John 17:23
verses.text: I in them, and you in me, that they may be perfected into one; that the world may know that you sent me, and loved them, even as you loved me.

verses.name: John 17:24
verses.text: Father, I desire that they also whom you have given me be with me where I am, that they may see my glory, which you have given me, for you loved me before the foundation of the world.

verses.name: John 17:25
verses.text: Righteous Father, the world hasn’t known you, but I knew you; and these knew that you sent me.

verses.name: John 17:26
verses.text: I made known to them your name, and will make it known; that the love with which you loved me may be in them, and I in them.”

verses.name: John 18:1
verses.text: When Jesus had spoken these words, he went out with his disciples over the brook Kidron, where there was a garden, into which he and his disciples entered.

verses.name: John 18:2
verses.text: Now Judas, who betrayed him, also knew the place, for Jesus often met there with his disciples.

verses.name: John 18:3
verses.text: Judas then, having taken a detachment of soldiers and officers from the chief priests and the Pharisees, came there with lanterns, torches, and weapons.

verses.name: John 18:4
verses.text: Jesus therefore, knowing all the things that were happening to him, went out, and said to them, “Who are you looking for?”

verses.name: John 18:5
verses.text: They answered him, “Jesus of Nazareth.” Jesus said to them, “I am he.” Judas also, who betrayed him, was standing with them.

verses.name: John 18:6
verses.text: When therefore he said to them, “I am he,” they went backward, and fell to the ground.

verses.name: John 18:7
verses.text: Again therefore he asked them, “Who are you looking for?” They said, “Jesus of Nazareth.”

verses.name: John 18:8
verses.text: Jesus answered, “I told you that I am he. If therefore you seek me, let these go their way,”

verses.name: John 18:9
verses.text: that the word might be fulfilled which he spoke, “Of those whom you have given me, I have lost none.”

verses.name: John 18:10
verses.text: Simon Peter therefore, having a sword, drew it, and struck the high priest’s servant, and cut off his right ear. The servant’s name was Malchus.

verses.name: John 18:11
verses.text: Jesus therefore said to Peter, “Put the sword into its sheath. The cup which the Father has given me, shall I not surely drink it?”

verses.name: John 18:12
verses.text: So the detachment, the commanding officer, and the officers of the Jews, seized Jesus and bound him,

verses.name: John 18:13
verses.text: and led him to Annas first, for he was father-in-law to Caiaphas, who was high priest that year.

verses.name: John 18:14
verses.text: Now it was Caiaphas who advised the Jews that it was expedient that one man should perish for the people.

verses.name: John 18:15
verses.text: Simon Peter followed Jesus, as did another disciple. Now that disciple was known to the high priest, and entered in with Jesus into the court of the high priest;

verses.name: John 18:16
verses.text: but Peter was standing at the door outside. So the other disciple, who was known to the high priest, went out and spoke to her who kept the door, and brought in Peter.

verses.name: John 18:17
verses.text: Then the maid who kept the door said to Peter, “Are you also one of this man’s disciples?” He said, “I am not.”

verses.name: John 18:18
verses.text: Now the servants and the officers were standing there, having made a fire of coals, for it was cold. They were warming themselves. Peter was with them, standing and warming himself.

verses.name: John 18:19
verses.text: The high priest therefore asked Jesus about his disciples, and about his teaching.

verses.name: John 18:20
verses.text: Jesus answered him, “I spoke openly to the world. I always taught in synagogues, and in the temple, where the Jews always meet. I said nothing in secret.

verses.name: John 18:21
verses.text: Why do you ask me? Ask those who have heard me what I said to them. Behold, these know the things which I said.”

verses.name: John 18:22
verses.text: When he had said this, one of the officers standing by slapped Jesus with his hand, saying, “Do you answer the high priest like that?”

verses.name: John 18:23
verses.text: Jesus answered him, “If I have spoken evil, testify of the evil; but if well, why do you beat me?”

verses.name: John 18:24
verses.text: Annas sent him bound to Caiaphas, the high priest.

verses.name: John 18:25
verses.text: Now Simon Peter was standing and warming himself. They said therefore to him, “You aren’t also one of his disciples, are you?” He denied it, and said, “I am not.”

verses.name: John 18:26
verses.text: One of the servants of the high priest, being a relative of him whose ear Peter had cut off, said, “Didn’t I see you in the garden with him?”

verses.name: John 18:27
verses.text: Peter therefore denied it again, and immediately the rooster crowed.

verses.name: John 18:28
verses.text: They led Jesus therefore from Caiaphas into the Praetorium. It was early, and they themselves didn’t enter into the Praetorium, that they might not be defiled, but might eat the Passover.

verses.name: John 18:29
verses.text: Pilate therefore went out to them, and said, “What accusation do you bring against this man?”

verses.name: John 18:30
verses.text: They answered him, “If this man weren’t an evildoer, we wouldn’t have delivered him up to you.”

verses.name: John 18:31
verses.text: Pilate therefore said to them, “Take him yourselves, and judge him according to your law.” Therefore the Jews said to him, “It is not lawful for us to put anyone to death,”

verses.name: John 18:32
verses.text: that the word of Jesus might be fulfilled, which he spoke, signifying by what kind of death he should die.

verses.name: John 18:33
verses.text: Pilate therefore entered again into the Praetorium, called Jesus, and said to him, “Are you the King of the Jews?”

verses.name: John 18:34
verses.text: Jesus answered him, “Do you say this by yourself, or did others tell you about me?”

verses.name: John 18:35
verses.text: Pilate answered, “I’m not a Jew, am I? Your own nation and the chief priests delivered you to me. What have you done?”

verses.name: John 18:36
verses.text: Jesus answered, “My Kingdom is not of this world. If my Kingdom were of this world, then my servants would fight, that I wouldn’t be delivered to the Jews. But now my Kingdom is not from here.”

verses.name: John 18:37
verses.text: Pilate therefore said to him, “Are you a king then?” Jesus answered, “You say that I am a king. For this reason I have been born, and for this reason I have come into the world, that I should testify to the truth. Everyone who is of the truth listens to my voice.”

verses.name: John 18:38
verses.text: Pilate said to him, “What is truth?” When he had said this, he went out again to the Jews, and said to them, “I find no basis for a charge against him.

verses.name: John 18:39
verses.text: But you have a custom, that I should release someone to you at the Passover. Therefore, do you want me to release to you the King of the Jews?”

verses.name: John 18:40
verses.text: Then they all shouted again, saying, “Not this man, but Barabbas!” Now Barabbas was a robber.

verses.name: John 19:1
verses.text: So Pilate then took Jesus, and flogged him.

verses.name: John 19:2
verses.text: The soldiers twisted thorns into a crown, and put it on his head, and dressed him in a purple garment.

verses.name: John 19:3
verses.text: They kept saying, “Hail, King of the Jews!” and they kept slapping him.

verses.name: John 19:4
verses.text: Then Pilate went out again, and said to them, “Behold, I bring him out to you, that you may know that I find no basis for a charge against him.”

verses.name: John 19:5
verses.text: Jesus therefore came out, wearing the crown of thorns and the purple garment. Pilate said to them, “Behold, the man!”

verses.name: John 19:6
verses.text: When therefore the chief priests and the officers saw him, they shouted, saying, “Crucify! Crucify!” Pilate said to them, “Take him yourselves, and crucify him, for I find no basis for a charge against him.”

verses.name: John 19:7
verses.text: The Jews answered him, “We have a law, and by our law he ought to die, because he made himself the Son of God.”

verses.name: John 19:8
verses.text: When therefore Pilate heard this saying, he was more afraid.

verses.name: John 19:9
verses.text: He entered into the Praetorium again, and said to Jesus, “Where are you from?” But Jesus gave him no answer.

verses.name: John 19:10
verses.text: Pilate therefore said to him, “Aren’t you speaking to me? Don’t you know that I have power to release you, and have power to crucify you?”

verses.name: John 19:11
verses.text: Jesus answered, “You would have no power at all against me, unless it were given to you from above. Therefore he who delivered me to you has greater sin.”

verses.name: John 19:12
verses.text: At this, Pilate was seeking to release him, but the Jews cried out, saying, “If you release this man, you aren’t Caesar’s friend! Everyone who makes himself a king speaks against Caesar!”

verses.name: John 19:13
verses.text: When Pilate therefore heard these words, he brought Jesus out, and sat down on the judgment seat at a place called “The Pavement”, but in Hebrew, “Gabbatha.”

verses.name: John 19:14
verses.text: Now it was the Preparation Day of the Passover, at about the sixth hour. He said to the Jews, “Behold, your King!”

verses.name: John 19:15
verses.text: They cried out, “Away with him! Away with him! Crucify him!” Pilate said to them, “Shall I crucify your King?” The chief priests answered, “We have no king but Caesar!”

verses.name: John 19:16
verses.text: So then he delivered him to them to be crucified. So they took Jesus and led him away.

verses.name: John 19:17
verses.text: He went out, bearing his cross, to the place called “The Place of a Skull”, which is called in Hebrew, “Golgotha”,

verses.name: John 19:18
verses.text: where they crucified him, and with him two others, on either side one, and Jesus in the middle.

verses.name: John 19:19
verses.text: Pilate wrote a title also, and put it on the cross. There was written, “JESUS OF NAZARETH, THE KING OF THE JEWS.”

verses.name: John 19:20
verses.text: Therefore many of the Jews read this title, for the place where Jesus was crucified was near the city; and it was written in Hebrew, in Latin, and in Greek.

verses.name: John 19:21
verses.text: The chief priests of the Jews therefore said to Pilate, “Don’t write, ‘The King of the Jews,’ but, ‘he said, “I am King of the Jews.”’”

verses.name: John 19:22
verses.text: Pilate answered, “What I have written, I have written.”

verses.name: John 19:23
verses.text: Then the soldiers, when they had crucified Jesus, took his garments and made four parts, to every soldier a part; and also the coat. Now the coat was without seam, woven from the top throughout.

verses.name: John 19:24
verses.text: Then they said to one another, “Let’s not tear it, but cast lots for it to decide whose it will be,” that the Scripture might be fulfilled, which says, “They parted my garments among them. For my cloak they cast lots.” Therefore the soldiers did these things.

verses.name: John 19:25
verses.text: But there were standing by the cross of Jesus his mother, and his mother’s sister, Mary the wife of Clopas, and Mary Magdalene.

verses.name: John 19:26
verses.text: Therefore when Jesus saw his mother, and the disciple whom he loved standing there, he said to his mother, “Woman, behold, your son!”

verses.name: John 19:27
verses.text: Then he said to the disciple, “Behold, your mother!” From that hour, the disciple took her to his own home.

verses.name: John 19:28
verses.text: After this, Jesus, seeing that all things were now finished, that the Scripture might be fulfilled, said, “I am thirsty.”

verses.name: John 19:29
verses.text: Now a vessel full of vinegar was set there; so they put a sponge full of the vinegar on hyssop, and held it at his mouth.

verses.name: John 19:30
verses.text: When Jesus therefore had received the vinegar, he said, “It is finished.” He bowed his head, and gave up his spirit.

verses.name: John 19:31
verses.text: Therefore the Jews, because it was the Preparation Day, so that the bodies wouldn’t remain on the cross on the Sabbath (for that Sabbath was a special one), asked of Pilate that their legs might be broken, and that they might be taken away.

verses.name: John 19:32
verses.text: Therefore the soldiers came, and broke the legs of the first, and of the other who was crucified with him;

verses.name: John 19:33
verses.text: but when they came to Jesus, and saw that he was already dead, they didn’t break his legs.

verses.name: John 19:34
verses.text: However one of the soldiers pierced his side with a spear, and immediately blood and water came out.

verses.name: John 19:35
verses.text: He who has seen has testified, and his testimony is true. He knows that he tells the truth, that you may believe.

verses.name: John 19:36
verses.text: For these things happened, that the Scripture might be fulfilled, “A bone of him will not be broken.”

verses.name: John 19:37
verses.text: Again another Scripture says, “They will look on him whom they pierced.”

verses.name: John 19:38
verses.text: After these things, Joseph of Arimathaea, being a disciple of Jesus, but secretly for fear of the Jews, asked of Pilate that he might take away Jesus’ body. Pilate gave him permission. He came therefore and took away his body.

verses.name: John 19:39
verses.text: Nicodemus, who at first came to Jesus by night, also came bringing a mixture of myrrh and aloes, about a hundred Roman pounds.

verses.name: John 19:40
verses.text: So they took Jesus’ body, and bound it in linen cloths with the spices, as the custom of the Jews is to bury.

verses.name: John 19:41
verses.text: Now in the place where he was crucified there was a garden. In the garden was a new tomb in which no man had ever yet been laid.

verses.name: John 19:42
verses.text: Then because of the Jews’ Preparation Day (for the tomb was near at hand) they laid Jesus there.

verses.name: John 20:1
verses.text: Now on the first day of the week, Mary Magdalene went early, while it was still dark, to the tomb, and saw the stone taken away from the tomb.

verses.name: John 20:2
verses.text: Therefore she ran and came to Simon Peter, and to the other disciple whom Jesus loved, and said to them, “They have taken away the Lord out of the tomb, and we don’t know where they have laid him!”

verses.name: John 20:3
verses.text: Therefore Peter and the other disciple went out, and they went toward the tomb.

verses.name: John 20:4
verses.text: They both ran together. The other disciple outran Peter, and came to the tomb first.

verses.name: John 20:5
verses.text: Stooping and looking in, he saw the linen cloths lying, yet he didn’t enter in.

verses.name: John 20:6
verses.text: Then Simon Peter came, following him, and entered into the tomb. He saw the linen cloths lying,

verses.name: John 20:7
verses.text: and the cloth that had been on his head, not lying with the linen cloths, but rolled up in a place by itself.

verses.name: John 20:8
verses.text: So then the other disciple who came first to the tomb also entered in, and he saw and believed.

verses.name: John 20:9
verses.text: For as yet they didn’t know the Scripture, that he must rise from the dead.

verses.name: John 20:10
verses.text: So the disciples went away again to their own homes.

verses.name: John 20:11
verses.text: But Mary was standing outside at the tomb weeping. So, as she wept, she stooped and looked into the tomb,

verses.name: John 20:12
verses.text: and she saw two angels in white sitting, one at the head, and one at the feet, where the body of Jesus had lain.

verses.name: John 20:13
verses.text: They told her, “Woman, why are you weeping?” She said to them, “Because they have taken away my Lord, and I don’t know where they have laid him.”

verses.name: John 20:14
verses.text: When she had said this, she turned around and saw Jesus standing, and didn’t know that it was Jesus.

verses.name: John 20:15
verses.text: Jesus said to her, “Woman, why are you weeping? Who are you looking for?” She, supposing him to be the gardener, said to him, “Sir, if you have carried him away, tell me where you have laid him, and I will take him away.”

verses.name: John 20:16
verses.text: Jesus said to her, “Mary.” She turned and said to him, “Rabboni!” which is to say, “Teacher!”

verses.name: John 20:17
verses.text: Jesus said to her, “Don’t hold me, for I haven’t yet ascended to my Father; but go to my brothers, and tell them, ‘I am ascending to my Father and your Father, to my God and your God.’”

verses.name: John 20:18
verses.text: Mary Magdalene came and told the disciples that she had seen the Lord, and that he had said these things to her.

verses.name: John 20:19
