verses.text: When therefore it was evening, on that day, the first day of the week, and when the doors were locked where the disciples were assembled, for fear of the Jews, Jesus came and stood in the middle, and said to them, “Peace be to you.”

verses.name: John 20:20
verses.text: When he had said this, he showed them his hands and his side. The disciples therefore were glad when they saw the Lord.

verses.name: John 20:21
verses.text: Jesus therefore said to them again, “Peace be to you. As the Father has sent me, even so I send you.”

verses.name: John 20:22
verses.text: When he had said this, he breathed on them, and said to them, “Receive the Holy Spirit!

verses.name: John 20:23
verses.text: If you forgive anyone’s sins, they have been forgiven them. If you retain anyone’s sins, they have been retained.”

verses.name: John 20:24
verses.text: But Thomas, one of the twelve, called Didymus, wasn’t with them when Jesus came.

verses.name: John 20:25
verses.text: The other disciples therefore said to him, “We have seen the Lord!” But he said to them, “Unless I see in his hands the print of the nails, put my finger into the print of the nails, and put my hand into his side, I will not believe.”

verses.name: John 20:26
verses.text: After eight days again his disciples were inside, and Thomas was with them. Jesus came, the doors being locked, and stood in the middle, and said, “Peace be to you.”

verses.name: John 20:27
verses.text: Then he said to Thomas, “Reach here your finger, and see my hands. Reach here your hand, and put it into my side. Don’t be unbelieving, but believing.”

verses.name: John 20:28
verses.text: Thomas answered him, “My Lord and my God!”

verses.name: John 20:29
verses.text: Jesus said to him, “Because you have seen me, you have believed. Blessed are those who have not seen, and have believed.”

verses.name: John 20:30
verses.text: Therefore Jesus did many other signs in the presence of his disciples, which are not written in this book;

verses.name: John 20:31
verses.text: but these are written, that you may believe that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God, and that believing you may have life in his name.

verses.name: John 21:1
verses.text: After these things, Jesus revealed himself again to the disciples at the sea of Tiberias. He revealed himself this way.

verses.name: John 21:2
verses.text: Simon Peter, Thomas called Didymus, Nathanael of Cana in Galilee, and the sons of Zebedee, and two others of his disciples were together.

verses.name: John 21:3
verses.text: Simon Peter said to them, “I’m going fishing.” They told him, “We are also coming with you.” They immediately went out, and entered into the boat. That night, they caught nothing.

verses.name: John 21:4
verses.text: But when day had already come, Jesus stood on the beach, yet the disciples didn’t know that it was Jesus.

verses.name: John 21:5
verses.text: Jesus therefore said to them, “Children, have you anything to eat?” They answered him, “No.”

verses.name: John 21:6
verses.text: He said to them, “Cast the net on the right side of the boat, and you will find some.” They cast it therefore, and now they weren’t able to draw it in for the multitude of fish.

verses.name: John 21:7
verses.text: That disciple therefore whom Jesus loved said to Peter, “It’s the Lord!” So when Simon Peter heard that it was the Lord, he wrapped his coat around him (for he was naked), and threw himself into the sea.

verses.name: John 21:8
verses.text: But the other disciples came in the little boat (for they were not far from the land, but about two hundred cubits away), dragging the net full of fish.

verses.name: John 21:9
verses.text: So when they got out on the land, they saw a fire of coals there, and fish laid on it, and bread.

verses.name: John 21:10
verses.text: Jesus said to them, “Bring some of the fish which you have just caught.”

verses.name: John 21:11
verses.text: Simon Peter went up, and drew the net to land, full of great fish, one hundred fifty-three; and even though there were so many, the net wasn’t torn.

verses.name: John 21:12
verses.text: Jesus said to them, “Come and eat breakfast.” None of the disciples dared inquire of him, “Who are you?” knowing that it was the Lord.

verses.name: John 21:13
verses.text: Then Jesus came and took the bread, gave it to them, and the fish likewise.

verses.name: John 21:14
verses.text: This is now the third time that Jesus was revealed to his disciples, after he had risen from the dead.

verses.name: John 21:15
verses.text: So when they had eaten their breakfast, Jesus said to Simon Peter, “Simon, son of Jonah, do you love me more than these?” He said to him, “Yes, Lord; you know that I have affection for you.” He said to him, “Feed my lambs.”

verses.name: John 21:16
verses.text: He said to him again a second time, “Simon, son of Jonah, do you love me?” He said to him, “Yes, Lord; you know that I have affection for you.” He said to him, “Tend my sheep.”

verses.name: John 21:17
verses.text: He said to him the third time, “Simon, son of Jonah, do you have affection for me?” Peter was grieved because he asked him the third time, “Do you have affection for me?” He said to him, “Lord, you know everything. You know that I have affection for you.” Jesus said to him, “Feed my sheep.

verses.name: John 21:18
verses.text: Most certainly I tell you, when you were young, you dressed yourself, and walked where you wanted to. But when you are old, you will stretch out your hands, and another will dress you, and carry you where you don’t want to go.”

verses.name: John 21:19
verses.text: Now he said this, signifying by what kind of death he would glorify God. When he had said this, he said to him, “Follow me.”

verses.name: John 21:20
verses.text: Then Peter, turning around, saw a disciple following. This was the disciple whom Jesus loved, the one who had also leaned on Jesus’ breast at the supper and asked, “Lord, who is going to betray You?”

verses.name: John 21:21
verses.text: Peter seeing him, said to Jesus, “Lord, what about this man?”

verses.name: John 21:22
verses.text: Jesus said to him, “If I desire that he stay until I come, what is that to you? You follow me.”

verses.name: John 21:23
verses.text: This saying therefore went out among the brothers, that this disciple wouldn’t die. Yet Jesus didn’t say to him that he wouldn’t die, but, “If I desire that he stay until I come, what is that to you?”

verses.name: John 21:24
verses.text: This is the disciple who testifies about these things, and wrote these things. We know that his witness is true.

verses.name: John 21:25
verses.text: There are also many other things which Jesus did, which if they would all be written, I suppose that even the world itself wouldn’t have room for the books that would be written.

verses.name: Acts 1:1
verses.text: The first book I wrote, Theophilus, concerned all that Jesus began both to do and to teach,

verses.name: Acts 1:2
verses.text: until the day in which he was received up, after he had given commandment through the Holy Spirit to the apostles whom he had chosen.

verses.name: Acts 1:3
verses.text: To these he also showed himself alive after he suffered, by many proofs, appearing to them over a period of forty days, and speaking about God’s Kingdom.

verses.name: Acts 1:4
verses.text: Being assembled together with them, he commanded them, “Don’t depart from Jerusalem, but wait for the promise of the Father, which you heard from me.

verses.name: Acts 1:5
verses.text: For John indeed baptized in water, but you will be baptized in the Holy Spirit not many days from now.”

verses.name: Acts 1:6
verses.text: Therefore when they had come together, they asked him, “Lord, are you now restoring the kingdom to Israel?”

verses.name: Acts 1:7
verses.text: He said to them, “It isn’t for you to know times or seasons which the Father has set within his own authority.

verses.name: Acts 1:8
verses.text: But you will receive power when the Holy Spirit has come upon you. You will be witnesses to me in Jerusalem, in all Judea and Samaria, and to the uttermost parts of the earth.”

verses.name: Acts 1:9
verses.text: When he had said these things, as they were looking, he was taken up, and a cloud received him out of their sight.

verses.name: Acts 1:10
verses.text: While they were looking steadfastly into the sky as he went, behold, two men stood by them in white clothing,

verses.name: Acts 1:11
verses.text: who also said, “You men of Galilee, why do you stand looking into the sky? This Jesus, who was received up from you into the sky, will come back in the same way as you saw him going into the sky.”

verses.name: Acts 1:12
verses.text: Then they returned to Jerusalem from the mountain called Olivet, which is near Jerusalem, a Sabbath day’s journey away.

verses.name: Acts 1:13
verses.text: When they had come in, they went up into the upper room, where they were staying; that is Peter, John, James, Andrew, Philip, Thomas, Bartholomew, Matthew, James the son of Alphaeus, Simon the Zealot, and Judas the son of James.

verses.name: Acts 1:14
verses.text: All these with one accord continued steadfastly in prayer and supplication, along with the women, and Mary the mother of Jesus, and with his brothers.

verses.name: Acts 1:15
verses.text: In these days, Peter stood up in the middle of the disciples (and the number of names was about one hundred twenty), and said,

verses.name: Acts 1:16
verses.text: “Brothers, it was necessary that this Scripture should be fulfilled, which the Holy Spirit spoke before by the mouth of David concerning Judas, who was guide to those who took Jesus.

verses.name: Acts 1:17
verses.text: For he was counted with us, and received his portion in this ministry.

verses.name: Acts 1:18
verses.text: Now this man obtained a field with the reward for his wickedness, and falling headlong, his body burst open, and all his intestines gushed out.

verses.name: Acts 1:19
verses.text: It became known to everyone who lived in Jerusalem that in their language that field was called ‘Akeldama,’ that is, ‘The field of blood.’

verses.name: Acts 1:20
verses.text: For it is written in the book of Psalms, ‘Let his habitation be made desolate. Let no one dwell therein;’ and, ‘Let another take his office.’

verses.name: Acts 1:21
verses.text: “Of the men therefore who have accompanied us all the time that the Lord Jesus went in and out among us,

verses.name: Acts 1:22
verses.text: beginning from the baptism of John, to the day that he was received up from us, of these one must become a witness with us of his resurrection.”

verses.name: Acts 1:23
verses.text: They put forward two, Joseph called Barsabbas, who was also called Justus, and Matthias.

verses.name: Acts 1:24
verses.text: They prayed, and said, “You, Lord, who know the hearts of all men, show which one of these two you have chosen

verses.name: Acts 1:25
verses.text: to take part in this ministry and apostleship from which Judas fell away, that he might go to his own place.”

verses.name: Acts 1:26
verses.text: They drew lots for them, and the lot fell on Matthias, and he was counted with the eleven apostles.

verses.name: Acts 2:1
verses.text: Now when the day of Pentecost had come, they were all with one accord in one place.

verses.name: Acts 2:2
verses.text: Suddenly there came from the sky a sound like the rushing of a mighty wind, and it filled all the house where they were sitting.

verses.name: Acts 2:3
verses.text: Tongues like fire appeared and were distributed to them, and one sat on each of them.

verses.name: Acts 2:4
verses.text: They were all filled with the Holy Spirit, and began to speak with other languages, as the Spirit gave them the ability to speak.

verses.name: Acts 2:5
verses.text: Now there were dwelling in Jerusalem Jews, devout men, from every nation under the sky.

verses.name: Acts 2:6
verses.text: When this sound was heard, the multitude came together, and were bewildered, because everyone heard them speaking in his own language.

verses.name: Acts 2:7
verses.text: They were all amazed and marveled, saying to one another, “Behold, aren’t all these who speak Galileans?

verses.name: Acts 2:8
verses.text: How do we hear, everyone in our own native language?

verses.name: Acts 2:9
verses.text: Parthians, Medes, Elamites, and people from Mesopotamia, Judea, Cappadocia, Pontus, Asia,

verses.name: Acts 2:10
verses.text: Phrygia, Pamphylia, Egypt, the parts of Libya around Cyrene, visitors from Rome, both Jews and proselytes,

verses.name: Acts 2:11
verses.text: Cretans and Arabians: we hear them speaking in our languages the mighty works of God!”

verses.name: Acts 2:12
verses.text: They were all amazed, and were perplexed, saying to one another, “What does this mean?”

verses.name: Acts 2:13
verses.text: Others, mocking, said, “They are filled with new wine.”

verses.name: Acts 2:14
verses.text: But Peter, standing up with the eleven, lifted up his voice, and spoke out to them, “You men of Judea, and all you who dwell at Jerusalem, let this be known to you, and listen to my words.

verses.name: Acts 2:15
verses.text: For these aren’t drunken, as you suppose, seeing it is only the third hour of the day.

verses.name: Acts 2:16
verses.text: But this is what has been spoken through the prophet Joel:

verses.name: Acts 2:17
verses.text: ‘It will be in the last days, says God, that I will pour out my Spirit on all flesh. Your sons and your daughters will prophesy. Your young men will see visions. Your old men will dream dreams.

verses.name: Acts 2:18
verses.text: Yes, and on my servants and on my handmaidens in those days, I will pour out my Spirit, and they will prophesy.

verses.name: Acts 2:19
verses.text: I will show wonders in the sky above, and signs on the earth beneath; blood, and fire, and billows of smoke.

verses.name: Acts 2:20
verses.text: The sun will be turned into darkness, and the moon into blood, before the great and glorious day of the Lord comes.

verses.name: Acts 2:21
verses.text: It will be that whoever will call on the name of the Lord will be saved.’

verses.name: Acts 2:22
verses.text: “Men of Israel, hear these words! Jesus of Nazareth, a man approved by God to you by mighty works and wonders and signs which God did by him among you, even as you yourselves know,

verses.name: Acts 2:23
verses.text: him, being delivered up by the determined counsel and foreknowledge of God, you have taken by the hand of lawless men, crucified and killed;

verses.name: Acts 2:24
verses.text: whom God raised up, having freed him from the agony of death, because it was not possible that he should be held by it.

verses.name: Acts 2:25
verses.text: For David says concerning him, ‘I saw the Lord always before my face, For he is on my right hand, that I should not be moved.

verses.name: Acts 2:26
verses.text: Therefore my heart was glad, and my tongue rejoiced. Moreover my flesh also will dwell in hope;

verses.name: Acts 2:27
verses.text: because you will not leave my soul in Hades, neither will you allow your Holy One to see decay.

verses.name: Acts 2:28
verses.text: You made known to me the ways of life. You will make me full of gladness with your presence.’

verses.name: Acts 2:29
verses.text: “Brothers, I may tell you freely of the patriarch David, that he both died and was buried, and his tomb is with us to this day.

verses.name: Acts 2:30
verses.text: Therefore, being a prophet, and knowing that God had sworn with an oath to him that of the fruit of his body, according to the flesh, he would raise up the Christ to sit on his throne,

verses.name: Acts 2:31
verses.text: he foreseeing this spoke about the resurrection of the Christ, that his soul wasn’t left in Hades, and his flesh didn’t see decay.

verses.name: Acts 2:32
verses.text: This Jesus God raised up, to which we all are witnesses.

verses.name: Acts 2:33
verses.text: Being therefore exalted by the right hand of God, and having received from the Father the promise of the Holy Spirit, he has poured out this, which you now see and hear.

verses.name: Acts 2:34
verses.text: For David didn’t ascend into the heavens, but he says himself, ‘The Lord said to my Lord, “Sit by my right hand,

verses.name: Acts 2:35
verses.text: until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet.”’

verses.name: Acts 2:36
verses.text: “Let all the house of Israel therefore know certainly that God has made him both Lord and Christ, this Jesus whom you crucified.”

verses.name: Acts 2:37
verses.text: Now when they heard this, they were cut to the heart, and said to Peter and the rest of the apostles, “Brothers, what shall we do?”

verses.name: Acts 2:38
verses.text: Peter said to them, “Repent, and be baptized, every one of you, in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of sins, and you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.

verses.name: Acts 2:39
verses.text: For the promise is to you, and to your children, and to all who are far off, even as many as the Lord our God will call to himself.”

verses.name: Acts 2:40
verses.text: With many other words he testified, and exhorted them, saying, “Save yourselves from this crooked generation!”

verses.name: Acts 2:41
verses.text: Then those who gladly received his word were baptized. There were added that day about three thousand souls.

verses.name: Acts 2:42
verses.text: They continued steadfastly in the apostles’ teaching and fellowship, in the breaking of bread, and prayer.

verses.name: Acts 2:43
verses.text: Fear came on every soul, and many wonders and signs were done through the apostles.

verses.name: Acts 2:44
verses.text: All who believed were together, and had all things in common.

verses.name: Acts 2:45
verses.text: They sold their possessions and goods, and distributed them to all, according as anyone had need.

verses.name: Acts 2:46
verses.text: Day by day, continuing steadfastly with one accord in the temple, and breaking bread at home, they took their food with gladness and singleness of heart,

verses.name: Acts 2:47
verses.text: praising God, and having favor with all the people. The Lord added to the assembly day by day those who were being saved.

verses.name: Acts 3:1
verses.text: Peter and John were going up into the temple at the hour of prayer, the ninth hour.

verses.name: Acts 3:2
verses.text: A certain man who was lame from his mother’s womb was being carried, whom they laid daily at the door of the temple which is called Beautiful, to ask gifts for the needy of those who entered into the temple.

verses.name: Acts 3:3
verses.text: Seeing Peter and John about to go into the temple, he asked to receive gifts for the needy.

verses.name: Acts 3:4
verses.text: Peter, fastening his eyes on him, with John, said, “Look at us.”

verses.name: Acts 3:5
verses.text: He listened to them, expecting to receive something from them.

verses.name: Acts 3:6
verses.text: But Peter said, “I have no silver or gold, but what I have, that I give you. In the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, get up and walk!”

verses.name: Acts 3:7
verses.text: He took him by the right hand, and raised him up. Immediately his feet and his ankle bones received strength.

verses.name: Acts 3:8
verses.text: Leaping up, he stood, and began to walk. He entered with them into the temple, walking, leaping, and praising God.

verses.name: Acts 3:9
verses.text: All the people saw him walking and praising God.

verses.name: Acts 3:10
verses.text: They recognized him, that it was he who used to sit begging for gifts for the needy at the Beautiful Gate of the temple. They were filled with wonder and amazement at what had happened to him.

verses.name: Acts 3:11
verses.text: As the lame man who was healed held on to Peter and John, all the people ran together to them in the porch that is called Solomon’s, greatly wondering.

verses.name: Acts 3:12
verses.text: When Peter saw it, he responded to the people, “You men of Israel, why do you marvel at this man? Why do you fasten your eyes on us, as though by our own power or godliness we had made him walk?

verses.name: Acts 3:13
verses.text: The God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, the God of our fathers, has glorified his Servant Jesus, whom you delivered up, and denied in the presence of Pilate, when he had determined to release him.

verses.name: Acts 3:14
verses.text: But you denied the Holy and Righteous One, and asked for a murderer to be granted to you,

verses.name: Acts 3:15
verses.text: and killed the Prince of life, whom God raised from the dead, to which we are witnesses.

verses.name: Acts 3:16
verses.text: By faith in his name, his name has made this man strong, whom you see and know. Yes, the faith which is through him has given him this perfect soundness in the presence of you all.

verses.name: Acts 3:17
verses.text: “Now, brothers, I know that you did this in ignorance, as did also your rulers.

verses.name: Acts 3:18
verses.text: But the things which God announced by the mouth of all his prophets, that Christ should suffer, he thus fulfilled.

verses.name: Acts 3:19
verses.text: “Repent therefore, and turn again, that your sins may be blotted out, so that there may come times of refreshing from the presence of the Lord,

verses.name: Acts 3:20
verses.text: and that he may send Christ Jesus, who was ordained for you before,

verses.name: Acts 3:21
verses.text: whom heaven must receive until the times of restoration of all things, which God spoke long ago by the mouth of his holy prophets.

verses.name: Acts 3:22
verses.text: For Moses indeed said to the fathers, ‘The Lord God will raise up a prophet for you from among your brothers, like me. You shall listen to him in all things whatever he says to you.

verses.name: Acts 3:23
verses.text: It will be that every soul that will not listen to that prophet will be utterly destroyed from among the people.’

verses.name: Acts 3:24
verses.text: Yes, and all the prophets from Samuel and those who followed after, as many as have spoken, they also told of these days.

verses.name: Acts 3:25
verses.text: You are the children of the prophets, and of the covenant which God made with our fathers, saying to Abraham, ‘In your offspring will all the families of the earth be blessed.’

verses.name: Acts 3:26
verses.text: God, having raised up his servant Jesus, sent him to you first to bless you, in turning away every one of you from your wickedness.”

verses.name: Acts 4:1
verses.text: As they spoke to the people, the priests and the captain of the temple and the Sadducees came to them,

verses.name: Acts 4:2
verses.text: being upset because they taught the people and proclaimed in Jesus the resurrection from the dead.

verses.name: Acts 4:3
verses.text: They laid hands on them, and put them in custody until the next day, for it was now evening.

verses.name: Acts 4:4
verses.text: But many of those who heard the word believed, and the number of the men came to be about five thousand.

verses.name: Acts 4:5
verses.text: In the morning, their rulers, elders, and scribes were gathered together in Jerusalem.

verses.name: Acts 4:6
verses.text: Annas the high priest was there, with Caiaphas, John, Alexander, and as many as were relatives of the high priest.

verses.name: Acts 4:7
verses.text: When they had stood Peter and John in the middle of them, they inquired, “By what power, or in what name, have you done this?”

verses.name: Acts 4:8
verses.text: Then Peter, filled with the Holy Spirit, said to them, “You rulers of the people, and elders of Israel,

verses.name: Acts 4:9
verses.text: if we are examined today concerning a good deed done to a crippled man, by what means this man has been healed,

verses.name: Acts 4:10
verses.text: be it known to you all, and to all the people of Israel, that in the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, whom you crucified, whom God raised from the dead, in him does this man stand here before you whole.

verses.name: Acts 4:11
verses.text: He is ‘the stone which was regarded as worthless by you, the builders, which has become the head of the corner.’

verses.name: Acts 4:12
verses.text: There is salvation in none other, for neither is there any other name under heaven, that is given among men, by which we must be saved!”

verses.name: Acts 4:13
verses.text: Now when they saw the boldness of Peter and John, and had perceived that they were unlearned and ignorant men, they marveled. They recognized that they had been with Jesus.

verses.name: Acts 4:14
verses.text: Seeing the man who was healed standing with them, they could say nothing against it.

verses.name: Acts 4:15
verses.text: But when they had commanded them to go aside out of the council, they conferred among themselves,

verses.name: Acts 4:16
verses.text: saying, “What shall we do to these men? Because indeed a notable miracle has been done through them, as can be plainly seen by all who dwell in Jerusalem, and we can’t deny it.

verses.name: Acts 4:17
verses.text: But so that this spreads no further among the people, let’s threaten them, that from now on they don’t speak to anyone in this name.”

verses.name: Acts 4:18
verses.text: They called them, and commanded them not to speak at all nor teach in the name of Jesus.

verses.name: Acts 4:19
verses.text: But Peter and John answered them, “Whether it is right in the sight of God to listen to you rather than to God, judge for yourselves,

verses.name: Acts 4:20
verses.text: for we can’t help telling the things which we saw and heard.”

verses.name: Acts 4:21
verses.text: When they had further threatened them, they let them go, finding no way to punish them, because of the people; for everyone glorified God for that which was done.

verses.name: Acts 4:22
verses.text: For the man on whom this miracle of healing was performed was more than forty years old.

verses.name: Acts 4:23
verses.text: Being let go, they came to their own company, and reported all that the chief priests and the elders had said to them.

verses.name: Acts 4:24
verses.text: When they heard it, they lifted up their voice to God with one accord, and said, “O Lord, you are God, who made the heaven, the earth, the sea, and all that is in them;

verses.name: Acts 4:25
verses.text: who by the mouth of your servant, David, said, ‘Why do the nations rage, and the peoples plot a vain thing?

verses.name: Acts 4:26
verses.text: The kings of the earth take a stand, and the rulers take council together, against the Lord, and against his Christ.’

verses.name: Acts 4:27
verses.text: “For truly, in this city against your holy servant, Jesus, whom you anointed, both Herod and Pontius Pilate, with the Gentiles and the people of Israel, were gathered together

verses.name: Acts 4:28
verses.text: to do whatever your hand and your council foreordained to happen.

verses.name: Acts 4:29
verses.text: Now, Lord, look at their threats, and grant to your servants to speak your word with all boldness,

verses.name: Acts 4:30
verses.text: while you stretch out your hand to heal; and that signs and wonders may be done through the name of your holy Servant Jesus.”

verses.name: Acts 4:31
verses.text: When they had prayed, the place was shaken where they were gathered together. They were all filled with the Holy Spirit, and they spoke the word of God with boldness.

verses.name: Acts 4:32
verses.text: The multitude of those who believed were of one heart and soul. Not one of them claimed that anything of the things which he possessed was his own, but they had all things in common.

verses.name: Acts 4:33
verses.text: With great power, the apostles gave their testimony of the resurrection of the Lord Jesus. Great grace was on them all.

verses.name: Acts 4:34
verses.text: For neither was there among them any who lacked, for as many as were owners of lands or houses sold them, and brought the proceeds of the things that were sold,

verses.name: Acts 4:35
verses.text: and laid them at the apostles’ feet, and distribution was made to each, according as anyone had need.

verses.name: Acts 4:36
verses.text: Joses, who by the apostles was also called Barnabas (which is, being interpreted, Son of Encouragement), a Levite, a man of Cyprus by race,

verses.name: Acts 4:37
verses.text: having a field, sold it, and brought the money and laid it at the apostles’ feet.

verses.name: Acts 5:1
verses.text: But a certain man named Ananias, with Sapphira, his wife, sold a possession,

verses.name: Acts 5:2
verses.text: and kept back part of the price, his wife also being aware of it, and brought a certain part, and laid it at the apostles’ feet.

verses.name: Acts 5:3
verses.text: But Peter said, “Ananias, why has Satan filled your heart to lie to the Holy Spirit, and to keep back part of the price of the land?

verses.name: Acts 5:4
verses.text: While you kept it, didn’t it remain your own? After it was sold, wasn’t it in your power? How is it that you have conceived this thing in your heart? You haven’t lied to men, but to God.”

verses.name: Acts 5:5
verses.text: Ananias, hearing these words, fell down and died. Great fear came on all who heard these things.

verses.name: Acts 5:6
verses.text: The young men arose and wrapped him up, and they carried him out and buried him.

verses.name: Acts 5:7
verses.text: About three hours later, his wife, not knowing what had happened, came in.

verses.name: Acts 5:8
verses.text: Peter answered her, “Tell me whether you sold the land for so much.” She said, “Yes, for so much.”

verses.name: Acts 5:9
verses.text: But Peter asked her, “How is it that you have agreed together to tempt the Spirit of the Lord? Behold, the feet of those who have buried your husband are at the door, and they will carry you out.”

verses.name: Acts 5:10
verses.text: She fell down immediately at his feet, and died. The young men came in and found her dead, and they carried her out and buried her by her husband.

verses.name: Acts 5:11
verses.text: Great fear came on the whole assembly, and on all who heard these things.

verses.name: Acts 5:12
verses.text: By the hands of the apostles many signs and wonders were done among the people. They were all with one accord in Solomon’s porch.

verses.name: Acts 5:13
verses.text: None of the rest dared to join them, however the people honored them.

verses.name: Acts 5:14
verses.text: More believers were added to the Lord, multitudes of both men and women.

verses.name: Acts 5:15
verses.text: They even carried out the sick into the streets, and laid them on cots and mattresses, so that as Peter came by, at the least his shadow might overshadow some of them.

verses.name: Acts 5:16
verses.text: The multitude also came together from the cities around Jerusalem, bringing sick people, and those who were tormented by unclean spirits: and they were all healed.

verses.name: Acts 5:17
verses.text: But the high priest rose up, and all those who were with him (which is the sect of the Sadducees), and they were filled with jealousy,

verses.name: Acts 5:18
verses.text: and laid hands on the apostles, and put them in public custody.

verses.name: Acts 5:19
verses.text: But an angel of the Lord opened the prison doors by night, and brought them out, and said,

verses.name: Acts 5:20
verses.text: “Go stand and speak in the temple to the people all the words of this life.”

verses.name: Acts 5:21
verses.text: When they heard this, they entered into the temple about daybreak, and taught. But the high priest came, and those who were with him, and called the council together, and all the senate of the children of Israel, and sent to the prison to have them brought.

verses.name: Acts 5:22
verses.text: But the officers who came didn’t find them in the prison. They returned and reported,

verses.name: Acts 5:23
verses.text: “We found the prison shut and locked, and the guards standing before the doors, but when we opened them, we found no one inside!”

verses.name: Acts 5:24
verses.text: Now when the high priest, the captain of the temple, and the chief priests heard these words, they were very perplexed about them and what might become of this.

verses.name: Acts 5:25
verses.text: One came and told them, “Behold, the men whom you put in prison are in the temple, standing and teaching the people.”

verses.name: Acts 5:26
verses.text: Then the captain went with the officers, and brought them without violence, for they were afraid that the people might stone them.

verses.name: Acts 5:27
verses.text: When they had brought them, they set them before the council. The high priest questioned them,

verses.name: Acts 5:28
verses.text: saying, “Didn’t we strictly command you not to teach in this name? Behold, you have filled Jerusalem with your teaching, and intend to bring this man’s blood on us.”

verses.name: Acts 5:29
verses.text: But Peter and the apostles answered, “We must obey God rather than men.

verses.name: Acts 5:30
verses.text: The God of our fathers raised up Jesus, whom you killed, hanging him on a tree.

verses.name: Acts 5:31
verses.text: God exalted him with his right hand to be a Prince and a Savior, to give repentance to Israel, and remission of sins.

verses.name: Acts 5:32
verses.text: We are His witnesses of these things; and so also is the Holy Spirit, whom God has given to those who obey him.”

verses.name: Acts 5:33
verses.text: But they, when they heard this, were cut to the heart, and were determined to kill them.

verses.name: Acts 5:34
verses.text: But one stood up in the council, a Pharisee named Gamaliel, a teacher of the law, honored by all the people, and commanded to put the apostles out for a little while.

verses.name: Acts 5:35
verses.text: He said to them, “You men of Israel, be careful concerning these men, what you are about to do.

verses.name: Acts 5:36
verses.text: For before these days Theudas rose up, making himself out to be somebody; to whom a number of men, about four hundred, joined themselves: who was slain; and all, as many as obeyed him, were dispersed, and came to nothing.

verses.name: Acts 5:37
verses.text: After this man, Judas of Galilee rose up in the days of the enrollment, and drew away some people after him. He also perished, and all, as many as obeyed him, were scattered abroad.

verses.name: Acts 5:38
verses.text: Now I tell you, withdraw from these men, and leave them alone. For if this counsel or this work is of men, it will be overthrown.

verses.name: Acts 5:39
verses.text: But if it is of God, you will not be able to overthrow it, and you would be found even to be fighting against God!”

verses.name: Acts 5:40
verses.text: They agreed with him. Summoning the apostles, they beat them and commanded them not to speak in the name of Jesus, and let them go.

verses.name: Acts 5:41
verses.text: They therefore departed from the presence of the council, rejoicing that they were counted worthy to suffer dishonor for Jesus’ name.

verses.name: Acts 5:42
verses.text: Every day, in the temple and at home, they never stopped teaching and preaching Jesus, the Christ.

verses.name: Acts 6:1
verses.text: Now in those days, when the number of the disciples was multiplying, a complaint arose from the Hellenists against the Hebrews, because their widows were neglected in the daily service.

verses.name: Acts 6:2
verses.text: The twelve summoned the multitude of the disciples and said, “It is not appropriate for us to forsake the word of God and serve tables.

verses.name: Acts 6:3
verses.text: Therefore select from among you, brothers, seven men of good report, full of the Holy Spirit and of wisdom, whom we may appoint over this business.

verses.name: Acts 6:4
verses.text: But we will continue steadfastly in prayer and in the ministry of the word.”

verses.name: Acts 6:5
verses.text: These words pleased the whole multitude. They chose Stephen, a man full of faith and of the Holy Spirit, Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and Nicolaus, a proselyte of Antioch;

verses.name: Acts 6:6
verses.text: whom they set before the apostles. When they had prayed, they laid their hands on them.

verses.name: Acts 6:7
verses.text: The word of God increased and the number of the disciples multiplied in Jerusalem exceedingly. A great company of the priests were obedient to the faith.

verses.name: Acts 6:8
verses.text: Stephen, full of faith and power, performed great wonders and signs among the people.

verses.name: Acts 6:9
verses.text: But some of those who were of the synagogue called “The Libertines”, and of the Cyrenians, of the Alexandrians, and of those of Cilicia and Asia arose, disputing with Stephen.

verses.name: Acts 6:10
verses.text: They weren’t able to withstand the wisdom and the Spirit by which he spoke.

verses.name: Acts 6:11
verses.text: Then they secretly induced men to say, “We have heard him speak blasphemous words against Moses and God.”

verses.name: Acts 6:12
verses.text: They stirred up the people, the elders, and the scribes, and came against him and seized him, and brought him in to the council,

verses.name: Acts 6:13
verses.text: and set up false witnesses who said, “This man never stops speaking blasphemous words against this holy place and the law.

verses.name: Acts 6:14
verses.text: For we have heard him say that this Jesus of Nazareth will destroy this place, and will change the customs which Moses delivered to us.”

verses.name: Acts 6:15
verses.text: All who sat in the council, fastening their eyes on him, saw his face like it was the face of an angel.

verses.name: Acts 7:1
verses.text: The high priest said, “Are these things so?”

verses.name: Acts 7:2
verses.text: He said, “Brothers and fathers, listen. The God of glory appeared to our father Abraham, when he was in Mesopotamia, before he lived in Haran,

verses.name: Acts 7:3
verses.text: and said to him, ‘Get out of your land, and from your relatives, and come into a land which I will show you.’

verses.name: Acts 7:4
verses.text: Then he came out of the land of the Chaldaeans, and lived in Haran. From there, when his father was dead, God moved him into this land, where you are now living.

verses.name: Acts 7:5
verses.text: He gave him no inheritance in it, no, not so much as to set his foot on. He promised that he would give it to him for a possession, and to his offspring after him, when he still had no child.

verses.name: Acts 7:6
verses.text: God spoke in this way: that his offspring would live as aliens in a strange land, and that they would be enslaved and mistreated for four hundred years.

verses.name: Acts 7:7
verses.text: ‘I will judge the nation to which they will be in bondage,’ said God, ‘and after that will they come out, and serve me in this place.’

verses.name: Acts 7:8
verses.text: He gave him the covenant of circumcision. So Abraham became the father of Isaac, and circumcised him the eighth day. Isaac became the father of Jacob, and Jacob became the father of the twelve patriarchs.

verses.name: Acts 7:9
verses.text: “The patriarchs, moved with jealousy against Joseph, sold him into Egypt. God was with him,

verses.name: Acts 7:10
verses.text: and delivered him out of all his afflictions, and gave him favor and wisdom before Pharaoh, king of Egypt. He made him governor over Egypt and all his house.

verses.name: Acts 7:11
verses.text: Now a famine came over all the land of Egypt and Canaan, and great affliction. Our fathers found no food.

verses.name: Acts 7:12
verses.text: But when Jacob heard that there was grain in Egypt, he sent out our fathers the first time.

verses.name: Acts 7:13
verses.text: On the second time Joseph was made known to his brothers, and Joseph’s race was revealed to Pharaoh.

verses.name: Acts 7:14
verses.text: Joseph sent, and summoned Jacob, his father, and all his relatives, seventy-five souls.

verses.name: Acts 7:15
verses.text: Jacob went down into Egypt, and he died, himself and our fathers,

verses.name: Acts 7:16
verses.text: and they were brought back to Shechem, and laid in the tomb that Abraham bought for a price in silver from the children of Hamor of Shechem.

verses.name: Acts 7:17
verses.text: “But as the time of the promise came close which God had sworn to Abraham, the people grew and multiplied in Egypt,

verses.name: Acts 7:18
verses.text: until there arose a different king, who didn’t know Joseph.

verses.name: Acts 7:19
verses.text: The same took advantage of our race, and mistreated our fathers, and forced them to throw out their babies, so that they wouldn’t stay alive.

verses.name: Acts 7:20
verses.text: At that time Moses was born, and was exceedingly handsome. He was nourished three months in his father’s house.

verses.name: Acts 7:21
verses.text: When he was thrown out, Pharaoh’s daughter took him up, and reared him as her own son.

verses.name: Acts 7:22
verses.text: Moses was instructed in all the wisdom of the Egyptians. He was mighty in his words and works.

verses.name: Acts 7:23
verses.text: But when he was forty years old, it came into his heart to visit his brothers, the children of Israel.

verses.name: Acts 7:24
verses.text: Seeing one of them suffer wrong, he defended him, and avenged him who was oppressed, striking the Egyptian.

verses.name: Acts 7:25
verses.text: He supposed that his brothers understood that God, by his hand, was giving them deliverance; but they didn’t understand.

verses.name: Acts 7:26
verses.text: “The day following, he appeared to them as they fought, and urged them to be at peace again, saying, ‘Sirs, you are brothers. Why do you wrong one another?’

verses.name: Acts 7:27
verses.text: But he who did his neighbor wrong pushed him away, saying, ‘Who made you a ruler and a judge over us?

verses.name: Acts 7:28
verses.text: Do you want to kill me, as you killed the Egyptian yesterday?’

verses.name: Acts 7:29
verses.text: Moses fled at this saying, and became a stranger in the land of Midian, where he became the father of two sons.

verses.name: Acts 7:30
verses.text: “When forty years were fulfilled, an angel of the Lord appeared to him in the wilderness of Mount Sinai, in a flame of fire in a bush.

verses.name: Acts 7:31
verses.text: When Moses saw it, he wondered at the sight. As he came close to see, a voice of the Lord came to him,

verses.name: Acts 7:32
verses.text: ‘I am the God of your fathers, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.’ Moses trembled, and dared not look.

verses.name: Acts 7:33
verses.text: The Lord said to him, ‘Take off your sandals, for the place where you stand is holy ground.

verses.name: Acts 7:34
verses.text: I have surely seen the affliction of my people that is in Egypt, and have heard their groaning. I have come down to deliver them. Now come, I will send you into Egypt.’

verses.name: Acts 7:35
verses.text: “This Moses, whom they refused, saying, ‘Who made you a ruler and a judge?’—God has sent him as both a ruler and a deliverer by the hand of the angel who appeared to him in the bush.

verses.name: Acts 7:36
verses.text: This man led them out, having worked wonders and signs in Egypt, in the Red Sea, and in the wilderness for forty years.

verses.name: Acts 7:37
verses.text: This is that Moses, who said to the children of Israel, ‘The Lord our God will raise up a prophet for you from among your brothers, like me.’

verses.name: Acts 7:38
verses.text: This is he who was in the assembly in the wilderness with the angel that spoke to him on Mount Sinai, and with our fathers, who received living revelations to give to us,

verses.name: Acts 7:39
verses.text: to whom our fathers wouldn’t be obedient, but rejected him, and turned back in their hearts to Egypt,

verses.name: Acts 7:40
verses.text: saying to Aaron, ‘Make us gods that will go before us, for as for this Moses, who led us out of the land of Egypt, we don’t know what has become of him.’

verses.name: Acts 7:41
verses.text: They made a calf in those days, and brought a sacrifice to the idol, and rejoiced in the works of their hands.

verses.name: Acts 7:42
verses.text: But God turned, and gave them up to serve the army of the sky, as it is written in the book of the prophets, ‘Did you offer to me slain animals and sacrifices forty years in the wilderness, O house of Israel?

verses.name: Acts 7:43
verses.text: You took up the tabernacle of Moloch, the star of your god Rephan, the figures which you made to worship. I will carry you away beyond Babylon.’

verses.name: Acts 7:44
verses.text: “Our fathers had the tabernacle of the testimony in the wilderness, even as he who spoke to Moses commanded him to make it according to the pattern that he had seen;

verses.name: Acts 7:45
verses.text: which also our fathers, in their turn, brought in with Joshua when they entered into the possession of the nations, whom God drove out before the face of our fathers, to the days of David,

verses.name: Acts 7:46
verses.text: who found favor in the sight of God, and asked to find a habitation for the God of Jacob.

verses.name: Acts 7:47
verses.text: But Solomon built him a house.

verses.name: Acts 7:48
verses.text: However, the Most High doesn’t dwell in temples made with hands, as the prophet says,

verses.name: Acts 7:49
verses.text: ‘heaven is my throne, and the earth a footstool for my feet. What kind of house will you build me?’ says the Lord. ‘Or what is the place of my rest?

verses.name: Acts 7:50
verses.text: Didn’t my hand make all these things?’

verses.name: Acts 7:51
verses.text: “You stiff-necked and uncircumcised in heart and ears, you always resist the Holy Spirit! As your fathers did, so you do.

verses.name: Acts 7:52
verses.text: Which of the prophets didn’t your fathers persecute? They killed those who foretold the coming of the Righteous One, of whom you have now become betrayers and murderers.

verses.name: Acts 7:53
verses.text: You received the law as it was ordained by angels, and didn’t keep it!”

verses.name: Acts 7:54
verses.text: Now when they heard these things, they were cut to the heart, and they gnashed at him with their teeth.

verses.name: Acts 7:55
verses.text: But he, being full of the Holy Spirit, looked up steadfastly into heaven, and saw the glory of God, and Jesus standing on the right hand of God,

verses.name: Acts 7:56
verses.text: and said, “Behold, I see the heavens opened, and the Son of Man standing at the right hand of God!”

verses.name: Acts 7:57
verses.text: But they cried out with a loud voice, and stopped their ears, and rushed at him with one accord.

verses.name: Acts 7:58
verses.text: They threw him out of the city, and stoned him. The witnesses placed their garments at the feet of a young man named Saul.

verses.name: Acts 7:59
verses.text: They stoned Stephen as he called out, saying, “Lord Jesus, receive my spirit!”

verses.name: Acts 7:60
verses.text: He kneeled down, and cried with a loud voice, “Lord, don’t hold this sin against them!” When he had said this, he fell asleep.

verses.name: Acts 8:1
verses.text: Saul was consenting to his death. A great persecution arose against the assembly which was in Jerusalem in that day. They were all scattered abroad throughout the regions of Judea and Samaria, except for the apostles.

verses.name: Acts 8:2
verses.text: Devout men buried Stephen, and lamented greatly over him.

verses.name: Acts 8:3
verses.text: But Saul ravaged the assembly, entering into every house, and dragged both men and women off to prison.

verses.name: Acts 8:4
verses.text: Therefore those who were scattered abroad went around preaching the word.

verses.name: Acts 8:5
verses.text: Philip went down to the city of Samaria, and proclaimed to them the Christ.

verses.name: Acts 8:6
verses.text: The multitudes listened with one accord to the things that were spoken by Philip, when they heard and saw the signs which he did.

verses.name: Acts 8:7
verses.text: For unclean spirits came out of many of those who had them. They came out, crying with a loud voice. Many who had been paralyzed and lame were healed.

verses.name: Acts 8:8
verses.text: There was great joy in that city.

verses.name: Acts 8:9
verses.text: But there was a certain man, Simon by name, who used to practice sorcery in the city, and amazed the people of Samaria, making himself out to be some great one,

verses.name: Acts 8:10
verses.text: to whom they all listened, from the least to the greatest, saying, “This man is that great power of God.”

verses.name: Acts 8:11
verses.text: They listened to him, because for a long time he had amazed them with his sorceries.

verses.name: Acts 8:12
verses.text: But when they believed Philip preaching good news concerning God’s Kingdom and the name of Jesus Christ, they were baptized, both men and women.

verses.name: Acts 8:13
verses.text: Simon himself also believed. Being baptized, he continued with Philip. Seeing signs and great miracles occurring, he was amazed.

verses.name: Acts 8:14
verses.text: Now when the apostles who were at Jerusalem heard that Samaria had received the word of God, they sent Peter and John to them,

verses.name: Acts 8:15
verses.text: who, when they had come down, prayed for them, that they might receive the Holy Spirit;

verses.name: Acts 8:16
verses.text: for as yet he had fallen on none of them. They had only been baptized in the name of Christ Jesus.

verses.name: Acts 8:17
verses.text: Then they laid their hands on them, and they received the Holy Spirit.

verses.name: Acts 8:18
verses.text: Now when Simon saw that the Holy Spirit was given through the laying on of the apostles’ hands, he offered them money,

verses.name: Acts 8:19
verses.text: saying, “Give me also this power, that whomever I lay my hands on may receive the Holy Spirit.”

verses.name: Acts 8:20
verses.text: But Peter said to him, “May your silver perish with you, because you thought you could obtain the gift of God with money!

verses.name: Acts 8:21
verses.text: You have neither part nor lot in this matter, for your heart isn’t right before God.

verses.name: Acts 8:22
verses.text: Repent therefore of this, your wickedness, and ask God if perhaps the thought of your heart may be forgiven you.

verses.name: Acts 8:23
verses.text: For I see that you are in the poison of bitterness and in the bondage of iniquity.”

verses.name: Acts 8:24
verses.text: Simon answered, “Pray for me to the Lord, that none of the things which you have spoken happen to me.”

verses.name: Acts 8:25
verses.text: They therefore, when they had testified and spoken the word of the Lord, returned to Jerusalem, and preached the Good News to many villages of the Samaritans.

verses.name: Acts 8:26
verses.text: But an angel of the Lord spoke to Philip, saying, “Arise, and go toward the south to the way that goes down from Jerusalem to Gaza. This is a desert.”

verses.name: Acts 8:27
verses.text: He arose and went; and behold, there was a man of Ethiopia, a eunuch of great authority under Candace, queen of the Ethiopians, who was over all her treasure, who had come to Jerusalem to worship.

verses.name: Acts 8:28
verses.text: He was returning and sitting in his chariot, and was reading the prophet Isaiah.

verses.name: Acts 8:29
verses.text: The Spirit said to Philip, “Go near, and join yourself to this chariot.”

verses.name: Acts 8:30
verses.text: Philip ran to him, and heard him reading Isaiah the prophet, and said, “Do you understand what you are reading?”

verses.name: Acts 8:31
verses.text: He said, “How can I, unless someone explains it to me?” He begged Philip to come up and sit with him.

verses.name: Acts 8:32
verses.text: Now the passage of the Scripture which he was reading was this, “He was led as a sheep to the slaughter. As a lamb before his shearer is silent, so he doesn’t open his mouth.

verses.name: Acts 8:33
verses.text: In his humiliation, his judgment was taken away. Who will declare His generation? For his life is taken from the earth.”

verses.name: Acts 8:34
verses.text: The eunuch answered Philip, “Who is the prophet talking about? About himself, or about someone else?”

verses.name: Acts 8:35
verses.text: Philip opened his mouth, and beginning from this Scripture, preached to him about Jesus.

verses.name: Acts 8:36
verses.text: As they went on the way, they came to some water, and the eunuch said, “Behold, here is water. What is keeping me from being baptized?”

verses.name: Acts 8:38
verses.text: He commanded the chariot to stand still, and they both went down into the water, both Philip and the eunuch, and he baptized him.

verses.name: Acts 8:39
verses.text: When they came up out of the water, the Spirit of the Lord caught Philip away, and the eunuch didn’t see him any more, for he went on his way rejoicing.

verses.name: Acts 8:40
verses.text: But Philip was found at Azotus. Passing through, he preached the Good News to all the cities, until he came to Caesarea.

verses.name: Acts 9:1
verses.text: But Saul, still breathing threats and slaughter against the disciples of the Lord, went to the high priest,

verses.name: Acts 9:2
verses.text: and asked for letters from him to the synagogues of Damascus, that if he found any who were of the Way, whether men or women, he might bring them bound to Jerusalem.

verses.name: Acts 9:3
verses.text: As he traveled, he got close to Damascus, and suddenly a light from the sky shone around him.

verses.name: Acts 9:4
verses.text: He fell on the earth, and heard a voice saying to him, “Saul, Saul, why do you persecute me?”

verses.name: Acts 9:5
verses.text: He said, “Who are you, Lord?” The Lord said, “I am Jesus, whom you are persecuting.

verses.name: Acts 9:6
verses.text: But rise up, and enter into the city, and you will be told what you must do.”

verses.name: Acts 9:7
verses.text: The men who traveled with him stood speechless, hearing the sound, but seeing no one.

verses.name: Acts 9:8
verses.text: Saul arose from the ground, and when his eyes were opened, he saw no one. They led him by the hand, and brought him into Damascus.

verses.name: Acts 9:9
verses.text: He was without sight for three days, and neither ate nor drank.

verses.name: Acts 9:10
verses.text: Now there was a certain disciple at Damascus named Ananias. The Lord said to him in a vision, “Ananias!” He said, “Behold, it’s me, Lord.”

verses.name: Acts 9:11
verses.text: The Lord said to him, “Arise, and go to the street which is called Straight, and inquire in the house of Judah for one named Saul, a man of Tarsus. For behold, he is praying,

verses.name: Acts 9:12
verses.text: and in a vision he has seen a man named Ananias coming in, and laying his hands on him, that he might receive his sight.”

verses.name: Acts 9:13
verses.text: But Ananias answered, “Lord, I have heard from many about this man, how much evil he did to your saints at Jerusalem.

verses.name: Acts 9:14
verses.text: Here he has authority from the chief priests to bind all who call on your name.”

verses.name: Acts 9:15
verses.text: But the Lord said to him, “Go your way, for he is my chosen vessel to bear my name before the nations and kings, and the children of Israel.

verses.name: Acts 9:16
verses.text: For I will show him how many things he must suffer for my name’s sake.”

verses.name: Acts 9:17
verses.text: Ananias departed, and entered into the house. Laying his hands on him, he said, “Brother Saul, the Lord, who appeared to you on the road by which you came, has sent me, that you may receive your sight, and be filled with the Holy Spirit.”

verses.name: Acts 9:18
verses.text: Immediately something like scales fell from his eyes, and he received his sight. He arose and was baptized.

verses.name: Acts 9:19
verses.text: He took food and was strengthened. Saul stayed several days with the disciples who were at Damascus.

verses.name: Acts 9:20
verses.text: Immediately in the synagogues he proclaimed the Christ, that he is the Son of God.

verses.name: Acts 9:21
verses.text: All who heard him were amazed, and said, “Isn’t this he who in Jerusalem made havoc of those who called on this name? And he had come here intending to bring them bound before the chief priests!”

verses.name: Acts 9:22
verses.text: But Saul increased more in strength, and confounded the Jews who lived at Damascus, proving that this is the Christ.

verses.name: Acts 9:23
verses.text: When many days were fulfilled, the Jews conspired together to kill him,

verses.name: Acts 9:24
verses.text: but their plot became known to Saul. They watched the gates both day and night that they might kill him,

verses.name: Acts 9:25
verses.text: but his disciples took him by night, and let him down through the wall, lowering him in a basket.

verses.name: Acts 9:26
verses.text: When Saul had come to Jerusalem, he tried to join himself to the disciples; but they were all afraid of him, not believing that he was a disciple.

verses.name: Acts 9:27
verses.text: But Barnabas took him, and brought him to the apostles, and declared to them how he had seen the Lord on the way, and that he had spoken to him, and how at Damascus he had preached boldly in the name of Jesus.

verses.name: Acts 9:28
verses.text: He was with them entering into Jerusalem,

verses.name: Acts 9:29
verses.text: preaching boldly in the name of the Lord Jesus. He spoke and disputed against the Hellenists, but they were seeking to kill him.

verses.name: Acts 9:30
verses.text: When the brothers knew it, they brought him down to Caesarea, and sent him off to Tarsus.

verses.name: Acts 9:31
verses.text: So the assemblies throughout all Judea and Galilee and Samaria had peace, and were built up. They were multiplied, walking in the fear of the Lord and in the comfort of the Holy Spirit.

verses.name: Acts 9:32
verses.text: As Peter went throughout all those parts, he came down also to the saints who lived at Lydda.

verses.name: Acts 9:33
verses.text: There he found a certain man named Aeneas, who had been bedridden for eight years, because he was paralyzed.

verses.name: Acts 9:34
verses.text: Peter said to him, “Aeneas, Jesus Christ heals you. Get up and make your bed!” Immediately he arose.

verses.name: Acts 9:35
verses.text: All who lived at Lydda and in Sharon saw him, and they turned to the Lord.

verses.name: Acts 9:36
verses.text: Now there was at Joppa a certain disciple named Tabitha, which when translated, means Dorcas. This woman was full of good works and acts of mercy which she did.

verses.name: Acts 9:37
verses.text: In those days, she became sick, and died. When they had washed her, they laid her in an upper room.

verses.name: Acts 9:38
verses.text: As Lydda was near Joppa, the disciples, hearing that Peter was there, sent two men to him, imploring him not to delay in coming to them.

verses.name: Acts 9:39
verses.text: Peter got up and went with them. When he had come, they brought him into the upper room. All the widows stood by him weeping, and showing the coats and garments which Dorcas had made while she was with them.

verses.name: Acts 9:40
verses.text: Peter sent them all out, and knelt down and prayed. Turning to the body, he said, “Tabitha, get up!” She opened her eyes, and when she saw Peter, she sat up.

verses.name: Acts 9:41
verses.text: He gave her his hand, and raised her up. Calling the saints and widows, he presented her alive.

verses.name: Acts 9:42
verses.text: And it became known throughout all Joppa, and many believed in the Lord.

verses.name: Acts 9:43
verses.text: He stayed many days in Joppa with a tanner named Simon.

verses.name: Acts 10:1
verses.text: Now there was a certain man in Caesarea, Cornelius by name, a centurion of what was called the Italian Regiment,

verses.name: Acts 10:2
verses.text: a devout man, and one who feared God with all his house, who gave gifts for the needy generously to the people, and always prayed to God.

verses.name: Acts 10:3
verses.text: At about the ninth hour of the day, he clearly saw in a vision an angel of God coming to him, and saying to him, “Cornelius!”

verses.name: Acts 10:4
verses.text: He, fastening his eyes on him, and being frightened, said, “What is it, Lord?” He said to him, “Your prayers and your gifts to the needy have gone up for a memorial before God.

verses.name: Acts 10:5
verses.text: Now send men to Joppa, and get Simon, who is also called Peter.

verses.name: Acts 10:6
verses.text: He lodges with a tanner named Simon, whose house is by the seaside.

verses.name: Acts 10:7
verses.text: When the angel who spoke to him had departed, Cornelius called two of his household servants and a devout soldier of those who waited on him continually.

verses.name: Acts 10:8
verses.text: Having explained everything to them, he sent them to Joppa.

verses.name: Acts 10:9
verses.text: Now on the next day as they were on their journey, and got close to the city, Peter went up on the housetop to pray at about noon.

verses.name: Acts 10:10
verses.text: He became hungry and desired to eat, but while they were preparing, he fell into a trance.

verses.name: Acts 10:11
verses.text: He saw heaven opened and a certain container descending to him, like a great sheet let down by four corners on the earth,

verses.name: Acts 10:12
verses.text: in which were all kinds of four-footed animals of the earth, wild animals, reptiles, and birds of the sky.

verses.name: Acts 10:13
verses.text: A voice came to him, “Rise, Peter, kill and eat!”

verses.name: Acts 10:14
verses.text: But Peter said, “Not so, Lord; for I have never eaten anything that is common or unclean.”

verses.name: Acts 10:15
verses.text: A voice came to him again the second time, “What God has cleansed, you must not call unclean.”

verses.name: Acts 10:16
verses.text: This was done three times, and immediately the vessel was received up into heaven.

verses.name: Acts 10:17
verses.text: Now while Peter was very perplexed in himself what the vision which he had seen might mean, behold, the men who were sent by Cornelius, having made inquiry for Simon’s house, stood before the gate,

verses.name: Acts 10:18
verses.text: and called and asked whether Simon, who was also called Peter, was lodging there.

verses.name: Acts 10:19
verses.text: While Peter was pondering the vision, the Spirit said to him, “Behold, three men seek you.

verses.name: Acts 10:20
verses.text: But arise, get down, and go with them, doubting nothing; for I have sent them.”

verses.name: Acts 10:21
verses.text: Peter went down to the men, and said, “Behold, I am he whom you seek. Why have you come?”

verses.name: Acts 10:22
verses.text: They said, “Cornelius, a centurion, a righteous man and one who fears God, and well spoken of by all the nation of the Jews, was directed by a holy angel to invite you to his house, and to listen to what you say.”

verses.name: Acts 10:23
verses.text: So he called them in and provided a place to stay. On the next day Peter arose and went out with them, and some of the brothers from Joppa accompanied him.

verses.name: Acts 10:24
verses.text: On the next day they entered into Caesarea. Cornelius was waiting for them, having called together his relatives and his near friends.

verses.name: Acts 10:25
verses.text: When Peter entered, Cornelius met him, fell down at his feet, and worshiped him.

verses.name: Acts 10:26
verses.text: But Peter raised him up, saying, “Stand up! I myself am also a man.”

verses.name: Acts 10:27
verses.text: As he talked with him, he went in and found many gathered together.

verses.name: Acts 10:28
verses.text: He said to them, “You yourselves know how it is an unlawful thing for a man who is a Jew to join himself or come to one of another nation, but God has shown me that I shouldn’t call any man unholy or unclean.

verses.name: Acts 10:29
verses.text: Therefore also I came without complaint when I was sent for. I ask therefore, why did you send for me?”

verses.name: Acts 10:30
verses.text: Cornelius said, “Four days ago, I was fasting until this hour, and at the ninth hour, I prayed in my house, and behold, a man stood before me in bright clothing,

verses.name: Acts 10:31
verses.text: and said, ‘Cornelius, your prayer is heard, and your gifts to the needy are remembered in the sight of God.

verses.name: Acts 10:32
verses.text: Send therefore to Joppa, and summon Simon, who is also called Peter. He lodges in the house of a tanner named Simon, by the seaside. When he comes, he will speak to you.’

verses.name: Acts 10:33
verses.text: Therefore I sent to you at once, and it was good of you to come. Now therefore we are all here present in the sight of God to hear all things that have been commanded you by God.”

verses.name: Acts 10:34
verses.text: Peter opened his mouth and said, “Truly I perceive that God doesn’t show favoritism;

verses.name: Acts 10:35
verses.text: but in every nation he who fears him and works righteousness is acceptable to him.

verses.name: Acts 10:36
verses.text: The word which he sent to the children of Israel, preaching good news of peace by Jesus Christ—he is Lord of all—

verses.name: Acts 10:37
verses.text: you yourselves know what happened, which was proclaimed throughout all Judea, beginning from Galilee, after the baptism which John preached;

verses.name: Acts 10:38
verses.text: even Jesus of Nazareth, how God anointed him with the Holy Spirit and with power, who went about doing good and healing all who were oppressed by the devil, for God was with him.

verses.name: Acts 10:39
verses.text: We are witnesses of everything he did both in the country of the Jews, and in Jerusalem; whom they also killed, hanging him on a tree.

verses.name: Acts 10:40
verses.text: God raised him up the third day, and gave him to be revealed,

verses.name: Acts 10:41
verses.text: not to all the people, but to witnesses who were chosen before by God, to us, who ate and drank with him after he rose from the dead.

verses.name: Acts 10:42
verses.text: He commanded us to preach to the people and to testify that this is he who is appointed by God as the Judge of the living and the dead.

verses.name: Acts 10:43
verses.text: All the prophets testify about him, that through his name everyone who believes in him will receive remission of sins.”

verses.name: Acts 10:44
verses.text: While Peter was still speaking these words, the Holy Spirit fell on all those who heard the word.

verses.name: Acts 10:45
verses.text: They of the circumcision who believed were amazed, as many as came with Peter, because the gift of the Holy Spirit was also poured out on the Gentiles.

verses.name: Acts 10:46
verses.text: For they heard them speaking in other languages and magnifying God. Then Peter answered,

verses.name: Acts 10:47
verses.text: “Can anyone forbid these people from being baptized with water? They have received the Holy Spirit just like us.”

verses.name: Acts 10:48
verses.text: He commanded them to be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ. Then they asked him to stay some days.

verses.name: Acts 11:1
verses.text: Now the apostles and the brothers who were in Judea heard that the Gentiles had also received the word of God.

verses.name: Acts 11:2
verses.text: When Peter had come up to Jerusalem, those who were of the circumcision contended with him,

verses.name: Acts 11:3
verses.text: saying, “You went in to uncircumcised men, and ate with them!”

verses.name: Acts 11:4
verses.text: But Peter began, and explained to them in order, saying,

verses.name: Acts 11:5
verses.text: “I was in the city of Joppa praying, and in a trance I saw a vision: a certain container descending, like it was a great sheet let down from heaven by four corners. It came as far as me.

verses.name: Acts 11:6
verses.text: When I had looked intently at it, I considered, and saw the four-footed animals of the earth, wild animals, creeping things, and birds of the sky.

verses.name: Acts 11:7
verses.text: I also heard a voice saying to me, ‘Rise, Peter, kill and eat!’

verses.name: Acts 11:8
verses.text: But I said, ‘Not so, Lord, for nothing unholy or unclean has ever entered into my mouth.’

verses.name: Acts 11:9
verses.text: But a voice answered me the second time out of heaven, ‘What God has cleansed, don’t you call unclean.’

verses.name: Acts 11:10
verses.text: This was done three times, and all were drawn up again into heaven.

verses.name: Acts 11:11
verses.text: Behold, immediately three men stood before the house where I was, having been sent from Caesarea to me.

verses.name: Acts 11:12
verses.text: The Spirit told me to go with them, without discriminating. These six brothers also accompanied me, and we entered into the man’s house.

verses.name: Acts 11:13
verses.text: He told us how he had seen the angel standing in his house, and saying to him, ‘Send to Joppa, and get Simon, who is called Peter,

verses.name: Acts 11:14
verses.text: who will speak to you words by which you will be saved, you and all your house.’

verses.name: Acts 11:15
verses.text: As I began to speak, the Holy Spirit fell on them, even as on us at the beginning.

verses.name: Acts 11:16
verses.text: I remembered the word of the Lord, how he said, ‘John indeed baptized in water, but you will be baptized in the Holy Spirit.’

verses.name: Acts 11:17
verses.text: If then God gave to them the same gift as us, when we believed in the Lord Jesus Christ, who was I, that I could withstand God?”

verses.name: Acts 11:18
verses.text: When they heard these things, they held their peace, and glorified God, saying, “Then God has also granted to the Gentiles repentance to life!”

verses.name: Acts 11:19
verses.text: They therefore who were scattered abroad by the oppression that arose about Stephen traveled as far as Phoenicia, Cyprus, and Antioch, speaking the word to no one except to Jews only.

verses.name: Acts 11:20
verses.text: But there were some of them, men of Cyprus and Cyrene, who, when they had come to Antioch, spoke to the Hellenists, preaching the Lord Jesus.

verses.name: Acts 11:21
verses.text: The hand of the Lord was with them, and a great number believed and turned to the Lord.

verses.name: Acts 11:22
verses.text: The report concerning them came to the ears of the assembly which was in Jerusalem. They sent out Barnabas to go as far as Antioch,

verses.name: Acts 11:23
verses.text: who, when he had come, and had seen the grace of God, was glad. He exhorted them all, that with purpose of heart they should remain near to the Lord.

verses.name: Acts 11:24
verses.text: For he was a good man, and full of the Holy Spirit and of faith, and many people were added to the Lord.

verses.name: Acts 11:25
verses.text: Barnabas went out to Tarsus to look for Saul.

verses.name: Acts 11:26
verses.text: When he had found him, he brought him to Antioch. For a whole year they were gathered together with the assembly, and taught many people. The disciples were first called Christians in Antioch.

verses.name: Acts 11:27
verses.text: Now in these days, prophets came down from Jerusalem to Antioch.

verses.name: Acts 11:28
verses.text: One of them named Agabus stood up, and indicated by the Spirit that there should be a great famine all over the world, which also happened in the days of Claudius.

verses.name: Acts 11:29
verses.text: As any of the disciples had plenty, each determined to send relief to the brothers who lived in Judea;

verses.name: Acts 11:30
verses.text: which they also did, sending it to the elders by the hands of Barnabas and Saul.

verses.name: Acts 12:1
verses.text: Now about that time, King Herod stretched out his hands to oppress some of the assembly.

verses.name: Acts 12:2
verses.text: He killed James, the brother of John, with the sword.

verses.name: Acts 12:3
verses.text: When he saw that it pleased the Jews, he proceeded to seize Peter also. This was during the days of unleavened bread.

verses.name: Acts 12:4
verses.text: When he had arrested him, he put him in prison, and delivered him to four squads of four soldiers each to guard him, intending to bring him out to the people after the Passover.

verses.name: Acts 12:5
verses.text: Peter therefore was kept in the prison, but constant prayer was made by the assembly to God for him.

verses.name: Acts 12:6
verses.text: The same night when Herod was about to bring him out, Peter was sleeping between two soldiers, bound with two chains. Guards in front of the door kept the prison.

verses.name: Acts 12:7
verses.text: And behold, an angel of the Lord stood by him, and a light shone in the cell. He struck Peter on the side, and woke him up, saying, “Stand up quickly!” His chains fell off from his hands.

verses.name: Acts 12:8
verses.text: The angel said to him, “Get dressed and put on your sandals.” He did so. He said to him, “Put on your cloak, and follow me.”

verses.name: Acts 12:9
verses.text: And he went out and followed him. He didn’t know that what was being done by the angel was real, but thought he saw a vision.

verses.name: Acts 12:10
verses.text: When they were past the first and the second guard, they came to the iron gate that leads into the city, which opened to them by itself. They went out, and went down one street, and immediately the angel departed from him.

verses.name: Acts 12:11
verses.text: When Peter had come to himself, he said, “Now I truly know that the Lord has sent out his angel and delivered me out of the hand of Herod, and from everything the Jewish people were expecting.”

verses.name: Acts 12:12
verses.text: Thinking about that, he came to the house of Mary, the mother of John who was called Mark, where many were gathered together and were praying.

verses.name: Acts 12:13
verses.text: When Peter knocked at the door of the gate, a maid named Rhoda came to answer.

verses.name: Acts 12:14
verses.text: When she recognized Peter’s voice, she didn’t open the gate for joy, but ran in, and reported that Peter was standing in front of the gate.

verses.name: Acts 12:15
verses.text: They said to her, “You are crazy!” But she insisted that it was so. They said, “It is his angel.”

verses.name: Acts 12:16
verses.text: But Peter continued knocking. When they had opened, they saw him, and were amazed.

verses.name: Acts 12:17
verses.text: But he, beckoning to them with his hand to be silent, declared to them how the Lord had brought him out of the prison. He said, “Tell these things to James, and to the brothers.” Then he departed, and went to another place.

verses.name: Acts 12:18
verses.text: Now as soon as it was day, there was no small stir among the soldiers about what had become of Peter.

verses.name: Acts 12:19
verses.text: When Herod had sought for him, and didn’t find him, he examined the guards, and commanded that they should be put to death. He went down from Judea to Caesarea, and stayed there.

verses.name: Acts 12:20
verses.text: Now Herod was very angry with the people of Tyre and Sidon. They came with one accord to him, and, having made Blastus, the king’s personal aide, their friend, they asked for peace, because their country depended on the king’s country for food.

verses.name: Acts 12:21
verses.text: On an appointed day, Herod dressed himself in royal clothing, sat on the throne, and gave a speech to them.

verses.name: Acts 12:22
verses.text: The people shouted, “The voice of a god, and not of a man!”

verses.name: Acts 12:23
verses.text: Immediately an angel of the Lord struck him, because he didn’t give God the glory, and he was eaten by worms and died.

verses.name: Acts 12:24
verses.text: But the word of God grew and multiplied.

verses.name: Acts 12:25
verses.text: Barnabas and Saul returned to Jerusalem, when they had fulfilled their service, also taking with them John who was called Mark.

verses.name: Acts 13:1
verses.text: Now in the assembly that was at Antioch there were some prophets and teachers: Barnabas, Simeon who was called Niger, Lucius of Cyrene, Manaen the foster brother of Herod the tetrarch, and Saul.

verses.name: Acts 13:2
verses.text: As they served the Lord and fasted, the Holy Spirit said, “Separate Barnabas and Saul for me, for the work to which I have called them.”

verses.name: Acts 13:3
verses.text: Then, when they had fasted and prayed and laid their hands on them, they sent them away.

verses.name: Acts 13:4
verses.text: So, being sent out by the Holy Spirit, they went down to Seleucia. From there they sailed to Cyprus.

verses.name: Acts 13:5
verses.text: When they were at Salamis, they proclaimed God’s word in the Jewish synagogues. They had also John as their attendant.

verses.name: Acts 13:6
verses.text: When they had gone through the island to Paphos, they found a certain sorcerer, a false prophet, a Jew, whose name was Bar Jesus,

verses.name: Acts 13:7
verses.text: who was with the proconsul, Sergius Paulus, a man of understanding. This man summoned Barnabas and Saul, and sought to hear the word of God.

verses.name: Acts 13:8
verses.text: But Elymas the sorcerer (for so is his name by interpretation) withstood them, seeking to turn the proconsul away from the faith.

verses.name: Acts 13:9
verses.text: But Saul, who is also called Paul, filled with the Holy Spirit, fastened his eyes on him,

verses.name: Acts 13:10
verses.text: and said, “Full of all deceit and all cunning, you son of the devil, you enemy of all righteousness, will you not cease to pervert the right ways of the Lord?

verses.name: Acts 13:11
verses.text: Now, behold, the hand of the Lord is on you, and you will be blind, not seeing the sun for a season!” Immediately a mist and darkness fell on him. He went around seeking someone to lead him by the hand.

verses.name: Acts 13:12
verses.text: Then the proconsul, when he saw what was done, believed, being astonished at the teaching of the Lord.

verses.name: Acts 13:13
verses.text: Now Paul and his company set sail from Paphos, and came to Perga in Pamphylia. John departed from them and returned to Jerusalem.

verses.name: Acts 13:14
verses.text: But they, passing on from Perga, came to Antioch of Pisidia. They went into the synagogue on the Sabbath day, and sat down.

verses.name: Acts 13:15
verses.text: After the reading of the law and the prophets, the rulers of the synagogue sent to them, saying, “Brothers, if you have any word of exhortation for the people, speak.”

verses.name: Acts 13:16
verses.text: Paul stood up, and beckoning with his hand said, “Men of Israel, and you who fear God, listen.

verses.name: Acts 13:17
verses.text: The God of this people chose our fathers, and exalted the people when they stayed as aliens in the land of Egypt, and with an uplifted arm, he led them out of it.

verses.name: Acts 13:18
verses.text: For a period of about forty years he put up with them in the wilderness.

verses.name: Acts 13:19
verses.text: When he had destroyed seven nations in the land of Canaan, he gave them their land for an inheritance, for about four hundred fifty years.

verses.name: Acts 13:20
verses.text: After these things he gave them judges until Samuel the prophet.

verses.name: Acts 13:21
verses.text: Afterward they asked for a king, and God gave to them Saul the son of Kish, a man of the tribe of Benjamin, for forty years.

verses.name: Acts 13:22
verses.text: When he had removed him, he raised up David to be their king, to whom he also testified, ‘I have found David the son of Jesse, a man after my heart, who will do all my will.’

verses.name: Acts 13:23
verses.text: From this man’s offspring, God has brought salvation to Israel according to his promise,

verses.name: Acts 13:24
verses.text: before his coming, when John had first preached the baptism of repentance to Israel.

verses.name: Acts 13:25
verses.text: As John was fulfilling his course, he said, ‘What do you suppose that I am? I am not he. But behold, one comes after me, the sandals of whose feet I am not worthy to untie.’

verses.name: Acts 13:26
verses.text: Brothers, children of the stock of Abraham, and those among you who fear God, the word of this salvation is sent out to you.

verses.name: Acts 13:27
