verses.text: doing nothing through rivalry or through conceit, but in humility, each counting others better than himself;

verses.name: Philippians 2:4
verses.text: each of you not just looking to his own things, but each of you also to the things of others.

verses.name: Philippians 2:5
verses.text: Have this in your mind, which was also in Christ Jesus,

verses.name: Philippians 2:6
verses.text: who, existing in the form of God, didn’t consider equality with God a thing to be grasped,

verses.name: Philippians 2:7
verses.text: but emptied himself, taking the form of a servant, being made in the likeness of men.

verses.name: Philippians 2:8
verses.text: And being found in human form, he humbled himself, becoming obedient to death, yes, the death of the cross.

verses.name: Philippians 2:9
verses.text: Therefore God also highly exalted him, and gave to him the name which is above every name;

verses.name: Philippians 2:10
verses.text: that at the name of Jesus every knee should bow, of those in heaven, those on earth, and those under the earth,

verses.name: Philippians 2:11
verses.text: and that every tongue should confess that Jesus Christ is Lord, to the glory of God the Father.

verses.name: Philippians 2:12
verses.text: So then, my beloved, even as you have always obeyed, not only in my presence, but now much more in my absence, work out your own salvation with fear and trembling.

verses.name: Philippians 2:13
verses.text: For it is God who works in you both to will and to work, for his good pleasure.

verses.name: Philippians 2:14
verses.text: Do all things without murmurings and disputes,

verses.name: Philippians 2:15
verses.text: that you may become blameless and harmless, children of God without defect in the middle of a crooked and perverse generation, among whom you are seen as lights in the world,

verses.name: Philippians 2:16
verses.text: holding up the word of life; that I may have something to boast in the day of Christ, that I didn’t run in vain nor labor in vain.

verses.name: Philippians 2:17
verses.text: Yes, and if I am poured out on the sacrifice and service of your faith, I rejoice, and rejoice with you all.

verses.name: Philippians 2:18
verses.text: In the same way, you also rejoice, and rejoice with me.

verses.name: Philippians 2:19
verses.text: But I hope in the Lord Jesus to send Timothy to you soon, that I also may be cheered up when I know how you are doing.

verses.name: Philippians 2:20
verses.text: For I have no one else like-minded, who will truly care about you.

verses.name: Philippians 2:21
verses.text: For they all seek their own, not the things of Jesus Christ.

verses.name: Philippians 2:22
verses.text: But you know the proof of him, that, as a child serves a father, so he served with me in furtherance of the Good News.

verses.name: Philippians 2:23
verses.text: Therefore I hope to send him at once, as soon as I see how it will go with me.

verses.name: Philippians 2:24
verses.text: But I trust in the Lord that I myself also will come shortly.

verses.name: Philippians 2:25
verses.text: But I counted it necessary to send to you Epaphroditus, my brother, fellow worker, fellow soldier, and your apostle and servant of my need;

verses.name: Philippians 2:26
verses.text: since he longed for you all, and was very troubled, because you had heard that he was sick.

verses.name: Philippians 2:27
verses.text: For indeed he was sick, nearly to death, but God had mercy on him; and not on him only, but on me also, that I might not have sorrow on sorrow.

verses.name: Philippians 2:28
verses.text: I have sent him therefore the more diligently, that, when you see him again, you may rejoice, and that I may be the less sorrowful.

verses.name: Philippians 2:29
verses.text: Receive him therefore in the Lord with all joy, and hold such in honor,

verses.name: Philippians 2:30
verses.text: because for the work of Christ he came near to death, risking his life to supply that which was lacking in your service toward me.

verses.name: Philippians 3:1
verses.text: Finally, my brothers, rejoice in the Lord. To write the same things to you, to me indeed is not tiresome, but for you it is safe.

verses.name: Philippians 3:2
verses.text: Beware of the dogs, beware of the evil workers, beware of the false circumcision.

verses.name: Philippians 3:3
verses.text: For we are the circumcision, who worship God in the Spirit, and rejoice in Christ Jesus, and have no confidence in the flesh;

verses.name: Philippians 3:4
verses.text: though I myself might have confidence even in the flesh. If any other man thinks that he has confidence in the flesh, I yet more:

verses.name: Philippians 3:5
verses.text: circumcised the eighth day, of the stock of Israel, of the tribe of Benjamin, a Hebrew of Hebrews; concerning the law, a Pharisee;

verses.name: Philippians 3:6
verses.text: concerning zeal, persecuting the assembly; concerning the righteousness which is in the law, found blameless.

verses.name: Philippians 3:7
verses.text: However, I consider those things that were gain to me as a loss for Christ.

verses.name: Philippians 3:8
verses.text: Yes most certainly, and I count all things to be a loss for the excellency of the knowledge of Christ Jesus, my Lord, for whom I suffered the loss of all things, and count them nothing but refuse, that I may gain Christ

verses.name: Philippians 3:9
verses.text: and be found in him, not having a righteousness of my own, that which is of the law, but that which is through faith in Christ, the righteousness which is from God by faith;

verses.name: Philippians 3:10
verses.text: that I may know him, and the power of his resurrection, and the fellowship of his sufferings, becoming conformed to his death;

verses.name: Philippians 3:11
verses.text: if by any means I may attain to the resurrection from the dead.

verses.name: Philippians 3:12
verses.text: Not that I have already obtained, or am already made perfect; but I press on, that I may take hold of that for which also I was taken hold of by Christ Jesus.

verses.name: Philippians 3:13
verses.text: Brothers, I don’t regard myself as yet having taken hold, but one thing I do. Forgetting the things which are behind, and stretching forward to the things which are before,

verses.name: Philippians 3:14
verses.text: I press on toward the goal for the prize of the high calling of God in Christ Jesus.

verses.name: Philippians 3:15
verses.text: Let us therefore, as many as are perfect, think this way. If in anything you think otherwise, God will also reveal that to you.

verses.name: Philippians 3:16
verses.text: Nevertheless, to the extent that we have already attained, let us walk by the same rule. Let us be of the same mind.

verses.name: Philippians 3:17
verses.text: Brothers, be imitators together of me, and note those who walk this way, even as you have us for an example.

verses.name: Philippians 3:18
verses.text: For many walk, of whom I told you often, and now tell you even weeping, as the enemies of the cross of Christ,

verses.name: Philippians 3:19
verses.text: whose end is destruction, whose god is the belly, and whose glory is in their shame, who think about earthly things.

verses.name: Philippians 3:20
verses.text: For our citizenship is in heaven, from where we also wait for a Savior, the Lord Jesus Christ;

verses.name: Philippians 3:21
verses.text: who will change the body of our humiliation to be conformed to the body of his glory, according to the working by which he is able even to subject all things to himself.

verses.name: Philippians 4:1
verses.text: Therefore, my brothers, beloved and longed for, my joy and crown, so stand firm in the Lord, my beloved.

verses.name: Philippians 4:2
verses.text: I exhort Euodia, and I exhort Syntyche, to think the same way in the Lord.

verses.name: Philippians 4:3
verses.text: Yes, I beg you also, true partner, help these women, for they labored with me in the Good News, with Clement also, and the rest of my fellow workers, whose names are in the book of life.

verses.name: Philippians 4:4
verses.text: Rejoice in the Lord always! Again I will say, “Rejoice!”

verses.name: Philippians 4:5
verses.text: Let your gentleness be known to all men. The Lord is at hand.

verses.name: Philippians 4:6
verses.text: In nothing be anxious, but in everything, by prayer and petition with thanksgiving, let your requests be made known to God.

verses.name: Philippians 4:7
verses.text: And the peace of God, which surpasses all understanding, will guard your hearts and your thoughts in Christ Jesus.

verses.name: Philippians 4:8
verses.text: Finally, brothers, whatever things are true, whatever things are honorable, whatever things are just, whatever things are pure, whatever things are lovely, whatever things are of good report; if there is any virtue, and if there is any praise, think about these things.

verses.name: Philippians 4:9
verses.text: The things which you learned, received, heard, and saw in me: do these things, and the God of peace will be with you.

verses.name: Philippians 4:10
verses.text: But I rejoice in the Lord greatly, that now at length you have revived your thought for me; in which you did indeed take thought, but you lacked opportunity.

verses.name: Philippians 4:11
verses.text: Not that I speak because of lack, for I have learned in whatever state I am, to be content in it.

verses.name: Philippians 4:12
verses.text: I know how to be humbled, and I know also how to abound. In everything and in all things I have learned the secret both to be filled and to be hungry, both to abound and to be in need.

verses.name: Philippians 4:13
verses.text: I can do all things through Christ, who strengthens me.

verses.name: Philippians 4:14
verses.text: However you did well that you shared in my affliction.

verses.name: Philippians 4:15
verses.text: You yourselves also know, you Philippians, that in the beginning of the Good News, when I departed from Macedonia, no assembly shared with me in the matter of giving and receiving but you only.

verses.name: Philippians 4:16
verses.text: For even in Thessalonica you sent once and again to my need.

verses.name: Philippians 4:17
verses.text: Not that I seek for the gift, but I seek for the fruit that increases to your account.

verses.name: Philippians 4:18
verses.text: But I have all things, and abound. I am filled, having received from Epaphroditus the things that came from you, a sweet-smelling fragrance, an acceptable and well-pleasing sacrifice to God.

verses.name: Philippians 4:19
verses.text: My God will supply every need of yours according to his riches in glory in Christ Jesus.

verses.name: Philippians 4:20
verses.text: Now to our God and Father be the glory forever and ever! Amen.

verses.name: Philippians 4:21
verses.text: Greet every saint in Christ Jesus. The brothers who are with me greet you.

verses.name: Philippians 4:22
verses.text: All the saints greet you, especially those who are of Caesar’s household.

verses.name: Philippians 4:23
verses.text: The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. Amen.

verses.name: Colossians 1:1
verses.text: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus through the will of God, and Timothy our brother,

verses.name: Colossians 1:2
verses.text: to the saints and faithful brothers in Christ at Colossae: Grace to you and peace from God our Father, and the Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.name: Colossians 1:3
verses.text: We give thanks to God the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, praying always for you,

verses.name: Colossians 1:4
verses.text: having heard of your faith in Christ Jesus, and of the love which you have toward all the saints,

verses.name: Colossians 1:5
verses.text: because of the hope which is laid up for you in the heavens, of which you heard before in the word of the truth of the Good News,

verses.name: Colossians 1:6
verses.text: which has come to you; even as it is in all the world and is bearing fruit and growing, as it does in you also, since the day you heard and knew the grace of God in truth;

verses.name: Colossians 1:7
verses.text: even as you learned of Epaphras our beloved fellow servant, who is a faithful servant of Christ on our behalf,

verses.name: Colossians 1:8
verses.text: who also declared to us your love in the Spirit.

verses.name: Colossians 1:9
verses.text: For this cause, we also, since the day we heard this, don’t cease praying and making requests for you, that you may be filled with the knowledge of his will in all spiritual wisdom and understanding,

verses.name: Colossians 1:10
verses.text: that you may walk worthily of the Lord, to please him in all respects, bearing fruit in every good work, and increasing in the knowledge of God;

verses.name: Colossians 1:11
verses.text: strengthened with all power, according to the might of his glory, for all endurance and perseverance with joy;

verses.name: Colossians 1:12
verses.text: giving thanks to the Father, who made us fit to be partakers of the inheritance of the saints in light;

verses.name: Colossians 1:13
verses.text: who delivered us out of the power of darkness, and translated us into the Kingdom of the Son of his love;

verses.name: Colossians 1:14
verses.text: in whom we have our redemption, the forgiveness of our sins;

verses.name: Colossians 1:15
verses.text: who is the image of the invisible God, the firstborn of all creation.

verses.name: Colossians 1:16
verses.text: For by him all things were created, in the heavens and on the earth, things visible and things invisible, whether thrones or dominions or principalities or powers; all things have been created through him, and for him.

verses.name: Colossians 1:17
verses.text: He is before all things, and in him all things are held together.

verses.name: Colossians 1:18
verses.text: He is the head of the body, the assembly, who is the beginning, the firstborn from the dead; that in all things he might have the preeminence.

verses.name: Colossians 1:19
verses.text: For all the fullness was pleased to dwell in him;

verses.name: Colossians 1:20
verses.text: and through him to reconcile all things to himself, by him, whether things on the earth, or things in the heavens, having made peace through the blood of his cross.

verses.name: Colossians 1:21
verses.text: You, being in past times alienated and enemies in your mind in your evil deeds,

verses.name: Colossians 1:22
verses.text: yet now he has reconciled in the body of his flesh through death, to present you holy and without defect and blameless before him,

verses.name: Colossians 1:23
verses.text: if it is so that you continue in the faith, grounded and steadfast, and not moved away from the hope of the Good News which you heard, which is being proclaimed in all creation under heaven; of which I, Paul, was made a servant.

verses.name: Colossians 1:24
verses.text: Now I rejoice in my sufferings for your sake, and fill up on my part that which is lacking of the afflictions of Christ in my flesh for his body’s sake, which is the assembly;

verses.name: Colossians 1:25
verses.text: of which I was made a servant, according to the stewardship of God which was given me toward you, to fulfill the word of God,

verses.name: Colossians 1:26
verses.text: the mystery which has been hidden for ages and generations. But now it has been revealed to his saints,

verses.name: Colossians 1:27
verses.text: to whom God was pleased to make known what are the riches of the glory of this mystery among the Gentiles, which is Christ in you, the hope of glory;

verses.name: Colossians 1:28
verses.text: whom we proclaim, admonishing every man and teaching every man in all wisdom, that we may present every man perfect in Christ Jesus;

verses.name: Colossians 1:29
verses.text: for which I also labor, striving according to his working, which works in me mightily.

verses.name: Colossians 2:1
verses.text: For I desire to have you know how greatly I struggle for you, and for those at Laodicea, and for as many as have not seen my face in the flesh;

verses.name: Colossians 2:2
verses.text: that their hearts may be comforted, they being knit together in love, and gaining all riches of the full assurance of understanding, that they may know the mystery of God, both of the Father and of Christ,

verses.name: Colossians 2:3
verses.text: in whom are all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge hidden.

verses.name: Colossians 2:4
verses.text: Now this I say that no one may delude you with persuasiveness of speech.

verses.name: Colossians 2:5
verses.text: For though I am absent in the flesh, yet am I with you in the spirit, rejoicing and seeing your order, and the steadfastness of your faith in Christ.

verses.name: Colossians 2:6
verses.text: As therefore you received Christ Jesus, the Lord, walk in him,

verses.name: Colossians 2:7
verses.text: rooted and built up in him, and established in the faith, even as you were taught, abounding in it in thanksgiving.

verses.name: Colossians 2:8
verses.text: Be careful that you don’t let anyone rob you through his philosophy and vain deceit, after the tradition of men, after the elements of the world, and not after Christ.

verses.name: Colossians 2:9
verses.text: For in him all the fullness of the Deity dwells bodily,

verses.name: Colossians 2:10
verses.text: and in him you are made full, who is the head of all principality and power;

verses.name: Colossians 2:11
verses.text: in whom you were also circumcised with a circumcision not made with hands, in the putting off of the body of the sins of the flesh, in the circumcision of Christ;

verses.name: Colossians 2:12
verses.text: having been buried with him in baptism, in which you were also raised with him through faith in the working of God, who raised him from the dead.

verses.name: Colossians 2:13
verses.text: You were dead through your trespasses and the uncircumcision of your flesh. He made you alive together with him, having forgiven us all our trespasses,

verses.name: Colossians 2:14
verses.text: wiping out the handwriting in ordinances which was against us; and he has taken it out of the way, nailing it to the cross;

verses.name: Colossians 2:15
verses.text: having stripped the principalities and the powers, he made a show of them openly, triumphing over them in it.

verses.name: Colossians 2:16
verses.text: Let no one therefore judge you in eating, or in drinking, or with respect to a feast day or a new moon or a Sabbath day,

verses.name: Colossians 2:17
verses.text: which are a shadow of the things to come; but the body is Christ’s.

verses.name: Colossians 2:18
verses.text: Let no one rob you of your prize by a voluntary humility and worshiping of the angels, dwelling in the things which he has not seen, vainly puffed up by his fleshly mind,

verses.name: Colossians 2:19
verses.text: and not holding firmly to the Head, from whom all the body, being supplied and knit together through the joints and ligaments, grows with God’s growth.

verses.name: Colossians 2:20
verses.text: If you died with Christ from the elements of the world, why, as though living in the world, do you subject yourselves to ordinances,

verses.name: Colossians 2:21
verses.text: “Don’t handle, nor taste, nor touch”

verses.name: Colossians 2:22
verses.text: (all of which perish with use), according to the precepts and doctrines of men?

verses.name: Colossians 2:23
verses.text: Which things indeed appear like wisdom in self-imposed worship, and humility, and severity to the body; but aren’t of any value against the indulgence of the flesh.

verses.name: Colossians 3:1
verses.text: If then you were raised together with Christ, seek the things that are above, where Christ is, seated on the right hand of God.

verses.name: Colossians 3:2
verses.text: Set your mind on the things that are above, not on the things that are on the earth.

verses.name: Colossians 3:3
verses.text: For you died, and your life is hidden with Christ in God.

verses.name: Colossians 3:4
verses.text: When Christ, our life, is revealed, then you will also be revealed with him in glory.

verses.name: Colossians 3:5
verses.text: Put to death therefore your members which are on the earth: sexual immorality, uncleanness, depraved passion, evil desire, and covetousness, which is idolatry;

verses.name: Colossians 3:6
verses.text: for which things’ sake the wrath of God comes on the children of disobedience.

verses.name: Colossians 3:7
verses.text: You also once walked in those, when you lived in them;

verses.name: Colossians 3:8
verses.text: but now you also put them all away: anger, wrath, malice, slander, and shameful speaking out of your mouth.

verses.name: Colossians 3:9
verses.text: Don’t lie to one another, seeing that you have put off the old man with his doings,

verses.name: Colossians 3:10
verses.text: and have put on the new man, who is being renewed in knowledge after the image of his Creator,

verses.name: Colossians 3:11
verses.text: where there can’t be Greek and Jew, circumcision and uncircumcision, barbarian, Scythian, bondservant, freeman; but Christ is all, and in all.

verses.name: Colossians 3:12
verses.text: Put on therefore, as God’s chosen ones, holy and beloved, a heart of compassion, kindness, lowliness, humility, and perseverance;

verses.name: Colossians 3:13
verses.text: bearing with one another, and forgiving each other, if any man has a complaint against any; even as Christ forgave you, so you also do.

verses.name: Colossians 3:14
verses.text: Above all these things, walk in love, which is the bond of perfection.

verses.name: Colossians 3:15
verses.text: And let the peace of God rule in your hearts, to which also you were called in one body; and be thankful.

verses.name: Colossians 3:16
verses.text: Let the word of Christ dwell in you richly; in all wisdom teaching and admonishing one another with psalms, hymns, and spiritual songs, singing with grace in your heart to the Lord.

verses.name: Colossians 3:17
verses.text: Whatever you do, in word or in deed, do all in the name of the Lord Jesus, giving thanks to God the Father, through him.

verses.name: Colossians 3:18
verses.text: Wives, be in subjection to your husbands, as is fitting in the Lord.

verses.name: Colossians 3:19
verses.text: Husbands, love your wives, and don’t be bitter against them.

verses.name: Colossians 3:20
verses.text: Children, obey your parents in all things, for this pleases the Lord.

verses.name: Colossians 3:21
verses.text: Fathers, don’t provoke your children, so that they won’t be discouraged.

verses.name: Colossians 3:22
verses.text: Servants, obey in all things those who are your masters according to the flesh, not just when they are looking, as men pleasers, but in singleness of heart, fearing God.

verses.name: Colossians 3:23
verses.text: And whatever you do, work heartily, as for the Lord, and not for men,

verses.name: Colossians 3:24
verses.text: knowing that from the Lord you will receive the reward of the inheritance; for you serve the Lord Christ.

verses.name: Colossians 3:25
verses.text: But he who does wrong will receive again for the wrong that he has done, and there is no partiality.

verses.name: Colossians 4:1
verses.text: Masters, give to your servants that which is just and equal, knowing that you also have a Master in heaven.

verses.name: Colossians 4:2
verses.text: Continue steadfastly in prayer, watching therein with thanksgiving;

verses.name: Colossians 4:3
verses.text: praying together for us also, that God may open to us a door for the word, to speak the mystery of Christ, for which I am also in bonds;

verses.name: Colossians 4:4
verses.text: that I may reveal it as I ought to speak.

verses.name: Colossians 4:5
verses.text: Walk in wisdom toward those who are outside, redeeming the time.

verses.name: Colossians 4:6
verses.text: Let your speech always be with grace, seasoned with salt, that you may know how you ought to answer each one.

verses.name: Colossians 4:7
verses.text: All my affairs will be made known to you by Tychicus, the beloved brother, faithful servant, and fellow bondservant in the Lord.

verses.name: Colossians 4:8
verses.text: I am sending him to you for this very purpose, that he may know your circumstances and comfort your hearts,

verses.name: Colossians 4:9
verses.text: together with Onesimus, the faithful and beloved brother, who is one of you. They will make known to you everything that is going on here.

verses.name: Colossians 4:10
verses.text: Aristarchus, my fellow prisoner, greets you, and Mark, the cousin of Barnabas (concerning whom you received commandments, “if he comes to you, receive him”),

verses.name: Colossians 4:11
verses.text: and Jesus who is called Justus. These are my only fellow workers for God’s Kingdom who are of the circumcision, men who have been a comfort to me.

verses.name: Colossians 4:12
verses.text: Epaphras, who is one of you, a servant of Christ, salutes you, always striving for you in his prayers, that you may stand perfect and complete in all the will of God.

verses.name: Colossians 4:13
verses.text: For I testify about him, that he has great zeal for you, and for those in Laodicea, and for those in Hierapolis.

verses.name: Colossians 4:14
verses.text: Luke, the beloved physician, and Demas greet you.

verses.name: Colossians 4:15
verses.text: Greet the brothers who are in Laodicea, and Nymphas, and the assembly that is in his house.

verses.name: Colossians 4:16
verses.text: When this letter has been read among you, cause it to be read also in the assembly of the Laodiceans; and that you also read the letter from Laodicea.

verses.name: Colossians 4:17
verses.text: Tell Archippus, “Take heed to the ministry which you have received in the Lord, that you fulfill it.”

verses.name: Colossians 4:18
verses.text: The salutation of me, Paul, with my own hand: remember my bonds. Grace be with you. Amen.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 1:1
verses.text: Paul, Silvanus, and Timothy, to the assembly of the Thessalonians in God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 1:2
verses.text: We always give thanks to God for all of you, mentioning you in our prayers,

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 1:3
verses.text: remembering without ceasing your work of faith and labor of love and perseverance of hope in our Lord Jesus Christ, before our God and Father.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 1:4
verses.text: We know, brothers loved by God, that you are chosen,

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 1:5
verses.text: and that our Good News came to you not in word only, but also in power, and in the Holy Spirit, and with much assurance. You know what kind of men we showed ourselves to be among you for your sake.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 1:6
verses.text: You became imitators of us, and of the Lord, having received the word in much affliction, with joy of the Holy Spirit,

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 1:7
verses.text: so that you became an example to all who believe in Macedonia and in Achaia.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 1:8
verses.text: For from you the word of the Lord has been declared, not only in Macedonia and Achaia, but also in every place your faith toward God has gone out; so that we need not to say anything.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 1:9
verses.text: For they themselves report concerning us what kind of a reception we had from you; and how you turned to God from idols, to serve a living and true God,

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 1:10
verses.text: and to wait for his Son from heaven, whom he raised from the dead—Jesus, who delivers us from the wrath to come.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 2:1
verses.text: For you yourselves know, brothers, our visit to you wasn’t in vain,

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 2:2
verses.text: but having suffered before and been shamefully treated, as you know, at Philippi, we grew bold in our God to tell you the Good News of God in much conflict.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 2:3
verses.text: For our exhortation is not of error, nor of uncleanness, nor in deception.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 2:4
verses.text: But even as we have been approved by God to be entrusted with the Good News, so we speak; not as pleasing men, but God, who tests our hearts.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 2:5
verses.text: For neither were we at any time found using words of flattery, as you know, nor a cloak of covetousness (God is witness),

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 2:6
verses.text: nor seeking glory from men (neither from you nor from others), when we might have claimed authority as apostles of Christ.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 2:7
verses.text: But we were gentle among you, like a nursing mother cherishes her own children.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 2:8
verses.text: Even so, affectionately longing for you, we were well pleased to impart to you, not the Good News of God only, but also our own souls, because you had become very dear to us.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 2:9
verses.text: For you remember, brothers, our labor and travail; for working night and day, that we might not burden any of you, we preached to you the Good News of God.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 2:10
verses.text: You are witnesses with God, how holy, righteously, and blamelessly we behaved ourselves toward you who believe.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 2:11
verses.text: As you know, we exhorted, comforted, and implored every one of you, as a father does his own children,

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 2:12
verses.text: to the end that you should walk worthily of God, who calls you into his own Kingdom and glory.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 2:13
verses.text: For this cause we also thank God without ceasing, that, when you received from us the word of the message of God, you accepted it not as the word of men, but, as it is in truth, the word of God, which also works in you who believe.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 2:14
verses.text: For you, brothers, became imitators of the assemblies of God which are in Judea in Christ Jesus; for you also suffered the same things from your own countrymen, even as they did from the Jews;

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 2:15
verses.text: who killed both the Lord Jesus and their own prophets, and drove us out, and didn’t please God, and are contrary to all men;

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 2:16
verses.text: forbidding us to speak to the Gentiles that they may be saved; to fill up their sins always. But wrath has come on them to the uttermost.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 2:17
verses.text: But we, brothers, being bereaved of you for a short season, in presence, not in heart, tried even harder to see your face with great desire,

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 2:18
verses.text: because we wanted to come to you—indeed, I, Paul, once and again—but Satan hindered us.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 2:19
verses.text: For what is our hope, or joy, or crown of rejoicing? Isn’t it even you, before our Lord Jesus at his coming?

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 2:20
verses.text: For you are our glory and our joy.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 3:1
verses.text: Therefore when we couldn’t stand it any longer, we thought it good to be left behind at Athens alone,

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 3:2
verses.text: and sent Timothy, our brother and God’s servant in the Good News of Christ, to establish you, and to comfort you concerning your faith;

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 3:3
verses.text: that no one be moved by these afflictions. For you know that we are appointed to this task.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 3:4
verses.text: For most certainly, when we were with you, we told you beforehand that we are to suffer affliction, even as it happened, and you know.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 3:5
verses.text: For this cause I also, when I couldn’t stand it any longer, sent that I might know your faith, for fear that by any means the tempter had tempted you, and our labor would have been in vain.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 3:6
verses.text: But when Timothy came just now to us from you, and brought us glad news of your faith and love, and that you have good memories of us always, longing to see us, even as we also long to see you;

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 3:7
verses.text: for this cause, brothers, we were comforted over you in all our distress and affliction through your faith.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 3:8
verses.text: For now we live, if you stand fast in the Lord.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 3:9
verses.text: For what thanksgiving can we render again to God for you, for all the joy with which we rejoice for your sakes before our God;

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 3:10
verses.text: night and day praying exceedingly that we may see your face, and may perfect that which is lacking in your faith?

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 3:11
verses.text: Now may our God and Father himself, and our Lord Jesus Christ, direct our way to you;

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 3:12
verses.text: and the Lord make you to increase and abound in love one toward another, and toward all men, even as we also do toward you,

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 3:13
verses.text: to the end he may establish your hearts blameless in holiness before our God and Father, at the coming of our Lord Jesus with all his saints.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 4:1
verses.text: Finally then, brothers, we beg and exhort you in the Lord Jesus, that as you received from us how you ought to walk and to please God, that you abound more and more.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 4:2
verses.text: For you know what instructions we gave you through the Lord Jesus.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 4:3
verses.text: For this is the will of God: your sanctification, that you abstain from sexual immorality,

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 4:4
verses.text: that each one of you know how to control his own body in sanctification and honor,

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 4:5
verses.text: not in the passion of lust, even as the Gentiles who don’t know God;

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 4:6
verses.text: that no one should take advantage of and wrong a brother or sister in this matter; because the Lord is an avenger in all these things, as also we forewarned you and testified.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 4:7
verses.text: For God called us not for uncleanness, but in sanctification.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 4:8
verses.text: Therefore he who rejects this doesn’t reject man, but God, who has also given his Holy Spirit to you.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 4:9
verses.text: But concerning brotherly love, you have no need that one write to you. For you yourselves are taught by God to love one another,

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 4:10
verses.text: for indeed you do it toward all the brothers who are in all Macedonia. But we exhort you, brothers, that you abound more and more;

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 4:11
verses.text: and that you make it your ambition to lead a quiet life, and to do your own business, and to work with your own hands, even as we instructed you;

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 4:12
verses.text: that you may walk properly toward those who are outside, and may have need of nothing.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 4:13
verses.text: But we don’t want you to be ignorant, brothers, concerning those who have fallen asleep, so that you don’t grieve like the rest, who have no hope.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 4:14
verses.text: For if we believe that Jesus died and rose again, even so God will bring with him those who have fallen asleep in Jesus.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 4:15
verses.text: For this we tell you by the word of the Lord, that we who are alive, who are left until the coming of the Lord, will in no way precede those who have fallen asleep.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 4:16
verses.text: For the Lord himself will descend from heaven with a shout, with the voice of the archangel, and with God’s trumpet. The dead in Christ will rise first,

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 4:17
verses.text: then we who are alive, who are left, will be caught up together with them in the clouds, to meet the Lord in the air. So we will be with the Lord forever.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 4:18
verses.text: Therefore comfort one another with these words.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 5:1
verses.text: But concerning the times and the seasons, brothers, you have no need that anything be written to you.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 5:2
verses.text: For you yourselves know well that the day of the Lord comes like a thief in the night.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 5:3
verses.text: For when they are saying, “Peace and safety,” then sudden destruction will come on them, like birth pains on a pregnant woman; and they will in no way escape.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 5:4
verses.text: But you, brothers, aren’t in darkness, that the day should overtake you like a thief.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 5:5
verses.text: You are all children of light, and children of the day. We don’t belong to the night, nor to darkness,

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 5:6
verses.text: so then let’s not sleep, as the rest do, but let’s watch and be sober.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 5:7
verses.text: For those who sleep, sleep in the night; and those who are drunk are drunk in the night.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 5:8
verses.text: But let us, since we belong to the day, be sober, putting on the breastplate of faith and love, and, for a helmet, the hope of salvation.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 5:9
verses.text: For God didn’t appoint us to wrath, but to the obtaining of salvation through our Lord Jesus Christ,

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 5:10
verses.text: who died for us, that, whether we wake or sleep, we should live together with him.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 5:11
verses.text: Therefore exhort one another, and build each other up, even as you also do.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 5:12
verses.text: But we beg you, brothers, to know those who labor among you, and are over you in the Lord, and admonish you,

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 5:13
verses.text: and to respect and honor them in love for their work’s sake. Be at peace among yourselves.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 5:14
verses.text: We exhort you, brothers, admonish the disorderly, encourage the faint-hearted, support the weak, be patient toward all.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 5:15
verses.text: See that no one returns evil for evil to anyone, but always follow after that which is good, for one another, and for all.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 5:16
verses.text: Rejoice always.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 5:17
verses.text: Pray without ceasing.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 5:18
verses.text: In everything give thanks, for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus toward you.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 5:19
verses.text: Don’t quench the Spirit.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 5:20
verses.text: Don’t despise prophesies.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 5:21
verses.text: Test all things, and hold firmly that which is good.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 5:22
verses.text: Abstain from every form of evil.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 5:23
verses.text: May the God of peace himself sanctify you completely. May your whole spirit, soul, and body be preserved blameless at the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 5:24
verses.text: He who calls you is faithful, who will also do it.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 5:25
verses.text: Brothers, pray for us.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 5:26
verses.text: Greet all the brothers with a holy kiss.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 5:27
verses.text: I solemnly command you by the Lord that this letter be read to all the holy brothers.

verses.name: 1 Thessalonians 5:28
verses.text: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you. Amen.

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 1:1
verses.text: Paul, Silvanus, and Timothy, to the assembly of the Thessalonians in God our Father, and the Lord Jesus Christ:

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 1:2
verses.text: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 1:3
verses.text: We are bound to always give thanks to God for you, brothers, even as it is appropriate, because your faith grows exceedingly, and the love of each and every one of you towards one another abounds;

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 1:4
verses.text: so that we ourselves boast about you in the assemblies of God for your perseverance and faith in all your persecutions and in the afflictions which you endure.

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 1:5
verses.text: This is an obvious sign of the righteous judgment of God, to the end that you may be counted worthy of God’s Kingdom, for which you also suffer.

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 1:6
verses.text: Since it is a righteous thing with God to repay affliction to those who afflict you,

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 1:7
verses.text: and to give relief to you who are afflicted with us, when the Lord Jesus is revealed from heaven with his mighty angels in flaming fire,

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 1:8
verses.text: punishing those who don’t know God, and to those who don’t obey the Good News of our Lord Jesus,

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 1:9
verses.text: who will pay the penalty: eternal destruction from the face of the Lord and from the glory of his might,

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 1:10
verses.text: when he comes in that day to be glorified in his saints, and to be admired among all those who have believed, because our testimony to you was believed.

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 1:11
verses.text: To this end we also pray always for you, that our God may count you worthy of your calling, and fulfill every desire of goodness and work of faith, with power;

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 1:12
verses.text: that the name of our Lord Jesus may be glorified in you, and you in him, according to the grace of our God and the Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 2:1
verses.text: Now, brothers, concerning the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ, and our gathering together to him, we ask you

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 2:2
verses.text: not to be quickly shaken in your mind, and not be troubled, either by spirit, or by word, or by letter as if from us, saying that the day of Christ has already come.

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 2:3
verses.text: Let no one deceive you in any way. For it will not be, unless the rebellion comes first, and the man of sin is revealed, the son of destruction,

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 2:4
verses.text: he who opposes and exalts himself against all that is called God or that is worshiped; so that he sits as God in the temple of God, setting himself up as God.

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 2:5
verses.text: Don’t you remember that, when I was still with you, I told you these things?

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 2:6
verses.text: Now you know what is restraining him, to the end that he may be revealed in his own season.

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 2:7
verses.text: For the mystery of lawlessness already works. Only there is one who restrains now, until he is taken out of the way.

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 2:8
verses.text: Then the lawless one will be revealed, whom the Lord will kill with the breath of his mouth, and destroy by the manifestation of his coming;

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 2:9
verses.text: even he whose coming is according to the working of Satan with all power and signs and lying wonders,

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 2:10
verses.text: and with all deception of wickedness for those who are being lost, because they didn’t receive the love of the truth, that they might be saved.

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 2:11
verses.text: Because of this, God sends them a working of error, that they should believe a lie;

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 2:12
verses.text: that they all might be judged who didn’t believe the truth, but had pleasure in unrighteousness.

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 2:13
verses.text: But we are bound to always give thanks to God for you, brothers loved by the Lord, because God chose you from the beginning for salvation through sanctification of the Spirit and belief in the truth;

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 2:14
verses.text: to which he called you through our Good News, for the obtaining of the glory of our Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 2:15
verses.text: So then, brothers, stand firm, and hold the traditions which you were taught by us, whether by word, or by letter.

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 2:16
verses.text: Now our Lord Jesus Christ himself, and God our Father, who loved us and gave us eternal comfort and good hope through grace,

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 2:17
verses.text: comfort your hearts and establish you in every good work and word.

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 3:1
verses.text: Finally, brothers, pray for us, that the word of the Lord may spread rapidly and be glorified, even as also with you;

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 3:2
verses.text: and that we may be delivered from unreasonable and evil men; for not all have faith.

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 3:3
verses.text: But the Lord is faithful, who will establish you, and guard you from the evil one.

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 3:4
verses.text: We have confidence in the Lord concerning you, that you both do and will do the things we command.

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 3:5
verses.text: May the Lord direct your hearts into God’s love, and into the perseverance of Christ.

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 3:6
verses.text: Now we command you, brothers, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, that you withdraw yourselves from every brother who walks in rebellion, and not after the tradition which they received from us.

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 3:7
verses.text: For you know how you ought to imitate us. For we didn’t behave ourselves rebelliously among you,

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 3:8
verses.text: neither did we eat bread from anyone’s hand without paying for it, but in labor and travail worked night and day, that we might not burden any of you;

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 3:9
verses.text: not because we don’t have the right, but to make ourselves an example to you, that you should imitate us.

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 3:10
verses.text: For even when we were with you, we commanded you this: “If anyone will not work, don’t let him eat.”

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 3:11
verses.text: For we hear of some who walk among you in rebellion, who don’t work at all, but are busybodies.

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 3:12
verses.text: Now those who are that way, we command and exhort in the Lord Jesus Christ, that with quietness they work, and eat their own bread.

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 3:13
verses.text: But you, brothers, don’t be weary in doing what is right.

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 3:14
verses.text: If any man doesn’t obey our word in this letter, note that man, that you have no company with him, to the end that he may be ashamed.

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 3:15
verses.text: Don’t count him as an enemy, but admonish him as a brother.

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 3:16
verses.text: Now may the Lord of peace himself give you peace at all times in all ways. The Lord be with you all.

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 3:17
verses.text: The greeting of me, Paul, with my own hand, which is the sign in every letter: this is how I write.

verses.name: 2 Thessalonians 3:18
verses.text: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. Amen.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 1:1
verses.text: Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ according to the commandment of God our Savior and the Lord Jesus Christ our hope;

verses.name: 1 Timothy 1:2
verses.text: to Timothy, my true child in faith: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God our Father and Christ Jesus our Lord.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 1:3
verses.text: As I urged you when I was going into Macedonia, stay at Ephesus that you might command certain men not to teach a different doctrine,

verses.name: 1 Timothy 1:4
verses.text: and not to pay attention to myths and endless genealogies, which cause disputes, rather than God’s stewardship, which is in faith—

verses.name: 1 Timothy 1:5
verses.text: but the goal of this command is love, out of a pure heart and a good conscience and sincere faith;

verses.name: 1 Timothy 1:6
verses.text: from which things some, having missed the mark, have turned away to vain talking;

verses.name: 1 Timothy 1:7
verses.text: desiring to be teachers of the law, though they understand neither what they say, nor about what they strongly affirm.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 1:8
verses.text: But we know that the law is good, if a person uses it lawfully,

verses.name: 1 Timothy 1:9
verses.text: as knowing this, that law is not made for a righteous person, but for the lawless and insubordinate, for the ungodly and sinners, for the unholy and profane, for murderers of fathers and murderers of mothers, for manslayers,

verses.name: 1 Timothy 1:10
verses.text: for the sexually immoral, for homosexuals, for slave-traders, for liars, for perjurers, and for any other thing contrary to the sound doctrine;

verses.name: 1 Timothy 1:11
verses.text: according to the Good News of the glory of the blessed God, which was committed to my trust.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 1:12
verses.text: And I thank him who enabled me, Christ Jesus our Lord, because he counted me faithful, appointing me to service;

verses.name: 1 Timothy 1:13
verses.text: although I was before a blasphemer, a persecutor, and insolent. However, I obtained mercy, because I did it ignorantly in unbelief.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 1:14
verses.text: The grace of our Lord abounded exceedingly with faith and love which is in Christ Jesus.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 1:15
verses.text: The saying is faithful and worthy of all acceptance, that Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners; of whom I am chief.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 1:16
verses.text: However, for this cause I obtained mercy, that in me first, Jesus Christ might display all his patience, for an example of those who were going to believe in him for eternal life.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 1:17
verses.text: Now to the King eternal, immortal, invisible, to God who alone is wise, be honor and glory forever and ever. Amen.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 1:18
verses.text: This instruction I commit to you, my child Timothy, according to the prophecies which were given to you before, that by them you may wage the good warfare;

verses.name: 1 Timothy 1:19
verses.text: holding faith and a good conscience; which some having thrust away made a shipwreck concerning the faith;

verses.name: 1 Timothy 1:20
verses.text: of whom are Hymenaeus and Alexander; whom I delivered to Satan, that they might be taught not to blaspheme.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 2:1
verses.text: I exhort therefore, first of all, that petitions, prayers, intercessions, and givings of thanks, be made for all men:

verses.name: 1 Timothy 2:2
verses.text: for kings and all who are in high places; that we may lead a tranquil and quiet life in all godliness and reverence.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 2:3
verses.text: For this is good and acceptable in the sight of God our Savior;

verses.name: 1 Timothy 2:4
verses.text: who desires all people to be saved and come to full knowledge of the truth.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 2:5
verses.text: For there is one God, and one mediator between God and men, the man Christ Jesus,

verses.name: 1 Timothy 2:6
verses.text: who gave himself as a ransom for all; the testimony in its own times;

verses.name: 1 Timothy 2:7
verses.text: to which I was appointed a preacher and an apostle (I am telling the truth in Christ, not lying), a teacher of the Gentiles in faith and truth.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 2:8
verses.text: I desire therefore that the men in every place pray, lifting up holy hands without anger and doubting.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 2:9
verses.text: In the same way, that women also adorn themselves in decent clothing, with modesty and propriety; not just with braided hair, gold, pearls, or expensive clothing;

verses.name: 1 Timothy 2:10
verses.text: but (which becomes women professing godliness) with good works.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 2:11
verses.text: Let a woman learn in quietness with full submission.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 2:12
verses.text: But I don’t permit a woman to teach, nor to exercise authority over a man, but to be in quietness.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 2:13
verses.text: For Adam was first formed, then Eve.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 2:14
verses.text: Adam wasn’t deceived, but the woman, being deceived, has fallen into disobedience;

verses.name: 1 Timothy 2:15
verses.text: but she will be saved through her childbearing, if they continue in faith, love, and sanctification with sobriety.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 3:1
verses.text: This is a faithful saying: if a man seeks the office of an overseer, he desires a good work.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 3:2
verses.text: The overseer therefore must be without reproach, the husband of one wife, temperate, sensible, modest, hospitable, good at teaching;

verses.name: 1 Timothy 3:3
verses.text: not a drinker, not violent, not greedy for money, but gentle, not quarrelsome, not covetous;

verses.name: 1 Timothy 3:4
verses.text: one who rules his own house well, having children in subjection with all reverence;

verses.name: 1 Timothy 3:5
verses.text: (but if a man doesn’t know how to rule his own house, how will he take care of the assembly of God?)

verses.name: 1 Timothy 3:6
verses.text: not a new convert, lest being puffed up he fall into the same condemnation as the devil.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 3:7
verses.text: Moreover he must have good testimony from those who are outside, to avoid falling into reproach and the snare of the devil.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 3:8
verses.text: Servants, in the same way, must be reverent, not double-tongued, not addicted to much wine, not greedy for money;

verses.name: 1 Timothy 3:9
verses.text: holding the mystery of the faith in a pure conscience.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 3:10
verses.text: Let them also first be tested; then let them serve if they are blameless.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 3:11
verses.text: Their wives in the same way must be reverent, not slanderers, temperate, faithful in all things.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 3:12
verses.text: Let servants be husbands of one wife, ruling their children and their own houses well.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 3:13
verses.text: For those who have served well gain for themselves a good standing, and great boldness in the faith which is in Christ Jesus.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 3:14
verses.text: These things I write to you, hoping to come to you shortly;

verses.name: 1 Timothy 3:15
verses.text: but if I wait long, that you may know how men ought to behave themselves in God’s house, which is the assembly of the living God, the pillar and ground of the truth.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 3:16
verses.text: Without controversy, the mystery of godliness is great: God was revealed in the flesh, justified in the spirit, seen by angels, preached among the nations, believed on in the world, and received up in glory.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 4:1
verses.text: But the Spirit says expressly that in later times some will fall away from the faith, paying attention to seducing spirits and doctrines of demons,

verses.name: 1 Timothy 4:2
verses.text: through the hypocrisy of men who speak lies, branded in their own conscience as with a hot iron;

verses.name: 1 Timothy 4:3
verses.text: forbidding marriage and commanding to abstain from foods which God created to be received with thanksgiving by those who believe and know the truth.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 4:4
verses.text: For every creature of God is good, and nothing is to be rejected, if it is received with thanksgiving.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 4:5
verses.text: For it is sanctified through the word of God and prayer.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 4:6
verses.text: If you instruct the brothers of these things, you will be a good servant of Christ Jesus, nourished in the words of the faith, and of the good doctrine which you have followed.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 4:7
verses.text: But refuse profane and old wives’ fables. Exercise yourself toward godliness.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 4:8
verses.text: For bodily exercise has some value, but godliness has value in all things, having the promise of the life which is now, and of that which is to come.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 4:9
verses.text: This saying is faithful and worthy of all acceptance.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 4:10
verses.text: For to this end we both labor and suffer reproach, because we have set our trust in the living God, who is the Savior of all men, especially of those who believe.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 4:11
verses.text: Command and teach these things.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 4:12
verses.text: Let no man despise your youth; but be an example to those who believe, in word, in your way of life, in love, in spirit, in faith, and in purity.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 4:13
verses.text: Until I come, pay attention to reading, to exhortation, and to teaching.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 4:14
verses.text: Don’t neglect the gift that is in you, which was given to you by prophecy, with the laying on of the hands of the elders.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 4:15
verses.text: Be diligent in these things. Give yourself wholly to them, that your progress may be revealed to all.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 4:16
verses.text: Pay attention to yourself, and to your teaching. Continue in these things, for in doing this you will save both yourself and those who hear you.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 5:1
verses.text: Don’t rebuke an older man, but exhort him as a father; the younger men as brothers;

verses.name: 1 Timothy 5:2
verses.text: the elder women as mothers; the younger as sisters, in all purity.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 5:3
verses.text: Honor widows who are widows indeed.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 5:4
verses.text: But if any widow has children or grandchildren, let them learn first to show piety towards their own family, and to repay their parents, for this is acceptable in the sight of God.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 5:5
verses.text: Now she who is a widow indeed, and desolate, has her hope set on God, and continues in petitions and prayers night and day.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 5:6
verses.text: But she who gives herself to pleasure is dead while she lives.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 5:7
verses.text: Also command these things, that they may be without reproach.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 5:8
verses.text: But if anyone doesn’t provide for his own, and especially his own household, he has denied the faith, and is worse than an unbeliever.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 5:9
verses.text: Let no one be enrolled as a widow under sixty years old, having been the wife of one man,

verses.name: 1 Timothy 5:10
verses.text: being approved by good works, if she has brought up children, if she has been hospitable to strangers, if she has washed the saints’ feet, if she has relieved the afflicted, and if she has diligently followed every good work.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 5:11
verses.text: But refuse younger widows, for when they have grown wanton against Christ, they desire to marry;

verses.name: 1 Timothy 5:12
verses.text: having condemnation, because they have rejected their first pledge.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 5:13
verses.text: Besides, they also learn to be idle, going about from house to house. Not only idle, but also gossips and busybodies, saying things which they ought not.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 5:14
verses.text: I desire therefore that the younger widows marry, bear children, rule the household, and give no occasion to the adversary for insulting.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 5:15
verses.text: For already some have turned away after Satan.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 5:16
verses.text: If any man or woman who believes has widows, let them relieve them, and don’t let the assembly be burdened; that it might relieve those who are widows indeed.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 5:17
verses.text: Let the elders who rule well be counted worthy of double honor, especially those who labor in the word and in teaching.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 5:18
verses.text: For the Scripture says, “You shall not muzzle the ox when it treads out the grain.” And, “The laborer is worthy of his wages.”

verses.name: 1 Timothy 5:19
verses.text: Don’t receive an accusation against an elder, except at the word of two or three witnesses.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 5:20
verses.text: Those who sin, reprove in the sight of all, that the rest also may be in fear.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 5:21
verses.text: I command you in the sight of God, and the Lord Jesus Christ, and the chosen angels, that you observe these things without prejudice, doing nothing by partiality.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 5:22
verses.text: Lay hands hastily on no one, neither be a participant in other men’s sins. Keep yourself pure.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 5:23
verses.text: Be no longer a drinker of water only, but use a little wine for your stomach’s sake and your frequent infirmities.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 5:24
verses.text: Some men’s sins are evident, preceding them to judgment, and some also follow later.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 5:25
verses.text: In the same way also there are good works that are obvious, and those that are otherwise can’t be hidden.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 6:1
verses.text: Let as many as are bondservants under the yoke count their own masters worthy of all honor, that the name of God and the doctrine not be blasphemed.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 6:2
verses.text: Those who have believing masters, let them not despise them, because they are brothers, but rather let them serve them, because those who partake of the benefit are believing and beloved. Teach and exhort these things.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 6:3
verses.text: If anyone teaches a different doctrine, and doesn’t consent to sound words, the words of our Lord Jesus Christ, and to the doctrine which is according to godliness,

verses.name: 1 Timothy 6:4
verses.text: he is conceited, knowing nothing, but obsessed with arguments, disputes, and word battles, from which come envy, strife, insulting, evil suspicions,

verses.name: 1 Timothy 6:5
verses.text: constant friction of people of corrupt minds and destitute of the truth, who suppose that godliness is a means of gain. Withdraw yourself from such.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 6:6
verses.text: But godliness with contentment is great gain.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 6:7
verses.text: For we brought nothing into the world, and we certainly can’t carry anything out.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 6:8
verses.text: But having food and clothing, we will be content with that.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 6:9
verses.text: But those who are determined to be rich fall into a temptation and a snare and many foolish and harmful lusts, such as drown men in ruin and destruction.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 6:10
verses.text: For the love of money is a root of all kinds of evil. Some have been led astray from the faith in their greed, and have pierced themselves through with many sorrows.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 6:11
verses.text: But you, man of God, flee these things, and follow after righteousness, godliness, faith, love, perseverance, and gentleness.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 6:12
verses.text: Fight the good fight of faith. Take hold of the eternal life to which you were called, and you confessed the good confession in the sight of many witnesses.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 6:13
verses.text: I command you before God, who gives life to all things, and before Christ Jesus, who before Pontius Pilate testified the good confession,

verses.name: 1 Timothy 6:14
verses.text: that you keep the commandment without spot, blameless, until the appearing of our Lord Jesus Christ;

verses.name: 1 Timothy 6:15
verses.text: which in its own times he will show, who is the blessed and only Ruler, the King of kings, and Lord of lords;

verses.name: 1 Timothy 6:16
verses.text: who alone has immortality, dwelling in unapproachable light; whom no man has seen, nor can see: to whom be honor and eternal power. Amen.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 6:17
verses.text: Charge those who are rich in this present world that they not be haughty, nor have their hope set on the uncertainty of riches, but on the living God, who richly provides us with everything to enjoy;

verses.name: 1 Timothy 6:18
verses.text: that they do good, that they be rich in good works, that they be ready to distribute, willing to share;

verses.name: 1 Timothy 6:19
verses.text: laying up in store for themselves a good foundation against the time to come, that they may lay hold of eternal life.

verses.name: 1 Timothy 6:20
verses.text: Timothy, guard that which is committed to you, turning away from the empty chatter and oppositions of what is falsely called knowledge;

verses.name: 1 Timothy 6:21
verses.text: which some profess, and thus have wandered from the faith. Grace be with you. Amen.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 1:1
verses.text: Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ through the will of God, according to the promise of the life which is in Christ Jesus,

verses.name: 2 Timothy 1:2
verses.text: to Timothy, my beloved child: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 1:3
verses.text: I thank God, whom I serve as my forefathers did, with a pure conscience. How unceasing is my memory of you in my petitions, night and day

verses.name: 2 Timothy 1:4
verses.text: longing to see you, remembering your tears, that I may be filled with joy;

verses.name: 2 Timothy 1:5
verses.text: having been reminded of the sincere faith that is in you; which lived first in your grandmother Lois, and your mother Eunice, and, I am persuaded, in you also.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 1:6
verses.text: For this cause, I remind you that you should stir up the gift of God which is in you through the laying on of my hands.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 1:7
verses.text: For God didn’t give us a spirit of fear, but of power, love, and self-control.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 1:8
verses.text: Therefore don’t be ashamed of the testimony of our Lord, nor of me his prisoner; but endure hardship for the Good News according to the power of God,

verses.name: 2 Timothy 1:9
verses.text: who saved us and called us with a holy calling, not according to our works, but according to his own purpose and grace, which was given to us in Christ Jesus before times eternal,

verses.name: 2 Timothy 1:10
verses.text: but has now been revealed by the appearing of our Savior, Christ Jesus, who abolished death, and brought life and immortality to light through the Good News.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 1:11
verses.text: For this, I was appointed as a preacher, an apostle, and a teacher of the Gentiles.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 1:12
verses.text: For this cause I also suffer these things. Yet I am not ashamed, for I know him whom I have believed, and I am persuaded that he is able to guard that which I have committed to him against that day.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 1:13
verses.text: Hold the pattern of sound words which you have heard from me, in faith and love which is in Christ Jesus.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 1:14
verses.text: That good thing which was committed to you, guard through the Holy Spirit who dwells in us.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 1:15
verses.text: This you know, that all who are in Asia turned away from me; of whom are Phygelus and Hermogenes.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 1:16
verses.text: May the Lord grant mercy to the house of Onesiphorus, for he often refreshed me, and was not ashamed of my chain,

verses.name: 2 Timothy 1:17
verses.text: but when he was in Rome, he sought me diligently, and found me

verses.name: 2 Timothy 1:18
verses.text: (the Lord grant to him to find the Lord’s mercy in that day); and in how many things he served at Ephesus, you know very well.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 2:1
verses.text: You therefore, my child, be strengthened in the grace that is in Christ Jesus.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 2:2
verses.text: The things which you have heard from me among many witnesses, commit the same to faithful men, who will be able to teach others also.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 2:3
verses.text: You therefore must endure hardship, as a good soldier of Christ Jesus.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 2:4
verses.text: No soldier on duty entangles himself in the affairs of life, that he may please him who enrolled him as a soldier.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 2:5
verses.text: Also, if anyone competes in athletics, he isn’t crowned unless he has competed by the rules.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 2:6
verses.text: The farmer who labors must be the first to get a share of the crops.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 2:7
verses.text: Consider what I say, and may the Lord give you understanding in all things.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 2:8
verses.text: Remember Jesus Christ, risen from the dead, of the offspring of David, according to my Good News,

verses.name: 2 Timothy 2:9
verses.text: in which I suffer hardship to the point of chains as a criminal. But God’s word isn’t chained.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 2:10
verses.text: Therefore I endure all things for the chosen ones’ sake, that they also may obtain the salvation which is in Christ Jesus with eternal glory.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 2:11
verses.text: This saying is trustworthy: “For if we died with him, we will also live with him.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 2:12
verses.text: If we endure, we will also reign with him. If we deny him, he also will deny us.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 2:13
verses.text: If we are faithless, he remains faithful. For he can’t deny himself.”

verses.name: 2 Timothy 2:14
verses.text: Remind them of these things, charging them in the sight of the Lord, that they don’t argue about words, to no profit, to the subverting of those who hear.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 2:15
verses.text: Give diligence to present yourself approved by God, a workman who doesn’t need to be ashamed, properly handling the Word of Truth.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 2:16
verses.text: But shun empty chatter, for it will go further in ungodliness,

verses.name: 2 Timothy 2:17
verses.text: and those words will consume like gangrene, of whom is Hymenaeus and Philetus;

verses.name: 2 Timothy 2:18
verses.text: men who have erred concerning the truth, saying that the resurrection is already past, and overthrowing the faith of some.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 2:19
verses.text: However God’s firm foundation stands, having this seal, “The Lord knows those who are his,” and, “Let every one who names the name of the Lord depart from unrighteousness.”

verses.name: 2 Timothy 2:20
verses.text: Now in a large house there are not only vessels of gold and of silver, but also of wood and of clay. Some are for honor, and some for dishonor.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 2:21
verses.text: If anyone therefore purges himself from these, he will be a vessel for honor, sanctified, and suitable for the master’s use, prepared for every good work.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 2:22
verses.text: Flee from youthful lusts; but pursue righteousness, faith, love, and peace with those who call on the Lord out of a pure heart.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 2:23
verses.text: But refuse foolish and ignorant questionings, knowing that they generate strife.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 2:24
verses.text: The Lord’s servant must not quarrel, but be gentle towards all, able to teach, patient,

verses.name: 2 Timothy 2:25
verses.text: in gentleness correcting those who oppose him: perhaps God may give them repentance leading to a full knowledge of the truth,

verses.name: 2 Timothy 2:26
verses.text: and they may recover themselves out of the devil’s snare, having been taken captive by him to his will.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 3:1
verses.text: But know this, that in the last days, grievous times will come.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 3:2
verses.text: For men will be lovers of self, lovers of money, boastful, arrogant, blasphemers, disobedient to parents, unthankful, unholy,

verses.name: 2 Timothy 3:3
verses.text: without natural affection, unforgiving, slanderers, without self-control, fierce, not lovers of good,

verses.name: 2 Timothy 3:4
verses.text: traitors, headstrong, conceited, lovers of pleasure rather than lovers of God;

verses.name: 2 Timothy 3:5
verses.text: holding a form of godliness, but having denied its power. Turn away from these, also.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 3:6
verses.text: For some of these are people who creep into houses, and take captive gullible women loaded down with sins, led away by various lusts,

verses.name: 2 Timothy 3:7
verses.text: always learning, and never able to come to the knowledge of the truth.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 3:8
verses.text: Even as Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses, so do these also oppose the truth; men corrupted in mind, who concerning the faith are rejected.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 3:9
verses.text: But they will proceed no further. For their folly will be evident to all men, as theirs also came to be.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 3:10
verses.text: But you did follow my teaching, conduct, purpose, faith, patience, love, steadfastness,

verses.name: 2 Timothy 3:11
verses.text: persecutions, and sufferings: those things that happened to me at Antioch, Iconium, and Lystra. I endured those persecutions. The Lord delivered me out of them all.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 3:12
verses.text: Yes, and all who desire to live godly in Christ Jesus will suffer persecution.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 3:13
verses.text: But evil men and impostors will grow worse and worse, deceiving and being deceived.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 3:14
verses.text: But you remain in the things which you have learned and have been assured of, knowing from whom you have learned them.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 3:15
verses.text: From infancy, you have known the holy Scriptures which are able to make you wise for salvation through faith, which is in Christ Jesus.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 3:16
verses.text: Every Scripture is God-breathed and profitable for teaching, for reproof, for correction, and for instruction in righteousness,

verses.name: 2 Timothy 3:17
verses.text: that the man of God may be complete, thoroughly equipped for every good work.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 4:1
verses.text: I command you therefore before God and the Lord Jesus Christ, who will judge the living and the dead at his appearing and his Kingdom:

verses.name: 2 Timothy 4:2
verses.text: preach the word; be urgent in season and out of season; reprove, rebuke, and exhort, with all patience and teaching.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 4:3
verses.text: For the time will come when they will not listen to the sound doctrine, but, having itching ears, will heap up for themselves teachers after their own lusts;

verses.name: 2 Timothy 4:4
verses.text: and will turn away their ears from the truth, and turn away to fables.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 4:5
verses.text: But you be sober in all things, suffer hardship, do the work of an evangelist, and fulfill your ministry.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 4:6
verses.text: For I am already being offered, and the time of my departure has come.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 4:7
verses.text: I have fought the good fight. I have finished the course. I have kept the faith.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 4:8
verses.text: From now on, there is stored up for me the crown of righteousness, which the Lord, the righteous judge, will give to me on that day; and not to me only, but also to all those who have loved his appearing.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 4:9
verses.text: Be diligent to come to me soon,

verses.name: 2 Timothy 4:10
verses.text: for Demas left me, having loved this present world, and went to Thessalonica; Crescens to Galatia, and Titus to Dalmatia.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 4:11
verses.text: Only Luke is with me. Take Mark, and bring him with you, for he is useful to me for service.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 4:12
verses.text: But I sent Tychicus to Ephesus.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 4:13
verses.text: Bring the cloak that I left at Troas with Carpus when you come, and the books, especially the parchments.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 4:14
verses.text: Alexander, the coppersmith, did much evil to me. The Lord will repay him according to his deeds,

verses.name: 2 Timothy 4:15
verses.text: of whom you also must beware; for he greatly opposed our words.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 4:16
verses.text: At my first defense, no one came to help me, but all left me. May it not be held against them.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 4:17
verses.text: But the Lord stood by me, and strengthened me, that through me the message might be fully proclaimed, and that all the Gentiles might hear; and I was delivered out of the mouth of the lion.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 4:18
verses.text: And the Lord will deliver me from every evil work, and will preserve me for his heavenly Kingdom; to whom be the glory forever and ever. Amen.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 4:19
verses.text: Greet Prisca and Aquila, and the house of Onesiphorus.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 4:20
verses.text: Erastus remained at Corinth, but I left Trophimus at Miletus sick.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 4:21
verses.text: Be diligent to come before winter. Eubulus salutes you, as do Pudens, Linus, Claudia, and all the brothers.

verses.name: 2 Timothy 4:22
verses.text: The Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Grace be with you. Amen.

verses.name: Titus 1:1
verses.text: Paul, a servant of God, and an apostle of Jesus Christ, according to the faith of God’s chosen ones, and the knowledge of the truth which is according to godliness,

verses.name: Titus 1:2
verses.text: in hope of eternal life, which God, who can’t lie, promised before time began;

verses.name: Titus 1:3
verses.text: but in his own time revealed his word in the message with which I was entrusted according to the commandment of God our Savior;

verses.name: Titus 1:4
verses.text: to Titus, my true child according to a common faith: Grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ our Savior.

verses.name: Titus 1:5
verses.text: I left you in Crete for this reason, that you would set in order the things that were lacking, and appoint elders in every city, as I directed you;

verses.name: Titus 1:6
verses.text: if anyone is blameless, the husband of one wife, having children who believe, who are not accused of loose or unruly behavior.

verses.name: Titus 1:7
verses.text: For the overseer must be blameless, as God’s steward; not self-pleasing, not easily angered, not given to wine, not violent, not greedy for dishonest gain;

verses.name: Titus 1:8
verses.text: but given to hospitality, a lover of good, sober minded, fair, holy, self-controlled;

verses.name: Titus 1:9
verses.text: holding to the faithful word which is according to the teaching, that he may be able to exhort in the sound doctrine, and to convict those who contradict him.

verses.name: Titus 1:10
verses.text: For there are also many unruly men, vain talkers and deceivers, especially those of the circumcision,

verses.name: Titus 1:11
verses.text: whose mouths must be stopped; men who overthrow whole houses, teaching things which they ought not, for dishonest gain’s sake.

verses.name: Titus 1:12
verses.text: One of them, a prophet of their own, said, “Cretans are always liars, evil beasts, and idle gluttons.”

verses.name: Titus 1:13
verses.text: This testimony is true. For this cause, reprove them sharply, that they may be sound in the faith,

verses.name: Titus 1:14
verses.text: not paying attention to Jewish fables and commandments of men who turn away from the truth.

verses.name: Titus 1:15
verses.text: To the pure, all things are pure; but to those who are defiled and unbelieving, nothing is pure; but both their mind and their conscience are defiled.

verses.name: Titus 1:16
verses.text: They profess that they know God, but by their deeds they deny him, being abominable, disobedient, and unfit for any good work.

verses.name: Titus 2:1
