verses.text: But say the things which fit sound doctrine,

verses.name: Titus 2:2
verses.text: that older men should be temperate, sensible, sober minded, sound in faith, in love, and in perseverance:

verses.name: Titus 2:3
verses.text: and that older women likewise be reverent in behavior, not slanderers nor enslaved to much wine, teachers of that which is good;

verses.name: Titus 2:4
verses.text: that they may train the young wives to love their husbands, to love their children,

verses.name: Titus 2:5
verses.text: to be sober minded, chaste, workers at home, kind, being in subjection to their own husbands, that God’s word may not be blasphemed.

verses.name: Titus 2:6
verses.text: Likewise, exhort the younger men to be sober minded;

verses.name: Titus 2:7
verses.text: in all things showing yourself an example of good works; in your teaching showing integrity, seriousness, incorruptibility,

verses.name: Titus 2:8
verses.text: and soundness of speech that can’t be condemned; that he who opposes you may be ashamed, having no evil thing to say about us.

verses.name: Titus 2:9
verses.text: Exhort servants to be in subjection to their own masters, and to be well-pleasing in all things; not contradicting;

verses.name: Titus 2:10
verses.text: not stealing, but showing all good fidelity; that they may adorn the doctrine of God, our Savior, in all things.

verses.name: Titus 2:11
verses.text: For the grace of God has appeared, bringing salvation to all men,

verses.name: Titus 2:12
verses.text: instructing us to the intent that, denying ungodliness and worldly lusts, we would live soberly, righteously, and godly in this present age;

verses.name: Titus 2:13
verses.text: looking for the blessed hope and appearing of the glory of our great God and Savior, Jesus Christ;

verses.name: Titus 2:14
verses.text: who gave himself for us, that he might redeem us from all iniquity, and purify for himself a people for his own possession, zealous for good works.

verses.name: Titus 2:15
verses.text: Say these things and exhort and reprove with all authority. Let no man despise you.

verses.name: Titus 3:1
verses.text: Remind them to be in subjection to rulers and to authorities, to be obedient, to be ready for every good work,

verses.name: Titus 3:2
verses.text: to speak evil of no one, not to be contentious, to be gentle, showing all humility toward all men.

verses.name: Titus 3:3
verses.text: For we were also once foolish, disobedient, deceived, serving various lusts and pleasures, living in malice and envy, hateful, and hating one another.

verses.name: Titus 3:4
verses.text: But when the kindness of God our Savior and his love toward mankind appeared,

verses.name: Titus 3:5
verses.text: not by works of righteousness which we did ourselves, but according to his mercy, he saved us through the washing of regeneration and renewing by the Holy Spirit,

verses.name: Titus 3:6
verses.text: whom he poured out on us richly, through Jesus Christ our Savior;

verses.name: Titus 3:7
verses.text: that being justified by his grace, we might be made heirs according to the hope of eternal life.

verses.name: Titus 3:8
verses.text: This saying is faithful, and concerning these things I desire that you affirm confidently, so that those who have believed God may be careful to maintain good works. These things are good and profitable to men;

verses.name: Titus 3:9
verses.text: but shun foolish questionings, genealogies, strife, and disputes about the law; for they are unprofitable and vain.

verses.name: Titus 3:10
verses.text: Avoid a factious man after a first and second warning,

verses.name: Titus 3:11
verses.text: knowing that such a one is perverted and sins, being self-condemned.

verses.name: Titus 3:12
verses.text: When I send Artemas to you, or Tychicus, be diligent to come to me to Nicopolis, for I have determined to winter there.

verses.name: Titus 3:13
verses.text: Send Zenas, the lawyer, and Apollos on their journey speedily, that nothing may be lacking for them.

verses.name: Titus 3:14
verses.text: Let our people also learn to maintain good works for necessary uses, that they may not be unfruitful.

verses.name: Titus 3:15
verses.text: All who are with me greet you. Greet those who love us in faith. Grace be with you all. Amen.

verses.name: Philemon 1:1
verses.text: Paul, a prisoner of Christ Jesus, and Timothy our brother, to Philemon, our beloved fellow worker,

verses.name: Philemon 1:2
verses.text: to the beloved Apphia, to Archippus, our fellow soldier, and to the assembly in your house:

verses.name: Philemon 1:3
verses.text: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.name: Philemon 1:4
verses.text: I thank my God always, making mention of you in my prayers,

verses.name: Philemon 1:5
verses.text: hearing of your love, and of the faith which you have toward the Lord Jesus, and toward all the saints;

verses.name: Philemon 1:6
verses.text: that the fellowship of your faith may become effective, in the knowledge of every good thing which is in us in Christ Jesus.

verses.name: Philemon 1:7
verses.text: For we have much joy and comfort in your love, because the hearts of the saints have been refreshed through you, brother.

verses.name: Philemon 1:8
verses.text: Therefore though I have all boldness in Christ to command you that which is appropriate,

verses.name: Philemon 1:9
verses.text: yet for love’s sake I rather beg, being such a one as Paul, the aged, but also a prisoner of Jesus Christ.

verses.name: Philemon 1:10
verses.text: I beg you for my child, whom I have become the father of in my chains, Onesimus,

verses.name: Philemon 1:11
verses.text: who once was useless to you, but now is useful to you and to me.

verses.name: Philemon 1:12
verses.text: I am sending him back. Therefore receive him, that is, my own heart,

verses.name: Philemon 1:13
verses.text: whom I desired to keep with me, that on your behalf he might serve me in my chains for the Good News.

verses.name: Philemon 1:14
verses.text: But I was willing to do nothing without your consent, that your goodness would not be as of necessity, but of free will.

verses.name: Philemon 1:15
verses.text: For perhaps he was therefore separated from you for a while, that you would have him forever,

verses.name: Philemon 1:16
verses.text: no longer as a slave, but more than a slave, a beloved brother, especially to me, but how much rather to you, both in the flesh and in the Lord.

verses.name: Philemon 1:17
verses.text: If then you count me a partner, receive him as you would receive me.

verses.name: Philemon 1:18
verses.text: But if he has wronged you at all, or owes you anything, put that to my account.

verses.name: Philemon 1:19
verses.text: I, Paul, write this with my own hand: I will repay it (not to mention to you that you owe to me even your own self besides).

verses.name: Philemon 1:20
verses.text: Yes, brother, let me have joy from you in the Lord. Refresh my heart in the Lord.

verses.name: Philemon 1:21
verses.text: Having confidence in your obedience, I write to you, knowing that you will do even beyond what I say.

verses.name: Philemon 1:22
verses.text: Also, prepare a guest room for me, for I hope that through your prayers I will be restored to you.

verses.name: Philemon 1:23
verses.text: Epaphras, my fellow prisoner in Christ Jesus, greets you,

verses.name: Philemon 1:24
verses.text: as do Mark, Aristarchus, Demas, and Luke, my fellow workers.

verses.name: Philemon 1:25
verses.text: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen.

verses.name: Hebrews 1:1
verses.text: God, having in the past spoken to the fathers through the prophets at many times and in various ways,

verses.name: Hebrews 1:2
verses.text: has at the end of these days spoken to us by his Son, whom he appointed heir of all things, through whom also he made the worlds.

verses.name: Hebrews 1:3
verses.text: His Son is the radiance of his glory, the very image of his substance, and upholding all things by the word of his power, who, when he had by himself purified us of our sins, sat down on the right hand of the Majesty on high;

verses.name: Hebrews 1:4
verses.text: having become so much better than the angels, as he has inherited a more excellent name than they have.

verses.name: Hebrews 1:5
verses.text: For to which of the angels did he say at any time, “You are my Son. Today I have become your father?” and again, “I will be to him a Father, and he will be to me a Son?”

verses.name: Hebrews 1:6
verses.text: When he again brings in the firstborn into the world he says, “Let all the angels of God worship him.”

verses.name: Hebrews 1:7
verses.text: Of the angels he says, “Who makes his angels winds, and his servants a flame of fire.”

verses.name: Hebrews 1:8
verses.text: But of the Son he says, “Your throne, O God, is forever and ever. The scepter of uprightness is the scepter of your Kingdom.

verses.name: Hebrews 1:9
verses.text: You have loved righteousness, and hated iniquity; therefore God, your God, has anointed you with the oil of gladness above your fellows.”

verses.name: Hebrews 1:10
verses.text: And, “You, Lord, in the beginning, laid the foundation of the earth. The heavens are the works of your hands.

verses.name: Hebrews 1:11
verses.text: They will perish, but you continue. They all will grow old like a garment does.

verses.name: Hebrews 1:12
verses.text: You will roll them up like a mantle, and they will be changed; but you are the same. Your years will not fail.”

verses.name: Hebrews 1:13
verses.text: But which of the angels has he told at any time, “Sit at my right hand, until I make your enemies the footstool of your feet?”

verses.name: Hebrews 1:14
verses.text: Aren’t they all serving spirits, sent out to do service for the sake of those who will inherit salvation?

verses.name: Hebrews 2:1
verses.text: Therefore we ought to pay greater attention to the things that were heard, lest perhaps we drift away.

verses.name: Hebrews 2:2
verses.text: For if the word spoken through angels proved steadfast, and every transgression and disobedience received a just recompense;

verses.name: Hebrews 2:3
verses.text: how will we escape if we neglect so great a salvation—which at the first having been spoken through the Lord, was confirmed to us by those who heard;

verses.name: Hebrews 2:4
verses.text: God also testifying with them, both by signs and wonders, by various works of power, and by gifts of the Holy Spirit, according to his own will?

verses.name: Hebrews 2:5
verses.text: For he didn’t subject the world to come, of which we speak, to angels.

verses.name: Hebrews 2:6
verses.text: But one has somewhere testified, saying, “What is man, that you think of him? Or the son of man, that you care for him?

verses.name: Hebrews 2:7
verses.text: You made him a little lower than the angels. You crowned him with glory and honor.

verses.name: Hebrews 2:8
verses.text: You have put all things in subjection under his feet.” For in that he subjected all things to him, he left nothing that is not subject to him. But now we don’t see all things subjected to him, yet.

verses.name: Hebrews 2:9
verses.text: But we see him who has been made a little lower than the angels, Jesus, because of the suffering of death crowned with glory and honor, that by the grace of God he should taste of death for everyone.

verses.name: Hebrews 2:10
verses.text: For it became him, for whom are all things, and through whom are all things, in bringing many children to glory, to make the author of their salvation perfect through sufferings.

verses.name: Hebrews 2:11
verses.text: For both he who sanctifies and those who are sanctified are all from one, for which cause he is not ashamed to call them brothers,

verses.name: Hebrews 2:12
verses.text: saying, “I will declare your name to my brothers. Among of the congregation I will sing your praise.”

verses.name: Hebrews 2:13
verses.text: Again, “I will put my trust in him.” Again, “Behold, here I am with the children whom God has given me.”

verses.name: Hebrews 2:14
verses.text: Since then the children have shared in flesh and blood, he also himself in the same way partook of the same, that through death he might bring to nothing him who had the power of death, that is, the devil,

verses.name: Hebrews 2:15
verses.text: and might deliver all of them who through fear of death were all their lifetime subject to bondage.

verses.name: Hebrews 2:16
verses.text: For most certainly, he doesn’t give help to angels, but he gives help to the offspring of Abraham.

verses.name: Hebrews 2:17
verses.text: Therefore he was obligated in all things to be made like his brothers, that he might become a merciful and faithful high priest in things pertaining to God, to make atonement for the sins of the people.

verses.name: Hebrews 2:18
verses.text: For in that he himself has suffered being tempted, he is able to help those who are tempted.

verses.name: Hebrews 3:1
verses.text: Therefore, holy brothers, partakers of a heavenly calling, consider the Apostle and High Priest of our confession, Jesus;

verses.name: Hebrews 3:2
verses.text: who was faithful to him who appointed him, as also was Moses in all his house.

verses.name: Hebrews 3:3
verses.text: For he has been counted worthy of more glory than Moses, because he who built the house has more honor than the house.

verses.name: Hebrews 3:4
verses.text: For every house is built by someone; but he who built all things is God.

verses.name: Hebrews 3:5
verses.text: Moses indeed was faithful in all his house as a servant, for a testimony of those things which were afterward to be spoken,

verses.name: Hebrews 3:6
verses.text: but Christ is faithful as a Son over his house; whose house we are, if we hold fast our confidence and the glorying of our hope firm to the end.

verses.name: Hebrews 3:7
verses.text: Therefore, even as the Holy Spirit says, “Today if you will hear his voice,

verses.name: Hebrews 3:8
verses.text: don’t harden your hearts, as in the rebellion, like as in the day of the trial in the wilderness,

verses.name: Hebrews 3:9
verses.text: where your fathers tested me by proving me, and saw my deeds for forty years.

verses.name: Hebrews 3:10
verses.text: Therefore I was displeased with that generation, and said, ‘They always err in their heart, but they didn’t know my ways;’

verses.name: Hebrews 3:11
verses.text: as I swore in my wrath, ‘They will not enter into my rest.’”

verses.name: Hebrews 3:12
verses.text: Beware, brothers, lest perhaps there be in any one of you an evil heart of unbelief, in falling away from the living God;

verses.name: Hebrews 3:13
verses.text: but exhort one another day by day, so long as it is called “today”; lest any one of you be hardened by the deceitfulness of sin.

verses.name: Hebrews 3:14
verses.text: For we have become partakers of Christ, if we hold fast the beginning of our confidence firm to the end:

verses.name: Hebrews 3:15
verses.text: while it is said, “Today if you will hear his voice, don’t harden your hearts, as in the rebellion.”

verses.name: Hebrews 3:16
verses.text: For who, when they heard, rebelled? No, didn’t all those who came out of Egypt by Moses?

verses.name: Hebrews 3:17
verses.text: With whom was he displeased forty years? Wasn’t it with those who sinned, whose bodies fell in the wilderness?

verses.name: Hebrews 3:18
verses.text: To whom did he swear that they wouldn’t enter into his rest, but to those who were disobedient?

verses.name: Hebrews 3:19
verses.text: We see that they were not able to enter in because of unbelief.

verses.name: Hebrews 4:1
verses.text: Let us fear therefore, lest perhaps anyone of you should seem to have come short of a promise of entering into his rest.

verses.name: Hebrews 4:2
verses.text: For indeed we have had good news preached to us, even as they also did, but the word they heard didn’t profit them, because it wasn’t mixed with faith by those who heard.

verses.name: Hebrews 4:3
verses.text: For we who have believed do enter into that rest, even as he has said, “As I swore in my wrath, they will not enter into my rest”; although the works were finished from the foundation of the world.

verses.name: Hebrews 4:4
verses.text: For he has said this somewhere about the seventh day, “God rested on the seventh day from all his works”;

verses.name: Hebrews 4:5
verses.text: and in this place again, “They will not enter into my rest.”

verses.name: Hebrews 4:6
verses.text: Seeing therefore it remains that some should enter into it, and they to whom the good news was preached before failed to enter in because of disobedience,

verses.name: Hebrews 4:7
verses.text: he again defines a certain day, today, saying through David so long a time afterward (just as has been said), “Today if you will hear his voice, don’t harden your hearts.”

verses.name: Hebrews 4:8
verses.text: For if Joshua had given them rest, he would not have spoken afterward of another day.

verses.name: Hebrews 4:9
verses.text: There remains therefore a Sabbath rest for the people of God.

verses.name: Hebrews 4:10
verses.text: For he who has entered into his rest has himself also rested from his works, as God did from his.

verses.name: Hebrews 4:11
verses.text: Let us therefore give diligence to enter into that rest, lest anyone fall after the same example of disobedience.

verses.name: Hebrews 4:12
verses.text: For the word of God is living and active, and sharper than any two-edged sword, piercing even to the dividing of soul and spirit, of both joints and marrow, and is able to discern the thoughts and intentions of the heart.

verses.name: Hebrews 4:13
verses.text: There is no creature that is hidden from his sight, but all things are naked and laid open before the eyes of him to whom we must give an account.

verses.name: Hebrews 4:14
verses.text: Having then a great high priest, who has passed through the heavens, Jesus, the Son of God, let us hold tightly to our confession.

verses.name: Hebrews 4:15
verses.text: For we don’t have a high priest who can’t be touched with the feeling of our infirmities, but one who has been in all points tempted like we are, yet without sin.

verses.name: Hebrews 4:16
verses.text: Let us therefore draw near with boldness to the throne of grace, that we may receive mercy, and may find grace for help in time of need.

verses.name: Hebrews 5:1
verses.text: For every high priest, being taken from among men, is appointed for men in things pertaining to God, that he may offer both gifts and sacrifices for sins.

verses.name: Hebrews 5:2
verses.text: The high priest can deal gently with those who are ignorant and going astray, because he himself is also surrounded with weakness.

verses.name: Hebrews 5:3
verses.text: Because of this, he must offer sacrifices for sins for the people, as well as for himself.

verses.name: Hebrews 5:4
verses.text: Nobody takes this honor on himself, but he is called by God, just like Aaron was.

verses.name: Hebrews 5:5
verses.text: So also Christ didn’t glorify himself to be made a high priest, but it was he who said to him, “You are my Son. Today I have become your father.”

verses.name: Hebrews 5:6
verses.text: As he says also in another place, “You are a priest forever, after the order of Melchizedek.”

verses.name: Hebrews 5:7
verses.text: He, in the days of his flesh, having offered up prayers and petitions with strong crying and tears to him who was able to save him from death, and having been heard for his godly fear,

verses.name: Hebrews 5:8
verses.text: though he was a Son, yet learned obedience by the things which he suffered.

verses.name: Hebrews 5:9
verses.text: Having been made perfect, he became to all of those who obey him the author of eternal salvation,

verses.name: Hebrews 5:10
verses.text: named by God a high priest after the order of Melchizedek.

verses.name: Hebrews 5:11
verses.text: About him we have many words to say, and hard to interpret, seeing you have become dull of hearing.

verses.name: Hebrews 5:12
verses.text: For although by this time you should be teachers, you again need to have someone teach you the rudiments of the first principles of the revelations of God. You have come to need milk, and not solid food.

verses.name: Hebrews 5:13
verses.text: For everyone who lives on milk is not experienced in the word of righteousness, for he is a baby.

verses.name: Hebrews 5:14
verses.text: But solid food is for those who are full grown, who by reason of use have their senses exercised to discern good and evil.

verses.name: Hebrews 6:1
verses.text: Therefore leaving the teaching of the first principles of Christ, let us press on to perfection—not laying again a foundation of repentance from dead works, of faith toward God,

verses.name: Hebrews 6:2
verses.text: of the teaching of baptisms, of laying on of hands, of resurrection of the dead, and of eternal judgment.

verses.name: Hebrews 6:3
verses.text: This will we do, if God permits.

verses.name: Hebrews 6:4
verses.text: For concerning those who were once enlightened and tasted of the heavenly gift, and were made partakers of the Holy Spirit,

verses.name: Hebrews 6:5
verses.text: and tasted the good word of God, and the powers of the age to come,

verses.name: Hebrews 6:6
verses.text: and then fell away, it is impossible to renew them again to repentance; seeing they crucify the Son of God for themselves again, and put him to open shame.

verses.name: Hebrews 6:7
verses.text: For the land which has drunk the rain that comes often on it, and produces a crop suitable for them for whose sake it is also tilled, receives blessing from God;

verses.name: Hebrews 6:8
verses.text: but if it bears thorns and thistles, it is rejected and near being cursed, whose end is to be burned.

verses.name: Hebrews 6:9
verses.text: But, beloved, we are persuaded of better things for you, and things that accompany salvation, even though we speak like this.

verses.name: Hebrews 6:10
verses.text: For God is not unrighteous, so as to forget your work and the labor of love which you showed toward his name, in that you served the saints, and still do serve them.

verses.name: Hebrews 6:11
verses.text: We desire that each one of you may show the same diligence to the fullness of hope even to the end,

verses.name: Hebrews 6:12
verses.text: that you won’t be sluggish, but imitators of those who through faith and perseverance inherited the promises.

verses.name: Hebrews 6:13
verses.text: For when God made a promise to Abraham, since he could swear by no one greater, he swore by himself,

verses.name: Hebrews 6:14
verses.text: saying, “Surely blessing I will bless you, and multiplying I will multiply you.”

verses.name: Hebrews 6:15
verses.text: Thus, having patiently endured, he obtained the promise.

verses.name: Hebrews 6:16
verses.text: For men indeed swear by a greater one, and in every dispute of theirs the oath is final for confirmation.

verses.name: Hebrews 6:17
verses.text: In this way God, being determined to show more abundantly to the heirs of the promise the immutability of his counsel, interposed with an oath;

verses.name: Hebrews 6:18
verses.text: that by two immutable things, in which it is impossible for God to lie, we may have a strong encouragement, who have fled for refuge to take hold of the hope set before us.

verses.name: Hebrews 6:19
verses.text: This hope we have as an anchor of the soul, a hope both sure and steadfast and entering into that which is within the veil;

verses.name: Hebrews 6:20
verses.text: where as a forerunner Jesus entered for us, having become a high priest forever after the order of Melchizedek.

verses.name: Hebrews 7:1
verses.text: For this Melchizedek, king of Salem, priest of God Most High, who met Abraham returning from the slaughter of the kings and blessed him,

verses.name: Hebrews 7:2
verses.text: to whom also Abraham divided a tenth part of all (being first, by interpretation, “king of righteousness”, and then also “king of Salem”, which means “king of peace”;

verses.name: Hebrews 7:3
verses.text: without father, without mother, without genealogy, having neither beginning of days nor end of life, but made like the Son of God), remains a priest continually.

verses.name: Hebrews 7:4
verses.text: Now consider how great this man was, to whom even Abraham, the patriarch, gave a tenth out of the best plunder.

verses.name: Hebrews 7:5
verses.text: They indeed of the sons of Levi who receive the priest’s office have a commandment to take tithes of the people according to the law, that is, of their brothers, though these have come out of the body of Abraham,

verses.name: Hebrews 7:6
verses.text: but he whose genealogy is not counted from them has accepted tithes from Abraham, and has blessed him who has the promises.

verses.name: Hebrews 7:7
verses.text: But without any dispute the lesser is blessed by the greater.

verses.name: Hebrews 7:8
verses.text: Here people who die receive tithes, but there one receives tithes of whom it is testified that he lives.

verses.name: Hebrews 7:9
verses.text: We can say that through Abraham even Levi, who receives tithes, has paid tithes,

verses.name: Hebrews 7:10
verses.text: for he was yet in the body of his father when Melchizedek met him.

verses.name: Hebrews 7:11
verses.text: Now if there were perfection through the Levitical priesthood (for under it the people have received the law), what further need was there for another priest to arise after the order of Melchizedek, and not be called after the order of Aaron?

verses.name: Hebrews 7:12
verses.text: For the priesthood being changed, there is of necessity a change made also in the law.

verses.name: Hebrews 7:13
verses.text: For he of whom these things are said belongs to another tribe, from which no one has officiated at the altar.

verses.name: Hebrews 7:14
verses.text: For it is evident that our Lord has sprung out of Judah, about which tribe Moses spoke nothing concerning priesthood.

verses.name: Hebrews 7:15
verses.text: This is yet more abundantly evident, if after the likeness of Melchizedek there arises another priest,

verses.name: Hebrews 7:16
verses.text: who has been made, not after the law of a fleshly commandment, but after the power of an endless life:

verses.name: Hebrews 7:17
verses.text: for it is testified, “You are a priest forever, according to the order of Melchizedek.”

verses.name: Hebrews 7:18
verses.text: For there is an annulling of a foregoing commandment because of its weakness and uselessness

verses.name: Hebrews 7:19
verses.text: (for the law made nothing perfect), and a bringing in of a better hope, through which we draw near to God.

verses.name: Hebrews 7:20
verses.text: Inasmuch as he was not made priest without the taking of an oath

verses.name: Hebrews 7:21
verses.text: (for they indeed have been made priests without an oath), but he with an oath by him that says of him, “The Lord swore and will not change his mind, ‘You are a priest forever, according to the order of Melchizedek.’”

verses.name: Hebrews 7:22
verses.text: By so much, Jesus has become the collateral of a better covenant.

verses.name: Hebrews 7:23
verses.text: Many, indeed, have been made priests, because they are hindered from continuing by death.

verses.name: Hebrews 7:24
verses.text: But he, because he lives forever, has his priesthood unchangeable.

verses.name: Hebrews 7:25
verses.text: Therefore he is also able to save to the uttermost those who draw near to God through him, seeing that he lives forever to make intercession for them.

verses.name: Hebrews 7:26
verses.text: For such a high priest was fitting for us: holy, guiltless, undefiled, separated from sinners, and made higher than the heavens;

verses.name: Hebrews 7:27
verses.text: who doesn’t need, like those high priests, to offer up sacrifices daily, first for his own sins, and then for the sins of the people. For he did this once for all, when he offered up himself.

verses.name: Hebrews 7:28
verses.text: For the law appoints men as high priests who have weakness, but the word of the oath which came after the law appoints a Son forever who has been perfected.

verses.name: Hebrews 8:1
verses.text: Now in the things which we are saying, the main point is this. We have such a high priest, who sat down on the right hand of the throne of the Majesty in the heavens,

verses.name: Hebrews 8:2
verses.text: a servant of the sanctuary, and of the true tabernacle, which the Lord pitched, not man.

verses.name: Hebrews 8:3
verses.text: For every high priest is appointed to offer both gifts and sacrifices. Therefore it is necessary that this high priest also have something to offer.

verses.name: Hebrews 8:4
verses.text: For if he were on earth, he would not be a priest at all, seeing there are priests who offer the gifts according to the law;

verses.name: Hebrews 8:5
verses.text: who serve a copy and shadow of the heavenly things, even as Moses was warned by God when he was about to make the tabernacle, for he said, “See, you shall make everything according to the pattern that was shown to you on the mountain.”

verses.name: Hebrews 8:6
verses.text: But now he has obtained a more excellent ministry, by so much as he is also the mediator of a better covenant, which on better promises has been given as law.

verses.name: Hebrews 8:7
verses.text: For if that first covenant had been faultless, then no place would have been sought for a second.

verses.name: Hebrews 8:8
verses.text: For finding fault with them, he said, “Behold, the days come”, says the Lord, “that I will make a new covenant with the house of Israel and with the house of Judah;

verses.name: Hebrews 8:9
verses.text: not according to the covenant that I made with their fathers, in the day that I took them by the hand to lead them out of the land of Egypt; for they didn’t continue in my covenant, and I disregarded them,” says the Lord.

verses.name: Hebrews 8:10
verses.text: “For this is the covenant that I will make with the house of Israel. After those days,” says the Lord; “I will put my laws into their mind, I will also write them on their heart. I will be their God, and they will be my people.

verses.name: Hebrews 8:11
verses.text: They will not teach every man his fellow citizen, and every man his brother, saying, ‘Know the Lord,’ for all will know me, from their least to their greatest.

verses.name: Hebrews 8:12
verses.text: For I will be merciful to their unrighteousness. I will remember their sins and lawless deeds no more.”

verses.name: Hebrews 8:13
verses.text: In that he says, “A new covenant”, he has made the first old. But that which is becoming old and grows aged is near to vanishing away.

verses.name: Hebrews 9:1
verses.text: Now indeed even the first covenant had ordinances of divine service, and an earthly sanctuary.

verses.name: Hebrews 9:2
verses.text: For a tabernacle was prepared. In the first part were the lamp stand, the table, and the show bread; which is called the Holy Place.

verses.name: Hebrews 9:3
verses.text: After the second veil was the tabernacle which is called the Holy of Holies,

verses.name: Hebrews 9:4
verses.text: having a golden altar of incense, and the ark of the covenant overlaid on all sides with gold, in which was a golden pot holding the manna, Aaron’s rod that budded, and the tablets of the covenant;

verses.name: Hebrews 9:5
verses.text: and above it cherubim of glory overshadowing the mercy seat, of which things we can’t speak now in detail.

verses.name: Hebrews 9:6
verses.text: Now these things having been thus prepared, the priests go in continually into the first tabernacle, accomplishing the services,

verses.name: Hebrews 9:7
verses.text: but into the second the high priest alone, once in the year, not without blood, which he offers for himself, and for the errors of the people.

verses.name: Hebrews 9:8
verses.text: The Holy Spirit is indicating this, that the way into the Holy Place wasn’t yet revealed while the first tabernacle was still standing;

verses.name: Hebrews 9:9
verses.text: which is a symbol of the present age, where gifts and sacrifices are offered that are incapable, concerning the conscience, of making the worshiper perfect;

verses.name: Hebrews 9:10
verses.text: being only (with meats and drinks and various washings) fleshly ordinances, imposed until a time of reformation.

verses.name: Hebrews 9:11
verses.text: But Christ having come as a high priest of the coming good things, through the greater and more perfect tabernacle, not made with hands, that is to say, not of this creation,

verses.name: Hebrews 9:12
verses.text: nor yet through the blood of goats and calves, but through his own blood, entered in once for all into the Holy Place, having obtained eternal redemption.

verses.name: Hebrews 9:13
verses.text: For if the blood of goats and bulls, and the ashes of a heifer sprinkling those who have been defiled, sanctify to the cleanness of the flesh:

verses.name: Hebrews 9:14
verses.text: how much more will the blood of Christ, who through the eternal Spirit offered himself without defect to God, cleanse your conscience from dead works to serve the living God?

verses.name: Hebrews 9:15
verses.text: For this reason he is the mediator of a new covenant, since a death has occurred for the redemption of the transgressions that were under the first covenant, that those who have been called may receive the promise of the eternal inheritance.

verses.name: Hebrews 9:16
verses.text: For where a last will and testament is, there must of necessity be the death of him who made it.

verses.name: Hebrews 9:17
verses.text: For a will is in force where there has been death, for it is never in force while he who made it lives.

verses.name: Hebrews 9:18
verses.text: Therefore even the first covenant has not been dedicated without blood.

verses.name: Hebrews 9:19
verses.text: For when every commandment had been spoken by Moses to all the people according to the law, he took the blood of the calves and the goats, with water and scarlet wool and hyssop, and sprinkled both the book itself and all the people,

verses.name: Hebrews 9:20
verses.text: saying, “This is the blood of the covenant which God has commanded you.”

verses.name: Hebrews 9:21
verses.text: Moreover he sprinkled the tabernacle and all the vessels of the ministry in the same way with the blood.

verses.name: Hebrews 9:22
verses.text: According to the law, nearly everything is cleansed with blood, and apart from shedding of blood there is no remission.

verses.name: Hebrews 9:23
verses.text: It was necessary therefore that the copies of the things in the heavens should be cleansed with these; but the heavenly things themselves with better sacrifices than these.

verses.name: Hebrews 9:24
verses.text: For Christ hasn’t entered into holy places made with hands, which are representations of the true, but into heaven itself, now to appear in the presence of God for us;

verses.name: Hebrews 9:25
verses.text: nor yet that he should offer himself often, as the high priest enters into the holy place year by year with blood not his own,

verses.name: Hebrews 9:26
verses.text: or else he must have suffered often since the foundation of the world. But now once at the end of the ages, he has been revealed to put away sin by the sacrifice of himself.

verses.name: Hebrews 9:27
verses.text: Inasmuch as it is appointed for men to die once, and after this, judgment,

verses.name: Hebrews 9:28
verses.text: so Christ also, having been offered once to bear the sins of many, will appear a second time, without sin, to those who are eagerly waiting for him for salvation.

verses.name: Hebrews 10:1
verses.text: For the law, having a shadow of the good to come, not the very image of the things, can never with the same sacrifices year by year, which they offer continually, make perfect those who draw near.

verses.name: Hebrews 10:2
verses.text: Or else wouldn’t they have ceased to be offered, because the worshipers, having been once cleansed, would have had no more consciousness of sins?

verses.name: Hebrews 10:3
verses.text: But in those sacrifices there is a yearly reminder of sins.

verses.name: Hebrews 10:4
verses.text: For it is impossible that the blood of bulls and goats should take away sins.

verses.name: Hebrews 10:5
verses.text: Therefore when he comes into the world, he says, “Sacrifice and offering you didn’t desire, but you prepared a body for me.

verses.name: Hebrews 10:6
verses.text: You had no pleasure in whole burnt offerings and sacrifices for sin.

verses.name: Hebrews 10:7
verses.text: Then I said, ‘Behold, I have come (in the scroll of the book it is written of me) to do your will, O God.’”

verses.name: Hebrews 10:8
verses.text: Previously saying, “Sacrifices and offerings and whole burnt offerings and sacrifices for sin you didn’t desire, neither had pleasure in them” (those which are offered according to the law),

verses.name: Hebrews 10:9
verses.text: then he has said, “Behold, I have come to do your will.” He takes away the first, that he may establish the second,

verses.name: Hebrews 10:10
verses.text: by which will we have been sanctified through the offering of the body of Jesus Christ once for all.

verses.name: Hebrews 10:11
verses.text: Every priest indeed stands day by day serving and often offering the same sacrifices, which can never take away sins,

verses.name: Hebrews 10:12
verses.text: but he, when he had offered one sacrifice for sins forever, sat down on the right hand of God;

verses.name: Hebrews 10:13
verses.text: from that time waiting until his enemies are made the footstool of his feet.

verses.name: Hebrews 10:14
verses.text: For by one offering he has perfected forever those who are being sanctified.

verses.name: Hebrews 10:15
verses.text: The Holy Spirit also testifies to us, for after saying,

verses.name: Hebrews 10:16
verses.text: “This is the covenant that I will make with them: ‘After those days,’ says the Lord, ‘I will put my laws on their heart, I will also write them on their mind;’” then he says,

verses.name: Hebrews 10:17
verses.text: “I will remember their sins and their iniquities no more.”

verses.name: Hebrews 10:18
verses.text: Now where remission of these is, there is no more offering for sin.

verses.name: Hebrews 10:19
verses.text: Having therefore, brothers, boldness to enter into the holy place by the blood of Jesus,

verses.name: Hebrews 10:20
verses.text: by the way which he dedicated for us, a new and living way, through the veil, that is to say, his flesh;

verses.name: Hebrews 10:21
verses.text: and having a great priest over God’s house,

verses.name: Hebrews 10:22
verses.text: let’s draw near with a true heart in fullness of faith, having our hearts sprinkled from an evil conscience, and having our body washed with pure water,

verses.name: Hebrews 10:23
verses.text: let us hold fast the confession of our hope without wavering; for he who promised is faithful.

verses.name: Hebrews 10:24
verses.text: Let us consider how to provoke one another to love and good works,

verses.name: Hebrews 10:25
verses.text: not forsaking our own assembling together, as the custom of some is, but exhorting one another; and so much the more, as you see the Day approaching.

verses.name: Hebrews 10:26
verses.text: For if we sin willfully after we have received the knowledge of the truth, there remains no more a sacrifice for sins,

verses.name: Hebrews 10:27
verses.text: but a certain fearful expectation of judgment, and a fierceness of fire which will devour the adversaries.

verses.name: Hebrews 10:28
verses.text: A man who disregards Moses’ law dies without compassion on the word of two or three witnesses.

verses.name: Hebrews 10:29
verses.text: How much worse punishment do you think he will be judged worthy of who has trodden under foot the Son of God, and has counted the blood of the covenant with which he was sanctified an unholy thing, and has insulted the Spirit of grace?

verses.name: Hebrews 10:30
verses.text: For we know him who said, “Vengeance belongs to me,” says the Lord, “I will repay.” Again, “The Lord will judge his people.”

verses.name: Hebrews 10:31
verses.text: It is a fearful thing to fall into the hands of the living God.

verses.name: Hebrews 10:32
verses.text: But remember the former days, in which, after you were enlightened, you endured a great struggle with sufferings;

verses.name: Hebrews 10:33
verses.text: partly, being exposed to both reproaches and oppressions; and partly, becoming partakers with those who were treated so.

verses.name: Hebrews 10:34
verses.text: For you both had compassion on me in my chains, and joyfully accepted the plundering of your possessions, knowing that you have for yourselves a better possession and an enduring one in the heavens.

verses.name: Hebrews 10:35
verses.text: Therefore don’t throw away your boldness, which has a great reward.

verses.name: Hebrews 10:36
verses.text: For you need endurance so that, having done the will of God, you may receive the promise.

verses.name: Hebrews 10:37
verses.text: “In a very little while, he who comes will come, and will not wait.

verses.name: Hebrews 10:38
verses.text: But the righteous will live by faith. If he shrinks back, my soul has no pleasure in him.”

verses.name: Hebrews 10:39
verses.text: But we are not of those who shrink back to destruction, but of those who have faith to the saving of the soul.

verses.name: Hebrews 11:1
verses.text: Now faith is assurance of things hoped for, proof of things not seen.

verses.name: Hebrews 11:2
verses.text: For by this, the elders obtained testimony.

verses.name: Hebrews 11:3
verses.text: By faith, we understand that the universe has been framed by the word of God, so that what is seen has not been made out of things which are visible.

verses.name: Hebrews 11:4
verses.text: By faith, Abel offered to God a more excellent sacrifice than Cain, through which he had testimony given to him that he was righteous, God testifying with respect to his gifts; and through it he, being dead, still speaks.

verses.name: Hebrews 11:5
verses.text: By faith, Enoch was taken away, so that he wouldn’t see death, and he was not found, because God translated him. For he has had testimony given to him that before his translation he had been well pleasing to God.

verses.name: Hebrews 11:6
verses.text: Without faith it is impossible to be well pleasing to him, for he who comes to God must believe that he exists, and that he is a rewarder of those who seek him.

verses.name: Hebrews 11:7
verses.text: By faith, Noah, being warned about things not yet seen, moved with godly fear, prepared a ship for the saving of his house, through which he condemned the world, and became heir of the righteousness which is according to faith.

verses.name: Hebrews 11:8
verses.text: By faith, Abraham, when he was called, obeyed to go out to the place which he was to receive for an inheritance. He went out, not knowing where he went.

verses.name: Hebrews 11:9
verses.text: By faith, he lived as an alien in the land of promise, as in a land not his own, dwelling in tents, with Isaac and Jacob, the heirs with him of the same promise.

verses.name: Hebrews 11:10
verses.text: For he looked for the city which has the foundations, whose builder and maker is God.

verses.name: Hebrews 11:11
verses.text: By faith, even Sarah herself received power to conceive, and she bore a child when she was past age, since she counted him faithful who had promised.

verses.name: Hebrews 11:12
verses.text: Therefore as many as the stars of the sky in multitude, and as innumerable as the sand which is by the sea shore, were fathered by one man, and him as good as dead.

verses.name: Hebrews 11:13
verses.text: These all died in faith, not having received the promises, but having seen them and embraced them from afar, and having confessed that they were strangers and pilgrims on the earth.

verses.name: Hebrews 11:14
verses.text: For those who say such things make it clear that they are seeking a country of their own.

verses.name: Hebrews 11:15
verses.text: If indeed they had been thinking of that country from which they went out, they would have had enough time to return.

verses.name: Hebrews 11:16
verses.text: But now they desire a better country, that is, a heavenly one. Therefore God is not ashamed of them, to be called their God, for he has prepared a city for them.

verses.name: Hebrews 11:17
verses.text: By faith, Abraham, being tested, offered up Isaac. Yes, he who had gladly received the promises was offering up his one and only son,

verses.name: Hebrews 11:18
verses.text: to whom it was said, “your offspring will be accounted as from Isaac”;

verses.name: Hebrews 11:19
verses.text: concluding that God is able to raise up even from the dead. Figuratively speaking, he also did receive him back from the dead.

verses.name: Hebrews 11:20
verses.text: By faith, Isaac blessed Jacob and Esau, even concerning things to come.

verses.name: Hebrews 11:21
verses.text: By faith, Jacob, when he was dying, blessed each of the sons of Joseph, and worshiped, leaning on the top of his staff.

verses.name: Hebrews 11:22
verses.text: By faith, Joseph, when his end was near, made mention of the departure of the children of Israel; and gave instructions concerning his bones.

verses.name: Hebrews 11:23
verses.text: By faith, Moses, when he was born, was hidden for three months by his parents, because they saw that he was a beautiful child, and they were not afraid of the king’s commandment.

verses.name: Hebrews 11:24
verses.text: By faith, Moses, when he had grown up, refused to be called the son of Pharaoh’s daughter,

verses.name: Hebrews 11:25
verses.text: choosing rather to share ill treatment with God’s people, than to enjoy the pleasures of sin for a time;

verses.name: Hebrews 11:26
verses.text: accounting the reproach of Christ greater riches than the treasures of Egypt; for he looked to the reward.

verses.name: Hebrews 11:27
verses.text: By faith, he left Egypt, not fearing the wrath of the king; for he endured, as seeing him who is invisible.

verses.name: Hebrews 11:28
verses.text: By faith, he kept the Passover, and the sprinkling of the blood, that the destroyer of the firstborn should not touch them.

verses.name: Hebrews 11:29
verses.text: By faith, they passed through the Red Sea as on dry land. When the Egyptians tried to do so, they were swallowed up.

verses.name: Hebrews 11:30
verses.text: By faith, the walls of Jericho fell down, after they had been encircled for seven days.

verses.name: Hebrews 11:31
verses.text: By faith, Rahab the prostitute, didn’t perish with those who were disobedient, having received the spies in peace.

verses.name: Hebrews 11:32
verses.text: What more shall I say? For the time would fail me if I told of Gideon, Barak, Samson, Jephthah, David, Samuel, and the prophets;

verses.name: Hebrews 11:33
verses.text: who, through faith subdued kingdoms, worked out righteousness, obtained promises, stopped the mouths of lions,

verses.name: Hebrews 11:34
verses.text: quenched the power of fire, escaped the edge of the sword, from weakness were made strong, grew mighty in war, and caused foreign armies to flee.

verses.name: Hebrews 11:35
verses.text: Women received their dead by resurrection. Others were tortured, not accepting their deliverance, that they might obtain a better resurrection.

verses.name: Hebrews 11:36
verses.text: Others were tried by mocking and scourging, yes, moreover by bonds and imprisonment.

verses.name: Hebrews 11:37
verses.text: They were stoned. They were sawn apart. They were tempted. They were slain with the sword. They went around in sheep skins and in goat skins; being destitute, afflicted, ill-treated

verses.name: Hebrews 11:38
verses.text: (of whom the world was not worthy), wandering in deserts, mountains, caves, and the holes of the earth.

verses.name: Hebrews 11:39
verses.text: These all, having had testimony given to them through their faith, didn’t receive the promise,

verses.name: Hebrews 11:40
verses.text: God having provided some better thing concerning us, so that apart from us they should not be made perfect.

verses.name: Hebrews 12:1
verses.text: Therefore let us also, seeing we are surrounded by so great a cloud of witnesses, lay aside every weight and the sin which so easily entangles us, and let us run with perseverance the race that is set before us,

verses.name: Hebrews 12:2
verses.text: looking to Jesus, the author and perfecter of faith, who for the joy that was set before him endured the cross, despising its shame, and has sat down at the right hand of the throne of God.

verses.name: Hebrews 12:3
verses.text: For consider him who has endured such contradiction of sinners against himself, that you don’t grow weary, fainting in your souls.

verses.name: Hebrews 12:4
verses.text: You have not yet resisted to blood, striving against sin;

verses.name: Hebrews 12:5
verses.text: and you have forgotten the exhortation which reasons with you as with children, “My son, don’t take lightly the chastening of the Lord, nor faint when you are reproved by him;

verses.name: Hebrews 12:6
verses.text: For whom the Lord loves, he chastens, and scourges every son whom he receives.”

verses.name: Hebrews 12:7
verses.text: It is for discipline that you endure. God deals with you as with children, for what son is there whom his father doesn’t discipline?

verses.name: Hebrews 12:8
verses.text: But if you are without discipline, of which all have been made partakers, then are you illegitimate, and not children.

verses.name: Hebrews 12:9
verses.text: Furthermore, we had the fathers of our flesh to chasten us, and we paid them respect. Shall we not much rather be in subjection to the Father of spirits, and live?

verses.name: Hebrews 12:10
verses.text: For they indeed, for a few days, punished us as seemed good to them; but he for our profit, that we may be partakers of his holiness.

verses.name: Hebrews 12:11
verses.text: All chastening seems for the present to be not joyous but grievous; yet afterward it yields the peaceful fruit of righteousness to those who have been exercised thereby.

verses.name: Hebrews 12:12
verses.text: Therefore lift up the hands that hang down and the feeble knees,

verses.name: Hebrews 12:13
verses.text: and make straight paths for your feet, so what is lame may not be dislocated, but rather be healed.

verses.name: Hebrews 12:14
verses.text: Follow after peace with all men, and the sanctification without which no man will see the Lord,

verses.name: Hebrews 12:15
verses.text: looking carefully lest there be any man who falls short of the grace of God; lest any root of bitterness springing up trouble you, and many be defiled by it;

verses.name: Hebrews 12:16
verses.text: lest there be any sexually immoral person, or profane person, like Esau, who sold his birthright for one meal.

verses.name: Hebrews 12:17
verses.text: For you know that even when he afterward desired to inherit the blessing, he was rejected, for he found no place for a change of mind though he sought it diligently with tears.

verses.name: Hebrews 12:18
verses.text: For you have not come to a mountain that might be touched, and that burned with fire, and to blackness, darkness, storm,

verses.name: Hebrews 12:19
verses.text: the sound of a trumpet, and the voice of words; which those who heard it begged that not one more word should be spoken to them,

verses.name: Hebrews 12:20
verses.text: for they could not stand that which was commanded, “If even an animal touches the mountain, it shall be stoned”;

verses.name: Hebrews 12:21
verses.text: and so fearful was the appearance that Moses said, “I am terrified and trembling.”

verses.name: Hebrews 12:22
verses.text: But you have come to Mount Zion, and to the city of the living God, the heavenly Jerusalem, and to innumerable multitudes of angels,

verses.name: Hebrews 12:23
verses.text: to the festal gathering and assembly of the firstborn who are enrolled in heaven, to God the Judge of all, to the spirits of just men made perfect,

verses.name: Hebrews 12:24
verses.text: to Jesus, the mediator of a new covenant, and to the blood of sprinkling that speaks better than that of Abel.

verses.name: Hebrews 12:25
verses.text: See that you don’t refuse him who speaks. For if they didn’t escape when they refused him who warned on the earth, how much more will we not escape who turn away from him who warns from heaven,

verses.name: Hebrews 12:26
verses.text: whose voice shook the earth then, but now he has promised, saying, “Yet once more I will shake not only the earth, but also the heavens.”

verses.name: Hebrews 12:27
verses.text: This phrase, “Yet once more”, signifies the removing of those things that are shaken, as of things that have been made, that those things which are not shaken may remain.

verses.name: Hebrews 12:28
verses.text: Therefore, receiving a Kingdom that can’t be shaken, let us have grace, through which we serve God acceptably, with reverence and awe,

verses.name: Hebrews 12:29
verses.text: for our God is a consuming fire.

verses.name: Hebrews 13:1
verses.text: Let brotherly love continue.

verses.name: Hebrews 13:2
verses.text: Don’t forget to show hospitality to strangers, for in doing so, some have entertained angels without knowing it.

verses.name: Hebrews 13:3
verses.text: Remember those who are in bonds, as bound with them; and those who are ill-treated, since you are also in the body.

verses.name: Hebrews 13:4
verses.text: Let marriage be held in honor among all, and let the bed be undefiled: but God will judge the sexually immoral and adulterers.

verses.name: Hebrews 13:5
verses.text: Be free from the love of money, content with such things as you have, for he has said, “I will in no way leave you, neither will I in any way forsake you.”

verses.name: Hebrews 13:6
verses.text: So that with good courage we say, “The Lord is my helper. I will not fear. What can man do to me?”

verses.name: Hebrews 13:7
verses.text: Remember your leaders, men who spoke to you the word of God, and considering the results of their conduct, imitate their faith.

verses.name: Hebrews 13:8
verses.text: Jesus Christ is the same yesterday, today, and forever.

verses.name: Hebrews 13:9
verses.text: Don’t be carried away by various and strange teachings, for it is good that the heart be established by grace, not by food, through which those who were so occupied were not benefited.

verses.name: Hebrews 13:10
verses.text: We have an altar from which those who serve the holy tabernacle have no right to eat.

verses.name: Hebrews 13:11
verses.text: For the bodies of those animals, whose blood is brought into the holy place by the high priest as an offering for sin, are burned outside of the camp.

verses.name: Hebrews 13:12
verses.text: Therefore Jesus also, that he might sanctify the people through his own blood, suffered outside of the gate.

verses.name: Hebrews 13:13
verses.text: Let us therefore go out to him outside of the camp, bearing his reproach.

verses.name: Hebrews 13:14
verses.text: For we don’t have here an enduring city, but we seek that which is to come.

verses.name: Hebrews 13:15
verses.text: Through him, then, let us offer up a sacrifice of praise to God continually, that is, the fruit of lips which proclaim allegiance to his name.

verses.name: Hebrews 13:16
verses.text: But don’t forget to be doing good and sharing, for with such sacrifices God is well pleased.

verses.name: Hebrews 13:17
verses.text: Obey your leaders and submit to them, for they watch on behalf of your souls, as those who will give account, that they may do this with joy, and not with groaning, for that would be unprofitable for you.

verses.name: Hebrews 13:18
verses.text: Pray for us, for we are persuaded that we have a good conscience, desiring to live honorably in all things.

verses.name: Hebrews 13:19
verses.text: I strongly urge you to do this, that I may be restored to you sooner.

verses.name: Hebrews 13:20
verses.text: Now may the God of peace, who brought again from the dead the great shepherd of the sheep with the blood of an eternal covenant, our Lord Jesus,

verses.name: Hebrews 13:21
verses.text: make you complete in every good work to do his will, working in you that which is well pleasing in his sight, through Jesus Christ, to whom be the glory forever and ever. Amen.

verses.name: Hebrews 13:22
verses.text: But I exhort you, brothers, endure the word of exhortation; for I have written to you in few words.

verses.name: Hebrews 13:23
verses.text: Know that our brother Timothy has been freed, with whom, if he comes shortly, I will see you.

verses.name: Hebrews 13:24
verses.text: Greet all of your leaders and all the saints. The Italians greet you.

verses.name: Hebrews 13:25
verses.text: Grace be with you all. Amen.

verses.name: James 1:1
verses.text: James, a servant of God and of the Lord Jesus Christ, to the twelve tribes which are in the Dispersion: Greetings.

verses.name: James 1:2
verses.text: Count it all joy, my brothers, when you fall into various temptations,

verses.name: James 1:3
verses.text: knowing that the testing of your faith produces endurance.

verses.name: James 1:4
verses.text: Let endurance have its perfect work, that you may be perfect and complete, lacking in nothing.

verses.name: James 1:5
verses.text: But if any of you lacks wisdom, let him ask of God, who gives to all liberally and without reproach; and it will be given to him.

verses.name: James 1:6
verses.text: But let him ask in faith, without any doubting, for he who doubts is like a wave of the sea, driven by the wind and tossed.

verses.name: James 1:7
verses.text: For that man shouldn’t think that he will receive anything from the Lord.

verses.name: James 1:8
verses.text: He is a double-minded man, unstable in all his ways.

verses.name: James 1:9
verses.text: But let the brother in humble circumstances glory in his high position;

verses.name: James 1:10
verses.text: and the rich, in that he is made humble, because like the flower in the grass, he will pass away.

verses.name: James 1:11
verses.text: For the sun arises with the scorching wind, and withers the grass, and the flower in it falls, and the beauty of its appearance perishes. So also will the rich man fade away in his pursuits.

verses.name: James 1:12
verses.text: Blessed is the man who endures temptation, for when he has been approved, he will receive the crown of life, which the Lord promised to those who love him.

verses.name: James 1:13
verses.text: Let no man say when he is tempted, “I am tempted by God,” for God can’t be tempted by evil, and he himself tempts no one.

verses.name: James 1:14
verses.text: But each one is tempted when he is drawn away by his own lust, and enticed.

verses.name: James 1:15
verses.text: Then the lust, when it has conceived, bears sin; and the sin, when it is full grown, produces death.

verses.name: James 1:16
verses.text: Don’t be deceived, my beloved brothers.

verses.name: James 1:17
verses.text: Every good gift and every perfect gift is from above, coming down from the Father of lights, with whom can be no variation, nor turning shadow.

verses.name: James 1:18
verses.text: Of his own will he gave birth to us by the word of truth, that we should be a kind of first fruits of his creatures.

verses.name: James 1:19
verses.text: So, then, my beloved brothers, let every man be swift to hear, slow to speak, and slow to anger;

verses.name: James 1:20
verses.text: for the anger of man doesn’t produce the righteousness of God.

verses.name: James 1:21
verses.text: Therefore, putting away all filthiness and overflowing of wickedness, receive with humility the implanted word, which is able to save your souls.

verses.name: James 1:22
verses.text: But be doers of the word, and not only hearers, deluding your own selves.

verses.name: James 1:23
verses.text: For if anyone is a hearer of the word and not a doer, he is like a man looking at his natural face in a mirror;

verses.name: James 1:24
verses.text: for he sees himself, and goes away, and immediately forgets what kind of man he was.

verses.name: James 1:25
verses.text: But he who looks into the perfect law of freedom, and continues, not being a hearer who forgets, but a doer of the work, this man will be blessed in what he does.

verses.name: James 1:26
verses.text: If anyone among you thinks himself to be religious while he doesn’t bridle his tongue, but deceives his heart, this man’s religion is worthless.

verses.name: James 1:27
verses.text: Pure religion and undefiled before our God and Father is this: to visit the fatherless and widows in their affliction, and to keep oneself unstained by the world.

verses.name: James 2:1
verses.text: My brothers, don’t hold the faith of our Lord Jesus Christ of glory with partiality.

verses.name: James 2:2
verses.text: For if a man with a gold ring, in fine clothing, comes into your synagogue, and a poor man in filthy clothing also comes in;

verses.name: James 2:3
verses.text: and you pay special attention to him who wears the fine clothing, and say, “Sit here in a good place”; and you tell the poor man, “Stand there,” or “Sit by my footstool”;

verses.name: James 2:4
verses.text: haven’t you shown partiality among yourselves, and become judges with evil thoughts?

verses.name: James 2:5
verses.text: Listen, my beloved brothers. Didn’t God choose those who are poor in this world to be rich in faith, and heirs of the Kingdom which he promised to those who love him?

verses.name: James 2:6
verses.text: But you have dishonored the poor man. Don’t the rich oppress you, and personally drag you before the courts?

verses.name: James 2:7
verses.text: Don’t they blaspheme the honorable name by which you are called?

verses.name: James 2:8
verses.text: However, if you fulfill the royal law, according to the Scripture, “You shall love your neighbor as yourself,” you do well.

verses.name: James 2:9
verses.text: But if you show partiality, you commit sin, being convicted by the law as transgressors.

verses.name: James 2:10
verses.text: For whoever keeps the whole law, and yet stumbles in one point, he has become guilty of all.

verses.name: James 2:11
verses.text: For he who said, “Do not commit adultery,” also said, “Do not commit murder.” Now if you do not commit adultery, but murder, you have become a transgressor of the law.

verses.name: James 2:12
verses.text: So speak, and so do, as men who are to be judged by a law of freedom.

verses.name: James 2:13
verses.text: For judgment is without mercy to him who has shown no mercy. Mercy triumphs over judgment.

verses.name: James 2:14
verses.text: What good is it, my brothers, if a man says he has faith, but has no works? Can faith save him?

verses.name: James 2:15
verses.text: And if a brother or sister is naked and in lack of daily food,

verses.name: James 2:16
verses.text: and one of you tells them, “Go in peace, be warmed and filled”; and yet you didn’t give them the things the body needs, what good is it?

verses.name: James 2:17
verses.text: Even so faith, if it has no works, is dead in itself.

verses.name: James 2:18
verses.text: Yes, a man will say, “You have faith, and I have works.” Show me your faith without works, and I by my works will show you my faith.

verses.name: James 2:19
verses.text: You believe that God is one. You do well. The demons also believe, and shudder.

verses.name: James 2:20
verses.text: But do you want to know, vain man, that faith apart from works is dead?

verses.name: James 2:21
verses.text: Wasn’t Abraham our father justified by works, in that he offered up Isaac his son on the altar?

verses.name: James 2:22
verses.text: You see that faith worked with his works, and by works faith was perfected;

verses.name: James 2:23
verses.text: and the Scripture was fulfilled which says, “Abraham believed God, and it was accounted to him as righteousness”; and he was called the friend of God.

verses.name: James 2:24
verses.text: You see then that by works, a man is justified, and not only by faith.

verses.name: James 2:25
verses.text: In the same way, wasn’t Rahab the prostitute also justified by works, in that she received the messengers, and sent them out another way?

verses.name: James 2:26
verses.text: For as the body apart from the spirit is dead, even so faith apart from works is dead.

verses.name: James 3:1
verses.text: Let not many of you be teachers, my brothers, knowing that we will receive heavier judgment.

verses.name: James 3:2
verses.text: For in many things we all stumble. If anyone doesn’t stumble in word, the same is a perfect man, able to bridle the whole body also.

verses.name: James 3:3
verses.text: Indeed, we put bits into the horses’ mouths so that they may obey us, and we guide their whole body.

verses.name: James 3:4
verses.text: Behold, the ships also, though they are so big and are driven by fierce winds, are yet guided by a very small rudder, wherever the pilot desires.

verses.name: James 3:5
verses.text: So the tongue is also a little member, and boasts great things. See how a small fire can spread to a large forest!

verses.name: James 3:6
verses.text: And the tongue is a fire. The world of iniquity among our members is the tongue, which defiles the whole body, and sets on fire the course of nature, and is set on fire by Gehenna.

verses.name: James 3:7
verses.text: For every kind of animal, bird, creeping thing, and sea creature, is tamed, and has been tamed by mankind;

verses.name: James 3:8
verses.text: but nobody can tame the tongue. It is a restless evil, full of deadly poison.

verses.name: James 3:9
verses.text: With it we bless our God and Father, and with it we curse men, who are made in the image of God.

verses.name: James 3:10
verses.text: Out of the same mouth comes blessing and cursing. My brothers, these things ought not to be so.

verses.name: James 3:11
verses.text: Does a spring send out from the same opening fresh and bitter water?

verses.name: James 3:12
verses.text: Can a fig tree, my brothers, yield olives, or a vine figs? Thus no spring yields both salt water and fresh water.

verses.name: James 3:13
verses.text: Who is wise and understanding among you? Let him show by his good conduct that his deeds are done in gentleness of wisdom.

verses.name: James 3:14
verses.text: But if you have bitter jealousy and selfish ambition in your heart, don’t boast and don’t lie against the truth.

verses.name: James 3:15
verses.text: This wisdom is not that which comes down from above, but is earthly, sensual, and demonic.

verses.name: James 3:16
verses.text: For where jealousy and selfish ambition are, there is confusion and every evil deed.

verses.name: James 3:17
verses.text: But the wisdom that is from above is first pure, then peaceful, gentle, reasonable, full of mercy and good fruits, without partiality, and without hypocrisy.

verses.name: James 3:18
verses.text: Now the fruit of righteousness is sown in peace by those who make peace.

verses.name: James 4:1
verses.text: Where do wars and fightings among you come from? Don’t they come from your pleasures that war in your members?

verses.name: James 4:2
verses.text: You lust, and don’t have. You murder and covet, and can’t obtain. You fight and make war. You don’t have, because you don’t ask.

verses.name: James 4:3
verses.text: You ask, and don’t receive, because you ask with wrong motives, so that you may spend it on your pleasures.

verses.name: James 4:4
verses.text: You adulterers and adulteresses, don’t you know that friendship with the world is hostility toward God? Whoever therefore wants to be a friend of the world makes himself an enemy of God.

verses.name: James 4:5
verses.text: Or do you think that the Scripture says in vain, “The Spirit who lives in us yearns jealously”?

verses.name: James 4:6
verses.text: But he gives more grace. Therefore it says, “God resists the proud, but gives grace to the humble.”

verses.name: James 4:7
verses.text: Be subject therefore to God. Resist the devil, and he will flee from you.

verses.name: James 4:8
verses.text: Draw near to God, and he will draw near to you. Cleanse your hands, you sinners; and purify your hearts, you double-minded.

verses.name: James 4:9
verses.text: Lament, mourn, and weep. Let your laughter be turned to mourning, and your joy to gloom.

verses.name: James 4:10
verses.text: Humble yourselves in the sight of the Lord, and he will exalt you.

verses.name: James 4:11
verses.text: Don’t speak against one another, brothers. He who speaks against a brother and judges his brother, speaks against the law and judges the law. But if you judge the law, you are not a doer of the law, but a judge.

verses.name: James 4:12
verses.text: Only one is the lawgiver, who is able to save and to destroy. But who are you to judge another?

verses.name: James 4:13
verses.text: Come now, you who say, “Today or tomorrow let’s go into this city, and spend a year there, trade, and make a profit.”

verses.name: James 4:14
verses.text: Whereas you don’t know what your life will be like tomorrow. For what is your life? For you are a vapor, that appears for a little time, and then vanishes away.

verses.name: James 4:15
verses.text: For you ought to say, “If the Lord wills, we will both live, and do this or that.”

verses.name: James 4:16
verses.text: But now you glory in your boasting. All such boasting is evil.

verses.name: James 4:17
verses.text: To him therefore who knows to do good, and doesn’t do it, to him it is sin.

verses.name: James 5:1
verses.text: Come now, you rich, weep and howl for your miseries that are coming on you.

verses.name: James 5:2
verses.text: Your riches are corrupted and your garments are moth-eaten.

verses.name: James 5:3
verses.text: Your gold and your silver are corroded, and their corrosion will be for a testimony against you, and will eat your flesh like fire. You have laid up your treasure in the last days.

verses.name: James 5:4
verses.text: Behold, the wages of the laborers who mowed your fields, which you have kept back by fraud, cry out, and the cries of those who reaped have entered into the ears of the Lord of Armies.

verses.name: James 5:5
verses.text: You have lived in luxury on the earth, and taken your pleasure. You have nourished your hearts as in a day of slaughter.

verses.name: James 5:6
verses.text: You have condemned, you have murdered the righteous one. He doesn’t resist you.

verses.name: James 5:7
verses.text: Be patient therefore, brothers, until the coming of the Lord. Behold, the farmer waits for the precious fruit of the earth, being patient over it, until it receives the early and late rain.

verses.name: James 5:8
verses.text: You also be patient. Establish your hearts, for the coming of the Lord is at hand.

verses.name: James 5:9
verses.text: Don’t grumble, brothers, against one another, so that you won’t be judged. Behold, the judge stands at the door.

verses.name: James 5:10
verses.text: Take, brothers, for an example of suffering and of perseverance, the prophets who spoke in the name of the Lord.

verses.name: James 5:11
verses.text: Behold, we call them blessed who endured. You have heard of the perseverance of Job, and have seen the Lord in the outcome, and how the Lord is full of compassion and mercy.

verses.name: James 5:12
verses.text: But above all things, my brothers, don’t swear— not by heaven, or by the earth, or by any other oath; but let your “yes” be “yes”, and your “no”, “no”; so that you don’t fall into hypocrisy.

verses.name: James 5:13
verses.text: Is any among you suffering? Let him pray. Is any cheerful? Let him sing praises.

verses.name: James 5:14
verses.text: Is any among you sick? Let him call for the elders of the assembly, and let them pray over him, anointing him with oil in the name of the Lord,

verses.name: James 5:15
verses.text: and the prayer of faith will heal him who is sick, and the Lord will raise him up. If he has committed sins, he will be forgiven.

verses.name: James 5:16
verses.text: Confess your offenses to one another, and pray for one another, that you may be healed. The insistent prayer of a righteous person is powerfully effective.

verses.name: James 5:17
verses.text: Elijah was a man with a nature like ours, and he prayed earnestly that it might not rain, and it didn’t rain on the earth for three years and six months.

verses.name: James 5:18
verses.text: He prayed again, and the sky gave rain, and the earth produced its fruit.

verses.name: James 5:19
verses.text: Brothers, if any among you wanders from the truth and someone turns him back,

verses.name: James 5:20
verses.text: let him know that he who turns a sinner from the error of his way will save a soul from death and will cover a multitude of sins.

verses.name: 1 Peter 1:1
verses.text: Peter, an apostle of Jesus Christ, to the chosen ones who are living as foreigners in the Dispersion in Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, and Bithynia,

verses.name: 1 Peter 1:2
verses.text: according to the foreknowledge of God the Father, in sanctification of the Spirit, that you may obey Jesus Christ and be sprinkled with his blood: Grace to you and peace be multiplied.

verses.name: 1 Peter 1:3
verses.text: Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, who according to his great mercy caused us to be born again to a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead,

verses.name: 1 Peter 1:4
verses.text: to an incorruptible and undefiled inheritance that doesn’t fade away, reserved in Heaven for you,

verses.name: 1 Peter 1:5
verses.text: who by the power of God are guarded through faith for a salvation ready to be revealed in the last time.

verses.name: 1 Peter 1:6
verses.text: Wherein you greatly rejoice, though now for a little while, if need be, you have been put to grief in various trials,

verses.name: 1 Peter 1:7
verses.text: that the proof of your faith, which is more precious than gold that perishes even though it is tested by fire, may be found to result in praise, glory, and honor at the revelation of Jesus Christ—

verses.name: 1 Peter 1:8
verses.text: whom not having known you love; in whom, though now you don’t see him, yet believing, you rejoice greatly with joy unspeakable and full of glory—

verses.name: 1 Peter 1:9
verses.text: receiving the result of your faith, the salvation of your souls.

verses.name: 1 Peter 1:10
verses.text: Concerning this salvation, the prophets sought and searched diligently, who prophesied of the grace that would come to you,

verses.name: 1 Peter 1:11
verses.text: searching for who or what kind of time the Spirit of Christ, which was in them, pointed to, when he predicted the sufferings of Christ, and the glories that would follow them.

verses.name: 1 Peter 1:12
verses.text: To them it was revealed, that not to themselves, but to you, they ministered these things, which now have been announced to you through those who preached the Good News to you by the Holy Spirit sent out from heaven; which things angels desire to look into.

verses.name: 1 Peter 1:13
verses.text: Therefore prepare your minds for action, be sober, and set your hope fully on the grace that will be brought to you at the revelation of Jesus Christ—

verses.name: 1 Peter 1:14
verses.text: as children of obedience, not conforming yourselves according to your former lusts as in your ignorance,

verses.name: 1 Peter 1:15
verses.text: but just as he who called you is holy, you yourselves also be holy in all of your behavior;

verses.name: 1 Peter 1:16
verses.text: because it is written, “You shall be holy; for I am holy.”

verses.name: 1 Peter 1:17
verses.text: If you call on him as Father, who without respect of persons judges according to each man’s work, pass the time of your living as foreigners here in reverent fear:

verses.name: 1 Peter 1:18
verses.text: knowing that you were redeemed, not with corruptible things, with silver or gold, from the useless way of life handed down from your fathers,

verses.name: 1 Peter 1:19
verses.text: but with precious blood, as of a lamb without blemish or spot, the blood of Christ;

verses.name: 1 Peter 1:20
verses.text: who was foreknown indeed before the foundation of the world, but was revealed in this last age for your sake,

verses.name: 1 Peter 1:21
verses.text: who through him are believers in God, who raised him from the dead, and gave him glory; so that your faith and hope might be in God.

verses.name: 1 Peter 1:22
verses.text: Seeing you have purified your souls in your obedience to the truth through the Spirit in sincere brotherly affection, love one another from the heart fervently:

verses.name: 1 Peter 1:23
verses.text: having been born again, not of corruptible seed, but of incorruptible, through the word of God, which lives and remains forever.

verses.name: 1 Peter 1:24
verses.text: For, “All flesh is like grass, and all of man’s glory like the flower in the grass. The grass withers, and its flower falls;

verses.name: 1 Peter 1:25
verses.text: but the Lord’s word endures forever.” This is the word of Good News which was preached to you.

verses.name: 1 Peter 2:1
verses.text: Putting away therefore all wickedness, all deceit, hypocrisies, envies, and all evil speaking,

verses.name: 1 Peter 2:2
verses.text: as newborn babies, long for the pure milk of the Word, that with it you may grow,

verses.name: 1 Peter 2:3
verses.text: if indeed you have tasted that the Lord is gracious:

verses.name: 1 Peter 2:4
verses.text: coming to him, a living stone, rejected indeed by men, but chosen by God, precious.

verses.name: 1 Peter 2:5
verses.text: You also, as living stones, are built up as a spiritual house, to be a holy priesthood, to offer up spiritual sacrifices, acceptable to God through Jesus Christ.

verses.name: 1 Peter 2:6
verses.text: Because it is contained in Scripture, “Behold, I lay in Zion a chief cornerstone, chosen, and precious: He who believes in him will not be disappointed.”

verses.name: 1 Peter 2:7
verses.text: For you who believe therefore is the honor, but for those who are disobedient, “The stone which the builders rejected, has become the chief cornerstone,”

verses.name: 1 Peter 2:8
verses.text: and, “a stone of stumbling, and a rock of offense.” For they stumble at the word, being disobedient, to which also they were appointed.

verses.name: 1 Peter 2:9
verses.text: But you are a chosen race, a royal priesthood, a holy nation, a people for God’s own possession, that you may proclaim the excellence of him who called you out of darkness into his marvelous light:

verses.name: 1 Peter 2:10
verses.text: who in time past were no people, but now are God’s people, who had not obtained mercy, but now have obtained mercy.

verses.name: 1 Peter 2:11
verses.text: Beloved, I beg you as foreigners and pilgrims, to abstain from fleshly lusts, which war against the soul;

verses.name: 1 Peter 2:12
verses.text: having good behavior among the nations, so in that of which they speak against you as evildoers, they may by your good works, which they see, glorify God in the day of visitation.

verses.name: 1 Peter 2:13
verses.text: Therefore subject yourselves to every ordinance of man for the Lord’s sake: whether to the king, as supreme;

verses.name: 1 Peter 2:14
verses.text: or to governors, as sent by him for vengeance on evildoers and for praise to those who do well.

verses.name: 1 Peter 2:15
verses.text: For this is the will of God, that by well-doing you should put to silence the ignorance of foolish men:

verses.name: 1 Peter 2:16
