verses.text: as free, and not using your freedom for a cloak of wickedness, but as bondservants of God.

verses.name: 1 Peter 2:17
verses.text: Honor all men. Love the brotherhood. Fear God. Honor the king.

verses.name: 1 Peter 2:18
verses.text: Servants, be in subjection to your masters with all fear; not only to the good and gentle, but also to the wicked.

verses.name: 1 Peter 2:19
verses.text: For it is commendable if someone endures pain, suffering unjustly, because of conscience toward God.

verses.name: 1 Peter 2:20
verses.text: For what glory is it if, when you sin, you patiently endure beating? But if, when you do well, you patiently endure suffering, this is commendable with God.

verses.name: 1 Peter 2:21
verses.text: For to this you were called, because Christ also suffered for us, leaving you an example, that you should follow his steps,

verses.name: 1 Peter 2:22
verses.text: who did not sin, “neither was deceit found in his mouth.”

verses.name: 1 Peter 2:23
verses.text: Who, when he was cursed, didn’t curse back. When he suffered, didn’t threaten, but committed himself to him who judges righteously.

verses.name: 1 Peter 2:24
verses.text: He himself bore our sins in his body on the tree, that we, having died to sins, might live to righteousness; by whose stripes you were healed.

verses.name: 1 Peter 2:25
verses.text: For you were going astray like sheep; but now have returned to the Shepherd and Overseer of your souls.

verses.name: 1 Peter 3:1
verses.text: In the same way, wives, be in subjection to your own husbands; so that, even if any don’t obey the Word, they may be won by the behavior of their wives without a word;

verses.name: 1 Peter 3:2
verses.text: seeing your pure behavior in fear.

verses.name: 1 Peter 3:3
verses.text: Let your beauty be not just the outward adorning of braiding the hair, and of wearing jewels of gold, or of putting on fine clothing;

verses.name: 1 Peter 3:4
verses.text: but in the hidden person of the heart, in the incorruptible adornment of a gentle and quiet spirit, which is in the sight of God very precious.

verses.name: 1 Peter 3:5
verses.text: For this is how the holy women before, who hoped in God also adorned themselves, being in subjection to their own husbands:

verses.name: 1 Peter 3:6
verses.text: as Sarah obeyed Abraham, calling him lord, whose children you now are, if you do well, and are not put in fear by any terror.

verses.name: 1 Peter 3:7
verses.text: You husbands, in the same way, live with your wives according to knowledge, giving honor to the woman, as to the weaker vessel, as being also joint heirs of the grace of life; that your prayers may not be hindered.

verses.name: 1 Peter 3:8
verses.text: Finally, be all like-minded, compassionate, loving as brothers, tender hearted, courteous,

verses.name: 1 Peter 3:9
verses.text: not rendering evil for evil, or insult for insult; but instead blessing; knowing that to this were you called, that you may inherit a blessing.

verses.name: 1 Peter 3:10
verses.text: For, “He who would love life, and see good days, let him keep his tongue from evil, and his lips from speaking deceit.

verses.name: 1 Peter 3:11
verses.text: Let him turn away from evil, and do good. Let him seek peace, and pursue it.

verses.name: 1 Peter 3:12
verses.text: For the eyes of the Lord are on the righteous, and his ears open to their prayer; but the face of the Lord is against those who do evil.”

verses.name: 1 Peter 3:13
verses.text: Now who is he who will harm you, if you become imitators of that which is good?

verses.name: 1 Peter 3:14
verses.text: But even if you should suffer for righteousness’ sake, you are blessed. “Don’t fear what they fear, neither be troubled.”

verses.name: 1 Peter 3:15
verses.text: But sanctify the Lord God in your hearts; and always be ready to give an answer to everyone who asks you a reason concerning the hope that is in you, with humility and fear:

verses.name: 1 Peter 3:16
verses.text: having a good conscience; that, while you are spoken against as evildoers, they may be disappointed who curse your good way of life in Christ.

verses.name: 1 Peter 3:17
verses.text: For it is better, if it is God’s will, that you suffer for doing well than for doing evil.

verses.name: 1 Peter 3:18
verses.text: Because Christ also suffered for sins once, the righteous for the unrighteous, that he might bring you to God; being put to death in the flesh, but made alive in the spirit;

verses.name: 1 Peter 3:19
verses.text: in which he also went and preached to the spirits in prison,

verses.name: 1 Peter 3:20
verses.text: who before were disobedient, when God waited patiently in the days of Noah, while the ship was being built. In it, few, that is, eight souls, were saved through water.

verses.name: 1 Peter 3:21
verses.text: This is a symbol of baptism, which now saves you—not the putting away of the filth of the flesh, but the answer of a good conscience toward God, through the resurrection of Jesus Christ,

verses.name: 1 Peter 3:22
verses.text: who is at the right hand of God, having gone into heaven, angels and authorities and powers being made subject to him.

verses.name: 1 Peter 4:1
verses.text: Therefore, since Christ suffered for us in the flesh, arm yourselves also with the same mind; for he who has suffered in the flesh has ceased from sin;

verses.name: 1 Peter 4:2
verses.text: that you no longer should live the rest of your time in the flesh for the lusts of men, but for the will of God.

verses.name: 1 Peter 4:3
verses.text: For we have spent enough of our past time doing the desire of the Gentiles, and having walked in lewdness, lusts, drunken binges, orgies, carousings, and abominable idolatries.

verses.name: 1 Peter 4:4
verses.text: They think it is strange that you don’t run with them into the same excess of riot, blaspheming:

verses.name: 1 Peter 4:5
verses.text: who will give account to him who is ready to judge the living and the dead.

verses.name: 1 Peter 4:6
verses.text: For to this end the Good News was preached even to the dead, that they might be judged indeed as men in the flesh, but live as to God in the spirit.

verses.name: 1 Peter 4:7
verses.text: But the end of all things is near. Therefore be of sound mind, self-controlled, and sober in prayer.

verses.name: 1 Peter 4:8
verses.text: And above all things be earnest in your love among yourselves, for love covers a multitude of sins.

verses.name: 1 Peter 4:9
verses.text: Be hospitable to one another without grumbling.

verses.name: 1 Peter 4:10
verses.text: As each has received a gift, employ it in serving one another, as good managers of the grace of God in its various forms.

verses.name: 1 Peter 4:11
verses.text: If anyone speaks, let it be as it were the very words of God. If anyone serves, let it be as of the strength which God supplies, that in all things God may be glorified through Jesus Christ, to whom belong the glory and the dominion forever and ever. Amen.

verses.name: 1 Peter 4:12
verses.text: Beloved, don’t be astonished at the fiery trial which has come upon you, to test you, as though a strange thing happened to you.

verses.name: 1 Peter 4:13
verses.text: But because you are partakers of Christ’s sufferings, rejoice; that at the revelation of his glory you also may rejoice with exceeding joy.

verses.name: 1 Peter 4:14
verses.text: If you are insulted for the name of Christ, you are blessed; because the Spirit of glory and of God rests on you. On their part he is blasphemed, but on your part he is glorified.

verses.name: 1 Peter 4:15
verses.text: For let none of you suffer as a murderer, or a thief, or an evil doer, or a meddler in other men’s matters.

verses.name: 1 Peter 4:16
verses.text: But if one of you suffers for being a Christian, let him not be ashamed; but let him glorify God in this matter.

verses.name: 1 Peter 4:17
verses.text: For the time has come for judgment to begin with the household of God. If it begins first with us, what will happen to those who don’t obey the Good News of God?

verses.name: 1 Peter 4:18
verses.text: “If it is hard for the righteous to be saved, what will happen to the ungodly and the sinner?”

verses.name: 1 Peter 4:19
verses.text: Therefore let them also who suffer according to the will of God in doing good entrust their souls to him, as to a faithful Creator.

verses.name: 1 Peter 5:1
verses.text: Therefore I exhort the elders among you, as a fellow elder, and a witness of the sufferings of Christ, and who will also share in the glory that will be revealed.

verses.name: 1 Peter 5:2
verses.text: Shepherd the flock of God which is among you, exercising the oversight, not under compulsion, but voluntarily, not for dishonest gain, but willingly;

verses.name: 1 Peter 5:3
verses.text: neither as lording it over those entrusted to you, but making yourselves examples to the flock.

verses.name: 1 Peter 5:4
verses.text: When the chief Shepherd is revealed, you will receive the crown of glory that doesn’t fade away.

verses.name: 1 Peter 5:5
verses.text: Likewise, you younger ones, be subject to the elder. Yes, all of you clothe yourselves with humility, to subject yourselves to one another; for “God resists the proud, but gives grace to the humble.”

verses.name: 1 Peter 5:6
verses.text: Humble yourselves therefore under the mighty hand of God, that he may exalt you in due time;

verses.name: 1 Peter 5:7
verses.text: casting all your worries on him, because he cares for you.

verses.name: 1 Peter 5:8
verses.text: Be sober and self-controlled. Be watchful. Your adversary, the devil, walks around like a roaring lion, seeking whom he may devour.

verses.name: 1 Peter 5:9
verses.text: Withstand him steadfast in your faith, knowing that your brothers who are in the world are undergoing the same sufferings.

verses.name: 1 Peter 5:10
verses.text: But may the God of all grace, who called you to his eternal glory by Christ Jesus, after you have suffered a little while, perfect, establish, strengthen, and settle you.

verses.name: 1 Peter 5:11
verses.text: To him be the glory and the power forever and ever. Amen.

verses.name: 1 Peter 5:12
verses.text: Through Silvanus, our faithful brother, as I consider him, I have written to you briefly, exhorting, and testifying that this is the true grace of God in which you stand.

verses.name: 1 Peter 5:13
verses.text: She who is in Babylon, chosen together with you, greets you; and so does Mark, my son.

verses.name: 1 Peter 5:14
verses.text: Greet one another with a kiss of love. Peace be to you all who are in Christ Jesus. Amen.

verses.name: 2 Peter 1:1
verses.text: Simon Peter, a servant and apostle of Jesus Christ, to those who have obtained a like precious faith with us in the righteousness of our God and Savior, Jesus Christ:

verses.name: 2 Peter 1:2
verses.text: Grace to you and peace be multiplied in the knowledge of God and of Jesus our Lord,

verses.name: 2 Peter 1:3
verses.text: seeing that his divine power has granted to us all things that pertain to life and godliness, through the knowledge of him who called us by his own glory and virtue;

verses.name: 2 Peter 1:4
verses.text: by which he has granted to us his precious and exceedingly great promises; that through these you may become partakers of the divine nature, having escaped from the corruption that is in the world by lust.

verses.name: 2 Peter 1:5
verses.text: Yes, and for this very cause adding on your part all diligence, in your faith supply moral excellence; and in moral excellence, knowledge;

verses.name: 2 Peter 1:6
verses.text: and in knowledge, self-control; and in self-control perseverance; and in perseverance godliness;

verses.name: 2 Peter 1:7
verses.text: and in godliness brotherly affection; and in brotherly affection, love.

verses.name: 2 Peter 1:8
verses.text: For if these things are yours and abound, they make you to be not idle or unfruitful in the knowledge of our Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.name: 2 Peter 1:9
verses.text: For he who lacks these things is blind, seeing only what is near, having forgotten the cleansing from his old sins.

verses.name: 2 Peter 1:10
verses.text: Therefore, brothers, be more diligent to make your calling and election sure. For if you do these things, you will never stumble.

verses.name: 2 Peter 1:11
verses.text: For thus you will be richly supplied with the entrance into the eternal Kingdom of our Lord and Savior, Jesus Christ.

verses.name: 2 Peter 1:12
verses.text: Therefore I will not be negligent to remind you of these things, though you know them, and are established in the present truth.

verses.name: 2 Peter 1:13
verses.text: I think it right, as long as I am in this tent, to stir you up by reminding you;

verses.name: 2 Peter 1:14
verses.text: knowing that the putting off of my tent comes swiftly, even as our Lord Jesus Christ made clear to me.

verses.name: 2 Peter 1:15
verses.text: Yes, I will make every effort that you may always be able to remember these things even after my departure.

verses.name: 2 Peter 1:16
verses.text: For we did not follow cunningly devised fables, when we made known to you the power and coming of our Lord Jesus Christ, but we were eyewitnesses of his majesty.

verses.name: 2 Peter 1:17
verses.text: For he received from God the Father honor and glory, when the voice came to him from the Majestic Glory, “This is my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased.”

verses.name: 2 Peter 1:18
verses.text: We heard this voice come out of heaven when we were with him on the holy mountain.

verses.name: 2 Peter 1:19
verses.text: We have the more sure word of prophecy; and you do well that you heed it, as to a lamp shining in a dark place, until the day dawns, and the morning star arises in your hearts:

verses.name: 2 Peter 1:20
verses.text: knowing this first, that no prophecy of Scripture is of private interpretation.

verses.name: 2 Peter 1:21
verses.text: For no prophecy ever came by the will of man: but holy men of God spoke, being moved by the Holy Spirit.

verses.name: 2 Peter 2:1
verses.text: But false prophets also arose among the people, as false teachers will also be among you, who will secretly bring in destructive heresies, denying even the Master who bought them, bringing on themselves swift destruction.

verses.name: 2 Peter 2:2
verses.text: Many will follow their immoral ways, and as a result, the way of the truth will be maligned.

verses.name: 2 Peter 2:3
verses.text: In covetousness they will exploit you with deceptive words: whose sentence now from of old doesn’t linger, and their destruction will not slumber.

verses.name: 2 Peter 2:4
verses.text: For if God didn’t spare angels when they sinned, but cast them down to Tartarus, and committed them to pits of darkness, to be reserved for judgment;

verses.name: 2 Peter 2:5
verses.text: and didn’t spare the ancient world, but preserved Noah with seven others, a preacher of righteousness, when he brought a flood on the world of the ungodly;

verses.name: 2 Peter 2:6
verses.text: and turning the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah into ashes, condemned them to destruction, having made them an example to those who would live in an ungodly way;

verses.name: 2 Peter 2:7
verses.text: and delivered righteous Lot, who was very distressed by the lustful life of the wicked

verses.name: 2 Peter 2:8
verses.text: (for that righteous man dwelling among them, was tormented in his righteous soul from day to day with seeing and hearing lawless deeds):

verses.name: 2 Peter 2:9
verses.text: the Lord knows how to deliver the godly out of temptation and to keep the unrighteous under punishment for the day of judgment;

verses.name: 2 Peter 2:10
verses.text: but chiefly those who walk after the flesh in the lust of defilement, and despise authority. Daring, self-willed, they are not afraid to speak evil of dignitaries;

verses.name: 2 Peter 2:11
verses.text: whereas angels, though greater in might and power, don’t bring a railing judgment against them before the Lord.

verses.name: 2 Peter 2:12
verses.text: But these, as unreasoning creatures, born natural animals to be taken and destroyed, speaking evil in matters about which they are ignorant, will in their destroying surely be destroyed,

verses.name: 2 Peter 2:13
verses.text: receiving the wages of unrighteousness; people who count it pleasure to revel in the daytime, spots and defects, reveling in their deceit while they feast with you;

verses.name: 2 Peter 2:14
verses.text: having eyes full of adultery, and who can’t cease from sin; enticing unsettled souls; having a heart trained in greed; children of cursing;

verses.name: 2 Peter 2:15
verses.text: forsaking the right way, they went astray, having followed the way of Balaam the son of Beor, who loved the wages of wrongdoing;

verses.name: 2 Peter 2:16
verses.text: but he was rebuked for his own disobedience. A mute donkey spoke with a man’s voice and stopped the madness of the prophet.

verses.name: 2 Peter 2:17
verses.text: These are wells without water, clouds driven by a storm; for whom the blackness of darkness has been reserved forever.

verses.name: 2 Peter 2:18
verses.text: For, uttering great swelling words of emptiness, they entice in the lusts of the flesh, by licentiousness, those who are indeed escaping from those who live in error;

verses.name: 2 Peter 2:19
verses.text: promising them liberty, while they themselves are bondservants of corruption; for a man is brought into bondage by whoever overcomes him.

verses.name: 2 Peter 2:20
verses.text: For if, after they have escaped the defilement of the world through the knowledge of the Lord and Savior Jesus Christ, they are again entangled in it and overcome, the last state has become worse for them than the first.

verses.name: 2 Peter 2:21
verses.text: For it would be better for them not to have known the way of righteousness, than, after knowing it, to turn back from the holy commandment delivered to them.

verses.name: 2 Peter 2:22
verses.text: But it has happened to them according to the true proverb, “The dog turns to his own vomit again,” and “the sow that has washed to wallowing in the mire.”

verses.name: 2 Peter 3:1
verses.text: This is now, beloved, the second letter that I have written to you; and in both of them I stir up your sincere mind by reminding you;

verses.name: 2 Peter 3:2
verses.text: that you should remember the words which were spoken before by the holy prophets, and the commandment of us, the apostles of the Lord and Savior:

verses.name: 2 Peter 3:3
verses.text: knowing this first, that in the last days mockers will come, walking after their own lusts,

verses.name: 2 Peter 3:4
verses.text: and saying, “Where is the promise of his coming? For, from the day that the fathers fell asleep, all things continue as they were from the beginning of the creation.”

verses.name: 2 Peter 3:5
verses.text: For this they willfully forget, that there were heavens from of old, and an earth formed out of water and amid water, by the word of God;

verses.name: 2 Peter 3:6
verses.text: by which means the world that then was, being overflowed with water, perished.

verses.name: 2 Peter 3:7
verses.text: But the heavens that now exist, and the earth, by the same word have been stored up for fire, being reserved against the day of judgment and destruction of ungodly men.

verses.name: 2 Peter 3:8
verses.text: But don’t forget this one thing, beloved, that one day is with the Lord as a thousand years, and a thousand years as one day.

verses.name: 2 Peter 3:9
verses.text: The Lord is not slow concerning his promise, as some count slowness; but is patient with us, not wishing that any should perish, but that all should come to repentance.

verses.name: 2 Peter 3:10
verses.text: But the day of the Lord will come as a thief in the night; in which the heavens will pass away with a great noise, and the elements will be dissolved with fervent heat, and the earth and the works that are in it will be burned up.

verses.name: 2 Peter 3:11
verses.text: Therefore since all these things will be destroyed like this, what kind of people ought you to be in holy living and godliness,

verses.name: 2 Peter 3:12
verses.text: looking for and earnestly desiring the coming of the day of God, which will cause the burning heavens to be dissolved, and the elements will melt with fervent heat?

verses.name: 2 Peter 3:13
verses.text: But, according to his promise, we look for new heavens and a new earth, in which righteousness dwells.

verses.name: 2 Peter 3:14
verses.text: Therefore, beloved, seeing that you look for these things, be diligent to be found in peace, without defect and blameless in his sight.

verses.name: 2 Peter 3:15
verses.text: Regard the patience of our Lord as salvation; even as our beloved brother Paul also, according to the wisdom given to him, wrote to you;

verses.name: 2 Peter 3:16
verses.text: as also in all of his letters, speaking in them of these things. In those, there are some things that are hard to understand, which the ignorant and unsettled twist, as they also do to the other Scriptures, to their own destruction.

verses.name: 2 Peter 3:17
verses.text: You therefore, beloved, knowing these things beforehand, beware, lest being carried away with the error of the wicked, you fall from your own steadfastness.

verses.name: 2 Peter 3:18
verses.text: But grow in the grace and knowledge of our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ. To him be the glory both now and forever. Amen.

verses.name: 1 John 1:1
verses.text: That which was from the beginning, that which we have heard, that which we have seen with our eyes, that which we saw, and our hands touched, concerning the Word of life

verses.name: 1 John 1:2
verses.text: (and the life was revealed, and we have seen, and testify, and declare to you the life, the eternal life, which was with the Father, and was revealed to us);

verses.name: 1 John 1:3
verses.text: that which we have seen and heard we declare to you, that you also may have fellowship with us. Yes, and our fellowship is with the Father, and with his Son, Jesus Christ.

verses.name: 1 John 1:4
verses.text: And we write these things to you, that our joy may be fulfilled.

verses.name: 1 John 1:5
verses.text: This is the message which we have heard from him and announce to you, that God is light, and in him is no darkness at all.

verses.name: 1 John 1:6
verses.text: If we say that we have fellowship with him and walk in the darkness, we lie, and don’t tell the truth.

verses.name: 1 John 1:7
verses.text: But if we walk in the light, as he is in the light, we have fellowship with one another, and the blood of Jesus Christ, his Son, cleanses us from all sin.

verses.name: 1 John 1:8
verses.text: If we say that we have no sin, we deceive ourselves, and the truth is not in us.

verses.name: 1 John 1:9
verses.text: If we confess our sins, he is faithful and righteous to forgive us the sins, and to cleanse us from all unrighteousness.

verses.name: 1 John 1:10
verses.text: If we say that we haven’t sinned, we make him a liar, and his word is not in us.

verses.name: 1 John 2:1
verses.text: My little children, I write these things to you so that you may not sin. If anyone sins, we have a Counselor with the Father, Jesus Christ, the righteous.

verses.name: 1 John 2:2
verses.text: And he is the atoning sacrifice for our sins, and not for ours only, but also for the whole world.

verses.name: 1 John 2:3
verses.text: This is how we know that we know him: if we keep his commandments.

verses.name: 1 John 2:4
verses.text: One who says, “I know him,” and doesn’t keep his commandments, is a liar, and the truth isn’t in him.

verses.name: 1 John 2:5
verses.text: But whoever keeps his word, God’s love has most certainly been perfected in him. This is how we know that we are in him:

verses.name: 1 John 2:6
verses.text: he who says he remains in him ought himself also to walk just like he walked.

verses.name: 1 John 2:7
verses.text: Brothers, I write no new commandment to you, but an old commandment which you had from the beginning. The old commandment is the word which you heard from the beginning.

verses.name: 1 John 2:8
verses.text: Again, I write a new commandment to you, which is true in him and in you; because the darkness is passing away, and the true light already shines.

verses.name: 1 John 2:9
verses.text: He who says he is in the light and hates his brother, is in the darkness even until now.

verses.name: 1 John 2:10
verses.text: He who loves his brother remains in the light, and there is no occasion for stumbling in him.

verses.name: 1 John 2:11
verses.text: But he who hates his brother is in the darkness, and walks in the darkness, and doesn’t know where he is going, because the darkness has blinded his eyes.

verses.name: 1 John 2:12
verses.text: I write to you, little children, because your sins are forgiven you for his name’s sake.

verses.name: 1 John 2:13
verses.text: I write to you, fathers, because you know him who is from the beginning. I write to you, young men, because you have overcome the evil one. I write to you, little children, because you know the Father.

verses.name: 1 John 2:14
verses.text: I have written to you, fathers, because you know him who is from the beginning. I have written to you, young men, because you are strong, and the word of God remains in you, and you have overcome the evil one.

verses.name: 1 John 2:15
verses.text: Don’t love the world or the things that are in the world. If anyone loves the world, the Father’s love isn’t in him.

verses.name: 1 John 2:16
verses.text: For all that is in the world, the lust of the flesh, the lust of the eyes, and the pride of life, isn’t the Father’s, but is the world’s.

verses.name: 1 John 2:17
verses.text: The world is passing away with its lusts, but he who does God’s will remains forever.

verses.name: 1 John 2:18
verses.text: Little children, these are the end times, and as you heard that the Antichrist is coming, even now many antichrists have arisen. By this we know that it is the final hour.

verses.name: 1 John 2:19
verses.text: They went out from us, but they didn’t belong to us; for if they had belonged to us, they would have continued with us. But they left, that they might be revealed that none of them belong to us.

verses.name: 1 John 2:20
verses.text: You have an anointing from the Holy One, and you all have knowledge.

verses.name: 1 John 2:21
verses.text: I have not written to you because you don’t know the truth, but because you know it, and because no lie is of the truth.

verses.name: 1 John 2:22
verses.text: Who is the liar but he who denies that Jesus is the Christ? This is the Antichrist, he who denies the Father and the Son.

verses.name: 1 John 2:23
verses.text: Whoever denies the Son, the same doesn’t have the Father. He who confesses the Son has the Father also.

verses.name: 1 John 2:24
verses.text: Therefore, as for you, let that remain in you which you heard from the beginning. If that which you heard from the beginning remains in you, you also will remain in the Son, and in the Father.

verses.name: 1 John 2:25
verses.text: This is the promise which he promised us, the eternal life.

verses.name: 1 John 2:26
verses.text: These things I have written to you concerning those who would lead you astray.

verses.name: 1 John 2:27
verses.text: As for you, the anointing which you received from him remains in you, and you don’t need for anyone to teach you. But as his anointing teaches you concerning all things, and is true, and is no lie, and even as it taught you, you will remain in him.

verses.name: 1 John 2:28
verses.text: Now, little children, remain in him, that when he appears, we may have boldness, and not be ashamed before him at his coming.

verses.name: 1 John 2:29
verses.text: If you know that he is righteous, you know that everyone who practices righteousness has been born of him.

verses.name: 1 John 3:1
verses.text: See how great a love the Father has given to us, that we should be called children of God! For this cause the world doesn’t know us, because it didn’t know him.

verses.name: 1 John 3:2
verses.text: Beloved, now we are children of God, and it is not yet revealed what we will be. But we know that, when he is revealed, we will be like him; for we will see him just as he is.

verses.name: 1 John 3:3
verses.text: Everyone who has this hope set on him purifies himself, even as he is pure.

verses.name: 1 John 3:4
verses.text: Everyone who sins also commits lawlessness. Sin is lawlessness.

verses.name: 1 John 3:5
verses.text: You know that he was revealed to take away our sins, and in him is no sin.

verses.name: 1 John 3:6
verses.text: Whoever remains in him doesn’t sin. Whoever sins hasn’t seen him and doesn’t know him.

verses.name: 1 John 3:7
verses.text: Little children, let no one lead you astray. He who does righteousness is righteous, even as he is righteous.

verses.name: 1 John 3:8
verses.text: He who sins is of the devil, for the devil has been sinning from the beginning. To this end the Son of God was revealed: that he might destroy the works of the devil.

verses.name: 1 John 3:9
verses.text: Whoever is born of God doesn’t commit sin, because his seed remains in him; and he can’t sin, because he is born of God.

verses.name: 1 John 3:10
verses.text: In this the children of God are revealed, and the children of the devil. Whoever doesn’t do righteousness is not of God, neither is he who doesn’t love his brother.

verses.name: 1 John 3:11
verses.text: For this is the message which you heard from the beginning, that we should love one another;

verses.name: 1 John 3:12
verses.text: unlike Cain, who was of the evil one, and killed his brother. Why did he kill him? Because his deeds were evil, and his brother’s righteous.

verses.name: 1 John 3:13
verses.text: Don’t be surprised, my brothers, if the world hates you.

verses.name: 1 John 3:14
verses.text: We know that we have passed out of death into life, because we love the brothers. He who doesn’t love his brother remains in death.

verses.name: 1 John 3:15
verses.text: Whoever hates his brother is a murderer, and you know that no murderer has eternal life remaining in him.

verses.name: 1 John 3:16
verses.text: By this we know love, because he laid down his life for us. And we ought to lay down our lives for the brothers.

verses.name: 1 John 3:17
verses.text: But whoever has the world’s goods, and sees his brother in need, and closes his heart of compassion against him, how does God’s love remain in him?

verses.name: 1 John 3:18
verses.text: My little children, let’s not love in word only, or with the tongue only, but in deed and truth.

verses.name: 1 John 3:19
verses.text: And by this we know that we are of the truth, and persuade our hearts before him,

verses.name: 1 John 3:20
verses.text: because if our heart condemns us, God is greater than our heart, and knows all things.

verses.name: 1 John 3:21
verses.text: Beloved, if our hearts don’t condemn us, we have boldness toward God;

verses.name: 1 John 3:22
verses.text: and whatever we ask, we receive from him, because we keep his commandments and do the things that are pleasing in his sight.

verses.name: 1 John 3:23
verses.text: This is his commandment, that we should believe in the name of his Son, Jesus Christ, and love one another, even as he commanded.

verses.name: 1 John 3:24
verses.text: He who keeps his commandments remains in him, and he in him. By this we know that he remains in us, by the Spirit which he gave us.

verses.name: 1 John 4:1
verses.text: Beloved, don’t believe every spirit, but test the spirits, whether they are of God, because many false prophets have gone out into the world.

verses.name: 1 John 4:2
verses.text: By this you know the Spirit of God: every spirit who confesses that Jesus Christ has come in the flesh is of God,

verses.name: 1 John 4:3
verses.text: and every spirit who doesn’t confess that Jesus Christ has come in the flesh is not of God, and this is the spirit of the Antichrist, of whom you have heard that it comes. Now it is in the world already.

verses.name: 1 John 4:4
verses.text: You are of God, little children, and have overcome them; because greater is he who is in you than he who is in the world.

verses.name: 1 John 4:5
verses.text: They are of the world. Therefore they speak of the world, and the world hears them.

verses.name: 1 John 4:6
verses.text: We are of God. He who knows God listens to us. He who is not of God doesn’t listen to us. By this we know the spirit of truth, and the spirit of error.

verses.name: 1 John 4:7
verses.text: Beloved, let us love one another, for love is of God; and everyone who loves has been born of God, and knows God.

verses.name: 1 John 4:8
verses.text: He who doesn’t love doesn’t know God, for God is love.

verses.name: 1 John 4:9
verses.text: By this God’s love was revealed in us, that God has sent his one and only Son into the world that we might live through him.

verses.name: 1 John 4:10
verses.text: In this is love, not that we loved God, but that he loved us, and sent his Son as the atoning sacrifice for our sins.

verses.name: 1 John 4:11
verses.text: Beloved, if God loved us in this way, we also ought to love one another.

verses.name: 1 John 4:12
verses.text: No one has seen God at any time. If we love one another, God remains in us, and his love has been perfected in us.

verses.name: 1 John 4:13
verses.text: By this we know that we remain in him and he in us, because he has given us of his Spirit.

verses.name: 1 John 4:14
verses.text: We have seen and testify that the Father has sent the Son as the Savior of the world.

verses.name: 1 John 4:15
verses.text: Whoever confesses that Jesus is the Son of God, God remains in him, and he in God.

verses.name: 1 John 4:16
verses.text: We know and have believed the love which God has for us. God is love, and he who remains in love remains in God, and God remains in him.

verses.name: 1 John 4:17
verses.text: In this love has been made perfect among us, that we may have boldness in the day of judgment, because as he is, even so are we in this world.

verses.name: 1 John 4:18
verses.text: There is no fear in love; but perfect love casts out fear, because fear has punishment. He who fears is not made perfect in love.

verses.name: 1 John 4:19
verses.text: We love him, because he first loved us.

verses.name: 1 John 4:20
verses.text: If a man says, “I love God,” and hates his brother, he is a liar; for he who doesn’t love his brother whom he has seen, how can he love God whom he has not seen?

verses.name: 1 John 4:21
verses.text: This commandment we have from him, that he who loves God should also love his brother.

verses.name: 1 John 5:1
verses.text: Whoever believes that Jesus is the Christ has been born of God. Whoever loves the Father also loves the child who is born of him.

verses.name: 1 John 5:2
verses.text: By this we know that we love the children of God, when we love God and keep his commandments.

verses.name: 1 John 5:3
verses.text: For this is loving God, that we keep his commandments. His commandments are not grievous.

verses.name: 1 John 5:4
verses.text: For whatever is born of God overcomes the world. This is the victory that has overcome the world: your faith.

verses.name: 1 John 5:5
verses.text: Who is he who overcomes the world, but he who believes that Jesus is the Son of God?

verses.name: 1 John 5:6
verses.text: This is he who came by water and blood, Jesus Christ; not with the water only, but with the water and the blood. It is the Spirit who testifies, because the Spirit is the truth.

verses.name: 1 John 5:7
verses.text: For there are three who testify:

verses.name: 1 John 5:8
verses.text: the Spirit, the water, and the blood; and the three agree as one.

verses.name: 1 John 5:9
verses.text: If we receive the witness of men, the witness of God is greater; for this is God’s testimony which he has testified concerning his Son.

verses.name: 1 John 5:10
verses.text: He who believes in the Son of God has the testimony in himself. He who doesn’t believe God has made him a liar, because he has not believed in the testimony that God has given concerning his Son.

verses.name: 1 John 5:11
verses.text: The testimony is this, that God gave to us eternal life, and this life is in his Son.

verses.name: 1 John 5:12
verses.text: He who has the Son has the life. He who doesn’t have God’s Son doesn’t have the life.

verses.name: 1 John 5:13
verses.text: These things I have written to you who believe in the name of the Son of God, that you may know that you have eternal life, and that you may continue to believe in the name of the Son of God.

verses.name: 1 John 5:14
verses.text: This is the boldness which we have toward him, that, if we ask anything according to his will, he listens to us.

verses.name: 1 John 5:15
verses.text: And if we know that he listens to us, whatever we ask, we know that we have the petitions which we have asked of him.

verses.name: 1 John 5:16
verses.text: If anyone sees his brother sinning a sin not leading to death, he shall ask, and God will give him life for those who sin not leading to death. There is a sin leading to death. I don’t say that he should make a request concerning this.

verses.name: 1 John 5:17
verses.text: All unrighteousness is sin, and there is a sin not leading to death.

verses.name: 1 John 5:18
verses.text: We know that whoever is born of God doesn’t sin, but he who was born of God keeps himself, and the evil one doesn’t touch him.

verses.name: 1 John 5:19
verses.text: We know that we are of God, and the whole world lies in the power of the evil one.

verses.name: 1 John 5:20
verses.text: We know that the Son of God has come, and has given us an understanding, that we know him who is true, and we are in him who is true, in his Son Jesus Christ. This is the true God, and eternal life.

verses.name: 1 John 5:21
verses.text: Little children, keep yourselves from idols.

verses.name: 2 John 1:1
verses.text: The elder, to the chosen lady and her children, whom I love in truth; and not I only, but also all those who know the truth;

verses.name: 2 John 1:2
verses.text: for the truth’s sake, which remains in us, and it will be with us forever:

verses.name: 2 John 1:3
verses.text: Grace, mercy, and peace will be with us, from God the Father, and from the Lord Jesus Christ, the Son of the Father, in truth and love.

verses.name: 2 John 1:4
verses.text: I rejoice greatly that I have found some of your children walking in truth, even as we have been commanded by the Father.

verses.name: 2 John 1:5
verses.text: Now I beg you, dear lady, not as though I wrote to you a new commandment, but that which we had from the beginning, that we love one another.

verses.name: 2 John 1:6
verses.text: This is love, that we should walk according to his commandments. This is the commandment, even as you heard from the beginning, that you should walk in it.

verses.name: 2 John 1:7
verses.text: For many deceivers have gone out into the world, those who don’t confess that Jesus Christ came in the flesh. This is the deceiver and the Antichrist.

verses.name: 2 John 1:8
verses.text: Watch yourselves, that we don’t lose the things which we have accomplished, but that we receive a full reward.

verses.name: 2 John 1:9
verses.text: Whoever transgresses and doesn’t remain in the teaching of Christ, doesn’t have God. He who remains in the teaching, the same has both the Father and the Son.

verses.name: 2 John 1:10
verses.text: If anyone comes to you, and doesn’t bring this teaching, don’t receive him into your house, and don’t welcome him,

verses.name: 2 John 1:11
verses.text: for he who welcomes him participates in his evil deeds.

verses.name: 2 John 1:12
verses.text: Having many things to write to you, I don’t want to do so with paper and ink, but I hope to come to you, and to speak face to face, that our joy may be made full.

verses.name: 2 John 1:13
verses.text: The children of your chosen sister greet you. Amen.

verses.name: 3 John 1:1
verses.text: The elder to Gaius the beloved, whom I love in truth.

verses.name: 3 John 1:2
verses.text: Beloved, I pray that you may prosper in all things and be healthy, even as your soul prospers.

verses.name: 3 John 1:3
verses.text: For I rejoiced greatly, when brothers came and testified about your truth, even as you walk in truth.

verses.name: 3 John 1:4
verses.text: I have no greater joy than this, to hear about my children walking in truth.

verses.name: 3 John 1:5
verses.text: Beloved, you do a faithful work in whatever you accomplish for those who are brothers and strangers.

verses.name: 3 John 1:6
verses.text: They have testified about your love before the assembly. You will do well to send them forward on their journey in a way worthy of God,

verses.name: 3 John 1:7
verses.text: because for the sake of the Name they went out, taking nothing from the Gentiles.

verses.name: 3 John 1:8
verses.text: We therefore ought to receive such, that we may be fellow workers for the truth.

verses.name: 3 John 1:9
verses.text: I wrote to the assembly, but Diotrephes, who loves to be first among them, doesn’t accept what we say.

verses.name: 3 John 1:10
verses.text: Therefore if I come, I will call attention to his deeds which he does, unjustly accusing us with wicked words. Not content with this, neither does he himself receive the brothers, and those who would, he forbids and throws out of the assembly.

verses.name: 3 John 1:11
verses.text: Beloved, don’t imitate that which is evil, but that which is good. He who does good is of God. He who does evil hasn’t seen God.

verses.name: 3 John 1:12
verses.text: Demetrius has the testimony of all, and of the truth itself; yes, we also testify, and you know that our testimony is true.

verses.name: 3 John 1:13
verses.text: I had many things to write to you, but I am unwilling to write to you with ink and pen;

verses.name: 3 John 1:14
verses.text: but I hope to see you soon, and we will speak face to face. Peace be to you. The friends greet you. Greet the friends by name.

verses.name: Jude 1:1
verses.text: Jude, a servant of Jesus Christ, and brother of James, to those who are called, sanctified by God the Father, and kept for Jesus Christ:

verses.name: Jude 1:2
verses.text: Mercy to you and peace and love be multiplied.

verses.name: Jude 1:3
verses.text: Beloved, while I was very eager to write to you about our common salvation, I was constrained to write to you exhorting you to contend earnestly for the faith which was once for all delivered to the saints.

verses.name: Jude 1:4
verses.text: For there are certain men who crept in secretly, even those who were long ago written about for this condemnation: ungodly men, turning the grace of our God into indecency, and denying our only Master, God, and Lord, Jesus Christ.

verses.name: Jude 1:5
verses.text: Now I desire to remind you, though you already know this, that the Lord, having saved a people out of the land of Egypt, afterward destroyed those who didn’t believe.

verses.name: Jude 1:6
verses.text: Angels who didn’t keep their first domain, but deserted their own dwelling place, he has kept in everlasting bonds under darkness for the judgment of the great day.

verses.name: Jude 1:7
verses.text: Even as Sodom and Gomorrah, and the cities around them, having, in the same way as these, given themselves over to sexual immorality and gone after strange flesh, are shown as an example, suffering the punishment of eternal fire.

verses.name: Jude 1:8
verses.text: Yet in the same way, these also in their dreaming defile the flesh, despise authority, and slander celestial beings.

verses.name: Jude 1:9
verses.text: But Michael, the archangel, when contending with the devil and arguing about the body of Moses, dared not bring against him an abusive condemnation, but said, “May the Lord rebuke you!”

verses.name: Jude 1:10
verses.text: But these speak evil of whatever things they don’t know. They are destroyed in these things that they understand naturally, like the creatures without reason.

verses.name: Jude 1:11
verses.text: Woe to them! For they went in the way of Cain, and ran riotously in the error of Balaam for hire, and perished in Korah’s rebellion.

verses.name: Jude 1:12
verses.text: These are hidden rocky reefs in your love feasts when they feast with you, shepherds who without fear feed themselves; clouds without water, carried along by winds; autumn trees without fruit, twice dead, plucked up by the roots;

verses.name: Jude 1:13
verses.text: wild waves of the sea, foaming out their own shame; wandering stars, for whom the blackness of darkness has been reserved forever.

verses.name: Jude 1:14
verses.text: About these also Enoch, the seventh from Adam, prophesied, saying, “Behold, the Lord came with ten thousands of his holy ones,

verses.name: Jude 1:15
verses.text: to execute judgment on all, and to convict all the ungodly of all their works of ungodliness which they have done in an ungodly way, and of all the hard things which ungodly sinners have spoken against him.”

verses.name: Jude 1:16
verses.text: These are murmurers and complainers, walking after their lusts (and their mouth speaks proud things), showing respect of persons to gain advantage.

verses.name: Jude 1:17
verses.text: But you, beloved, remember the words which have been spoken before by the apostles of our Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.name: Jude 1:18
verses.text: They said to you that “In the last time there will be mockers, walking after their own ungodly lusts.”

verses.name: Jude 1:19
verses.text: These are they who cause divisions, and are sensual, not having the Spirit.

verses.name: Jude 1:20
verses.text: But you, beloved, keep building up yourselves on your most holy faith, praying in the Holy Spirit.

verses.name: Jude 1:21
verses.text: Keep yourselves in God’s love, looking for the mercy of our Lord Jesus Christ to eternal life.

verses.name: Jude 1:22
verses.text: On some have compassion, making a distinction,

verses.name: Jude 1:23
verses.text: and some save, snatching them out of the fire with fear, hating even the clothing stained by the flesh.

verses.name: Jude 1:24
verses.text: Now to him who is able to keep them from stumbling, and to present you faultless before the presence of his glory in great joy,

verses.name: Jude 1:25
verses.text: to God our Savior, who alone is wise, be glory and majesty, dominion and power, both now and forever. Amen.

verses.name: Revelation 1:1
verses.text: This is the Revelation of Jesus Christ, which God gave him to show to his servants the things which must happen soon, which he sent and made known by his angel to his servant, John,

verses.name: Revelation 1:2
verses.text: who testified to God’s word, and of the testimony of Jesus Christ, about everything that he saw.

verses.name: Revelation 1:3
verses.text: Blessed is he who reads and those who hear the words of the prophecy, and keep the things that are written in it, for the time is at hand.

verses.name: Revelation 1:4
verses.text: John, to the seven assemblies that are in Asia: Grace to you and peace, from God, who is and who was and who is to come; and from the seven Spirits who are before his throne;

verses.name: Revelation 1:5
verses.text: and from Jesus Christ, the faithful witness, the firstborn of the dead, and the ruler of the kings of the earth. To him who loves us, and washed us from our sins by his blood;

verses.name: Revelation 1:6
verses.text: and he made us to be a Kingdom, priests to his God and Father; to him be the glory and the dominion forever and ever. Amen.

verses.name: Revelation 1:7
verses.text: Behold, he is coming with the clouds, and every eye will see him, including those who pierced him. All the tribes of the earth will mourn over him. Even so, Amen.

verses.name: Revelation 1:8
verses.text: “I am the Alpha and the Omega,” says the Lord God, “who is and who was and who is to come, the Almighty.”

verses.name: Revelation 1:9
verses.text: I John, your brother and partner with you in the oppression, Kingdom, and perseverance in Christ Jesus, was on the isle that is called Patmos because of God’s Word and the testimony of Jesus Christ.

verses.name: Revelation 1:10
verses.text: I was in the Spirit on the Lord’s day, and I heard behind me a loud voice, like a trumpet

verses.name: Revelation 1:11
verses.text: saying, “What you see, write in a book and send to the seven assemblies: to Ephesus, Smyrna, Pergamum, Thyatira, Sardis, Philadelphia, and to Laodicea.”

verses.name: Revelation 1:12
verses.text: I turned to see the voice that spoke with me. Having turned, I saw seven golden lamp stands.

verses.name: Revelation 1:13
verses.text: And among the lamp stands was one like a son of man, clothed with a robe reaching down to his feet, and with a golden sash around his chest.

verses.name: Revelation 1:14
verses.text: His head and his hair were white as white wool, like snow. His eyes were like a flame of fire.

verses.name: Revelation 1:15
verses.text: His feet were like burnished brass, as if it had been refined in a furnace. His voice was like the voice of many waters.

verses.name: Revelation 1:16
verses.text: He had seven stars in his right hand. Out of his mouth proceeded a sharp two-edged sword. His face was like the sun shining at its brightest.

verses.name: Revelation 1:17
verses.text: When I saw him, I fell at his feet like a dead man. He laid his right hand on me, saying, “Don’t be afraid. I am the first and the last,

verses.name: Revelation 1:18
verses.text: and the Living one. I was dead, and behold, I am alive forever and ever. Amen. I have the keys of Death and of Hades.

verses.name: Revelation 1:19
verses.text: Write therefore the things which you have seen, and the things which are, and the things which will happen hereafter;

verses.name: Revelation 1:20
verses.text: the mystery of the seven stars which you saw in my right hand, and the seven golden lamp stands. The seven stars are the angels of the seven assemblies. The seven lamp stands are seven assemblies.

verses.name: Revelation 2:1
verses.text: “To the angel of the assembly in Ephesus write: “He who holds the seven stars in his right hand, he who walks among the seven golden lamp stands says these things:

verses.name: Revelation 2:2
verses.text: “I know your works, and your toil and perseverance, and that you can’t tolerate evil men, and have tested those who call themselves apostles, and they are not, and found them false.

verses.name: Revelation 2:3
verses.text: You have perseverance and have endured for my name’s sake, and have not grown weary.

verses.name: Revelation 2:4
verses.text: But I have this against you, that you left your first love.

verses.name: Revelation 2:5
verses.text: Remember therefore from where you have fallen, and repent and do the first works; or else I am coming to you swiftly, and will move your lamp stand out of its place, unless you repent.

verses.name: Revelation 2:6
verses.text: But this you have, that you hate the works of the Nicolaitans, which I also hate.

verses.name: Revelation 2:7
verses.text: He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the assemblies. To him who overcomes I will give to eat from the tree of life, which is in the Paradise of my God.

verses.name: Revelation 2:8
verses.text: “To the angel of the assembly in Smyrna write: “The first and the last, who was dead, and has come to life says these things:

verses.name: Revelation 2:9
verses.text: “I know your works, oppression, and your poverty (but you are rich), and the blasphemy of those who say they are Jews, and they are not, but are a synagogue of Satan.

verses.name: Revelation 2:10
verses.text: Don’t be afraid of the things which you are about to suffer. Behold, the devil is about to throw some of you into prison, that you may be tested; and you will have oppression for ten days. Be faithful to death, and I will give you the crown of life.

verses.name: Revelation 2:11
verses.text: He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the assemblies. He who overcomes won’t be harmed by the second death.

verses.name: Revelation 2:12
verses.text: “To the angel of the assembly in Pergamum write: “He who has the sharp two-edged sword says these things:

verses.name: Revelation 2:13
verses.text: “I know your works and where you dwell, where Satan’s throne is. You hold firmly to my name, and didn’t deny my faith in the days of Antipas my witness, my faithful one, who was killed among you, where Satan dwells.

verses.name: Revelation 2:14
verses.text: But I have a few things against you, because you have there some who hold the teaching of Balaam, who taught Balak to throw a stumbling block before the children of Israel, to eat things sacrificed to idols, and to commit sexual immorality.

verses.name: Revelation 2:15
verses.text: So you also have some who hold to the teaching of the Nicolaitans likewise.

verses.name: Revelation 2:16
verses.text: Repent therefore, or else I am coming to you quickly, and I will make war against them with the sword of my mouth.

verses.name: Revelation 2:17
verses.text: He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the assemblies. To him who overcomes, to him I will give of the hidden manna, and I will give him a white stone, and on the stone a new name written, which no one knows but he who receives it.

verses.name: Revelation 2:18
verses.text: “To the angel of the assembly in Thyatira write: “The Son of God, who has his eyes like a flame of fire, and his feet are like burnished brass, says these things:

verses.name: Revelation 2:19
verses.text: “I know your works, your love, faith, service, patient endurance, and that your last works are more than the first.

verses.name: Revelation 2:20
verses.text: But I have this against you, that you tolerate your woman, Jezebel, who calls herself a prophetess. She teaches and seduces my servants to commit sexual immorality, and to eat things sacrificed to idols.

verses.name: Revelation 2:21
verses.text: I gave her time to repent, but she refuses to repent of her sexual immorality.

verses.name: Revelation 2:22
verses.text: Behold, I will throw her into a bed, and those who commit adultery with her into great oppression, unless they repent of her works.

verses.name: Revelation 2:23
verses.text: I will kill her children with Death, and all the assemblies will know that I am he who searches the minds and hearts. I will give to each one of you according to your deeds.

verses.name: Revelation 2:24
verses.text: But to you I say, to the rest who are in Thyatira, as many as don’t have this teaching, who don’t know what some call ‘the deep things of Satan,’ to you I say, I am not putting any other burden on you.

verses.name: Revelation 2:25
verses.text: Nevertheless, hold that which you have firmly until I come.

verses.name: Revelation 2:26
verses.text: He who overcomes, and he who keeps my works to the end, to him I will give authority over the nations.

verses.name: Revelation 2:27
verses.text: He will rule them with a rod of iron, shattering them like clay pots; as I also have received of my Father:

verses.name: Revelation 2:28
verses.text: and I will give him the morning star.

verses.name: Revelation 2:29
verses.text: He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the assemblies.

verses.name: Revelation 3:1
verses.text: “And to the angel of the assembly in Sardis write: “He who has the seven Spirits of God, and the seven stars says these things: “I know your works, that you have a reputation of being alive, but you are dead.

verses.name: Revelation 3:2
verses.text: Wake up, and keep the things that remain, which you were about to throw away, for I have found no works of yours perfected before my God.

verses.name: Revelation 3:3
verses.text: Remember therefore how you have received and heard. Keep it, and repent. If therefore you won’t watch, I will come as a thief, and you won’t know what hour I will come upon you.

verses.name: Revelation 3:4
verses.text: Nevertheless you have a few names in Sardis that did not defile their garments. They will walk with me in white, for they are worthy.

verses.name: Revelation 3:5
verses.text: He who overcomes will be arrayed in white garments, and I will in no way blot his name out of the book of life, and I will confess his name before my Father, and before his angels.

verses.name: Revelation 3:6
verses.text: He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the assemblies.

verses.name: Revelation 3:7
verses.text: “To the angel of the assembly in Philadelphia write: “He who is holy, he who is true, he who has the key of David, he who opens and no one can shut, and who shuts and no one opens, says these things:

verses.name: Revelation 3:8
verses.text: “I know your works (behold, I have set before you an open door, which no one can shut), that you have a little power, and kept my word, and didn’t deny my name.

verses.name: Revelation 3:9
verses.text: Behold, I give some of the synagogue of Satan, of those who say they are Jews, and they are not, but lie. Behold, I will make them to come and worship before your feet, and to know that I have loved you.

verses.name: Revelation 3:10
verses.text: Because you kept my command to endure, I also will keep you from the hour of testing, which is to come on the whole world, to test those who dwell on the earth.

verses.name: Revelation 3:11
verses.text: I am coming quickly! Hold firmly that which you have, so that no one takes your crown.

verses.name: Revelation 3:12
verses.text: He who overcomes, I will make him a pillar in the temple of my God, and he will go out from there no more. I will write on him the name of my God, and the name of the city of my God, the new Jerusalem, which comes down out of heaven from my God, and my own new name.

verses.name: Revelation 3:13
verses.text: He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the assemblies.

verses.name: Revelation 3:14
verses.text: “To the angel of the assembly in Laodicea write: “The Amen, the Faithful and True Witness, the Beginning of God’s creation, says these things:

verses.name: Revelation 3:15
verses.text: “I know your works, that you are neither cold nor hot. I wish you were cold or hot.

verses.name: Revelation 3:16
verses.text: So, because you are lukewarm, and neither hot nor cold, I will vomit you out of my mouth.

verses.name: Revelation 3:17
verses.text: Because you say, ‘I am rich, and have gotten riches, and have need of nothing;’ and don’t know that you are the wretched one, miserable, poor, blind, and naked;

verses.name: Revelation 3:18
verses.text: I counsel you to buy from me gold refined by fire, that you may become rich; and white garments, that you may clothe yourself, and that the shame of your nakedness may not be revealed; and eye salve to anoint your eyes, that you may see.

verses.name: Revelation 3:19
verses.text: As many as I love, I reprove and chasten. Be zealous therefore, and repent.

verses.name: Revelation 3:20
verses.text: Behold, I stand at the door and knock. If anyone hears my voice and opens the door, then I will come in to him, and will dine with him, and he with me.

verses.name: Revelation 3:21
verses.text: He who overcomes, I will give to him to sit down with me on my throne, as I also overcame, and sat down with my Father on his throne.

verses.name: Revelation 3:22
verses.text: He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the assemblies.”

verses.name: Revelation 4:1
verses.text: After these things I looked and saw a door opened in heaven, and the first voice that I heard, like a trumpet speaking with me, was one saying, “Come up here, and I will show you the things which must happen after this.”

verses.name: Revelation 4:2
verses.text: Immediately I was in the Spirit. Behold, there was a throne set in heaven, and one sitting on the throne

verses.name: Revelation 4:3
verses.text: that looked like a jasper stone and a sardius. There was a rainbow around the throne, like an emerald to look at.

verses.name: Revelation 4:4
verses.text: Around the throne were twenty-four thrones. On the thrones were twenty-four elders sitting, dressed in white garments, with crowns of gold on their heads.

verses.name: Revelation 4:5
verses.text: Out of the throne proceed lightnings, sounds, and thunders. There were seven lamps of fire burning before his throne, which are the seven Spirits of God.

verses.name: Revelation 4:6
verses.text: Before the throne was something like a sea of glass, similar to crystal. In the middle of the throne, and around the throne were four living creatures full of eyes before and behind.

verses.name: Revelation 4:7
verses.text: The first creature was like a lion, and the second creature like a calf, and the third creature had a face like a man, and the fourth was like a flying eagle.

verses.name: Revelation 4:8
verses.text: The four living creatures, each one of them having six wings, are full of eyes around and within. They have no rest day and night, saying, “Holy, holy, holy is the Lord God, the Almighty, who was and who is and who is to come!”

verses.name: Revelation 4:9
verses.text: When the living creatures give glory, honor, and thanks to him who sits on the throne, to him who lives forever and ever,

verses.name: Revelation 4:10
verses.text: the twenty-four elders fall down before him who sits on the throne, and worship him who lives forever and ever, and throw their crowns before the throne, saying,

verses.name: Revelation 4:11
verses.text: “Worthy are you, our Lord and God, the Holy One, to receive the glory, the honor, and the power, for you created all things, and because of your desire they existed, and were created!”

verses.name: Revelation 5:1
verses.text: I saw, in the right hand of him who sat on the throne, a book written inside and outside, sealed shut with seven seals.

verses.name: Revelation 5:2
verses.text: I saw a mighty angel proclaiming with a loud voice, “Who is worthy to open the book, and to break its seals?”

verses.name: Revelation 5:3
verses.text: No one in heaven above, or on the earth, or under the earth, was able to open the book, or to look in it.

verses.name: Revelation 5:4
verses.text: And I wept much, because no one was found worthy to open the book, or to look in it.

verses.name: Revelation 5:5
verses.text: One of the elders said to me, “Don’t weep. Behold, the Lion who is of the tribe of Judah, the Root of David, has overcome; he who opens the book and its seven seals.”

verses.name: Revelation 5:6
verses.text: I saw in the middle of the throne and of the four living creatures, and in the middle of the elders, a Lamb standing, as though it had been slain, having seven horns, and seven eyes, which are the seven Spirits of God, sent out into all the earth.

verses.name: Revelation 5:7
verses.text: Then he came, and he took it out of the right hand of him who sat on the throne.

verses.name: Revelation 5:8
verses.text: Now when he had taken the book, the four living creatures and the twenty-four elders fell down before the Lamb, each one having a harp, and golden bowls full of incense, which are the prayers of the saints.

verses.name: Revelation 5:9
verses.text: They sang a new song, saying, “You are worthy to take the book, and to open its seals: for you were killed, and bought us for God with your blood, out of every tribe, language, people, and nation,

verses.name: Revelation 5:10
verses.text: and made us kings and priests to our God, and we will reign on the earth.”

verses.name: Revelation 5:11
verses.text: I saw, and I heard something like a voice of many angels around the throne, the living creatures, and the elders; and the number of them was ten thousands of ten thousands, and thousands of thousands;

verses.name: Revelation 5:12
verses.text: saying with a loud voice, “Worthy is the Lamb who has been killed to receive the power, wealth, wisdom, strength, honor, glory, and blessing!”

verses.name: Revelation 5:13
verses.text: I heard every created thing which is in heaven, on the earth, under the earth, on the sea, and everything in them, saying, “To him who sits on the throne, and to the Lamb be the blessing, the honor, the glory, and the dominion, forever and ever! Amen!”

verses.name: Revelation 5:14
verses.text: The four living creatures said, “Amen!” Then the elders fell down and worshiped.

verses.name: Revelation 6:1
verses.text: I saw that the Lamb opened one of the seven seals, and I heard one of the four living creatures saying, as with a voice of thunder, “Come and see!”

verses.name: Revelation 6:2
verses.text: And behold, a white horse, and he who sat on it had a bow. A crown was given to him, and he came out conquering, and to conquer.

verses.name: Revelation 6:3
verses.text: When he opened the second seal, I heard the second living creature saying, “Come!”

verses.name: Revelation 6:4
verses.text: Another came out, a red horse. To him who sat on it was given power to take peace from the earth, and that they should kill one another. There was given to him a great sword.

verses.name: Revelation 6:5
verses.text: When he opened the third seal, I heard the third living creature saying, “Come and see!” And behold, a black horse, and he who sat on it had a balance in his hand.

verses.name: Revelation 6:6
verses.text: I heard a voice in the middle of the four living creatures saying, “A choenix of wheat for a denarius, and three choenix of barley for a denarius! Don’t damage the oil and the wine!”

verses.name: Revelation 6:7
verses.text: When he opened the fourth seal, I heard the fourth living creature saying, “Come and see!”

verses.name: Revelation 6:8
verses.text: And behold, a pale horse, and he who sat on it, his name was Death. Hades followed with him. Authority over one fourth of the earth, to kill with the sword, with famine, with death, and by the wild animals of the earth was given to him.

verses.name: Revelation 6:9
verses.text: When he opened the fifth seal, I saw underneath the altar the souls of those who had been killed for the Word of God, and for the testimony of the Lamb which they had.

verses.name: Revelation 6:10
verses.text: They cried with a loud voice, saying, “How long, Master, the holy and true, until you judge and avenge our blood on those who dwell on the earth?”

verses.name: Revelation 6:11
verses.text: A long white robe was given to each of them. They were told that they should rest yet for a while, until their fellow servants and their brothers, who would also be killed even as they were, should complete their course.

verses.name: Revelation 6:12
verses.text: I saw when he opened the sixth seal, and there was a great earthquake. The sun became black as sackcloth made of hair, and the whole moon became as blood.

verses.name: Revelation 6:13
verses.text: The stars of the sky fell to the earth, like a fig tree dropping its unripe figs when it is shaken by a great wind.

verses.name: Revelation 6:14
verses.text: The sky was removed like a scroll when it is rolled up. Every mountain and island were moved out of their places.

verses.name: Revelation 6:15
verses.text: The kings of the earth, the princes, the commanding officers, the rich, the strong, and every slave and free person, hid themselves in the caves and in the rocks of the mountains.

verses.name: Revelation 6:16
verses.text: They told the mountains and the rocks, “Fall on us, and hide us from the face of him who sits on the throne, and from the wrath of the Lamb,

verses.name: Revelation 6:17
verses.text: for the great day of his wrath has come; and who is able to stand?”

verses.name: Revelation 7:1
verses.text: After this, I saw four angels standing at the four corners of the earth, holding the four winds of the earth, so that no wind would blow on the earth, or on the sea, or on any tree.

verses.name: Revelation 7:2
verses.text: I saw another angel ascend from the sunrise, having the seal of the living God. He cried with a loud voice to the four angels to whom it was given to harm the earth and the sea,

verses.name: Revelation 7:3
verses.text: saying, “Don’t harm the earth, neither the sea, nor the trees, until we have sealed the bondservants of our God on their foreheads!”

verses.name: Revelation 7:4
verses.text: I heard the number of those who were sealed, one hundred forty-four thousand, sealed out of every tribe of the children of Israel:

verses.name: Revelation 7:5
verses.text: of the tribe of Judah were sealed twelve thousand, of the tribe of Reuben twelve thousand, of the tribe of Gad twelve thousand,

verses.name: Revelation 7:6
verses.text: of the tribe of Asher twelve thousand, of the tribe of Naphtali twelve thousand, of the tribe of Manasseh twelve thousand,

verses.name: Revelation 7:7
verses.text: of the tribe of Simeon twelve thousand, of the tribe of Levi twelve thousand, of the tribe of Issachar twelve thousand,

verses.name: Revelation 7:8
verses.text: of the tribe of Zebulun twelve thousand, of the tribe of Joseph twelve thousand, of the tribe of Benjamin were sealed twelve thousand.

verses.name: Revelation 7:9
verses.text: After these things I looked, and behold, a great multitude, which no man could count, out of every nation and of all tribes, peoples, and languages, standing before the throne and before the Lamb, dressed in white robes, with palm branches in their hands.

verses.name: Revelation 7:10
verses.text: They cried with a loud voice, saying, “Salvation be to our God, who sits on the throne, and to the Lamb!”

verses.name: Revelation 7:11
verses.text: All the angels were standing around the throne, the elders, and the four living creatures; and they fell on their faces before his throne, and worshiped God,

verses.name: Revelation 7:12
verses.text: saying, “Amen! Blessing, glory, wisdom, thanksgiving, honor, power, and might, be to our God forever and ever! Amen.”

verses.name: Revelation 7:13
verses.text: One of the elders answered, saying to me, “These who are arrayed in the white robes, who are they, and from where did they come?”

verses.name: Revelation 7:14
verses.text: I told him, “My lord, you know.” He said to me, “These are those who came out of the great tribulation. They washed their robes, and made them white in the Lamb’s blood.

verses.name: Revelation 7:15
verses.text: Therefore they are before the throne of God, they serve him day and night in his temple. He who sits on the throne will spread his tabernacle over them.

verses.name: Revelation 7:16
verses.text: They will never be hungry, neither thirsty any more; neither will the sun beat on them, nor any heat;

verses.name: Revelation 7:17
verses.text: for the Lamb who is in the middle of the throne shepherds them, and leads them to springs of life-giving waters. And God will wipe away every tear from their eyes.”

verses.name: Revelation 8:1
verses.text: When he opened the seventh seal, there was silence in heaven for about half an hour.

verses.name: Revelation 8:2
verses.text: I saw the seven angels who stand before God, and seven trumpets were given to them.

verses.name: Revelation 8:3
verses.text: Another angel came and stood over the altar, having a golden censer. Much incense was given to him, that he should add it to the prayers of all the saints on the golden altar which was before the throne.

verses.name: Revelation 8:4
verses.text: The smoke of the incense, with the prayers of the saints, went up before God out of the angel’s hand.

verses.name: Revelation 8:5
verses.text: The angel took the censer, and he filled it with the fire of the altar, and threw it on the earth. Thunders, sounds, lightnings, and an earthquake followed.

verses.name: Revelation 8:6
verses.text: The seven angels who had the seven trumpets prepared themselves to sound.

verses.name: Revelation 8:7
verses.text: The first sounded, and there followed hail and fire, mixed with blood, and they were thrown to the earth. One third of the earth was burned up, and one third of the trees were burned up, and all green grass was burned up.

verses.name: Revelation 8:8
verses.text: The second angel sounded, and something like a great burning mountain was thrown into the sea. One third of the sea became blood,

verses.name: Revelation 8:9
verses.text: and one third of the living creatures which were in the sea died. One third of the ships were destroyed.

verses.name: Revelation 8:10
verses.text: The third angel sounded, and a great star fell from the sky, burning like a torch, and it fell on one third of the rivers, and on the springs of the waters.

verses.name: Revelation 8:11
verses.text: The name of the star is called “Wormwood.” One third of the waters became wormwood. Many people died from the waters, because they were made bitter.

verses.name: Revelation 8:12
verses.text: The fourth angel sounded, and one third of the sun was struck, and one third of the moon, and one third of the stars; so that one third of them would be darkened, and the day wouldn’t shine for one third of it, and the night in the same way.

verses.name: Revelation 8:13
verses.text: I saw, and I heard an eagle, flying in mid heaven, saying with a loud voice, “Woe! Woe! Woe for those who dwell on the earth, because of the other voices of the trumpets of the three angels, who are yet to sound!”

verses.name: Revelation 9:1
verses.text: The fifth angel sounded, and I saw a star from the sky which had fallen to the earth. The key to the pit of the abyss was given to him.

verses.name: Revelation 9:2
verses.text: He opened the pit of the abyss, and smoke went up out of the pit, like the smoke from a burning furnace. The sun and the air were darkened because of the smoke from the pit.

verses.name: Revelation 9:3
verses.text: Then out of the smoke came locusts on the earth, and power was given to them, as the scorpions of the earth have power.

verses.name: Revelation 9:4
verses.text: They were told that they should not hurt the grass of the earth, neither any green thing, neither any tree, but only those people who don’t have God’s seal on their foreheads.

verses.name: Revelation 9:5
verses.text: They were given power, not to kill them, but to torment them for five months. Their torment was like the torment of a scorpion, when it strikes a person.

verses.name: Revelation 9:6
verses.text: In those days people will seek death, and will in no way find it. They will desire to die, and death will flee from them.

verses.name: Revelation 9:7
verses.text: The shapes of the locusts were like horses prepared for war. On their heads were something like golden crowns, and their faces were like people’s faces.

verses.name: Revelation 9:8
verses.text: They had hair like women’s hair, and their teeth were like those of lions.

verses.name: Revelation 9:9
verses.text: They had breastplates, like breastplates of iron. The sound of their wings was like the sound of chariots, or of many horses rushing to war.

verses.name: Revelation 9:10
verses.text: They have tails like those of scorpions, and stings. In their tails they have power to harm men for five months.

verses.name: Revelation 9:11
verses.text: They have over them as king the angel of the abyss. His name in Hebrew is “Abaddon”, but in Greek, he has the name “Apollyon”.

verses.name: Revelation 9:12
verses.text: The first woe is past. Behold, there are still two woes coming after this.

verses.name: Revelation 9:13
verses.text: The sixth angel sounded. I heard a voice from the horns of the golden altar which is before God,

verses.name: Revelation 9:14
verses.text: saying to the sixth angel who had the trumpet, “Free the four angels who are bound at the great river Euphrates!”

verses.name: Revelation 9:15
verses.text: The four angels were freed who had been prepared for that hour and day and month and year, so that they might kill one third of mankind.

verses.name: Revelation 9:16
verses.text: The number of the armies of the horsemen was two hundred million. I heard the number of them.

verses.name: Revelation 9:17
verses.text: Thus I saw the horses in the vision, and those who sat on them, having breastplates of fiery red, hyacinth blue, and sulfur yellow; and the horses’ heads resembled lions’ heads. Out of their mouths proceed fire, smoke, and sulfur.

verses.name: Revelation 9:18
verses.text: By these three plagues were one third of mankind killed: by the fire, the smoke, and the sulfur, which proceeded out of their mouths.

verses.name: Revelation 9:19
verses.text: For the power of the horses is in their mouths, and in their tails. For their tails are like serpents, and have heads, and with them they harm.

verses.name: Revelation 9:20
verses.text: The rest of mankind, who were not killed with these plagues, didn’t repent of the works of their hands, that they wouldn’t worship demons, and the idols of gold, and of silver, and of brass, and of stone, and of wood; which can’t see, hear, or walk.

verses.name: Revelation 9:21
verses.text: They didn’t repent of their murders, their sorceries, their sexual immorality, or their thefts.

verses.name: Revelation 10:1
verses.text: I saw a mighty angel coming down out of the sky, clothed with a cloud. A rainbow was on his head. His face was like the sun, and his feet like pillars of fire.

verses.name: Revelation 10:2
verses.text: He had in his hand a little open book. He set his right foot on the sea, and his left on the land.

verses.name: Revelation 10:3
verses.text: He cried with a loud voice, as a lion roars. When he cried, the seven thunders uttered their voices.

verses.name: Revelation 10:4
verses.text: When the seven thunders sounded, I was about to write; but I heard a voice from the sky saying, “Seal up the things which the seven thunders said, and don’t write them.”

verses.name: Revelation 10:5
verses.text: The angel whom I saw standing on the sea and on the land lifted up his right hand to the sky,

verses.name: Revelation 10:6
verses.text: and swore by him who lives forever and ever, who created heaven and the things that are in it, the earth and the things that are in it, and the sea and the things that are in it, that there will no longer be delay,

verses.name: Revelation 10:7
verses.text: but in the days of the voice of the seventh angel, when he is about to sound, then the mystery of God is finished, as he declared to his servants, the prophets.

verses.name: Revelation 10:8
verses.text: The voice which I heard from heaven, again speaking with me, said, “Go, take the book which is open in the hand of the angel who stands on the sea and on the land.”

verses.name: Revelation 10:9
verses.text: I went to the angel, telling him to give me the little book. He said to me, “Take it, and eat it up. It will make your stomach bitter, but in your mouth it will be as sweet as honey.”

verses.name: Revelation 10:10
verses.text: I took the little book out of the angel’s hand, and ate it up. It was as sweet as honey in my mouth. When I had eaten it, my stomach was made bitter.

verses.name: Revelation 10:11
verses.text: They told me, “You must prophesy again over many peoples, nations, languages, and kings.”

verses.name: Revelation 11:1
verses.text: A reed like a rod was given to me. Someone said, “Rise, and measure God’s temple, and the altar, and those who worship in it.

verses.name: Revelation 11:2
verses.text: Leave out the court which is outside of the temple, and don’t measure it, for it has been given to the nations. They will tread the holy city under foot for forty-two months.

verses.name: Revelation 11:3
verses.text: I will give power to my two witnesses, and they will prophesy one thousand two hundred sixty days, clothed in sackcloth.”

verses.name: Revelation 11:4
